# ▶ Archived 2026-06-30 from SESSION_HANDOFF.md (motif-branch trim)

> Moved out of the live handoff to keep it to the ACTIVE branch (feat/narrative-pattern-library / motif). Includes main's `Critical UX` + `Public MCP` tracks (merged into this branch on 2026-06-29) and all prior-session history (glossary / composition / roleplay / extraction / KG / campaign / Sessions 66–71). Every track's open-defer register is preserved verbatim below and still tracked on its own branch + in `main`.

>   `D-MCP-REFRESH-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - **#42 (`e8dd9bab`, knowledge)** — KG rebuild only wiped-and-redid-all. The incremental accumulate
>   path already existed (`scope=chapters`+range / `chapters_pending`, idempotent MERGE); added
>   `mode:"update"` to `RebuildRequest` that skips the confirm-gate + `_delete_project_graph` and routes

>   by #9 DI drift). Closes `D-KG-UPDATE-MODE`. Follow-ups: `D-KG-UPDATE-MODE-FE` (an "Update" button
>   distinct from destructive "Rebuild"), `D-KG-UPDATE-MODE-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - **#43 (`947dbac6`, glossary, M)** — 3000+ same-name/different-kind dups from pre-fix extractions;
>   nothing could clean them (mergeOne deliberately rejects cross-kind). Threaded a `crossKind` flag
>   through `mergeEntitiesCore`(required, 3 callers=false)→`mergeOne`(variadic) that skips the same-kind
>   gate + kind-scopes the EAV repoint to the winner's kind (same-kind path byte-identical); dedup
>   endpoint gains `?cross_kind=true`. Real-DB tests (collapse→1, winner keeps kind, loser soft-deleted;
>   default keeps kinds separate) + all Merge/Wiki regression green. **User runs
>   `POST /internal/books/{book_id}/dedup-name-variants?apply=true&cross_kind=true` per affected book**
>   (DRY-RUN by default). Follow-up: `D-CROSSKIND-DEDUP-FE` (user-facing trigger).

> **✅ DONE — #27/#29/#30 marker reconciliation (bookkeeping; verified, no code change).** The
> coalesce P2 shipped (`3cce85d9`/`a721c93c`/`cabbe35d`/`038bdbff`/`f61a83de`) but the bug-tracker
> markers were stale at `[I]`. Re-verified against code: the M2 `BatchConfirmCard` is **all-domain**,
> and `descriptorDomain` deliberately routes every `kg_`-prefixed descriptor → the `kg` domain
> (`ConfirmActionCard.tsx:66`, test `:302-318`), so **#29's KG schema-edit cards inherit the same
> coalesce** (N `kg_schema_edit` tokens fold into one "Confirm all", each commits via the single-confirm
> loop → `/v1/kg/actions/confirm`). #27/#29/#30 all flipped to `[x]`. **New deferred (optional residual,
> NOT the bug):** `D-KG-SCHEMA-PROPOSE-BATCH` (a deterministic `kg_schema_propose_batch` tool so the

> honest-partial loop) — both gate 2, token/UX optimizations only.

> **✅ DONE — #19 / `D-PLANNER-INFLIGHT-ABORT`: the propagation gap CLOSED.** Traced the full
> cancellation chain (chat disconnect → ai-gateway → glossary → provider-registry) and found it
> already cancels on connection-drop at EVERY hop EXCEPT one: `ai-gateway federation.executeTool`
> did `await client.callTool(params)` with NO abort signal, so a chat-turn stop closed the INBOUND
> MCP server (`res 'close'` → `server.close()` → the SDK aborts `extra.signal`) but left the OUTBOUND
> call to glossary running to completion (~39s zombie planner). Fix: thread `extra.signal` →
> `proxy-server.factory` → `handleCallTool` → `executeTool` → `client.callTool(params, undefined,
> {signal})`. Now an abort drops the glossary fetch → glossary `r.Context()` cancels → `runPlanner`
> `cctx` cancels → `client.Complete` aborts → provider-registry `r.Context()` cancels the provider
> call (the last two hops were ALREADY ctx-wired — verified by code + provider-registry stream_handler.go:13-16).
> +1 unit test (signal→executeTool) + updated the 4-arg assertion; ai-gateway 37/37; nest build clean;
> rebuilt+deployed, happy-path live smoke OK (real plan via ai-gateway on the fixed image).
> **▶ Deferred `D-PLANNER-INFLIGHT-ABORT-CAPTURE`** (gate 4: blocked on observability): the rigorous
> end-to-end ABORT A/B couldn't run here — the sync planner path writes NO `llm_jobs`/log row, and the
> local gemma is fast (~3s) + non-serializing (a queuing-timing A/B showed a post-abort call running
> in 0.9s, so it can't detect cancellation). Recipe for a future capture: add a sync-path observability
> row OR use a controlled slow+single-slot model, then assert the provider call terminates 'cancelled'
> shortly after the chat-turn stop. The CODE gap is closed regardless.

> **✅ DONE — #18 planner self-recheck loop: MECHANICAL hard-stop.** RC (already investigated):
> NO ReAct loop in the planner CODE — the "loops forever" is the chat agent re-calling the heavy
> (~39s) `glossary_plan` in a self-recheck cycle, bounded only by a SOFT skill rule. Fix
> ([stream_service.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/stream_service.py)): `PLANNER_TOOLS`
> may run AT MOST `PLANNER_CALLS_PER_TURN_CAP=1` per turn — a 2nd+ call is short-circuited BEFORE the
> MCP transport (no planner run) with a tool result steering the model to present/confirm the plan it
> already has (Kiro-style logic control). Cross-turn recovery re-plans = a fresh pass/counter, never
> blocked. Mirrors the existing Tier-A volume cap. +2 TDD tests (`TestPlannerHardStop`); test_stream_tools
> 40/40, frontend_tools+tool_discovery green. (test_stream_service's 13 fails proven PRE-EXISTING —
> `LLMModelNotFound`, identical on pristine HEAD — env needs a patched LLM client.) **#19 remainder
> stays `D-PLANNER-INFLIGHT-ABORT`** — aborting the IN-FLIGHT planner MCP call on session-stop is the
> larger cross-service cancellation (gate 2 + 4: structural + blocked on a live capture).

> across ALL children — only matters if a future caller batches >1 `execute_plan` with destructive ops
> (the card sends none today, so all such ops skip safely).

> **🚧 IN FLIGHT — public-MCP lazy tool-loading (L, session-scoped state machine).** The public
> edge dumps the full (permission-scoped) tool catalogue at an external agent → context bloat. User:
> minimum tool loads, default-on, **state machine BELONGS TO THE SESSION** (ChatGPT/Anthropic/LM
> Studio style — progressive disclosure, max control). The gateway already mints a stable session
> per key (`session_id = key_id`), so the activated set is Redis-backed per session. Plan:
> [docs/plans/2026-06-29-public-mcp-lazy-tool-loading.md].
> **✅ M1 DONE (this commit):** the SHARED `find_tools` in ai-gateway (`federation/find-tools.ts` —
> ported the proven `tool_discovery.py` search to TS; `handleListTools` prepends the find_tools
> meta-tool; `handleCallTool` intercepts find_tools LOCALLY, no provider route). Tests: find-tools 11
> + handlers 13 = 24 green; nest build clean.
> **✅ M2 DONE (this commit) — the per-SESSION state machine.** `session/tool-activation-store.ts`
> (Redis SADD+sliding-EXPIRE per session, keyed by session_id=key_id; in-memory fallback so it
> always works) + `session/tool-activation.ts` (fail-soft service: a store blip → minimal surface,
> never throws). `tool-policy.ts`: `find_tools` is the always-allowed discovery meta-tool (passes
> the request gate + survives the scope filter, any scope). `scope-filter.ts`: `filterListResponseText`
> takes an `activated` set → collapses the scoped list to `find_tools` ∪ activated (a FRESH session
> shows ONLY find_tools); `scopeFilterFindToolsResult` intersects ai-gateway's full-catalogue matches
> with the key's scope (anti-oracle) + harvests the in-scope names; `isFindToolsCall`. Controller:
> on a find_tools call → scope-filter the result + `activate()` the matches into the session; on
> tools/list → collapse to the session surface. **No scope widening** (gateRequestBody unchanged;
> activation governs VISIBILITY, not permission). Tests: tool-activation 18 new + 2 controller
> updated; full mcp-public-gateway suite 200/200; both gateways nest-build clean.
> **✅ /review-impl DONE — 1 MED found + FIXED.** Anti-oracle hole: `find_tools` smuggled inside a
> JSON-RPC **batch** bypassed `scopeFilterFindToolsResult` (`isFindToolsCall` is single-only), so a
> batched `[find_tools]` relayed its FULL-catalogue matches unfiltered (out-of-scope tool *names*
> leak — discovery, not callability; gateRequestBody still denies the call). Fix: `scope-filter.ts`
> extracts `filterOneFindToolsResult` (shared by single+batch) + new `findToolsCallIdKeys` /
> `scopeFilterFindToolsBatch` (id-matched, so a mixed batch's other-tool results are untouched);
> controller adds the batch branch. Tests: +4 (id-harvest, batch filter, mixed-batch-untouched,
> wildcard/empty/single short-circuit); full suite **204/204**; nest build clean. 2 accepted: find_tools
> absence bricking discovery (LOW — upstream always prepends it) + stale confidence note (COSMETIC).
> **✅ LIVE SMOKE DONE (`D-PMCP-LAZY-LIVE-SMOKE` CLEARED).** Both gateways rebuilt (`PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED=true`),
> minted a real SCOPED key (`read`+`domain:book`) via auth-service, drove an external-agent session
> against mcp-public-gateway :8219: (1) fresh `tools/list` → **only find_tools**; (2) `find_tools` →
> exactly the 3 in-scope book reads (no out-of-scope leak); (3) re-`tools/list` → **grew** to find_tools
> + the 3 activated; (4) `book_list` → relayed through ai-gateway → book-service, real books returned.
> **The live smoke caught a real bug my unit tests missed** (the recurring lesson): ai-gateway COLLAPSES
> a single-element batch REQUEST into a single OBJECT response, so the adversary's `[{find_tools}]` (the
> batch-bypass) returned an object my array-only `scopeFilterFindToolsBatch` skipped → out-of-scope leak.
> Fixed (handle object-or-array, id-matched) + re-smoked: BATCH cross-domain intent → leaked NONE; BATCH
> book intent → 3 reads, no write-tier leak. Suite 205/205.
> **▶ NEXT:** the lazy tool-loading feature (M1+M2+2 anti-oracle fixes) is COMPLETE + live-verified.
> Resume the critical-UX bug queue, or PR `fix/critical-ux-bugs`. Deferred: `D-FINDTOOLS-CHAT-SHARE`
> (chat-service still has its own local find_tools; pointing it at the gateway's is a separate refactor).

> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — #32 LLM-call full logging (XL, provider-registry + usage-billing).** "Not every call is logged + input/output not stored." **RC was wrong (4th this session):** usage-billing's store ALREADY persists encrypted request+response payloads + `request_status` — the gaps were pure plumbing in provider-registry: (a) `usage_outbox` only for `status=="completed"` + relay hardcoded `request_status:"success"` → failures unaudited; (b) `UsageOutbox` had no payload fields → empty `{}` stored. **Fix** (user chose full build, payloads capped): migration adds `usage_outbox.request_status/request_payload/response_payload`; `FinalizeWithUsageOutbox` `RETURNING …, input` + emits for EVERY terminal status (gate now `usage != nil`; cost 0 for failures so the cost SUM is unchanged — usage_logs is audit-only) with UTF-8-safe-truncated request+response payloads (cap env `LLM_USAGE_PAYLOAD_CAP_BYTES`=16384); relay carries status+payloads; usage-billing `parseUsageEvent` reads them → existing `writeUsageLog` encrypts. `usageLogParams.Input/OutputPayload` widened `map→any` (map for /record, string for jobs). Forward-compatible stream. **VERIFY:** both services full go test/vet green (provider-registry DB-mock INSERT-12/SELECT-13 + wire-contract; usage-billing payload/status parse). Plan [docs/plans/2026-06-28-llm-call-full-logging.md](../plans/2026-06-28-llm-call-full-logging.md). **Deferred `D-32-FULL-LOGGING-LIVE-SMOKE`** (full-stack real+forced-fail job round-trip). Bug doc #32 → `[x]`. **/review-impl ran → 2 fixes:** HIGH — user-cancel goes through `Cancel` (not the worker finalize), so cancelled calls were still unaudited → `Cancel` now emits the usage_outbox audit row (shared `insertUsageOutbox`); MED — stripped the plaintext payloads from the campaign_usage stream (`campaignFields`). +3 tests, full provider-registry suite green.

> **🔎 INVESTIGATED (not bounded) — #19 planner "stuck on gemma despite my selection".** Full STATIC trace found NO defect: both paths are wired end-to-end — session picker→`patchSession`→gateway transparent proxy→chat-service persist (`model_fields_set`)→`stream_service.py` read+inject as `model_ref`→glossary `toolPlan`; AND Settings `DefaultModelsCard`→`defaultModelsApi.set('planner')`→provider-registry `user_default_models`→`internalResolvePlannerModel` (reads `planner` default first, else best tool-calling chat → gemma when none set). No hardcoded literal, no passthrough drop. All 3 symptoms unify into ONE runtime cause: the planner is an **in-flight MCP call** that captured its resolution at turn start and **stopping the turn doesn't abort it** (~39s, finishes on whatever it resolved). → **Deferred `D-PLANNER-INFLIGHT-ABORT`** (gate 2 structural cross-service cancellation feature: chat-turn abort → cancel in-flight ai-gateway MCP call → provider call; gate 4 blocked on a live `model_ref` capture). **PARTIAL FIX SHIPPED** (/review-impl caught a static gap the trace missed): `glossary_plan` exposes `model_ref` to the LLM, and chat-service only injected the session pin when the model OMITTED it — a weak model filling `model_ref` overrode the user's session AND Settings planner. Fix: chat-service is now AUTHORITATIVE ([stream_service.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/stream_service.py) ~705 — session pin always wins; else strip the model's guess so the Settings default applies); 3 tests + 35 existing green. In-flight-abort remains deferred.
>
> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — #36 extraction LLM-call estimate undercount → feed the planner REAL chapter sizes (M, translation-service).** **The Explore RC was WRONG** (verified by probe): `plan_kind_batches` correctly caps at 3 kinds/batch (30 kinds → 10 batches, not "overloaded"), and the fallback already uses kind-derived `batches_per_chapter`. The REAL cause: both estimate call-sites fed the windowing-aware planner a HARDCODED `[{"text_length": 8000}] * N` — every chapter a fake 4000-token stand-in that NEVER windows, so the estimate collapsed to `chapters × batches` and was blind to real chapter size, while the executor windows large chapters (`windows × batches`). **Fix:** new best-effort `book_client.build_chapters_meta()` ([book_client.py](../../services/translation-service/app/book_client.py)) fetches real per-chapter `word_count_estimate` from the existing `GET /internal/books/{id}/chapters` (knowledge-service uses the same for its estimate) and feeds genuine `text_length`; both [extraction.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py) (HTTP/FE) and [mcp/server.py](../../services/translation-service/app/mcp/server.py) (agent) now use it. Best-effort: unfetchable size → legacy 8000 default; any fetch error → all-default (never blocks job creation). **VERIFY:** 8 new tests (word-counts filter/paginate/best-effort-error/empty + build-meta real-sizes/all-default + planner size-sensitivity probe-as-test) + admission/book_client green; 3 modules byte-compile; client field reads verified against the live book-service handler. **Deferred:** `D-EXTRACT-ESTIMATE-SCHEMA-PERBATCH` (separate opposite-direction over-count: schema of ALL kinds added to every per-batch unit — inflates INPUT/cost, coupled into windowing, needs its own analysis), `D-EXTRACT-ESTIMATE-SIZES-LIVE-SMOKE`. Bug doc #36 → `[x]`. **Lesson:** [[feedback_verify_explore_rootcause_before_fixing]] — caught a 3rd misdiagnosed Explore RC this session.
>
> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — #9 Build-KG progress "1/100" → server-computed real denominator (M, FS · knowledge-service + frontend).** The progress bar showed a meaningless "1/100": `items_total` came from the FE's OPTIONAL `StartJobRequest.items_total` (a placeholder), trusted verbatim. **Fix:** new shared `_count_scope_items()` ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py)) returns the real per-scope counts (chapters published-gated + pending chat turns + glossary entities), reused by the cost **estimate** AND both job **create** paths (`start` + `rebuild`) — so the stored denominator equals the work done and matches the preview. `StartJobRequest.items_total` is now **deprecated/ignored** (kept on the schema for back-compat, value discarded). Best-effort: a transient count failure stores NULL → FE indeterminate bar, never blocks the job start; counts run OUTSIDE the tx (H1). Public `start`/`rebuild` inject `book_client`+`pending_repo`+`glossary_client` (so test `dependency_overrides` apply); the internal-dispatch callers fall back to the per-worker singletons. Bonus: a correct `items_total` also tightens the bug-#37 `estimated_llm_calls` budget. **FE:** i18n label ×4 → "{{processed}} / {{total}} items (chapters + chat + glossary)". **VERIFY:** lifecycle test + **2 new** (server-compute ignores a client `99999` → stores `45+100+200=345`; best-effort NULL when glossary count raises) + 25 estimate/caller-pays tests green; 4 locale JSON valid, no test pins the old label. **Deferred `D-KG-ITEMS-TOTAL-LIVE-SMOKE`** (full-stack real extraction job). Bug doc #9 → `[x]`.
>
> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — #8 translation glossary truncation → content-aware output budget (S, translation-service).** "Translation glossary usually fails" on large entities: the glossary-translate worker hard-capped `max_tokens=4096` per entity, blind to glossary size → a long-description / many-attribute entity exceeded it → the structured JSON was truncated mid-output → `parse_translation_response` failed → entity marked failed. **Fix:** new `entity_output_budget(attributes)` ([glossary_translate_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_prompt.py)) mirrors the REAL payload — each attribute's actual `original_value` token count (CJK-aware via `estimate_tokens`) × a 3× CJK→Latin expansion + per-key JSON overhead — replacing the flat 4096 in the worker ([glossary_translate_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_worker.py) ~206). Clamped to `[4096 floor, env GLOSSARY_TRANSLATE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS=32768 ceiling]`: the floor keeps the old default for small entities (no regression), the budget scales above it so a large entity is no longer cut off. Per-attribute **chunking across calls** for a pathologically huge single entity stays **#26**'s scope (the ceiling caps it for now). **VERIFY:** 21 translation tests pass — 5 new budget unit (floor, grows-past-floor, ceiling-clamp, None/missing-safe, CJK-expands-more-than-Latin) + 2 new worker-wiring (large entity → max_tokens >4096; small → ==4096 back-compat) + 14 existing green. **Deferred `D-GLOSSARY-TRANSLATE-BUDGET-LIVE-SMOKE`** (lm_studio + a real large-entity job consumed off RabbitMQ — single-service, disproportionate now; worker test proves the budget reaches the LLM call). Bug doc #8 → `[x]`.
>
> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — #38/#39 cross-kind entity dedup at extraction write time (L, glossary-service).** The duplicate explosion ("739 entities for 5 chapters", "100% dups on re-run"). **User decision: cross-kind dedup at write time** (keep per-kind structure; resolve a name across ALL kinds before creating). New `findEntityCrossKind` ([extraction_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go)) matches by the app-maintained `normalized_name` column (same `textnorm.Normalize` fold as the per-kind resolver + dedup backstop → CJK trad/simpl + full-width + case variants collapse cross-kind); the writeback loop calls it on a same-kind miss and **merges into the matched entity under THAT entity's own kind** (`attrDefMap` holds all book kinds → compatible attrs land, rest skip), oldest-wins, race-safe under the existing per-book writeback advisory lock. Migration **`0042`** adds a `(book_id, normalized_name)` partial lookup index (seek, not per-book scan — the "extraction slow" angle). Pairs with #33 (kind-desc in prompt, already shipped) cutting wrong-kind tagging at source. **VERIFY:** build/vet clean; **3 cross-kind tests** (same-name-diff-kind reuses+merges description, distinct name still creates, 張若塵↔张若尘 folds to one) + extraction/resolver/pipeline/dedup suites green (full-package run only hit the 600s infra timeout, not a failure); migrate chain incl. 0042 applies. Bug doc #38/#39 → `[x]`. **Residual `D-CROSSKIND-DEDUP-REMEDIATION`** (prevents NEW dups; existing ones need #40 bulk-delete or a cross-kind extension of `/dedup-name-variants`). **Deferred:** the assistant `mcp_server.go` create path (line 407) wasn't given cross-kind dedup — low-volume, human-reviewed, and lacks the per-book lock; tracked as `D-MCP-PROPOSE-CROSSKIND-DEDUP`.
>
> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — #40 bulk-delete glossary entities (M, FS · glossary-service + frontend).** Gives the user the tool to clean up the #38/#39 extraction duplicates. **BE:** `POST /v1/glossary/books/{book_id}/entities/bulk-delete` ([entity_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/entity_handler.go) `bulkDeleteEntities`) mirrors `bulkSetEntityStatus` + the single-`deleteEntity` soft-delete — book-scoped, **Manage-grant** gated (destructive op, matches single delete), ≤1000 ids, drops malformed, soft-delete (`deleted_at`), returns `{deleted:N}` (foreign/already-deleted ids ignored). **FE:** the multi-select infra (checkboxes/select-all/`FloatingActionBar`) already existed for bulk-status — added a destructive Delete button → reusable `ConfirmDialog` → `bulkDeleteEntities` API → invalidate+clear ([GlossaryTab.tsx](../../frontend/src/pages/book-tabs/GlossaryTab.tsx), [api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/glossary/api.ts)); i18n ×4. **VERIFY:** glossary build/vet clean; **5 handler tests** (soft-delete book-scoped, already-deleted-not-recounted, empty→400, all-malformed→0, auth→401) + **grant-mapping guard** (view-grantee→403 confirms Manage) green on live test DB; FE tsc+eslint clean, 4 locales valid. **Residual `D-BULK-DELETE-UI-SMOKE`** (optional browser smoke — FE mirrors the working bulk-status pattern). Bug doc #40 → `[x]`.
>
> **✅ SHIPPED THIS SESSION — On-demand (lazy) MCP-tool loading is now the STANDARD for every agui chat surface (L, chat-service).** Root cause of the **empty/dead book-chat turn** (diagnosed from the user's lm_studio log `n_keep: 35791 >= n_ctx: 32000`): the **book/editor chat surfaces dumped the ENTIRE federated catalog — 145 tools ≈ ~35k tokens — on every turn**, overflowing qwen2.5-7b's 32k context (Gemma-4 has 131k, so it worked → the "model communication" red herring). Discovery mode (`find_tools` + per-pass `{core}∪{active}` advertisement) already existed but was gated to the *universal* surface only (the `else: tool_defs = catalog` branch shipped everything to book/editor). **Fix (user-directed "do both A and B, make it standard"):** discovery is now the standard transport for ALL agui non-admin surfaces (fresh + resume paths), with a **per-surface HOT SET** seeded into the discovery active-set on pass 1 = the domain(s) the surface's injected SKILL names directly (so the skill works with zero find_tools hop); every other domain is lazy via `find_tools`. **Live-proven against the real 145-tool catalog:** universal 145→**7** (95%↓, pure discovery), book-scoped 145→**51** (all 42 `glossary_*` + core + glossary frontend), editor 145→**52** (= book + `propose_edit`). All three now fit 32k. **Key files:** [tool_discovery.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/tool_discovery.py) (`surface_hot_domains`/`hot_tool_names` — prefix-scoped, new-domain-services lazy-by-default; **`propose_edit` demoted from always-on core → editor-frontend-only**, it's meaningless without an open chapter), [stream_service.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/stream_service.py) (`discovery_eligible` replaces `universal_surface`; `discovery_seed_names` threaded fresh+resume→`_emit_chat_turn`→`_stream_with_tools`; gateway-down agui still advertises frontend tools), [glossary_skill.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/glossary_skill.py) (hint: use `find_tools` for off-glossary asks). **VERIFY:** **437 chat-service tests pass** (1 pre-existing unrelated collection error `test_working_memory.py` = a mount-depth `parents[3]` artifact, NOT my code); 32 discovery tests incl. 9 new (hot-set scoping, lazy-tail, pass-1 advertisement); the previously-passing `test_book_context_advertises_glossary_edit_not_propose_edit` validated the propose_edit demotion. **Live smoke:** real catalog scoping (above) + chat-service boots healthy with the fix loaded. **Reverted** the prior session's wrong-premise SSE-keepalive change (it was uncommitted; based on the disproven cold-start theory). **Note:** this was a newly-discovered design defect, not one of the 40 numbered bugs.
>
> **🔎 /review-impl ran (1 fix-now applied, 1 documented):** **FIXED** — the `discovery_seed_names` threading (`stream_response → _emit_chat_turn → _stream_with_tools`) was unguarded: every integration test used a 1-tool catalog so the seed was always ∅, indistinguishable from a dropped param. Added `test_book_surface_seeds_glossary_hot_set_and_lazy_tail` (multi-domain catalog → asserts glossary seeded, book/translation lazy, both at the loop boundary + the first-pass payload). **Considered & REJECTED** — adding `active_tool_names` to the `find_tools` exclude: it would turn "you already have this tool" into an empty "no match" → the agent falsely denies a capability. Current re-suggest behavior is strictly safer; left as-is.
>
> **▶ Deferred (this fix):**
> - 🟡 `D-CHAT-RESUME-SURFACE-SEED` — `resume_stream_response` seeds the editor-superset `{glossary}` hot set for EVERY resume (it can't know the original surface — sets `editor_context={"resumed": True}`). A *universal*-surface suspend (generic `confirm_action`) thus resumes with 42 glossary tools it never had on the fresh turn (~10k wasted tokens, harmless). **Conscious tradeoff** (confirm cards are overwhelmingly glossary, so seeding glossary HELPS the dominant "confirm kind → propose attributes" follow-up). **Gate reason 2 (structural):** the precise fix stores the surface's hot domains in `chat_suspended_runs` and re-applies on resume — a schema/payload change. Low priority.
> - 🟡 `D-PROVIDER-STREAM-ERROR-SURFACE` — when a provider returns a 200 SSE that carries an `{"error": {...}}` frame (e.g. lm_studio context-overflow), `streamOpenAICompat` ([streamer.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/provider/streamer.go)) decodes only `choices`/`usage` → the error is swallowed into a silent empty turn instead of surfaced. **Gate reason 4 (blocked):** I could NOT reproduce the wire format — lm_studio is currently loaded with a larger context, so an overflow `data:` error frame never appeared on the wire (the `[ERROR]` is server-side log only). Adding a speculative `error`-field parser would be unverified. **Trigger:** revisit when a provider error frame is actually captured on the wire (load a small-`n_ctx` model + overflow it), then parse + surface it. Low blast radius — the root-cause tool-dump fix already prevents the overflow.
> - 🟡 `D-CHAT-LAZY-LOAD-LIVE-SMOKE` — the unit+integration tests + the real-catalog scoping smoke cover the gating; a full **chat-UI** turn (open book → glossary "Ask AI" on qwen2.5-7b → confirm a non-empty answer with the small tool payload) wasn't driven (the overflow no longer reproduces since lm_studio has a big context now, so it's not a discriminating smoke). Optional: drive it with a small-`n_ctx` model to see the before/after directly.

> **▶ IN FLIGHT — Confirm-card server-coalesce (#27/#29/#30/#18), XL.** Plan: [docs/plans/2026-06-28-confirm-card-server-coalesce.md](../plans/2026-06-28-confirm-card-server-coalesce.md). Root cause VERIFIED: the chat run lifecycle honours ONE confirm card per turn (`chat_suspended_runs` single `pending_tool_call` PK run_id; tool loop `break`s on the first frontend confirm, dropping the tail; first confirm deletes the run row). User chose **full server coalesce**. **P1 SHIPPED (this session):** new deterministic `glossary_propose_batch` MCP tool (explicit ops → `ValidatePlan` → ONE `execute_plan` card → existing deterministic executor, NO planner LLM) + skill hardened (one-card-per-turn HARD rule, no-loop prohibition, glossary_plan ≤1/turn). VERIFY: glossary `go build`+`vet` clean; **3 new propose-batch tests green against live test DB** (round-trip mint→confirm→2 kinds created via multi-op tier-ordered executor; unknown-op rejected; empty-ops rejected); 2 chat-service skill-prompt regression tests pass. **▶ P2 NEXT (load-bearing):** run-loop auto-coalesce of stray multi-card turns — chat-service accumulates every confirm_token minted in a turn; at the suspend point, if >1, bundle into ONE `glossary.batch` card; new glossary `POST /actions/confirm-batch` (+`/preview-batch`) loops the EXISTING per-descriptor effects, claims each jti, returns one aggregated summary. FE routes the batch descriptor. Then cross-service live smoke + mark #27/#29/#30/#18 `[x]`.

> **UX BUG TRACK** (`docs/bugs/2026-06-27-critical-ux-bugs.md`): fixing ~40 critical UX bugs. **All 40 triaged: 15 fixed `[x]`, 24 investigated `[I]` (RC+Fix written), 0 unknown.** Done this session: #34, #37, #35, #17, then the full glossary/KG INVESTIGATION (6 clusters, `14edaede`), then the **data-safety milestone**: **#14** KG-rebuild confirm-guard + AWS typed-confirm (`bf30eed3`), **#33** kind-description→extraction prompt (`73f87476`), **#31** entity-GET returns attribute options (`26012411`).
>
> **Two agent misdiagnoses caught by verifying before fixing:** #31's real cause was the entity GET dropping `ad.options` (NOT the extraction profile); **#10**'s FE language wiring is ALREADY complete (TimelineTab→useTimeline→listTimeline all pass `language`) — the real issue is events stored in English/romanized + unanchored participant names (extraction-language/anchoring), so #10 stays `[I]` and needs the user's repro. **Lesson:** an Explore agent's root-cause hypothesis must be code-verified before it drives a fix.
>
> **Recommended next milestones** (from the investigation): (2) Extraction correctness #38/#39/#7/#26 — needs the entity-identity decision (one entity per (book,name) vs per (book,kind,name)); (3) Confirm-card batching #27/#29/#30/#18 (shared run_id root, verified); (4) Kinds/standards FE #22/#21/#25. **Deferred follow-ups:** D-KG-UPDATE-MODE (incremental rebuild), D-KG-REBUILD-CONFIRM-LIVE-SMOKE, D-EXTRACTION-KIND-DESC-LIVE-SMOKE, D-GLOSSARY-SELECT-OPTIONS-LIVE-SMOKE.

# ▶▶ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE — **Public MCP Gateway P0+P1+kit+P2+P3 SHIPPED · P4 D+A+B+C+E+F SHIPPED (all 6 Wave-C slices) · D-PMCP-WORKER-CARRIER + E2E-LIVE-SMOKE CLEARED (priced worker-enqueue spend now key-attributed + per-key capped, proven live) · /review-impl found+fixed a HIGH (retry re-spend bypassed the per-key cap) · knowledge/worker-ai carrier ALSO live-proven (e2e smoke caught+fixed a 2nd HIGH: a router-level get_current_user 401'd the kg replay path) · D-PMCP-AUDIT-DOWNSTREAM-OUTCOME RESOLVED (edge audits `tool_error` for a downstream JSON-RPC error, live-smoked) · D-PMCP-BATCH-WCONFIRM-DIVERT RESOLVED (a default key's BATCH write_confirm propose now diverts per-item + token-stripped, fail-closed; /review-impl caught+fixed a mixed-list catalogue leak; edge 134/134) · **P5 OAuth slices 1+2a+2b SHIPPED — discovery (RFC 9728 PRM + RFC 8414 AS-meta + JWKS) + edge LOCAL RS256 verify w/ RFC 8707 aud binding (S9); auth-code+PKCE flow (authorize/consent/token, single-use codes, refresh rotation, anti-open-redirect) PG-proven; FE scope-aware consent page w/ per-scope downscoping (5/5 vitest); open DCR (RFC 7591) /oauth/register w/ flag+per-IP-rate-limit+append-only audit; catalog_* OD-7 public-discovery read provider (federation live-smoked: tools/list+tools/call 31 public books). **P5 OAUTH COMPLETE — all 4 slices**** · branch `feat/public-mcp-gateway` · 2026-06-28 · HEAD `f5e7bcf6` (+slice 4 uncommitted)

> **TRACK:** expose MCP to external/third-party agents over the internet with a NEW security model. Full doc set: [docs/specs/2026-06-26-public-mcp/](../specs/2026-06-26-public-mcp/) (README + 01 feature-catalog · 02 interface-matrix · 03 security-design+edge-cases+spikes · 04 plan · 05 tool-scope-map).
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P0 — the serial keystone):** new **`services/mcp-public-gateway`** (TS/NestJS) — the PUBLIC security edge. An external agent connects (via api-gateway-bff `/mcp`) with its OWN bearer credential; the edge authenticates it, **strips the entire inbound `x-*` namespace + mints a fresh envelope** (PUB-9 — a smuggled `X-Admin-Token`/`X-Internal-Token`/`X-User-Id` is discarded), and relays to **`ai-gateway/mcp` ONLY** (no `/mcp/admin` route, holds no admin token). Refuses a key in the query string (H-R). OFF unless `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED=true` (Q-GATE kill-switch). P0 uses a static test credential (`MCP_PUBLIC_TEST_KEY`→`MCP_PUBLIC_TEST_USER_ID`); read tools only, nothing priced/written.
> - **Wired:** BFF `/mcp` proxy ([gateway-setup.ts](../../services/api-gateway-bff/src/gateway-setup.ts) — unversioned, matched before /v1; optional+defaulted url so existing callers/tests unchanged) · docker-compose service `mcp-public-gateway` (:8219→8211, depends ai-gateway healthy) · `language-rule.yaml` row (typescript) · ai-gateway `X-Internal-Token` compare made **constant-time** ([util/auth.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/util/auth.ts), both /mcp + /mcp/admin).
> - **VERIFY:** mcp-public-gateway `nest build` clean + **8/8 unit tests** (auth gate, Q-GATE flag-off, H-R query-reject, PUB-9 strip/admin-isolation, 502 on upstream down, helpers); ai-gateway **55/55**; api-gateway-bff **115/115** (also fixed 2 PRE-EXISTING broken test fixtures missing learningUrl/compositionUrl). **⏳ NOT yet live-smoked** end-to-end on a stack-up (external client→BFF→edge→ai-gateway→provider) — `D-PMCP-P0-LIVE-SMOKE`.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P1 BACKEND — credential subsystem, run via /loom, size L):** the "new security setting" exists end-to-end on the BE.
> - **auth-service:** `mcp_api_keys` table (migration in [migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go) — Argon2id `key_hash`, `key_prefix` lookup, scopes, spend_cap, rate_limit_rpm, `allow_self_confirm`, status, expiry; CASCADE on user delete + active-prefix index). [mcp_keys.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_keys.go): `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /v1/account/mcp-keys` (JWT owner-only; **secret shown once** on create; creation Q-GATE-flag-gated → 403 when off) + `POST /internal/mcp-keys/resolve` (X-Internal-Token; prefix lookup → constant-time Argon2id verify → **account_status='active' check (H-L)** → returns {user_id,key_id,scopes,allow_self_confirm,caps}; uniform 401 anti-oracle; **per-prefix attempt cap before Argon2 (H-H)**). Reuses `authpwd.Hash/Verify` (Argon2id).
> - **mcp-public-gateway edge:** `KeyResolver` swapped from the static key to a real `POST ${AUTH_SERVICE_URL}/internal/mcp-keys/resolve` call with a ~45s in-mem TTL cache (bounds the hot path + revocation lag); identity is auth-derived, never client-supplied. Dev static key retained as an optional smoke convenience.
> - **Wired:** compose — edge gets `AUTH_SERVICE_URL` + `depends_on auth-service healthy`; auth-service gets `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED` passthrough.

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P2 knowledge-authz — H-U + OD-8, run via /loom + /review-impl, size M):** the security gate for public knowledge exposure. **knowledge-service only.**
> - **H-U** — new `_require_project_owner_memory(ctx)` ([executor.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/tools/executor.py)) wired into `memory_recall_entity` / `memory_timeline` / `memory_remember` (the 3 that had only IMPLICIT user_id scoping). `memory_search` (owner-keyed `projects_repo.get` — `WHERE user_id AND project_id`, verified) + `memory_forget` (user_id-scoped `_INVALIDATE_FACT_CYPHER`, verified) were already owner-safe → documented in-line, not double-gated.
> - **OD-8** — `_resolve_project_owner` / `_resolve_project_owner_and_level` ([graph_schema_tools.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/tools/graph_schema_tools.py)) now resolve **owned-only when `ctx.mcp_key_id` is set** (a public key never inherits the owner's share-grants). First-party (mcp_key_id None) keeps the grant path. This single chokepoint covers ALL `kg_*` (graph_schema) + `build_tools` (kg_build_graph/build_wiki/run_benchmark, which call `_resolve_project_owner_and_level`); `kg_project_create` is owner-only by construction (`resolve_grant == OWNER`). So every project-scoped knowledge tool is owner-gated for public keys.
> - **Behavior change (intentional):** `memory_remember` to an UNRESOLVABLE project (deleted / not-owned) now REJECTS ("project not found") instead of the old D6 "fall back to a direct write" — that fallback silently wrote a fact tagged to an orphan/foreign project_id. Test updated.

One accepted minor (memory_remember does 2 PG round-trips on the confirm path).

Verified: gate runs for all 4 memory tools, replace preserves mcp_key_id, UUID/empty/nonexistent edges.

The prior H-I(memory) commit (1460c9d8) added `project_id` to ARG_MODELS+TOOL_DEFINITIONS but NOT the server functions, so it was unreachable over MCP (only the bespoke /internal path). Added `project_id` (shared `_PROJECT_ID_ARG`) to the 4 memory + 5 kg-read server functions, threaded into dispatch.

Fixed: added `owner_only` param ([graph_views.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/graph_views.py), default False preserves the HTTP route) short-circuiting a non-owner public call before grants; MCP handler passes `owner_only=ctx.mcp_key_id is not None`. **MED** — the `project_id` arg on `kg_entity_edge_timeline` was a no-op (entity-scoped) → removed it (model/schema/server-fn).

Added the 2 params (real bug: MCP agents couldn't set reasoning effort).

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P2 edge scope-filter — H-E/H-F/OD-5, run via /loom + /review-impl, size L):** the keystone that makes Wave A safe — the public edge stops being a transport-transparent relay and **enforces scope** (the 2nd of the two PUB-3 checks; provider ownership/tier is the 1st). Plan: [2026-06-27-public-mcp-edge-scope-filter.md](../plans/2026-06-27-public-mcp-edge-scope-filter.md).
> - **Scope model (LOCKED, spec D3):** flat `scopes[]` mixing **tier** (`read`/`paid_read`/`write_auto`/`write_confirm`) + **domain** (`domain:book`…). A tool is callable iff key holds its tier **and** `domain:<d>` for EVERY domain it touches. **auth-service untouched** — `scopes TEXT[]` already stores `domain:*` verbatim; the FE composes the array.
> - **Edge (`services/mcp-public-gateway`):** new [tool-policy.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts) — authoritative **allowlist** (~95 tools × {tier, domains}) from scope-map §2, with H-F cross-domain (`translation_start_extraction`→[translation,glossary]; `composition_generate`→[composition,glossary,knowledge]; `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich`→[lore_enrichment,glossary,knowledge]) + `jobs`/`settings` own explicit domain; admin/secret/`book_delete`/`purge` ABSENT → denied. `isToolAllowed` is **default-deny** (unknown→deny=H-E; `*` dev-key bypass). new [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts) — `gateRequestBody` (deny a disallowed `tools/call` before relay, fail-closed; whole-batch deny) + `filterListResponseText` (rewrite `tools/list` to in-scope tools only; **drift-log** any federated tool absent from the table; fail-closed empty list on unparseable JSON). [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts) wires both around the relay.
> - **FE:** [api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/api.ts) `MCP_DOMAINS`+`domainScope`+`splitScopes`+`DEFAULT_MCP_DOMAINS` (OD-5 = book+glossary+knowledge); [McpCreateKeyDialog.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpCreateKeyDialog.tsx) domain multiselect → composes `domain:*` into `scopes`; [McpAccessTab.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpAccessTab.tsx) tier vs domain chips; i18n ×4.
> - **🔎 /review-impl: 1 MED fixed + 3 LOW accepted.** **MED** — a STORED `*` scope (the auth create endpoint doesn't validate `scopes[]`, so a user could `POST scopes:["*"]`) would bypass the whole filter; fixed by **stripping `*` from auth-resolved scopes** at the edge ([key-resolver.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/auth/key-resolver.ts) — the `*` full-bypass is the dev static key's privilege ONLY, resolved earlier). LOW (accepted): `-32601` for a denied call is intentional **anti-oracle** (denied ≡ nonexistent); policy-name drift→deny is fail-safe + drift-logged; exact-match scope tokens are FE-controlled. **Verified no bypass:** ai-gateway is tools-only (`capabilities:{tools:{}}`, no resources/prompts); edge relays its OWN re-serialized parsed body → no parser-differential.
> - **VERIFY:** edge **40/40** (tool-policy/scope-filter unit + controller integration incl. denied-call-no-relay, list-filter, wildcard-strip) + `nest build` clean; FE McpAccess **4/4** + `tsc` 0 + ESLint 0; i18n ×4 valid JSON; provider-gate OK.
> - **✅ LIVE-SMOKE DONE (D-PMCP-P2-LIVE-SMOKE):** rebuilt edge+auth with `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED=true` on the infra stack; logged in as the test user, minted a `read+domain:knowledge` key and a `read+domain:book` key via the real `POST /v1/account/mcp-keys` (P1 create proven live). Through the edge (`:8219/mcp`): (a) KG-key `tools/list` → **exactly the 10 knowledge reads**, book/glossary/etc. stripped; (b) KG-key `tools/call book_get` → **denied `-32601` "not available to this key"** (domain isolation); (c) KG-key `tools/call kg_schema_read` → **relayed** (reached the provider, downstream `x-session-id` error — NOT a scope deny); (d) BOOK-key `tools/list` → exactly the 5 book reads; (e) BOOK-key `tools/call book_list` → **real owner-scoped data** (Dracula etc., `access_level:owner` — full chain edge→ai-gateway→book-service, OD-8 holds). The **drift-log fired** for 19 federated tools absent from the policy table (proof H-E default-deny works on the live registry). Smoke keys revoked after. (Cross-*tenant* — key for user A vs user B's id — not separately smoked; domain isolation + owner-scoping proven, and the provider H-U/OD-8 gate is unit-proven.)
> - **TWO GAPS the smoke surfaced (deferred, NOT Wave-A-read blockers except knowledge):** (1) **policy-table drift** — `D-PMCP-POLICY-TABLE-DRIFT`: ~13 composition tools (`motif_*`×9, `arc_import_analyze`, `arc_suggest`, `conformance_run`, `get_mine_job`) + `settings_model_delete` exist in the live registry but postdate the scope-map §2 audit → correctly **default-denied** (safe), but need classifying when composition/settings get their P2 provider fanout. (2) **edge mints no `X-Session-Id`** — `D-PMCP-EDGE-SESSION-ID`: knowledge tools require it (`build_tool_context`) so knowledge reads reach the provider then error; **book reads work** (so it's knowledge-specific, not edge-wide). Belongs to the edge envelope (P0/P1), not the scope filter.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P2 OD-8 owned-only sweep — run via /loom + /review-impl ×2 + LIVE-SMOKE, size L):** closes the Wave-A **tenant-isolation gap** — a public MCP key (X-Mcp-Key-Id) could read/act on books merely SHARED to the owner. Now a public key reaches a book ONLY as its OWNER, never via an inherited collaboration grant.
> - **Kit pre-step:** Python [guards.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_mcp/guards.py) `require_book_owner` escalates the required level to OWNER when `is_owner_only(ctx)` → **auto-fixes composition + translation** (both consume the kit guard). Go [guard.go](../../sdks/go/loreweave_mcp/guard.go) `BookOwnerGuard` same; [identity.go](../../sdks/go/loreweave_mcp/identity.go) new exported `ContextWithMcpKeyID` (lets a bespoke middleware light up `OwnerOnlyFromCtx`).
> - **book-service** [mcp_guard.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/mcp_guard.go): `mcpRequireGrant` owner-equality (owner pre-resolved) when owner-only; [mcp_tools_read.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/mcp_tools_read.go): **`book_list` filter drops the collaborator clause** when owner-only (the leak the live-smoke found). **glossary-service** [ownership.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/ownership.go): `checkGrant` requires GrantOwner (separate check so the write-active gate is undisturbed); [mcp_server.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/mcp_server.go) bespoke middleware lifts `X-Mcp-Key-Id` via the kit helper.
> - **Design (verified):** `GrantOwner ⟺ actual owner` (E0 `none<view<edit<manage<owner`; shares confer ≤manage), so "require Owner when owner-only" = "owned, not shared". **Wiring verified on all 5 paths**: book via `NewStatelessHandler`→`IdentityMiddleware`; glossary via `ContextWithMcpKeyID`; composition/translation via `build_tool_context`; knowledge own.
> - **🔎 /review-impl ×2:** (1) found+fixed a misleading wiring doc comment; verified no entity-scoped bypass in glossary (every book tool → `checkGrant`, unlike knowledge's kg_entity_edge_timeline), cache can't poison across owner_only modes. (2) **LIVE-SMOKE found a HIGH** the unit tests missed — `book_list` (no per-book guard) enumerated SHARED books for a public key → fixed with an owner-only filter (`bookListFilter`).
> - **✅ LIVE-SMOKE DONE (D-PMCP-OD8-LIVE-SMOKE):** cross-tenant — user B owns a book shared (edit grant) to test-user A; A's first-party JWT reads it (HTTP 200, grant intact); A's PUBLIC key via the edge → `book_get`(shared)=**denied** "book not accessible", `book_get`(own)=ok, `book_list`=**shared book absent**. Proves OD-8 affects ONLY public keys.
> - **VERIFY:** Go kit OD-8 tests + book `TestMCP_ScopeGuard_OD8_OwnedOnly` + `TestBookListFilter_OD8` + glossary `TestCheckGrant_OD8_PublicKeyOwnedOnly` green; Py kit 45/45 (2 OD-8); composition 33/33 (OD-8 wiring test); translation 33 pass (1 pre-existing unrelated fail, proven via stash); vet + provider-gate clean. **Idempotency (H-G) deferred to Wave-B (writes only).**
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P3 slice 1 — edge per-key rate-limit / PUB-8, run via /loom + /review-impl + live-smoke, size L):** a leaked/abusive public key is bounded to its `rate_limit_rpm` via a Redis fixed-window counter, shared across edge replicas, enforced BEFORE any work.
> - **New:** [rate-limiter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/ratelimit/rate-limiter.ts) — driver-free `RateLimiter` over a `RateLimitStore` iface; [redis-store.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/ratelimit/redis-store.ts) — ioredis adapter (INCRBY + EXPIRE NX in one multi), config-gated from `REDIS_URL` (unset → limiter DISABLED/fail-open, logged once). **Wired** in [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts): resolve → **rate-limit** → scope-gate → relay; 429 + `Retry-After` + JSON-RPC `-32029`. [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts): `isWriteRequest` (fail-policy classifier) + `countToolCalls` (batch weight). compose: `REDIS_URL` + `depends_on redis`.
> - **PUB-8 fail policy:** Redis outage → **reads fail OPEN** (availability), **writes fail CLOSED** (safety). Counter weighted by tool-call count so a **batch can't smuggle N executions** past a per-request limit.
> - **🔎 /review-impl: 1 MED fixed** (batch amplification → weighted increment by `countToolCalls`); verified fail-policy ungameable (unknown "writes" fail-open but scope-gate denies them), bad keys don't consume counters, EXPIRE NX, atomic INCRBY, fast-fail on outage. Fixed-window 2× boundary burst = accepted v1 tradeoff.
> - **VERIFY:** edge **56/56** (limiter logic both fail directions, batch weight, isWriteRequest, countToolCalls, controller 429+Retry-After) + build + provider-gate clean. **Live-smoke:** rebuilt edge w/ `REDIS_URL`; key `rpm=3` → calls 1-3 `200`, calls 4-6 `429 / -32029 / retry_after=56 / limit=3` (per-key Redis counter, end-to-end). Single-service (edge only).
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P3 spend rails — PUB-12 BYOK-only + per-key attribution / H-B/H-C/PUB-10-11-12, run via /loom + /review-impl + live-smoke, size XL, A+B bundled per PO):** the money path. Plan: [2026-06-27-public-mcp-p3-spend-rails.md](../plans/2026-06-27-public-mcp-p3-spend-rails.md).
> - **KEY FINDING (reshaped the plan):** PUB-12 **cannot** live at the edge — whether a call uses BYOK (`user_model`) vs platform (`platform_model`) is decided at **provider-registry's `/v1/llm/jobs` submit** (`jobs_handler.go:139`), so the 402 lives there. The `X-Mcp-Key-Id` carrier was already wired end-to-end (ai-gateway forwards it; both kits lift to ctx) **except** the last link: merging it into `job_meta` at the submit chokepoint. Wave-gating bounds the live surface: every priced **async-job** tool (translation_start_job, composition_generate, kg_build_*, lore_enrich) is Tier-W → **Wave C/P4**, NOT exposed yet; the only Wave-B priced tool is `glossary_web_search` (Go **synchronous** path → deferred). So this slice = the generic **async-jobs spend rails**.
> - **PUB-12 (BYOK-only), no migration:** a shared helper ([byok_gate.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/byok_gate.go)) — `isPublicMcpKeyCall(r, jobMeta)` (X-Mcp-Key-Id header **or** `job_meta.mcp_key_id`) + `rejectPlatformDrawForPublicKey` (402 `LLM_BYOK_ONLY`). Applied **uniformly at every spend entry point** ([jobs_handler.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/jobs_handler.go) submit · [stream_handler.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/stream_handler.go) · [server.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/server.go) `doProxy`), **before any guardrail reserve** (no leaked hold), so no future Wave-C wiring can bypass it. Carrier: header on synchronous paths (still live), `job_meta` on jobs (header lost across the queue). `ParseJobMetaMcpKeyID` ([repo.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/jobs/repo.go), nil-tolerant, mirrors `parseJobMetaCampaignID`).
> - **Per-key attribution (H-C/PUB-11), 2 idempotent migrations:** `mcp_key_id` now rides `job_meta` → `usage_outbox` (col + INSERT, [repo.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/jobs/repo.go) `FinalizeWithUsageOutbox`) → relay `buildUsageFields` ([usage_relay.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/jobs/usage_relay.go)) → usage stream → usage-billing `parseUsageEvent`+`usageLogParams`+`writeUsageLog` → **`usage_logs.mcp_key_id`** ([usage_consumer.go](../../services/usage-billing-service/internal/api/usage_consumer.go), [server.go](../../services/usage-billing-service/internal/api/server.go)). Mirrors the `campaign_id` rail (which stops at the stream); `mcp_key_id` continues into the audit row. New internal endpoint **`GET /internal/billing/mcp-key-usage`** ([mcp_key_usage.go](../../services/usage-billing-service/internal/api/mcp_key_usage.go)) — per-key monthly rollup (filtered `request_status='success'`), basis for H-O owner view + H-K sub-cap.
> - **🔎 /review-impl: "fix all" — 3 findings ALL FIXED (no HIGH).** (1) **MED** — PUB-12 was jobs-path-only; the synchronous stream/proxy paths were ungated (not exploitable today — no job_meta field there + no priced sync tool — but a Wave-C wire could bypass) → **fixed by making PUB-12 uniform across jobs+stream+proxy via the shared helper** (header carrier on sync paths). (2) **LOW** — no proof the gate runs before the reserve → the router-only (pool-less) 402 tests ARE that proof (the reserve needs the DB; a post-reserve gate would 503/panic first). (3) **LOW** — rollup summed all statuses → filtered to `request_status='success'` (real incurred spend, for the H-K sub-cap). Verified safe: attribution survives `mergeJobMeta`'s cap path; campaign consumer tolerates the additive stream field (`.get()`); `model_source` keying is correct (`user_model`⟺BYOK).
> - **VERIFY:** provider-registry + usage-billing **full suites green** (+ helper unit tests, jobs/stream/proxy 402 wiring tests, attribution thread tests, rollup validation); `ai-provider-gate` clean. **✅ LIVE-SMOKE (rebuilt both images first):** (a) `platform_model`+`mcp_key_id` → **402 `LLM_BYOK_ONLY`** on jobs AND stream AND proxy; `user_model`+key & `platform_model`-no-key → 404 (passed the gate, not 402); (b) injected `usage_outbox.mcp_key_id` → flowed to `usage_logs.mcp_key_id` cross-service in <2s; (c) rollup endpoint returns the per-key aggregate (`request_count:1, tokens:140, cost:0.0042`), 401 without internal token.

> - **Pivot from H-K:** H-K (per-key spend sub-cap) needs the key's `spend_cap_usd` at provider-registry's atomic reserve = riding the **dormant Wave-C carrier** (`D-PMCP-KEYID-JOBMETA-WIRING`) → premature. H-O is live-meaningful NOW (the edge sees every call). H-K → Wave-C/P4.
> - **auth-service:** new append-only **`mcp_call_audit`** table ([migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go) — mirrors `admin_token_issuance_audit` incl. the `REVOKE UPDATE,DELETE` guard; `key_id` NOT an FK so audit OUTLIVES a deleted key, `owner_user_id` FK CASCADE). [mcp_audit.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_audit.go): `POST /internal/mcp-keys/audit` (X-Internal-Token; **batch** ingest — a JSON-RPC batch = N rows; skips bad-outcome/bad-uuid rows best-effort) + `GET /v1/account/mcp-keys/{key_id}/audit` (JWT, owner-scoped `WHERE owner_user_id=caller` → cross-owner reads 0, anti-oracle).
> - **edge:** new [audit-client.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/audit/audit-client.ts) `AuditClient` — fire-and-forget POST (detached, 2s `AbortSignal` timeout, never blocks/throws into the response). [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts) fires one row per `tools/call` at each branch: `relayed` (2xx) / `upstream_error` (non-2xx/transport) / `denied_scope` (gate) / `rate_limited` (429); non-call relays not audited (noise). Carrier = the minted `x-trace-id`. `toolCallNames`/`firstMethod` added to [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts).
> - **FE:** owner audit view — a History expander per key ([McpKeyAuditView.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpKeyAuditView.tsx)) → recent calls (tool · localized outcome chip · time) via `mcpKeysApi.audit` + `useMcpKeys.loadAudit`; i18n ×4 (`mcp.audit.*`).
> - **🔎 /review-impl: 2 LOW fixed + 3 documented.** Fixed: the detached audit fetch had no timeout (socket pile-up under auth hang → 2s AbortSignal); a `parseIntQuery` comment overclaimed "clamp". Documented: `relayed` = the EDGE forwarded + 2xx, NOT the downstream tool verdict (a provider 200-JSON-RPC-error reads as `relayed`) → `D-PMCP-AUDIT-DOWNSTREAM-OUTCOME`; the `unauthorized` enum value is unused by the edge (pre-resolve failures aren't per-key attributable); the outcome vocabulary is duplicated across SQL CHECK / Go / 2× TS (drift degrades gracefully). No HIGH/MED-must-fix.

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P4 slice D — carrier RAIL + per-key spend cap H-K, run via /loom + /review-impl, size XL):** the universal spend-attribution carrier + the per-key sub-cap. Plan: [2026-06-28-public-mcp-p4-wave-c.md](../plans/2026-06-28-public-mcp-p4-wave-c.md) §4b-D. PO chose: usage-billing reserve extension (not edge-Redis) + ALL priced providers in scope.
> - **Carrier (the `D-PMCP-KEYID-JOBMETA-WIRING` rail, now BUILT):** `mcp_key_id` + `spend_cap_usd` ride `job_meta` via a contextvar→merge in the **shared** `loreweave_llm` SDK ([attribution.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_llm/attribution.py) — server-set keys OVERWRITE caller values, anti-spoof; merged in `Client.submit_job`). **Universal hook:** `loreweave_mcp.build_tool_context` ([context.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_mcp/context.py)) soft-calls `set_public_key_attribution` per tool call (lifts `x-mcp-spend-cap-usd`), so EVERY in-process submit on translation/composition/lore-enrichment carries it with zero per-tool wiring. **knowledge** builds its own ctx → got the same hook added explicitly ([server.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/mcp/server.py) — the /review-impl HIGH).
> - **Per-key cap (H-K):** `mcp_key_id` col on `token_reservations` (migration); the reserve handler ([guardrail.go](../../services/usage-billing-service/internal/api/guardrail.go)) sums this key's COMMITTED spend (`usage_logs` this month) + HELD reservations vs `spend_cap_usd` → 402 `MCP_KEY_CAP_EXCEEDED`, **race-safe inside the existing owner-row FOR UPDATE lock** (no new lock; reconcile/release decrement held for free). provider-registry preflight reads the carrier from `job_meta` ([jobs_handler.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/jobs_handler.go) — `ParseJobMetaSpendCap`) → `Reserve(...,mcpKeyID,cap)` ([client.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/billing/client.go)). Cap breach is a HARD 402 (not max_tokens-salvaged). Edge + ai-gateway forward `x-mcp-spend-cap-usd` (only when the key has a cap).
> - **🔎 /review-impl: 1 HIGH fixed + 1 HIGH reclassified (2 deferrals) + 3 MED/LOW documented.** HIGH-fixed: knowledge bypassed the universal hook → priced `kg_*` carried no cap/attribution → added the hook to knowledge's `_build_tool_context`. HIGH-reclassified: the contextvar carrier only survives an **in-process** submit; all priced Tier-W tools spend at the **confirm-route effect** (slice B, not built) and worker-based effects (translation chapter-worker, worker-ai) cross a process boundary → e2e attribution is gated on slice B → `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-PMCP-WORKER-CARRIER`. The rail + cap themselves are sound + verified.
> - **VERIFY:** provider-registry (jobs/billing/api/vet) ✅; usage-billing api ✅ incl. **real-PG per-key cap** (held + committed counted, no-key-not-capped, `MCP_KEY_CAP_EXCEEDED`) on infra postgres:5555; SDK **7/7**; kit **47/47**; ai-gateway **17/17**; edge **17/17**. **live smoke: per-key cap enforced on real Postgres** (the H-K money core). 1 PRE-EXISTING env failure (`TestGuardrailPlatform_FreeTierLazyReset` — confirmed via `git stash` it fails identically on pristine code; Subsystem-A/config, unrelated). Full priced-job e2e gated on slice B (see deferrals).

Plan: [2026-06-28-public-mcp-p4-wave-c.md](../plans/2026-06-28-public-mcp-p4-wave-c.md) §4c. PO chose: **A2** (absorb the in-process confirm-route carrier-lift + live-smoke) + **auth→domain-direct** execute.
> - **A1 queue:** `mcp_pending_approvals` table ([auth migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go) — mutable status `pending/denied/expired/executed/failed`; key_id NOT a FK so it outlives revoke; owner_user_id FK CASCADE; `confirm_token` stored plaintext — already single-use jti + exp + user-bound).
> - **A2 auth handlers** ([mcp_approvals.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_approvals.go)): `POST /internal/mcp-keys/approvals` (edge divert ingest, X-Internal-Token), `GET /v1/account/mcp-keys/approvals?status=` (owner-scoped, lazy-expire), `…/{id}/approve` (replays the token to `{domain}/v1/<domain>/actions/confirm` with **X-Internal-Token + X-User-Id=owner + X-Mcp-Key-Id=key_id + X-Mcp-Spend-Cap-Usd** — cost attributes to the AGENT's key; 2xx→executed, 409→reprice surfaced, 410→expired, else→failed), `…/{id}/deny` (drops token). New domain→URL config map ([config.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/config/config.go)) + best-effort HTTP notification (no AMQP — notification-service `/internal/notifications/` ingest, new `mcp_approval` category). NO JWT-mint needed (confirm routes are internal-token+X-User-Id gated).
> - **A3 edge divert** ([propose-detect.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/propose-detect.ts) pure + [approval-client.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/approval/approval-client.ts) + [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts)): a single `write_confirm` propose by a default key → detect `confirm_token` in the relayed response → POST the approval (token-stripped preview) → return the agent only `{status:"pending_human_approval", approval_id}`. **Fail-closed** (queue unreachable → `isError`, never leak the token). A self-confirm key keeps the token (slice B path).
> - **A4 carrier-lift** (A2 money path): `apply_public_key_attribution_headers` kit helper ([context.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_mcp/context.py)) set/finally-clear around composition's IN-PROCESS confirm effect ([actions.py](../../services/composition-service/app/routers/actions.py)) → `composition.generate`'s in-process `submit_job` tags `job_meta` with the key (the confirm route is not an MCP tool call, so the universal hook never fired there).
> - **A5 FE** ([McpApprovalsPanel.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpApprovalsPanel.tsx) + [useMcpApprovals.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/useMcpApprovals.ts) + api): a polling pending-approvals panel in the MCP Access tab (Approve/Deny); i18n ×4.
> - **🔎 /review-impl: NO HIGH** (verified the gate's core assumption — every `write_confirm` tool is propose-pattern, confirmed book `toolPropose*` + composition; the `undoResult` is the confirm-EFFECT's return, not the propose tool). 6 LOW accept/defer: batch write_confirm not diverted (token inert — `D-PMCP-BATCH-WCONFIRM-DIVERT`); `detectProposeResult` + composition wiring not asserted vs the real wire shape (→ raises `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE` importance); revoked-key approval still executes + 16-min sync approve (idempotent via status latch) documented.

Full `composition_generate`→`usage_logs.mcp_key_id` LLM-spend e2e deferred → `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE`.

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P4 slice B — confirm_action + allow_self_confirm, run via /loom + /review-impl, size M):** the OPT-IN headless path — a key with BOTH `write_confirm` and `allow_self_confirm` executes a Tier-W action itself (no human queue). PO chose **B-via-auth** (edge gates + reuses slice-A replay).
> - **KEY FINDING:** `confirm_action` was only a chat-service FRONTEND tool ([frontend_tools.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/frontend_tools.py)) with NO server executor — the plan §4b-B ("ai-gateway decodes + routes it") was wrong. So B BUILDS the headless executor by reusing slice A's domain-replay.
> - **Auth** ([mcp_approvals.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_approvals.go)): new `POST /internal/mcp-keys/confirm` (X-Internal-Token) replays the token to `{domain}/v1/<domain>/actions/confirm` with `X-Mcp-Key-Id`+cap via the slice-A `replayConfirm`+`lookupKeySpendCap` (NO approval row). Refactored the approve/self-confirm response shaping into shared `confirmReplayLabel`/`writeConfirmReplayResult`.
> - **Edge** ([confirm-action.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/confirm-action.ts) pure + `selfConfirm` on [approval-client.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/approval/approval-client.ts) + [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts)): `confirm_action` is a SYNTHETIC edge tool (not federated / not in TOOL_POLICY) → intercepted BEFORE the scope gate. Gate = `write_confirm` **AND** `allow_self_confirm` **AND** `domain:<domain>` (else anti-oracle `-32601`); forwards `{confirm_token, domain}` to auth self-confirm; injects `confirm_action` into `tools/list` for dual-flag keys.
> - **🔎 /review-impl: NO HIGH; 2 fix-now hardenings applied+tested.** (1) the dual-flag gate also requires the action's `domain:<d>` scope (least-privilege; the domain confirm route re-verifies the token as a backstop). (2) `confirm_action` is classified a WRITE in `isWriteRequest` so it fails CLOSED on a rate-limit store outage (it triggers a spend). LOW documented: self-confirm trusts the internal-token edge-supplied key/owner pair; 16-min sync confirm is idempotent via the single-use token.
> - **VERIFY:** edge **92/92** (confirm-action unit + 4 controller integration: dual-flag execute / non-dual anti-oracle / domain-scope deny / list injection / write-classification), auth full api suite green on **real PG** (self-confirm replay X-Mcp-Key-Id+cap=4 to a stub domain + the refactored approve regression), provider-gate clean. **live smoke: auth self-confirm cross-process replay proven on real Postgres.** Full `composition_generate` LLM-spend e2e → `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE` (now fully code-enabled; needs stack rebuild + lm_studio + composition project setup).

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P4 tail C+E+F — FANNED OUT, 3 parallel build agents, run via /loom + consolidated /review-impl, size L):** the three independent P4 slices, built in parallel and integrated serially.
> - **E — `jobs_cancel` / `jobs_pause` (H-N):** Tier-A MCP tools in [jobs-service server.py](../../services/jobs-service/app/mcp/server.py) delegating to a shared owner-scoped `_control()` (envelope identity only; `get_job(owner,…)` → anti-oracle "not found or not accessible"; state-cap gate; `control.forward_control` re-verifies ownership at the owning service / M4). Edge [tool-policy.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts): both `write_auto`/`jobs`. So a public agent can abort its OWN runaway job, never another owner's. 18 jobs MCP tests + 8 edge policy tests.
> - **F — H17 partial-failure honesty:** a relayed JSON-RPC **batch** is annotated **per item** with an additive `_meta.step_outcome` (`relayed`/`failed`/`denied_scope`), **keeping the bare array shape** (PO chose per-item `_meta` over a `{step_outcomes,result}` wrapper at POST-REVIEW — H-M transport-transparency; a strict client ignores the field). Pure `parseRequestSteps`/`buildStepOutcomes`/`annotateBatchStepOutcomes` in [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts); single requests byte-for-byte unchanged; no fabrication on a non-array/SSE upstream. 19 unit + 3 controller tests.
> - **C — re-price (H-J):** the first-party [ConfirmActionCard.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/chat/components/ConfirmActionCard.tsx) now detects a domain 409 `reprice_required` (pure `parseRepriceError`), shows old→new cost + prompts re-propose; i18n×4. The HEADLESS reprice path was already shipped (A/B surface it from the confirm replay) — verified, untouched. `D-TRANSL-REPRICE-THRESHOLD-DRIFT` ✅ **CLEAN** (no FE hardcoded `1.25`/`0.50` — FE purely reacts to the BE-set 409). H-J uniform-contract note added to [03-security-design.md](../specs/2026-06-26-public-mcp/03-public-mcp-security-design.md). 21 FE tests.
> - **🔎 consolidated /review-impl: NO HIGH.** E owner-scoping + anti-oracle + M4 re-verify confirmed by reading `_control`. F batch wire-shape surfaced to PO → reworked to per-item `_meta` (bare array kept). C threshold-drift clean. The fan-out file sets were disjoint (E=jobs+tool-policy, F=scope-filter+controller, C=FE+docs); E+F's concurrent edge edits integrated cleanly.

(The edge advertise-side is DONE; this is the per-provider arg/ownership/idempotency hardening.)
> - **jobs H-N (P4):** add `jobs_cancel`/`jobs_pause` Tier-A MCP tools (+ classify in the edge policy table when built); edge `jobs_*` result-filtering to the key's domain scopes (H-F runtime, beyond advertise-gating).
> - **P3 ✅ · P4 Wave-C ALL SIX slices ✅ (D · A · B · C · E · F).** The public-MCP write/spend trust boundary is COMPLETE: priced spend is attributed + per-key capped (D), human-gated by default (A) or self-confirmable (B), reprice-honest (C), agent-cancellable (E), and partial-failure-honest (F). **▶ NEXT options:**

Exposable as `paid_read` now; tool-call metering is an optional future enhancement, not a blocker. See the deferred-section row.

>   - **Knowledge/worker-ai e2e live-smoke (2026-06-28) — PROVEN + caught a 2nd HIGH.** Drove `/v1/kg/actions/confirm` via the replay path (internal-token + X-User-Id + X-Mcp-Key-Id + cap, NO JWT, minted a `DESC_BUILD_GRAPH` token mirroring `_handle_kg_build_graph`) for project `019eef5d` (book `019eef55`, benchmark-passed bge-m3; published 1 chapter via the real API, unpublished after). **HIGH (fixed `0930d88b`):** `/v1/kg/actions` had a **router-level `dependencies=[Depends(get_current_user)]`** → every route incl. `/confirm` required a Bearer JWT → the replay path was 401'd "missing bearer token" BEFORE the dual-auth helper ran (a public key could NEVER confirm `kg_build_graph` — Gap-1 not actually closed for knowledge; unit tests missed it because they all send a Bearer). > - ✅ `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE` RESOLVED — proven live on the real stack (lm_studio Gemma-4 26B, BYOK). Drove the **auth-replay** confirm path directly (internal-token + X-User-Id + X-Mcp-Key-Id + cap, `?token=`, NO JWT — exactly what `auth.replayConfirm` sends) against `translation_start_job` on a real book/chapter. **(a) tagging:** job completed, **3 `usage_logs` rows tagged with the key** (real input/output tokens, `success`/`recorded`) — confirm→row→AMQP→chapter_worker→`set_public_key_attribution`→SDK `merge_attribution_into_job_meta`→provider-registry→usage_logs. **(b) per-key cap → 402:** a tiny `spend_cap_usd=0.0001` rode the carrier → worker LLM submit got `HTTP 402 Payment Required` / `MCP_KEY_CAP_EXCEEDED` (`per-key spend cap reached: estimated $0.00201130, key remaining $0.00010000` → `LLMQuotaExceeded`) → job **failed with zero spend recorded**. **KEY GOTCHA:** the cap needed `usage-billing-service` + `provider-registry-service` rebuilt too (the H-K `token_reservations.mcp_key_id` migration + reserve code shipped in P4-D `191fac6b` but the running images predated it — classic stale-image trap; rebuild ALL services whose code a live path touches, not just the ones you edited). `usage_logs.mcp_key_id` is a **UUID** column → a public key id is a UUID, not free text.
> - ✅ `D-PMCP-WEBSEARCH-BYOK` RESOLVED (verified exposable — NO code change needed). **Correction (2026-06-28, per PO): `web_search` is a TOOL, not an LLM/model** — the `user_models` row with `capability_flags:{web_search:true}` is a **tool credential** (the test user's is `searxng`, self-hosted/free; Tavily/Brave etc. would be the user's OWN paid API). So the LLM-spend lens (meter tokens → `usage_logs` → per-key cap, "pricing decision") was the WRONG model and does NOT apply — there is no token spend to meter/cap/attribute. The correct controls for a tool are already in place + proven: (1) **BYOK / no platform draw** — `internalWebSearch` ([server.go:2778](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/server.go#L2778)) resolves the *caller's own* explicitly-flagged `web_search` credential, 404 `WEBSEARCH_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` if none, no fallback. **Live-proven:** test user (searxng) → `HTTP 200, provider_model=searxng-default, 3 results`; a user with no cred → `HTTP 404` (no platform draw). (2) **scope-gate** — `glossary_web_search` advertised `{tier:'paid_read', domains:['glossary']}` ([tool-policy.ts:105](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts#L105)); `paid_read` is a selectable tier in `MCP_SCOPES` (FE create dialog), so a user can mint a `paid_read`+`domain:glossary` key. (3) **per-key rate-limit (PUB-8) + audit (H-O)** already live-proven edge-wide. `paid_read` = "may hit the user's OWN external provider", bounded by the rate-limit (not a token cap). **Optional FUTURE enhancement (NOT a blocker):** if the platform ever wants per-call accounting of paid web-search tools, add tool-call metering — but that's a separate product decision, and Wave-B `paid_read` does NOT need it.
> - ✅ `D-TRANSL-REPRICE-THRESHOLD-DRIFT` RESOLVED (P4-C): verified CLEAN — no FE file hardcodes the `1.25`/`0.50` re-price threshold; the FE purely reacts to the BE-set 409 `reprice_required` (threshold stays BE-owned). The first-party confirm card + the headless `useMcpApprovals` both just surface the BE signal.
> - ✅ `D-PMCP-BATCH-WCONFIRM-DIVERT` RESOLVED (P4-B target reached) — the edge now diverts a `write_confirm` propose that rides inside a JSON-RPC **batch** (not just a single message). For a DEFAULT key (`allow_self_confirm=false`), `divertBatchProposes` ([public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts)) walks the relayed batch response, and for each item whose id matches a `write_confirm` `tools/call` request step (`writeConfirmCallsById`, [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts)) AND whose result carries a routable propose (`detectProposeInItem`, [propose-detect.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/propose-detect.ts)) it creates an approval row + replaces the item with a token-stripped `pending_human_approval` (fail-closed per item → `isError`, never the token). It sets `text` and **falls through** to the existing list-filter + slice-F annotate (so a mixed `[tools/list, propose]` batch still scope-filters the list — the regression the /review-impl pass caught). Self-confirm keys are untouched (tokens flow to slice-B `confirm_action`). **VERIFY:** edge **134/134** (+9: per-item detect/builders/`writeConfirmCallsById` units + batch-divert/fail-closed/self-confirm/mixed-list controller integration), `nest build` clean, provider-gate OK. Single-service edge logic, unit-proven as the P4-A/B divert paths were.

New pure `singleToolCallErrored(body, text)` in [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts) (single-call only; batches keep their per-step `_meta.step_outcome` and a coarse audit row — documented); classified at the relay audit point in [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts). New `tool_error` outcome added across all 4 contract sites: edge `audit-client.ts` type, auth Go `validMcpAuditOutcomes` + the `mcp_call_audit.outcome` CHECK (idempotent ALTER widens an existing table — the inline CREATE CHECK only covers fresh DBs), FE `api.ts` type + `OUTCOME_STYLE` + i18n ×4. **VERIFY:** edge 125 (+6 helper tests), auth build+vet, FE tsc + McpAccess 8, provider-gate + i18n×4 valid. **live smoke:** rebuilt auth → drove a `tool_error` row through the real `/internal/mcp-keys/audit` → HTTP 200 + row landed `outcome='tool_error'` (proves the SQL CHECK ALTER applied on the existing DB + the Go validator accepts the new value — the cross-service contract units can't cover). Synthetic smoke row deleted.
