> **▶ P5 — OAuth 2.1 + discovery (XL, 4 slices; CLARIFY PO checkpoint passed).** Spec [2026-06-28-public-mcp-p5-oauth.md](../specs/2026-06-28-public-mcp-p5-oauth.md) · plan [2026-06-28-public-mcp-p5-oauth.md](../plans/2026-06-28-public-mcp-p5-oauth.md). **PO decisions:** open DCR (flag+rate-limit+audit) · full 4-slice build · scope-aware consent with per-scope downscoping.
> - ✅ **SLICE 1 SHIPPED — discovery + edge audience-verify (S9 foundation).** **auth-service:** `authjwt.SignOAuthAccessToken` ([oauth.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/authjwt/oauth.go)) reuses the admin RS256 `DigestSigner` via a generalized `assembleRS256(claims any)` but with a DISTINCT issuer (`OAUTH_ISSUER`, must ≠ admin `loreweave-auth`) + a resource audience; `GET /oauth/jwks` (RFC 7517 from `signer.PublicKey()`) + `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` (RFC 8414, PKCE S256 only) ([oauth_meta.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_meta.go)); `EnableOAuth` wired in [main.go](../../services/auth-service/cmd/auth-service/main.go) (same signer as admin) — on iff admin-issuance AND `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED`; config fail-closed on missing resource ([config.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/config/config.go)). **edge:** [token-verifier.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/oauth/token-verifier.ts) — LOCAL RS256 verify (Node crypto, zero deps; JWKS kid-cache + refresh-on-miss for rotation) pinning `iss`==OAUTH_ISSUER + `aud`==`MCP_RESOURCE_URL` (RFC 8707 — **rejects audience confusion, S9**) + exp; alg-confusion-safe (rejects non-RS256). [key-resolver.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/auth/key-resolver.ts) branches JWT→local-verify (`keyId`=grant_id, a UUID → uniform with API keys for attribution/audit/rate-limit), `lw_pk_`→existing resolve; OAuth `allow_self_confirm=false`, `spend_cap=null`. RFC 9728 PRM ([oauth-discovery.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/oauth/oauth-discovery.controller.ts) + pure [discovery.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/oauth/discovery.ts)) + `WWW-Authenticate` on the 401. **BFF:** routes `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`→edge, `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`+`/oauth/*`→auth ([gateway-setup.ts](../../services/api-gateway-bff/src/gateway-setup.ts)). **VERIFY:** auth authjwt+api+config tests green (incl. OAuth-token↔admin-token cross-rejection both ways) + `go vet`; edge **155/155** (token-verifier 17: aud/iss/exp/sig/alg/rotation/wildcard-strip + discovery + 4 controller incl. wrong-aud→401-no-relay + OAuth relay on-behalf-of); BFF build + **115**; provider-gate OK. **LIVE-SMOKE → `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE`** (deferred to slice 2's e2e — the real edge↔auth `/oauth/jwks`+aud-verify needs a token-mint endpoint, which lands in slice 2; slice-1 surface is unit-complete).
> - ✅ **SLICE 2a SHIPPED (backend auth-code + PKCE flow).** **migration** (3 tables, [migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go)): `mcp_oauth_clients` (public PKCE clients, `token_endpoint_auth_method='none'`), `mcp_oauth_grants` (per owner×client, `id`=grant_id that rides `x-mcp-key-id`, downscoped `scopes`, rotating `refresh_token_hash`, `UNIQUE(owner,client)`), `mcp_oauth_codes` (single-use auth codes, stored HASHED, PKCE-bound, short TTL). **handlers** ([oauth_flow.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_flow.go)): `GET /oauth/authorize` (validates client+redirect+S256+resource+scope; **anti-open-redirect** — client/redirect errors shown directly, never redirect to an unvalidated URI; else redirects a logged-in user to the FE consent page), `POST /v1/account/oauth/consent` (Bearer JWT owner — approves a **downscoped** grant: granted⊆requested + vocab-checked → upserts grant + mints single-use code), `POST /oauth/token` (auth-code: atomic single-use consume `UPDATE…WHERE consumed_at IS NULL AND expires_at>now() RETURNING` + PKCE S256 verify → access+refresh; refresh-token: rotate). Pure helpers ([oauth_pkce.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_pkce.go)): `pkceVerifyS256` (constant-time), `redirectURIRegistered` (exact match), `splitScopeParam`/`scopesAllKnown`/`scopesSubset`. `POST /internal/oauth/clients` (internal-token seed; slice 3 wraps it as public RFC 7591 DCR). Access token minted via slice-1 `SignOAuthAccessToken` (aud = the request's resource). **VERIFY:** auth build+vet+provider-gate clean; pure-helper units (PKCE/redirect/scope) + **PG-gated FULL-FLOW test PROVEN on real Postgres** (infra:5555 throwaway DB): register→consent(downscope read+domain:book from a wider req)→code→token→access+refresh, single-use code reuse→400, refresh rotation + old-refresh→400, bad-PKCE→400. This clears the **backend leg** of `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - ✅ **SLICE 2b SHIPPED (FE scope-aware consent page — per-scope downscoping).** New MVC `frontend/src/features/oauth/` — [api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/api.ts) (`oauthApi.consent` → `POST /v1/account/oauth/consent`, `ConsentParams`/`ConsentRequest`), [hooks/useOAuthConsent.ts](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/hooks/useOAuthConsent.ts) (controller: reads the validated authorize params off the query string, owns per-scope grant toggles starting from the full request, approve composes the **downscoped** `granted_scopes` + the full `requested_scopes` then follows the returned client `redirect_uri`; deny bounces to the client with `error=access_denied`; not-logged-in → `/login` preserving `pathname+search` so the query survives the round-trip — RequireAuth would drop it), [components/OAuthConsentView.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/components/OAuthConsentView.tsx) (render-only; tier+domain chips reuse `splitScopes`/`domainScope`). Page [OAuthConsentPage.tsx](../../frontend/src/pages/OAuthConsentPage.tsx) + route `/oauth/consent` ([App.tsx](../../frontend/src/App.tsx), in FullBleedLayout, NOT RequireAuth). i18n `oauth` ns ×4 ([en](../../frontend/src/i18n/locales/en/oauth.json)/vi/ja/zh-TW) registered in [i18n/index.ts](../../frontend/src/i18n/index.ts). **Backend tweak:** `oauthAuthorize` now forwards `client_name` to the consent URL (display-only — the FE otherwise only has the opaque `client_id`). **🔎 /review-impl: 1 MED fixed + 1 LOW fixed.** **MED** — the deny path built the redirect **client-side from the raw query string** (`new URL(params.redirectUri)`) with no backend validation → an **open redirect** on a direct link with an attacker-supplied unregistered `redirect_uri` (victim clicks Deny → bounced to evil.com; no token leak but phishing-grade, violates the anti-open-redirect invariant the approve path upholds). **Fixed:** deny now POSTs `action:"deny"` → `oauthConsent` validates the redirect_uri is registered for the client BEFORE bouncing (unregistered → 400, no redirect). LOW — `needsLogin` button was mislabeled `consent.allow` → added `consent.sign_in`. Verified safe: no XSS (React escapes the i18next-interpolated text node despite `escapeValue:false`), no scope-widening (UI renders only requested chips + backend enforces subset), approve open-redirect impossible (backend 400s an unregistered URI). **VERIFY:** FE `tsc` clean; **oauth consent 5/5 vitest** (all-scopes-prechecked, downscope omits untoggled from `granted_scopes` w/ `requested_scopes` intact + follows redirect, all-off→blocked+`no_scopes`, **deny routes through backend** w/ `action:deny`, missing-PKCE→`invalid_request`); auth-service api unit suite green + build+vet; **PG-gated deny test** (`TestOAuthConsentDeny_PG`: registered→access_denied bounce no code, **unregistered redirect→400**) + full-flow test re-proven on real Postgres. **Deploy note:** `OAUTH_CONSENT_URL` must point at the FE origin's `/oauth/consent`; the public host serves `/oauth/consent` from the SPA while routing the OAuth **API** endpoints (`/oauth/authorize|token|jwks`, `/.well-known/oauth-*`) to the gateway (FE nginx/vite proxy only `/v1`,`/ws`,`/languagetool` today → topology-dependent, see deferral).
> - ✅ **SLICE 3 SHIPPED (open Dynamic Client Registration, RFC 7591).** **auth-service only.** New public `POST /oauth/register` ([oauth_register.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_register.go)) — a third-party MCP client self-registers a PUBLIC PKCE client (no secret) → completes the fully self-served `register→authorize→consent→token→edge-read`. Reuses `insertOAuthClient` (stores `created_ip`). **Three locked controls (PO "open DCR behind flag+rate-limit+audit"):** (1) **kill-switch** `OAUTH_DCR_ENABLED` (default ON, [config.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/config/config.go)) → `403 registration_disabled`; (2) **per-IP rate-limit** (`OAUTH_DCR_RATE_PER_HOUR` default 10, dedicated `ratelimit.Limiter` on `oauthDeps`) checked **before any parse/DB write** → `429 rate_limited`; (3) **append-only audit** — new `mcp_oauth_client_registrations` table ([migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go), REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE) records every attempt (issued `registered` / `rejected`+reason; `client_id` NULL on reject). Validates redirect_uris (absolute, no fragment, http(s)-needs-host, custom native schemes ok), public-PKCE-only (`token_endpoint_auth_method=none`), grant/response types ⊆ supported, scope vocab. Spec-correct RFC `{error,error_description}` body via `writeOAuthErr`. `registration_endpoint` already in AS metadata (slice 1). **🔎 /review-impl: 1 MED + 2 LOW fixed.** **MED** — no input-size caps on the public endpoint → added 16 KiB `MaxBytesReader` + `redirect_uris ≤ 10` + `client_name ≤ 256` (resource-exhaustion guard). **LOW** — AS metadata advertised `registration_endpoint` even when DCR disabled → now conditional on `dcrEnabled` (RFC 8414). **LOW** — documented `OAUTH_DCR_RATE_PER_HOUR=0` = UNLIMITED (disable via the flag). Verified safe: open redirect impossible (code is PKCE-bound + consent-gated), audit can't be flooded (rate-gate precedes the write), no secret ever issued. **VERIFY:** `go build`/`vet` clean; `TestValidRegistrationRedirectURI` + `TestOAuthASMetadata_OmitsRegistrationWhenDCRDisabled` + **PG tests** (`TestOAuthDCR_PG`: 201+client_id+no-secret+audit-row, bad-redirect→400+rejected-audit, empty→400, >10 redirects→400, long name→400; `TestOAuthDCR_FlagOff_PG`→403; `TestOAuthDCR_RateLimit_PG`: 2 ok→429) green on real Postgres; full auth suite green; provider-gate OK. Full self-served browser round-trip folds into `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - ✅ **SLICE 4 SHIPPED (catalog_* OD-7 public-discovery read provider) — P5 COMPLETE.** **catalog-service:** new MCP server ([mcp_server.go](../../services/catalog-service/internal/api/mcp_server.go)) + 2 read tools ([mcp_tools.go](../../services/catalog-service/internal/api/mcp_tools.go)) — `catalog_list_public_books` (query/language/genre/author filters + sort) + `catalog_get_book`. Both delegate to a shared query core (`queryPublicBooks`/`queryPublicBook`, extracted from the HTTP handlers in [server.go](../../services/catalog-service/internal/api/server.go) so MCP + HTTP stay one source of truth). **OD-7:** catalog content is PUBLIC (sharing public gate + lifecycle filter), so the tools are owner-AGNOSTIC — `_meta.scope=none` (no kit guard), TierR; NO OD-8 owner gate (no private data). Mounted `/mcp`+`/mcp/*` via the kit `NewStatelessHandler` (X-Internal-Token gate); go.mod gets the kit + grantclient/llmgw replaces; Dockerfile copies the 3 new sdk dirs. **ai-gateway:** `catalog: 'catalog_'` in [DEFAULT_PREFIX_MAP](../../services/ai-gateway/src/config/config.ts) + `catalog=…/mcp` in compose `AI_GATEWAY_PROVIDERS`. **edge:** [tool-policy.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts) classifies both `catalog_*` = `{tier:'read', domains:['catalog']}` (the `'catalog'` Domain + OAuth `domain:catalog` scope already existed from slice 1). **FE:** `catalog` added to `MCP_DOMAINS` ([api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/api.ts)) + `mcp.domain.catalog` label ×4 locales. **VERIFY:** catalog `go build`/`vet` + api tests green (incl. new MCP tool tests: public-active-only, owner-agnostic via bare ctx, bad-author/uuid, non-public→not-found; existing HTTP tests pass = refactor preserved behavior); edge **155/155** + tsc; ai-gateway **63/63** + tsc; FE tsc clean (settings/oauth i18n parity holds; only the pre-existing `world` parity fails). provider-gate OK. **✅ LIVE-SMOKE (≥2 services — rebuilt catalog-service + ai-gateway, restarted):** ai-gateway federation log shows `federating [...catalog]` + no prefix-drop warning; `tools/list` (200) returns BOTH `catalog_list_public_books` + `catalog_get_book` among 147 federated tools; `tools/call catalog_list_public_books` → 200, `isError:false`, **31 public books across MULTIPLE owner_user_ids** (proves OD-7 owner-agnostic discovery — called as the test user, returned other users' public books); `tools/call catalog_get_book` → 200, `isError:false`, `visibility:"public"` detail. Edge policy layer is unit-proven (edge not up in this smoke). **🔎 /review-impl: NO HIGH/MED** — verified no private-data leak (sharing public gate on both tools), anti-oracle uniform not-found on get, refactor preserves HTTP behavior; 1 LOW closed (added the get live-smoke), 1 LOW accepted (construction test asserts valid meta, not specifically tier=R/scope=none — edge tool-policy is the real gate).
>
> **▶ P5 OAuth 2.1 + discovery — COMPLETE (all 4 slices shipped).** A standards-compliant third-party MCP client can now: self-register (open DCR) → authorize + PKCE → owner consents (scope-aware downscoping) → exchange for an audience-bound access token → call read tools on-behalf-of the user through the public edge, with an audience-confused token rejected at the edge (S9). OAuth is additive (API keys keep working).
>
> **▶▶ PR OPEN → main: [#48](https://github.com/letuhao/lore-weave/pull/48) (2026-06-29).** Branch merged `origin/main` (only 3 i18n `world.json` conflicts — resolved by taking main's wording; parity vitest 18/18). **mergeable: MERGEABLE.** CI gates fixed (all green): (1) capacity-budget entries for `mcp-public-gateway` + the pre-existing `jobs-service` gap; (2) gitleaks allowlist for the edge test fixtures (fake `lw_pk_` keys); (3) **`outbox-event-emit-lint` I13** — the `composition-service/app/worker/events.py` XADD flagged was a **stale-allowlist false positive, NOT a real I13 violation**: it's the `composition_jobs` JOB-TRIGGER stream (best-effort work-dispatch; Postgres job row = SSOT; sweeper re-drives), the identical sanctioned pattern to the already-exempt `lore-enrichment`/`chat`/`knowledge`/`learning` services. Fixed by adding the seam to the lint's exemption list (`scripts/` only — no composition code touched). Edge suite 183/183 on the merged tree. Remaining CI = heavier monorepo jobs (Rust/Go build+test, perf gates, conformance) still running — no failures. **Awaiting human merge.**

> | ~~`D-PMCP-BATCH-IDEMPOTENCY`~~ ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-29** | feature-gap | LOW | — | per-item H-G dedup for a JSON-RPC BATCH (`services/mcp-public-gateway/src/idempotency/idempotency.ts` + `mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts`): each `write_auto` element carrying an `idempotency_key` is stripped (so `ForbidExtra` never rejects it) + `begin()`-claimed on `(key_id, tool, key)`; a **replay** (cached) or **pending** (in-flight) element is EXCLUDED from the relay (never re-executed) and merged back by JSON-RPC id; **proceed** elements relay (reduced array) then cache/abort per id. Fully-cached batch → **no upstream call** (skip-relay). **Money-safety:** only `write_auto` (free) tools dedup — `write_confirm` propose/priced items are never cached and still divert (coexistence tested: token stripped before send). Fail-open on store outage; errored/missing items never cached (retryable); per-credential `key_id` scoping = no cross-tenant replay. Backward-compat: a batch with no idempotency keys relays verbatim (the divert/annotate paths unchanged). +12 tests (6 pure helper + 6 controller incl. partial-dedup, pending short-circuit, write_confirm coexistence); **edge 183/183**, tsc + nest build clean. /review-impl self-review: 1 accepted LOW (duplicate JSON-RPC ids in one batch could drop a colliding element — self-inflicted malformed request, same id-keying as the existing divert/annotate) |

The guard `tests/test_mcp_server.py::…inputschema_mirrors_bespoke…` already existed but is in `tests/` not `tests/unit/` (missed in the Slice-3 unit run) — **knowledge tool-schema changes must run `tests/test_mcp_server.py`**. **LIVE-PROVEN through the edge:** default key→`kg_build_graph` own project = diverted to approval (no token/spend); cross-tenant = `project not found` (OD-8). **Residual → Slice 4:** priced attribution live-smoke (carrier→`usage_logs.mcp_key_id`+cap on a real kg_build job). See [exposure plan](../plans/2026-06-28-public-mcp-wave-c-exposure.md) |

So "flip" = a per-provider live-smoke campaign. **DONE 2026-06-28 — all 5 providers + 4 tiers proven at the edge:** book (write_auto create), composition (write_auto create), glossary (paid_read real searxng search + PUB-10 plain-read denial), knowledge (write_confirm divert + OD-8 cross-tenant), translation (write_confirm divert + priced cost-estimate `$0.0038`). **Self-confirm real-LLM EXECUTION now ALSO proven** → `D-PMCP-SELFCONFIRM-ROUTES` ✅ RESOLVED below (Slice 5: env-wired + edge→auth→translation `executed` + 3 key-tagged `usage_logs` rows). Track COMPLETE. See [exposure plan](../plans/2026-06-28-public-mcp-wave-c-exposure.md) §Slice 4–5 |

Recreated auth-service (env-only, no rebuild). **LIVE-PROVEN end-to-end through the public edge** (test account, local lm_studio model = $0 cost, REAL inference): minted a self-confirm key (`write_confirm`+`domain:translation`+`allow_self_confirm`, cap $2) → `translation_start_job` propose (token KEPT, not diverted) → `confirm_action` → edge→auth `/internal/mcp-keys/confirm`→auth replays→translation `/v1/translation/actions/confirm` = **`status: executed`** (was `AUTH_CONFIRM_DOMAIN_UNROUTABLE`) → job `019f0f22…` ran to **`completed`** on local gemma → **3 `usage_logs` rows tagged `mcp_key_id`, 9079 tokens** (spend attribution carried through the self-confirm execution path). >
> **▶ Deferred (P5) — detail rows (consolidated in the OPEN register above):**
> - `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE` (LOW, **downgraded** — backend flow now PG-proven) — remaining = the cross-service leg only: edge fetching auth's live `/oauth/jwks` + verifying a token minted by the real `/oauth/token` through the running stack; wrong-aud→401; PLUS the full browser consent round-trip (authorize→FE consent→token). Best run at end-of-P5 / next stack rebuild. **NOTE the config gotcha:** auth `OAUTH_RESOURCE` MUST equal edge `MCP_RESOURCE_URL` (the aud both sides pin); `OAUTH_CONSENT_URL` → FE origin.
> - `D-PMCP-OAUTH-PUBLIC-ROUTING` (LOW, gate-reason 4 blocked-on-topology) — if the prod public host is the FE nginx (single front), it must proxy the OAuth **API** endpoints (`/oauth/authorize|token|jwks`, `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`, `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`) to the gateway/edge while keeping `/oauth/consent` on the SPA. No code change in the FE slice; resolve when the prod ingress topology is fixed (or if gateway is separately exposed, this is a no-op).
> - `D-AUTH-RATELIMIT-XFF-AND-UNBOUNDED-MAP` (LOW, gate-reason 1+2 platform-wide+pre-existing) — `ratelimit.ClientIP` trusts the first `X-Forwarded-For` hop (spoofable → evades the per-IP cap), and the in-process `Limiter.byKey` map never evicts (each distinct/spoofed IP = a permanent entry → slow memory growth). Shared by ALL auth rate-limits (login/register/reset); the DCR endpoint (slice 3) adds an entry point but doesn't introduce the weakness. Compensating: DCR is inert without consent + audited. Platform fix: the gateway should overwrite XFF to the true peer + add a TTL sweep/LRU to the limiter. Surfaced by slice-3 /review-impl.
> - `D-PMCP-CONSENT-CLIENT-LEGIBILITY` (LOW, gate-reason 1 slice-2b follow-up) — now that open DCR lets anyone register an arbitrary `client_name`, the consent page ([OAuthConsentView.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/components/OAuthConsentView.tsx)) should also show the **redirect_uri host** (and a "this app is not verified" hint) so a user can judge an unverified client before approving. Small FE addition; surfaced by slice-3 /review-impl.
> - `D-WORLD-I18N-PARITY` (LOW, gate-reason 1 out-of-scope, **pre-existing** — predates this slice, touches no OAuth file) — `frontend/src/i18n/locales/{vi,ja,zh-TW}/world.json` are missing the `graph.*`/`timeline.*`/`populate.addFailed` leaf keys present in `en/world.json` (added during the C21 world-feature work, never translated) → `compositionWorldParity.test.ts` "world" suite red (9 failing assertions across 3 locales). ~30-50 short UI strings to translate; belongs to the world feature, not OAuth. Fix in its own scoped commit.
>

# (composition branch — historical handoff below) — **Glossary plan-and-execute (Phase 1) SHIPPED** · branch `feat/composition-service` · HEAD `b2439fcf` · 2026-06-26

> **WHAT SHIPPED:** the glossary assistant can now do **multi-step ontology goals in ONE confirm** — the agent calls `glossary_plan(book_id, goal)`, a capable model emits a **typed plan**, and a **deterministic executor** applies it (no ReAct loop). Validated against the literature (typed-plan ≫ ReAct; control-flow-integrity against prompt injection). Built as **reusable SDK kits**, not ad-hoc — see [[project_agentic_sdk_lives_in_loreweave_mcp_kit]]: this project will run many agents, so the pattern lives in `sdks/`.
>
> **Specs/plan:** architecture [2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md) · kit mechanism [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md](../specs/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md) (Part I envelope/executor/error-classes, Part II concrete Go types + glossary op-set + caps + resolved-questions ledger) · multi-agent execution plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md).
>
> **The two new SDK kits (`sdks/go/`):**
> - **`loreweave_mcp`** gained the **plan-action kit** — `plan.go` (frozen K0 contract: `Plan`/`Op`/`OpOutcome`/`Summary`, sentinel errors, `OpSpec`/`Registry`, `NewRegistry` panics on non-idempotent ops, `ValidatePlan` does dedupe + duplicate-conflict + frozen op-N ids + `MaxPlanOps=50` cap + reject-empty) and `execute.go` (`Execute(ctx,userID,plan,enabledOps,reg) Summary` — tier-stable-sort, per-op `errors.Is`→outcome mapping, destructive-skip via `enabledOps`, internal-error abort). Constants: `PlanVersion=1`, `DescriptorExecutePlan="execute_plan"`, `PlanTokenTTL=30m`.
> - **`loreweave_llm` — NEW Go LLM SDK (the missing piece; user-chosen this session).** Sync streaming client over the provider-registry LLM gateway. `client.go` (`Complete()` accumulates SSE token deltas; reasoning kept separate; internal `/internal/llm/stream?user_id=`+X-Internal-Token OR jwt `/v1/llm/stream`+Bearer), `models.go` (`StreamRequest`, `ReasoningEffort` incl. `none` to disable hidden thinking, `Result{Text,Reasoning,Usage,FinishReason}`), `errors.go` (code-matched sentinels), `sse.go` (8MB-buffer scanner). Zero deps.
>
> **Glossary wiring:** `planRegistry()` ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)) registers **4 additive ops** — adopt_genres(t0), create_kinds(t1), add_attributes(t2), edit_attribute(t4); `glossary_plan` MCP tool ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go)) resolves model→reads ontology→runs planner (120s + 1 repair round, loose-emit→validate→repair)→mints a confirm card; confirm/execute path ([plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` re-validates then `mcp.Execute`). chat-service `glossary_skill.py` routes multi-step goals to `glossary_plan` (K=2 stop).
>
> **MED-6 (planner auto-resolve) DONE + live-smoked:** provider-registry `GET /internal/planner-model?user_id=` ([default_models_handler.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/default_models_handler.go) `internalResolvePlannerModel`) → explicit `planner` default → else best active chat model preferring `tool_calling` → 404 `PLANNER_MODEL_NONE`. Glossary `resolvePlannerModel` calls it; `glossary_plan` works with **zero manual model_ref**. Smoke: endpoint returned `019ebb72…` (Gemma-4 26B, tool_calling) via `source:chat_fallback`.
>
> **VERIFY/smokes:** all 3 Go modules `build`+`vet` green; kit + SDK unit tests pass; provider-gate OK. **✅ SDK live-smoke** (loreweave_llm → real provider-registry → LM Studio Qwen2.5-7B → valid plan JSON, `in=74 out=48 finish=stop`). **✅ MED-6 endpoint live-smoke** (real PG). **/review-impl** ran: HIGH-1 (edit_attribute base_version guard dead → dropped from planner vocab + fail-closed) + MED-6 fixed.
> - **⚠️ NOT yet smoked: the full chat-UI flow** (open book → "design an ontology" → plan card → confirm → kinds created in real DB). The executor-against-real-DB layer is the one unproven leg — `D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`. The stack is up + code deployed, so this is a user-run browser smoke away.
>
> **Commits (all pushed to `origin/feat/composition-service`):** `4a8f3123` foundation · `ae9f755b` recovery (a concurrent history-rewrite dropped the loreweave_llm SDK + G2 edits; recovered + pushed to protect) · `820d572e` review fixes · `c225f75a` Dockerfile SDK COPY · `658a0caa` MED-6.
>

> **✅ PHASE 2 SLICE 1 SHIPPED (this session) — destructive ontology deletes + per-op enable toggle.** Plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md). The planner can now emit **`delete_kind` / `delete_genre`** (tier 5, Destructive), and the confirm card renders each destructive op as an **opt-in checkbox (default OFF)** — a bare "approve" never deletes (G1).
> - **Ops** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)): 3 delete OpSpecs reuse the EXISTING soft-deprecate cascade primitives (`cascadeDeleteBookKind/Genre`, `softDeleteBookAttribute`); **deprecation-aware resolvers** split idempotency — never-existed→`target_gone` (failed), already-deprecated→`already_done` (skipped), live→delete (applied).
> - **The wire** ([action_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_confirm.go) `decodeConfirmToken` now reads `enabled_ops`; [plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` validates ids (unknown→422 `bad_enabled_op`) and passes the set to `mcp.Execute` — **replacing the hardcoded `nil`** that made destructive ops impossible). Preview stamps `op_id`+`destructive` per row + cascade-count notes.
> - **FE** ([ConfirmActionCard.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/chat/components/ConfirmActionCard.tsx)): opt-in checkbox per destructive row + "N will be SKIPPED unless enabled" hint; [actionsApi.ts](../../frontend/src/features/chat/actionsApi.ts) sends `enabled_ops` only when ≥1 checked; i18n ×4.
> - **🔎 /review-impl — 1 MED + 1 LOW-coverage FIXED, 1 regression caught:** **MED** — `ontologyStateSummary` (the planner's ENTIRE view of current state) listed only kind codes → `delete_genre`/`delete_attribute` referenced codes the planner had never seen (always `target_gone`). Fixed: summary now lists **genres too** (cheap) so `delete_genre` works; **`delete_attribute` dropped from planner vocab** (kept registered+tested — the `edit_attribute` precedent — since per-attribute context would bloat every plan call AND the GUI already deletes attributes). **LOW** — added a real-PG `delete_genre` round-trip (distinct cascade from kind). **Regression** — the wider test net caught `TestPlanRegistry_BuildsWithoutPanic` asserting Phase-1's `len==4`/no-destructive invariant; updated to the Phase-2 shape (7 ops, exactly the 3 deletes destructive).
> - **VERIFY:** glossary `go build`+`vet` clean; kit tests pass; **real-PG round-trips** (throwaway DB `loreweave_glossary_p2smoke`): `delete_kind` default-skip(`not_confirmed`, stays live)→enable→`applied`(+cascade)→re-run→`already_done`→bogus→`target_gone`; `delete_genre` apply→deprecated→`already_done`; existing confirm/preview round-trips **non-regressive** on real PG; FE **18 tests** (15 card + others) green, `tsc` clean. **⏳ NOT yet smoked: the full chat-UI flow** (`D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`).

> **✅ PHASE 2 SLICE 2 SHIPPED (this session) — planner orchestrates the merge action over detected duplicates.** Design+plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md). **Why this and not "entity ops":** two scouts established that glossary **entities are UUID-identified and their names are non-unique EAV values** → a planner can't safely address an existing entity by name, AND merge/research already exist as class-C confirm actions (`descMerge`/`descDeepResearch`). The user chose the **"planner orchestrates existing actions"** path: the planner *selects* from detected `merge_candidates` (which already carry UUIDs) rather than inventing references.
> - **The op** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go) `merge_candidate`, tier 5 Destructive): params `{candidate_id, winner_id?}` — the planner copies a **stable `candidate_id`** (one PK per cluster) from context, never a transcribed member-UUID or ambiguous name. Handler resolves the candidate's CURRENT members at execute time (no staleness), picks winner (`winner_id` if a member → else detector's `suggested_winner` → else `ErrBadParams`), and calls the EXISTING `mergeEntitiesCore` (journals + marks-merged). One op **per candidate** → each gets its own slice-1 enable toggle.
> - **Idempotency made independent of the best-effort `markCandidatesMerged` flip** (a test-driven fix — it lags under load): status≠`proposed`→`already_done` fast-path AND **0 losers actually merged → `already_done`**, so a re-run is deterministic. Bogus id→`target_gone`.
> - **Planner context** ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go) `mergeCandidatesSummary`, cap 25 by score, **best-effort** — additive, never fails an ontology plan): each cluster's `candidate_id` + member names/ids/link-counts + suggested winner + rationale. Vocab + destructive guidance updated. Preview resolves candidate→winner-kept/losers-merged names.
> - **🔎 /review-impl — 1 MED (prompt-injection) FIXED + tested:** the candidate context block embeds untrusted **entity names + detector rationale** (extraction/user content) into the planner's **SYSTEM** prompt — the first free-text there (kind/genre codes are slugs). The user-supplied `reference` is fenced as DATA in the user message, but the candidate block was not. Fixed: `safePromptField` runs `sanitize.NeutralizeCanonText` (the canon-boundary defense the deep-research path already uses — inert-izes chat-template/role markers + invisibles) + collapses whitespace (a smuggled newline can't forge a block field) + caps length. Regression tests assert a `<|im_start|>` / "ignore previous instructions" / newline-injected name is neutralized. (Blast radius was already bounded by the enable-toggle gate — every destructive op is default-OFF + shown — but untrusted text in the system prompt is a defense-in-depth gap worth closing.)
> - **VERIFY:** build/vet clean; kit green; **real-PG round-trip**: `merge_candidate` enable→`applied` (loser soft-deleted via `mergeEntitiesCore`)→re-run→`already_done`→bogus→`target_gone`; +DB-free validate/parse/dedupe + `resolveMergeWinner` + neutralization unit; registry guard now **8 ops** (4 destructive). FE **unchanged** — the slice-1 toggle already renders any destructive plan op (merge_candidate rows carry `op_id`+`destructive`). **⏳ chat-UI flow still `D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`** (now also covers the merge path).
