> - 🟡 `D-EXTRACTION-BATCH-CONCURRENCY` — **CORE SHIPPED (2026-06-26): the glossary-extraction worker now honors `concurrency_level`.** Root cause of "we can only run 1 LLM call, wastes the GPU": the translation-service extraction worker referenced `concurrency` **zero times** — the per-(window×batch) loop was a plain sequential `for`, and the knob never reached it. **Part A (propagate):** translation `create_extraction_job` `CreateExtractionJobPayload.concurrency_level` (≤64) → published in the `extraction.job` message (no migration — single publisher, no re-publish-from-row; redelivery carries it). **Part B (parallelize):** extracted the per-unit loop body into a nested `async def _run_unit` (no re-indent — nested def keeps the 8-space body; `continue`→`return`), driven under an `asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)` via `gather`; `concurrency<=1` keeps the EXACT sequential fast-path (back-compat). Chapters stay sequential (per-chapter known-entity accumulation); only a chapter's batches fan out. Safe because asyncio is single-threaded: the shared collectors (`_accept`/`all_entities`, `_record_outcome`/`batch_outcomes` keyed by stable `call_idx`, token sums via `nonlocal`, `batch_finish_reasons`) mutate atomically between awaits; cache keys are per-unit; writeback is whole-chapter post-loop. Hard ceiling `_EXTRACTION_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16`. VERIFY: py_compile clean; **18 extraction-worker tests** incl. new `test_batch_concurrency_runs_all_units_via_gather` (4 batches, concurrency=4 → 4 LLM calls, 4 outcomes, 4 entities, tokens summed) + 41 worker/truncation/cache/outcomes green (sequential path byte-unchanged). ✅ **Trigger-side SHIPPED:** the `features/extraction` ExtractionWizard `StepConfirm` gained a **"Parallel LLM calls"** number input (1–16, default 1) → `ExtractionJobRequest.concurrency_level` → `extractionApi.startJob` (sent only when >1). tsc clean; ExtractionWizard 3/3 green. So the full chain is live: FE field → payload → `extraction.job` message → worker semaphore. **▶ LIVE-SMOKE RAN (2026-06-26) — REVEALED A DEEPER BOTTLENECK + a follow-up `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG`.** Redeployed translation-service + worker (migration applied, worker has `_run_unit`+Semaphore). Drove a real extraction at concurrency=4 on the user's book (chapters 61-62) + the user's **Gemma 4 26B** (`019ebb6e`) via `mint_user_jwt` → `POST /v1/extraction/books/{id}/extract-glossary`. Job `019f009f` completed (9 batches all `ok`, 53 entities). **BUT the user reported "still only 1 call in LM Studio."** Root-caused: the worker correctly fans out 4 `submit_job` calls, but `submit_and_wait` is a **gateway job-submit+poll** path, and the gateway (provider-registry) **hardcaps LOCAL provider concurrency to 1** — `ratelimit.maxFor`: `if localKinds[kind] { return 1 }` (`localKinds={ollama,lm_studio}`), sizing the queue's per-kind semaphore + the governor gate to 1 for local. So the 4 jobs serialize at the gateway → LM Studio sees 1. **This is an anti-pattern (user call): the platform must NOT bake a local/cloud topology assumption into code.** **DECISION (user): concurrency is a property of the specific PROVIDER CREDENTIAL** (the user's lm_studio endpoint / their OpenAI key+tier), set by them — NOT per-kind, NOT per-cloud/local. **▶ `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG` (next, M):** (a) **DB** — `provider_credentials.max_concurrency INT` (default sensible); (b) **governor/queue refactor** — key the semaphore by the **credential** (`concKey(credential_id)`, `semFor(credential_id)`) with the limit from the credential, replacing `localKinds`/`maxFor(kind)`; the queue's `KindResolver` → returns `(credential_key, limit)`; (c) **FE** — a "Max concurrent requests" field in the BYOK provider/credential settings (ai-models). The worker-side concurrency (`c37629f1`) + wizard field (`73c7ff3e`) are correct + necessary — they're just gated by this gateway cap until it's fixed. (The MCP `translation_start_extraction` agent path can also pass `concurrency_level` now.) (Note: knowledge-service KG-build extraction is a SEPARATE system with its own concurrency_level — not this worker.)
> - ✅ `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG` — **SHIPPED + LIVE-PROVEN (2026-06-26), provider-registry + FE.** The gateway no longer bakes a local/cloud topology assumption into code; concurrency is a property of the specific **provider credential** (the user's decision), and the **default is UNLIMITED** (request-as-demand — neither the platform nor the user knows a cloud backend's real limit, so don't pretend to). **What changed:** (a) **migration** — `provider_credentials.max_concurrency INT` NULL (NULL/≤0 = unlimited). (b) **governor** ([governor.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/ratelimit/governor.go)) — deleted `localKinds`/`maxFor`/`MaxFor` (the `lm_studio→1` hardcap); `Acquire(ctx, concClass, limit)` short-circuits to a no-op release when `limit≤0` (never touches Redis). `Guard`/`ConcurrencyGovernor` now take `(concClass, limit)`. (c) **queue** ([queue.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/jobs/queue.go)) — `KindResolver`→`ConcurrencyResolver` returns `(concClass, limit, ok)`; `semFor(class, limit)` returns **nil** (no gate) when unlimited, else a buffered chan of `limit`; `handleDelivery` skips the gate when nil; dropped `cloudMax` from `NewJobQueue`. (d) **resolver** — new `repo.ResolveConcurrency` keys by `provider_credential_id` (user_model JOIN credential; platform_model → `platform:<id>`, unlimited); the worker computes `(concClass, concLimit)` once in `Process` (fail-open → unlimited) and threads it through `processChunks`/`streamWithRetry` (replacing `providerKind` in the Guard call); deleted the now-orphaned `ResolveKind`. (e) **CRUD contract** — `max_concurrency` in create/list/get + a **present-aware PATCH** (`optionalInt` distinguishes absent=keep from null=clear-to-unlimited via `CASE WHEN $present THEN $val ELSE max_concurrency END`). (f) **FE** — "Max concurrent requests" number field (''=unlimited) in `ProvidersTab` edit dialog (the AI-model provider editor) + `ProviderCredential.max_concurrency` type + create/patch payloads; i18n ×4. **VERIFY:** go build + all go tests green (jobs/ratelimit/api; new `TestGovernor_UnlimitedPassesThroughWithoutRedis`, `TestSemFor_PerCredentialSizing`, `TestResolveConcurrency_*`); FE tsc 0 + settings vitest 19/19. **✅ LIVE A/B on deployed stack (Gemma/lm_studio, sampling concurrent `running` llm_jobs — cache-independent):** cap=1 → **max 1 concurrent** (serialized); unlimited(default) → **max 4 concurrent** (the worker's `concurrency=4` fan-out now lands 4-wide on LM Studio — the exact win the user wanted). **✅ LIVE PATCH round-trip:** set 4→`4`, patch unrelated field→**keeps 4** (present-aware), clear null→**unlimited**. Credential left at unlimited. Closes the gateway-cap bottleneck that `D-EXTRACTION-BATCH-CONCURRENCY` surfaced; the worker fan-out (`c37629f1`) + wizard field (`73c7ff3e`) are now unblocked. **Known limitation (conscious):** the QUEUE `semFor` caches a credential's cap on first sight (a buffered chan can't resize), so ANY numeric→numeric runtime change (raise OR lower) needs a restart to affect the queue gate; switching TO unlimited is immediate (nil-sem path bypasses the cache). The Redis governor (per-CALL gate in `Guard`) reads the cap fresh each `Acquire`, so it honors a change immediately — during a post-change/pre-restart window the effective cap is the TIGHTER of {cached queue cap, fresh governor cap} (safe: never exceeds the new value; a raise just lags). **Also:** cloud/platform models (NULL cap) are now UNLIMITED-by-default — the old `GOVERNOR_CLOUD_MAX=8` per-kind cap is gone (the user's explicit "default unlimited"); backstop = the queue consumer pool (`workers≈GovernorCloudMax*2`, ~16), so one process still can't exceed ~16 concurrent jobs. **/review-impl fix folded in:** the queue's job→class resolver now FAILS-OPEN on a transient lookup error (runs ungoverned) instead of ack+dropping — fail-closed stranded a `pending` job forever (the truth-sweeper only recovers stuck `running`); only a genuinely-gone model is dropped. Mirrors `Process`'s fail-open.
> - ✅ `D-GLOSSARY-VC-FE-WIRING` — **SHIPPED + BROWSER-SMOKED (2026-06-26), FE-only.** The user's "glossary version-control has a backend but was never wired to the FE" was *nearly* right: the entity-revision UI (`EntityHistoryPanel` + `useEntityRevisions` + types + i18n) was **fully built but stranded in dead code** — `pages/book-tabs/EntityEditor.tsx`, a sidebar editor **never imported/mounted anywhere**. Users only ever see `components/entity-editor/EntityEditorModal.tsx` (rendered by `GlossaryTab.tsx`), which lacked it. Diff of the two editors: the dead one's ONLY delta was the History button — the live modal supersedes it on every other axis (evidence tab, translations, i18n, typed cards). **Fix = migrate-then-delete:** (a) made `EntityHistoryPanel` embeddable (`embedded?: boolean` drops its overlay header + inner scroller so it renders as tab content; `onClose` now optional, default false keeps existing callers/tests unchanged); (b) added a **History tab** to `EntityEditorModal` reusing the panel + hook (lazy fetch on tab open, mirrors the evidences tab; restore → `load()` + `onSaved()` refresh); (c) `modal.tab.history` i18n ×4; (d) **deleted the dead `EntityEditor.tsx`** (drift hazard). Backend (entity_revisions list/view/restore, async VG-1 projection capture, exact id-preserving reconcile) was already complete + wired. **VERIFY:** tsc 0; **105/105** glossary+entity-editor tests (new embedded-mode `EntityHistoryPanel` test); i18n parity 4 locales; no dangling ref to the deleted file. **✅ BROWSER-SMOKED on rebuilt frontend image** (test acct, Dracula book `019eeb09`, entity "The Night Journey" `019eeb22` w/ 4 revisions): History tab renders the revision list (localized ops/actors) → **View** expands the full whole-entity snapshot → **Restore** behind confirm → real server-side reconcile (entity reverted to rev #1: name vi→en, translations 8→0 pruned) → captured as new reversible revision #5 (DB-confirmed) → surfaced in the list on refetch; restored back to #4 (translations re-added → 8) to leave data as found. Lesson saved: [[feedback_feature_wired_into_dead_editor_is_invisible]]. (Wiki revisions — a SEPARATE VC system in the same service — remain their own FE concern; not in scope.)
> - ✅ `D-KG-TL-SIMPLIFIED-TRADITIONAL-DUP` — **SHIPPED via wire-then-wipe (2026-06-26).** **DECISION (user): the existing KG is disposable test data → wipe + re-extract, NO node-merge migration.** So: the multi-language normalizer (`name_normalize.py`) is now **WIRED into `canonicalize_entity_name`/`canonicalize_text`** (SDK `loreweave_extraction.canonical`) as their first step — NFKC + Unicode casefold + CJK traditional→simplified fold (vendored frozen table), accents PRESERVED. Variant spellings of one entity (張若塵/张若尘, full-width `Ｋａｉ`/`Kai`, ß/ss, composed/decomposed accents) now share ONE `canonical_id` instead of spawning duplicates. **Backward-compatible:** all 22 legacy canonical cases unchanged (ASCII casefold==lower, already-simplified CJK/kana/hangul untouched); only true variants fold. **Dry-run proof** (read-only, on 万古神帝 5705 entities BEFORE wiping): **69 same-source S/T duplicate groups** would collapse (incl. the protagonist 張若塵+张若尘 — the participant-localization miss). VERIFY: 12/12 SDK `name_normalize` tests + 22 legacy canonical cases + id-collapse + canonicalize_text fold. **✅ WIPED + DEPLOYED (2026-06-26):** Neo4j KG wiped (`infra-neo4j-1` batched DETACH DELETE, **6315 → 0 nodes**); knowledge-service + translation-service + translation-worker images rebuilt with the SDK change; deployed fold verified live (`infra-knowledge-service-1` canonicalizes 張若塵→张若尘, matches 张若尘). Re-extraction of any book now produces deduped entities at the source — no S/T duplicates. Migration design retained in the spec for reference (NOT built). **✅ LIVE DEDUP SMOKE PASSED (2026-06-26, deployed stack + real Neo4j):** drove the deployed `merge_entity` write primitive with 張若塵 (traditional) then 张若尘 (simplified) — both produced the **SAME `canonical_id` → ONE `:Entity` node** (`canonical_name='张若尘'`, `aliases=['張若塵','张若尘']` — both surface forms kept as aliases); a control 八王子 stayed a DISTINCT entity (no over-merge); total nodes = 2 as expected; cleaned up. Proves the exact bug fixed end-to-end on real Neo4j (more decisive than an LLM extraction, which on simplified-script source wouldn't exercise the trad→simpl fold). A full re-extraction is the user's normal next step (re-populates the KG with deduped entities). **Phase-1 detail (superseded by this wiring):** Generalized per the user to **multi-language** (not hard-coded CJK). Spec [docs/specs/2026-06-26-multilang-entity-normalization-dedup.md](../specs/2026-06-26-multilang-entity-normalization-dedup.md). **Root cause:** `canonicalize_entity_name` (SDK `loreweave_extraction.canonical`) does lowercase+honorific+punct only — no Unicode-equivalence/script folding — so variant spellings of one entity get different `canonical_id` (the Neo4j `:Entity` MERGE key) → duplicate nodes (張若塵 w/ vi vs 张若尘 w/o). **Converter decision (forced by determinism):** the CJK simplified/traditional fold uses a **vendored FROZEN table**, NOT opencc/hanziconv — `entity_canonical_id` must be stable forever, and an external lib could change its mapping between versions → silent mass re-keying. NFKC + casefold are stdlib. **✅ Phase 1 (PURE, no data touched):** new SDK `name_normalize.py` (`nfkc_casefold` → `fold_han_simplified` → `normalize_entity_name`; Han-gated; accents PRESERVED — folds equivalence not similarity, so vi `má`≠`ma`) + vendored `_han_simplified_table.py` (curated high-confidence T→S starter set incl. the 万古神帝 cast; documented-incomplete, full OpenCC import is a Phase-2 follow-up, API unchanged when it grows). NOT yet wired into the live id derivation (cutover lands WITH the migration). VERIFY: 12/12 SDK unit tests (ASCII unchanged, full-width→ascii, ß→ss, 張若塵→张若尘, accents kept, kana untouched, guards). **▶ Phase 2 (NEXT, needs go):** wire `canonicalize_entity_name`→`normalize_entity_name` (NO `canonical_version` bump → surgical: only changed-string entities affected, ASCII untouched) + a per-(user,project) **dedup migration** that groups entities by `(kind, normalize_entity_name(name))`, merges variant duplicates via the existing `merge_entities` primitive (entities.py:2488 — rewires relations/facts/evidence) **+ two gaps it predates:** repoint `:Event.participant_entity_ids` (loser→winner) + `entity_alias_map` redirects; updates the winner's `canonical_name` to the new form. **DRY-RUN mode first** (report duplicate groups + planned merges, write nothing) on 万古神帝 `019effe4` before any destructive run, then live-smoke (张若尘/張若塵 collapse → timeline localizes 张若尘). Risk: re-keys the entity primary key — mitigated by no-version-bump + dry-run + the tested merge primitive + per-project scoping + merge journal.
> - ✅ `D-GLOSSARY-ST-DEDUP` — **SHIPPED + LIVE-APPLIED (2026-06-26), glossary-service + new Go SDK.** The glossary-side sibling of `D-KG-TL-SIMPLIFIED-TRADITIONAL-DUP` (that one fixed the knowledge-service / Neo4j layer in Python; this fixes glossary's Go/Postgres entity dedup, which holds REAL user data → NO wipe, a journaled merge instead). Spec [2026-06-26-glossary-st-dedup.md](../specs/2026-06-26-glossary-st-dedup.md). Commits `fa8e702b`(M1) `a0ebb61c`(M2) `636c5843`(M3) `9c594865`(review fixes). **M1 — new Go SDK `sdks/go/loreweave_extraction`** (`NormalizeEntityName` = NFKC + Unicode casefold + CJK trad→simpl; mirror of the Python `name_normalize`). The Han **T2S table is code-generated from a NEW shared SoT** `sdks/data/han_t2s.tsv`; both the Go table and the frozen Python dict are pinned to it by **parity tests on each side** (Go `TestT2SParityWithSoT` + Python `test_t2s_parity`) so the fold can never silently drift across languages. **M2 — glossary `textnorm.Normalize` delegates to the SDK** → the **resolver** (`findEntityByNameOrAlias`, the PRIMARY dedup — it compares names IN GO) now folds, so NEW extractions of 张若尘/張若塵 resolve to one entity instead of spawning a dup. **M3a — `normalized_name` GENERATED→app-maintained:** migration `0040` `ALTER COLUMN … DROP EXPRESSION` (preserves values, non-destructive, attgenerated-guarded) — Postgres has no native casefold/t2s, so the fold can't live in SQL; `refreshEntityDedupKey(q, entityID)` recomputes it from `cached_name` via the SAME SDK fold, wired into **all 6 audited name-write paths** (createExtractedEntity, mergeExtractedEntity, patchAttributeValue[name/term], applyEntityEdit, reconcileEntityFromSnapshot, rekeyEntityToKind). **M3b — internal remediation endpoint** `POST /internal/books/{id}/dedup-name-variants[?apply=true]` (DRY-RUN default): groups live entities by `(kind, folded name)`, merges each >1 group into the richest winner (links+evidence, stable tie-break) via the journaled `mergeEntitiesCore` (reversible; emits the `merged` outbox event that re-syncs the KG), then re-stamps `normalized_name`. **🔎 /review-impl caught 2 HIGH before the destructive apply:** (1) re-stamp ran BEFORE merge → both live group members collided on `uq_entity_dedup` → apply 500s; fixed by merge-first-then-restamp + unique-violation tolerance. (2) the shared test helper `runK2aMigrations` ran `0032`(generated col) but not `0040` → every wired path hit "column can only be updated to DEFAULT"; fixed by running `0040` in the helper + rewriting `TestEntityDedup_UniqueIndexBackstop` to drive the backstop through the app path (the post-M3a invariant: the DB backstop is keyed when the APP stamps the key, not on a raw EAV write that bypasses the app — the accepted app-maintained tradeoff). **VERIFY:** Go SDK + textnorm + betterWinner unit tests green; **full glossary api integration suite green** (no generated-column errors). **✅ LIVE-APPLIED on 万古神帝 `019efae6` (deployed stack):** migration ran on boot (column now plain, ledger `0040`); dry-run found **63 S/T duplicate groups** (Go endpoint exactly matched a read-only Python dry-run — cross-confirming the fold); **apply merged all 63 cleanly + re-stamped 4001**, protagonist `張若塵`(live winner, normalized_name=张若尘) ← `张若尘`(soft-deleted, merged_into winner); **idempotent re-run → 0 groups** (total 5646→5583, exactly −63). **✅ Cross-service live-smoke:** 63 `glossary.entity_merged` + 63 `glossary.entity_updated` events emitted → knowledge-service consumed cleanly (winners synced to Neo4j; merges logged "KG nodes absent — no-op, reconverges" since the KG was wiped earlier — no errors). **✅ All 3 follow-ups CLEARED (2026-06-26, commit `b2439fcf`, deployed + live-verified):** (1) **`D-GLOSSARY-ST-RENAME-409`** — `refreshEntityDedupKey` returns a typed `errDuplicateName` on a `uq_entity_dedup` collision; the 3 user-facing name-write handlers (patchAttributeValue, applyEntityEdit, restore) map it to **409 `GLOSS_DUPLICATE_NAME`** (unit-asserted via `TestEntityDedup_UniqueIndexBackstop`). (2) **`D-GLOSSARY-DEDUP-ENDPOINT-LOCK`** — the apply path takes the per-book advisory lock (same key as the extraction writeback, NS `0x45585457`/`1163416663`) on a dedicated conn; try-lock → **409 `GLOSS_BOOK_BUSY`** when extraction holds it (dry-run stays lock-free). **Live-verified**: held the lock in psql → apply returned 409. (3) **Full OpenCC table** — `sdks/data/han_t2s.tsv` regenerated from OpenCC `TSCharacters.txt` (**224→3149 pairs**, unambiguous 1-to-1 non-identity only — multi-candidate context-dependent entries EXCLUDED to avoid over-merge); both the Go table + Python dict regenerated from it, parity tests green. **Live-applied on 万古神帝**: the fuller table unlocked **84 MORE genuine S/T dup groups** the curated 224 missed (88 entities merged, total 5583→5495), all verified genuine (`跡/蹟→迹`, `勳/勛→勋`, `氣→气`; `乾坤` correctly NOT folded — 乾 was an excluded multi-candidate, so no 乾/干 over-merge); 88 `entity_merged` events re-synced the KG cleanly. Idempotent re-run → 0 groups.
> - ✅ `D-KG-TL-PARTICIPANT-ANCHOR` — **SHIPPED + LIVE-SMOKED (2026-06-26), knowledge-service.** Spec [2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md](../specs/2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md) §(c) Option A · plan [2026-06-26-kg-tl-participant-anchor.md](../plans/2026-06-26-kg-tl-participant-anchor.md). Replaces M2's **read-time** participant name→entity resolution with a **stored anchor** resolved at extraction time. **What changed:** (a) shared `resolve_participant_anchors(session, user_id, project_id, names) -> {name: glossary_entity_id}` in [entities.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/entities.py) (anchored-first via `find_entities_by_name`) — ONE resolver for write + read-fallback + backfill. (b) `Event.participant_entity_ids: list[str] | None` (`Field(exclude=True)` — stored on the node, populated for the localizer, NEVER serialized → API wire unchanged); `merge_event` gains `participant_anchors` + builds the array ALIGNED to the deduped participants (glossary id, or `""` sentinel where unanchored — Neo4j lists can't hold nulls); `_MERGE_EVENT_CYPHER` writes it ON CREATE + appends new slots ON MATCH via an index-filter with `coalesce($participant_entity_ids[i], "")` (guards the empty-array/legacy case so no null ever enters the list). (c) [pass2_writer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/pass2_writer.py) resolves anchors before `merge_event` (Pass-1 entities already in graph). (d) [timeline_localizer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/timeline_localizer.py) `localize_participants` now PREFERS the stored anchor (length-guard: trust only an array whose len == participants) and resolves only the RESIDUAL names from un-backfilled events — a fully-backfilled page does ZERO read-time resolution; legacy/misaligned falls back to Option B. (e) backfill [backfill_participant_anchors.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/migrations/backfill_participant_anchors.py) + internal `POST /internal/projects/{id}/backfill-participant-anchors` (mirrors backfill-orders). AC-T3 preserved: unanchored → source name + marker, never a silent mix. **VERIFY:** py_compile clean; **6/6** new participant-anchor unit tests (prefer-stored / mixed-residual / misaligned-fallback / resolver / backfill / exclude-from-wire) + **11/11** timeline-localization (no fallback regression); full unit suite **2928 passed** (the 76 failures/16 errors ALL environmental — auth-401 on `test_internal_*` HTTP-endpoint tests run inside the live container + missing test-dep `respx` — none in changed logic); ON MATCH Cypher comprehension validated live in Neo4j (empty-ids → `["",""]`, aligned append → `[idA,idB,idC]`). **✅ LIVE-SMOKE (rebuilt knowledge image) on the 万古神帝 project `019effe4` (219 events, Chinese participants):** backfill → **191/219 events anchored, 264 anchors resolved**; stored arrays **aligned, 0 misaligned**; timeline read (`?language=vi`, minted owner JWT) localized participants FROM the stored anchors — 八王子→**Bát Vương Tử**, 林妃→**Lâm Phi** (`translated=True`), with 张若尘/池瑶 → source + marker (`translated=False`, their anchored entity has no vi name). **Deferred note `D-KG-TL-SIMPLIFIED-TRADITIONAL-DUP` (conscious, out-of-scope):** the smoke surfaced that simplified participant "张若尘" anchors to a simplified-script DUPLICATE glossary entity (`019f00de…`) lacking the vi translation, while the canonical traditional "張若塵" (`019efffb-c963…`) has it — an entity-dedup / CJK-variant resolution-quality issue in `find_entities_by_name` + the data, not this feature. **Residual `D-KG-TL-PA-REEXTRACT`:** existing events anchored via the backfill; a re-extraction of those chapters now also anchors at write time (pass2_writer path proven by the 8 new entities created mid-run resolving correctly).
> - ✅ `D-PENDING-REDEPLOY-SWEEP` — **CLEARED (2026-06-26, rebuilt stack).** Live-verified the three fixes previously flagged "⏳ NEEDS redeploy + live verify"; all deployed + working, so those flags are now stale (see each item's "NEEDS redeploy" note — superseded by this sweep). (A) **planner-default relax** (provider-registry) — full live round-trip through the gateway: `PUT /v1/model-registry/default-models/planner` with a **chat** model (`019ebb72` Gemma-4 26B) returned **200 ACCEPTED** (was 422 pre-relax, which demanded a non-existent `planner:true` flag), the internal resolver then returned `source:planner_default`, and clearing it reverted to `chat_fallback` (test account left as found). (B) **per-session planner override** (chat-service) — migration applied live (`chat_sessions.planner_model_source` + `planner_model_ref`) + deployed `stream_service.py` carries the inject (13 refs). (C) **translation reasoning toggle** (translation-service) — migration applied live (`translation_jobs.thinking_enabled boolean default false`) + deployed `session_translator.py` uses `thinking_llm_fields` (2) + `jobs.py` threads `thinking_enabled` (4). **Residual (optional, NOT run — deeper behavioral smokes, not redeploy concerns):** a full chat-session planner-override end-to-end (needs the chat UI flow, = `D-PLAN-UI-SMOKE`) and a `thinking_enabled=true` translation job (costs LLM tokens).
> - 🔵 **3 user-reported glossary/KG issues — ROOT-CAUSED 2026-06-26 (3 parallel investigations), CLARIFY-next (user chose "clarify them first").** Extra context from user: the **raw extraction cache already works → reuse it** for re-extraction (no re-LLM); **glossary version-control has a BACKEND but was never wired to the FE.**
>   - `D-KG-TIMELINE-I18N` — **Timeline tab mixes source-language + English.** Root cause: `/v1/knowledge/timeline` ([public/timeline.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/timeline.py)) serves `:Event` nodes whose `title`/`summary`/`participants` are stored SOURCE-language by design, with **no `language` param + no Layer-2 localization** — and `chapter_title_enricher.py` joins a book-/translated-language chapter heading onto each row (the visible mix). The multilingual design's C7 localization (plan [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../plans/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md)) only covered the **edge-timeline** in `graph_views.py`, never the Event timeline; spec still DRAFT. **Fix:** extend C7 to this endpoint (add `language`, resolve participant names via glossary join, pass `language` to chapter-title fetch). **DESIGN CALL:** free-text event `summary`/`time_cue` have no translation layer — keep source-lang + "untranslated" marker (spec AC1) vs add a derived-translation layer. Size M. **✅ SHIPPED (2026-06-26), spec [docs/specs/2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md](../specs/2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md), user decision = BOTH (localize names/titles + translate summaries on-demand+cached like glossary):** **M1** — `/v1/knowledge/timeline` resolves a reader language (explicit `?language=` → `get_reader_language` → none, mirroring graph_views) + threads it to the chapter-title fetch; **book-service** `/internal/chapters/titles` gained an optional `language` that LEFT-JOINs the sibling-language `chapters` row at the same `(book_id, sort_order)` (translated chapters are separate rows — Q3 resolved) + `primaryLangSubtag` fold. **M2** — `timeline_localizer.localize_participants` inverts participant NAME→glossary_entity_id (`find_entities_by_name`, anchored-first) then reuses C7's `fetch_entity_display_names`; per-slot source-fallback + `*_translated` flag. **M3** — new `event_text_translations` cache (glossary-shaped: PK(event_id,field,lang), confidence, source_hash staleness, project/user purge) + `EventTextTranslationsRepo` (coalesce-read, upsert-never-clobber-verified); `localize_event_text` fills from cache, misses fall back to source+marker (AC-T4) and fire a **pool-safe fire-and-forget** lazy translate via a NEW translation-service internal `POST /internal/translation/translate-text` (reuses `translate_text_core` → provider-registry, no SDK/no hardcoded model). FE: `TimelineEventRow` renders localized title/participants/summary with an accessible `SourceMarker` when a `*_translated` flag is false; `useTimeline`/`listTimeline`/`TimelineTab` forward `i18n.language`; i18n×4. `Event` `@model_serializer` omits the 8 new fields when unset → AC-T5 byte-identical back-compat. **VERIFY:** knowledge-service unit suite **3113 pass** (11 new localization + 2 DDL); book-service build+tests (new `TestPrimaryLangSubtag`); translation py_compile; FE tsc 0 + TimelineTab 15/15; **integration smoke: knowledge redeployed HEALTHY + `event_text_translations` migration applied + clean startup**; book/translation healthy. **Deferred:** ✅ `D-KG-TL-LIVE-SMOKE` — **CLEARED (2026-06-26, rebuilt stack).** The **knowledge→translation translate-and-cache** half is PROVEN live end-to-end: (a) direct `POST /internal/translation/translate-text` for the 万古神帝 owner (BYOK model `019ebb6e…`) translated the real mixed-language event summary `"Zhang Ruochen is killed by his fiancée, Chi Yao."` → `"Zhang Ruochen bị vị hôn thê của mình, Chi Yao, giết chết."` (knowledge→translation→provider-registry, no SDK/no hardcoded model); (b) the **full timeline cache cycle** on the test-account Dracula project `019ef35e` via the real `GET /v1/knowledge/timeline?language=vi`: read #1 = clean cache MISS → every field source-text + `*_translated=False` + lazy fill fires (AC-T4, GET never blocked); background fill wrote 3 events × {summary,title} into `event_text_translations`; read #2 = coalesce-read cache HIT → `summary_localized`/`title_localized` in Vietnamese + `*_translated=True`. Participant path also ran (English names → glossary anchor MISS → source + `translated=False`, the correct AC-T3 no-silent-mix outcome). **The knowledge→book vi-heading half could NOT be exercised** — every live `:Event` on both candidate projects has `chapter_id=NULL` (events extracted without chapter linkage), so the M1 sibling-language heading join has no live row to hit; it stays unit-tested (`TestPrimaryLangSubtag` + book-service titles tests) until an extraction that links chapters lands. `D-KG-TL-PARTICIPANT-ANCHOR` (Option A: store `participant_entity_ids` on the Event node + backfill — durable follow-up to M2's read-time name resolution).
>   - `D-EVIDENCE-PROVENANCE-GUI` — **evidence "metadata not collected / GUI unusable".** Calibrated: provenance (char_start/end, block, trust `provenance_status` exact/resolved/ambiguous/unmatched) **IS validated upstream + stored** ([extraction_worker.py:1091](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py#L1091) → glossary writeback) but **dropped at two seams** — the list-API SELECT ([evidence_handler.go:194](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/evidence_handler.go#L194)) omits the columns + the FE type lacks the fields. API **is already paginated** (20/page, sort+filters) — it does NOT load 10k at once. **One real COLLECTION bug:** writeback stamps `chapter_id` from `ChapterLinks[0]` (entity's FIRST chapter), not the quote's actual chapter ([extraction_handler.go:857](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L857)). **Fixes:** (a) surface char offsets + `provenance_status` through API+FE w/ trust badge; (b) fix chapter pointer (pass per-quote chapter in payload); (c) "open source" link → reader@chapter+offset (refs overload `block_or_line` w/ URL — render as link); (d) real pager (jump-to-page/page-size, keyset for deep offset). Size L. **✅ FULLY SHIPPED (2026-06-26), spec [docs/specs/2026-06-26-evidence-provenance-overhaul.md](../specs/2026-06-26-evidence-provenance-overhaul.md):** **M1** `0c42001a` — list API SELECT + FE `EvidenceListItem` surface `char_start/end/provenance_status`; `EvidenceCard` trust badge (ambiguous/unmatched/unverified; verified stays clean); i18n×4. **M2** `10910c18` — `evidenceChapterFor(req.ChapterID,…)` stamps the quote's REAL chapter (the per-chapter writeback scope) not `ChapterLinks[0]`; removed dead `firstChapterID`; `TestEvidenceChapterFor`. **M3** `a2172461` — `chapter_title` → reader deep-link `/books/{id}/chapters/{cid}/read?hl=start-end`; reference `block_or_line` URL → external link; bookId threaded. **M4** `ba7f7655` — pager: page-size (20/50/100) + jump-to-page + first/last (offset-based; keyset deferred = profiling-gated perf). **M5** `49c396fa` — evidence `ON CONFLICT` now refreshes chapter_id/title/index too, so the ③-M3 `rerun-merge` HEAL backfills historical evidence (wrong chapter + provenance) in place. Glossary redeployed. **Note:** reader `?hl=` highlight is a hint the ReaderPage doesn't consume yet (future reader enhancement).
>   - ✅ `D-MERGE-STRATEGY-UI` (review LOW ⑤) — **SHIPPED `e752acfa`:** the book-attribute ontology editor (`AttributeEditorPanel`) exposes a "Re-extraction merge" select (append/overwrite/fill_if_empty/manual) so authors override the seeded heuristic; `merge_strategy` is now patchable (`scanBookAttrFields`) + returned by the single + bulk ontology loads; i18n×4.
>   - `D-EXTRACT-ATTR-MERGE-DEFAULTS` — **re-extraction appends evidence only, never updates attributes.** Root cause: every layer defaults the per-attribute merge action to **`fill`** = write-once-if-empty → already-filled attrs skipped (`fill_occupied`, [extraction_handler.go:1376](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L1376)); FE/worker/DB (`merge_strategy DEFAULT 'fill_if_empty'`) all converge on fill. The engine **already supports `append`(multi-value)+`overwrite`** (tested) but nothing selects them + `append` isn't in the FE enum ([types.ts:29](../../frontend/src/features/extraction/types.ts#L29)). NOT a description/ontology-text issue. **Fix = config/code:** seed per-attribute `merge_strategy` (`append` for aliases/titles/abilities, `overwrite` for location/status, `fill` for identity) + expose `append` in FE; keep INV-8 verified-clobber guard. Reuse the raw cache to re-merge without re-LLM. Size M. **Spec:** [docs/specs/2026-06-26-extraction-attribute-merge-defaults.md](../specs/2026-06-26-extraction-attribute-merge-defaults.md). **▶ M1+M2 SHIPPED (2026-06-26):** **M1** — migration `0039_merge_strategy_heuristic` ([merge_strategy_heuristic.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/merge_strategy_heuristic.go)) re-seeds `merge_strategy` by type across all 3 attr tiers (`code IN(name,term)` or required-text→`fill_if_empty`; `field_type=tags`→`append`; else→`overwrite`), CONDITIONAL on the untouched `fill_if_empty` default so author/admin overrides are never clobbered (idempotent). **M2** — the resolver ([extraction_handler.go:1341](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L1341)) now treats an explicit `"default"` action like an omitted one (defer to authored strategy); the worker default-profile builders ([extraction.py:159](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py#L159), [mcp/server.py:729](../../services/translation-service/app/mcp/server.py#L729)) emit `"default"` instead of forcing `"fill"`; FE `AttributeAction` gains `default`+`append`, `StepProfile` auto-selects `default` (required stays `fill`) + adds Append/Default options & bulk buttons; i18n ×4. **VERIFY:** real-PG `TestMergeStrategy_DefaultSentinel` (default→append accumulates, default→overwrite replaces) + full extraction-writeback suite green; FE tsc 0 + 8/8; **migration applied LIVE** on real glossary DB (distribution tags→append 3422, textarea→overwrite 11673, text identity/state split, 1 pre-existing author `append` preserved); worker→glossary `extract-entities` **HTTP 200** on redeployed code (in-flight 4233-ch job 019f00e2 non-regressed). **▶ M3 SHIPPED (2026-06-26):** raw-cache re-merge HEAL to unfreeze ALREADY-extracted entities at $0 LLM. `replay_chapter_from_cache(use_authored_strategy=True)` ([extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py)) sends `attribute_actions={}` so the glossary resolver defers every attr to its authored strategy (append/overwrite), and adds a `"heal"` discriminator to the whole-chapter writeback_key so a chapter already written under the old frozen `fill` isn't deduped against the faithful write (re-running heal stays idempotent via the distinct key + per-item append dedup). New `rerun_merge_book` + endpoint `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/rerun-merge` ([internal_dispatch.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/internal_dispatch.py)) heals a whole book (or a `chapter_ids` subset), grant-gated EDIT + confirm-gated dry-run, bounded response (status counts + capped problem sample). **VERIFY:** 2 DB-free unit tests (aggregation + 50-cap) green; **LIVE on the user's running book:** dry-run → `mode:heal, would_write:60` for 2 cached chapters; confirm on 1 early chapter → `status_counts:{replayed:1}` (re-merged via translation→glossary under authored strategy, $0 LLM, no race with the in-flight 4233-ch job). **Use for the big book once its extraction is idle** to heal all chapters frozen under the old default.
> - 🟡 `D-LLM-FAILURE-RATE` — **NEW (2026-06-26), in progress. DIAGNOSIS CORRECTED via live data.** Initial theory (truncation/thinking burning the output budget) was **disproven on the running stack**: `extraction_raw_outputs` over the live run shows `reasoning_effort=none` + `effort_band=none` on **every** call, **0** outputs containing `<think>`, `finish_reason=stop` everywhere (**no** `length`-truncation), and **151/155 batches clean**. All 3 workers already disable thinking correctly (extraction via `reasoning_fields`, chapter-translate hardcodes `enable_thinking:False` [session_translator.py:925](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/session_translator.py#L925), glossary-translate via `thinking_llm_fields`), and the gateway forwards `chat_template_kwargs` to lm_studio (only strips it for the *default* OpenAI cloud endpoint). So the `completed_with_errors` wall is **overstated** — the flag fires on a SINGLE non-clean batch; real rate ≈5%, cause = **validation rejection on cleanly-finished output** (model occasionally returns wrong-kind / rarely-malformed JSON), NOT thinking/truncation. **Fix = structured-output enforcement (#1).** ✅ **Gateway enabling slice SHIPPED:** `streamRequest.ResponseFormat` + `buildChatStreamInput` thread `response_format` into the adapter input (the `forwardOptionalChatFields` allowlist already lists it but nothing wired it from the request); absent-when-unset; build/vet clean + 3 `buildChatStreamInput` tests (forwards + omits-when-unset). ✅ **Extraction worker slice SHIPPED:** `_entity_response_format(batch_kinds)` builds a LOOSE json_schema (top-level array; item requires `kind`=enum-of-batch-kinds + `name`; `additionalProperties:true` so per-kind profile fields stay free) attached as `response_format` when `_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED` (env `EXTRACTION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`, default ON); **safe fallback** — the call is wrapped so a provider `LLMInvalidRequest` (model/server rejects the shape) **retries ONCE without response_format**, then a 2nd failure propagates as the real permanent error. py_compile clean; 2 new schema-builder tests + 25 existing extraction tests green. **▶ NEXT:** (a) same pattern for **glossary-translate** ([glossary_translate_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_worker.py)) + **chapter-translate** ([session_translator.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/session_translator.py)) — their output schemas differ; (b) ✅ **`D-LLM-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT-LIVE-SMOKE` — PASSED (2026-06-26, deployed stack).** Verified the deployed provider-registry Go binary carries the `response_format` wiring + translation-worker has `_entity_response_format`. Then a real gateway call (`/internal/llm/stream`, test-account local **Qwen2.5 7B Instruct** `019eb620…`, the loose entity json_schema) returned **HTTP 200 + a clean conforming array** `[{"kind":"location","name":"royal martial arena"},{"kind":"character","name":"Zhang Ruochen"},{"kind":"character","name":"Eighth Prince"}]` (n=3, valid kinds from the enum). The **control WITHOUT response_format reproduced the bug**: malformed `["kind","Zhang Ruochen"]` arrays → PARSE FAILED — the exact `validation_rejected`/`completed_with_errors` failure mode. Proves: gateway forwards it, lm_studio honors the json_schema (no LLMInvalidRequest → fallback not even needed for this model), and the schema converts malformed output into clean valid-kind output. Residual: a full extraction-JOB end-to-end (worker→outcomes) wasn't driven (needs a cache-miss chapter), but the worker sends this exact response_format + is unit-tested. ✅ **Glossary-translate slice SHIPPED + object-schema live-verified:** `attr_response_format(expected_codes)` (a JSON **object** of string-valued props keyed by the attribute codes, `additionalProperties:false`) in [glossary_translate_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_prompt.py); the worker attaches it (gated by `TRANSLATION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`, default ON) + the same retry-without `LLMInvalidRequest` fallback. Kills the "Expecting ',' delimiter" parse failures that failed an entity. 3 schema tests + 102 translate-suite tests green; py_compile clean. **Object-schema live-smoke PASSED** (same gateway call, object json_schema → HTTP 200, valid `{"name":"…"}` object of strings — lm_studio accepts the object shape too). ⛔ **Chapter-translate EXCLUDED from #1 — NOT a JSON pipeline:** its output is `[BLOCK N]` marker TEXT ([block_batcher.py:254](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/block_batcher.py#L254) splits on the markers), so a JSON `response_format` would BREAK it; its failures are a different class (block-count/validation) already handled by its retry-with-correction loop. **#1 is COMPLETE for the two JSON pipelines (extraction + glossary-translate).** ✅ **DEPLOY CONFIRMED (2026-06-26, rebuilt stack):** the running `infra-translation-worker-1`/`-service-1` image carries `attr_response_format` + both gate flags `EXTRACTION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` and `TRANSLATION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` **defaulting to `"1"`** (env unset → ON fleet-wide), so both JSON pipelines emit `response_format` live. ▶ Remaining (optional only): a full extraction-job re-run on a cache-miss chapter to watch the `completed_with_errors` rate drop on real content (the gateway-level proof already showed the schema converts malformed→clean output; this is corroboration, not a gap). #3 (split-and-retry) deprioritized — no current truncation. #2 (context_window) is fine — user confirmed lm_studio loads 200K.
> - ✅ `D-PLAN-MERGE-CONTEXT-COST` — **CLEARED (2026-06-25).** `loadMergeCandidates` gained a `limit` arg: the planner passes `maxMergeCandidatesForPlan(=25)+1` so the SQL `LIMIT` caps BOTH the candidate rows AND the per-candidate member-detail load (`allMembers` only collects from the loaded set) — every plan call now loads ≤26 clusters, not the whole `proposed` backlog. Highest-score clusters survive (ORDER BY score before LIMIT); `mergeCandidatesSummary(cands, more)` shows an honest "more candidates exist" note when the cap elided lower-score ones. FE inbox + pipeline-read tool pass `limit=0` (unlimited — they want the full set to signal truncation), so their behaviour is unchanged. Kept the LIMIT over the alternative goal-keyword gate (multilingual goals make keyword gating fragile — same call as delete-attr-vocab). VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestLoadMergeCandidates_LimitCapsLoad` (limit=2 → top-2 by score; limit=0 → all 3) + merge/neutralization family green.
> - `D-PLAN-MERGE-DISMISSED-LABEL` — **CONSCIOUS won't-fix (gate 5):** a `merge_candidate` op naming a `dismissed` candidate returns `already_done` (skipped) — a slightly loose label (the merge never happened; it was rejected), but the OUTCOME (no merge, safe) is correct and the planner only ever sees `proposed` candidates in context. The kit's sentinel set has no "rejected" reason; not worth a new one.
> **✅ SLICE 2.5 SHIPPED — `dismiss_candidate` (non-destructive sibling of merge).** Same candidate-id addressing; the planner emits `dismiss_candidate {candidate_id}` to REJECT a detected cluster as not-duplicates (flips status→`dismissed`, no entity touched), reusing the existing `dismissMergeCandidateCore`. **NON-destructive** → applies on a plain confirm with NO enable toggle (the real-PG test asserts exactly this via `f.confirm` without `enabled_ops`). Idempotency: status≠`proposed`→`already_done`; bogus→`target_gone`. Registry guard now **9 ops** (5 non-destructive incl. dismiss, 4 destructive). **🔑 also fixed a latent test-isolation bug:** the candidate real-PG tests were silently depending on another test creating `merge_candidates` first — now they run the full idempotent `runMergeMigrations` chain (correct post-G4 `kind_id`→`book_kinds` FK), so they pass in isolation.
> - ✅ `D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` — **FULLY SHIPPED + e2e-COMPLETE + review-passed 2026-06-25 (M1 + M2 + M3).** **/review-impl (3 fixed):** (1) FE progress tracked `items_processed` (visits, incl. free skips) ÷ `items_total` (search cap) → could read ">100% / 13/10"; now `searches_run`÷cap, pinned 100% on complete. (2) launch-modal estimate `useEffect` re-ran on the unstable `fetchEstimate` closure → re-fetched + **clobbered the user's `max_entities`**; now a run-once ref guard. (3) resuming a `failed` job while another live job exists for the kind tripped the one-live unique index → 500; now mapped to a clean 409. **✅ FULL E2E on the deployed stack:** recreated `infra-glossary-service-1` with this branch → migration 0038 applied + ledgered + worker auto-started; deployed routes verified (list 200 / estimate 200 / create-bad-kind 404 / no-auth 401 via a minted test-account JWT); **full HTTP cycle**: `POST research-jobs` (owner-grant on an adopted owned book + 1 seeded entity) → `pending` → the **in-container worker** drained it → **`complete`** in 6s with `searches=1, sources=3` (real searxng "Mina Harker") → `GET status` confirmed live. Earlier: worker pipeline e2e (real searxng → evidence → complete on a throwaway DB) + stubbed worker unit tests. **✅ BROWSER E2E (Playwright, temp `infra-frontend` container on `infra_default` :5199 — host vite shadows :5174):** logged in as the test account → book → Glossary → Ontology → Manage → selected the `character` kind → the **KindResearchPanel rendered** ("Web research" / "Research entities" / "No research job yet") → launch modal showed the **live estimate** ("1 of 1 entities · ~$0.0100 indicative", max_entities defaulted to the entity count = fix #2 working) → typed a query → Start → the card polled pending→running→**Complete** ("Done — 5 sources across 1 entities"); DB confirmed `complete searches=1 sources=5` + 5 reference-evidence rows. Also visually confirmed the case-2 genre/kind **delete trash is always-visible**. All artifacts cleaned up; temp container removed. **THE FEATURE IS FULLY e2e-PROVEN INCLUDING THE BROWSER UI.** **M3 (FE):** `researchApi.ts` (6 endpoints + estimate) → `useKindResearch` controller hook (react-query, polls at 2.5s while a job is active, stops on terminal) → `ResearchJobCard` (render-only per-state: pending/running+progress-bar/paused/complete/failed/cancelled with pause/resume/retry/cancel) + `ResearchLaunchModal` (query template with `{name}`, max_entities, max_results, pre-flight entity-count + indicative cost) + `KindResearchPanel` orchestrator, wired into `ManageWorkspace` under the selected kind. i18n ×4 (`research.*`). VERIFY: `ResearchJobCard` render tests (per-state actions fire the right callbacks; complete is terminal-info; failed shows the error) + tiering suite 19 green; tsc clean; 4 locale JSONs valid. **M2 (FE-prior):** see below. Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` M1+M2 line~~ — superseded by the FULLY-SHIPPED line above. **M2 (worker + lifecycle):** in-process `RunResearchWorker` (started in main.go, gated on a web-search URL) drains `pending` jobs one entity at a time — claim via `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` + stale-`running` reclaim (crash recovery), per-entity heartbeat/cursor persist (resume from `cursor_entity_id`), paid-search cap on `max_entities` (already-researched / anchorless entities skipped FREE via reference-evidence check), web-search error → `failed` without advancing cursor (resume retries). pause/resume/cancel endpoints + a shared `transitionResearchJob` (404 vs 409, clears error on resume). VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG worker tests (cap honoured + evidence attached + cursor; skip-already-researched is free; web-search-error→failed; pause/resume/cancel/404/409 state machine); **✅ live cross-service smoke** glossary→provider-registry→searxng returned 3 real results (the worker's paid leg). Caught a real test bug: uuidv7's first 8 chars are a timestamp → non-unique name suffixes tripped `uq_entity_dedup`. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE (states pending/running+progress/paused/complete/failed) + a "research all entities of this kind" launch on the kind + polling + i18n ×4.
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` M1-only note~~ — superseded by the M1+M2 line above. M1 = `entity_research_jobs` table (migration 0038, one-live-per-(book,kind) partial unique index) + `researchOneEntity` refactor (the per-entity web-search/neutralize/attach core now SHARED by the sync `glossary_deep_research` and the future worker — deep-research tests green) + create/list/status endpoints + a pre-flight `research-estimate`. **BUILD REFINEMENT:** web-search returns no per-call cost (BYOK) → capped by **`max_entities`** (each = 1 search), `est_cost_usd` is indicative only, and the `paused_budget` state is dropped (the entity cap IS the scope). `researchJobMaxEntitiesHardCap=500`. VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestResearchJob_CRUD_RealPG` (kind-validate, live count ignores soft-deleted, insert→load round-trip, list, one-live unique constraint, slot-frees-on-complete). **M2:** in-process worker draining `pending` jobs (reuse glossary's `internal/events/*` background pattern), skip-already-researched idempotency, `cursor_entity_id` crash-resume, pause/resume/cancel endpoints, cost-metering, live web-search smoke. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE + launch action on the kind + polling + i18n ×4. Full design (with superseding M1 note): [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` (original design-checkpoint note)~~ — **DESIGNED 2026-06-25 (gate 2: large/structural, XL cross-cutting).** The research-orchestration item was scoped: wiring it as a **synchronous plan op** is the wrong vehicle (the `execute_plan` executor runs in-request → a paid N-entity web-search fan-out would be minutes-long, timeout-prone, no progress/cancel; AND deep-research's "agent summarizes the attached evidence" design has no agent in the deterministic executor). **Decision (user, 2026-06-25): build it as an async batch-research JOB** hosted in **glossary-service** (it owns entities/evidence/the BYOK web-search; glossary already runs in-process `internal/events/*` consumers — no need to proxy to knowledge like wiki does). Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md) — `entity_research_jobs` table + one-live-per-(book,kind) lock, a knowledge-modeled state machine (pending→running→paused_budget/paused_user→complete/failed/cancelled) with a per-search `max_spend` ceiling + cursor crash-resume, a `researchOneEntity` refactor that shares INV-6 web-search/neutralize/attach with the existing sync `glossary_deep_research`, 6 owner/grant-gated endpoints + pre-flight cost estimate, and a `ResearchJobCard` FE. Phased M1 (core+schema+endpoints) → M2 (worker+lifecycle) → M3 (FE). Entity create/edit/rename remain **out** (UUID-addressed, names ambiguous — GUI/EAV only).
>

>
> # ▶▶ (prior epic) **Multilingual KG epic COMPLETE (M1–M7)** · branch `feat/kg-multilingual` · 2026-06-24 — ready to PR

> **EPIC: canonical-source + derived-localization multilingual KG.** Design fully clarified + red-teamed + gate-validated. Spec [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md) · detailed design [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md) · plan (7 milestones) [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../plans/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md). Design commit `1a448661`.
>
> **Core decisions (locked):** Layer-1 graph stays source-language, NEVER re-extracted from translations; derive a localization layer (L2 labels) + language-aware retrieval (L3) on top. vi content is **index-only + label-only**. **Gates:** V1 PASS (bge-m3 genuinely multilingual → single-vector-space premise holds), V7 PASS-caveat (reranker BYOK-optional → D5 must work WITHOUT it), V6 FAIL (lexical leg not CJK-tokenized → D12 real). D5 uses **soft language-preference weighting** (not hard filter — robust to partial translation + dodges Neo4j post-filter limit).
>
> **✅ M1 FOUNDATION SHIPPED (this session) — `source_lang` tag + cost metering + skip-gate.** All additive + dormant (source_lang unread until M4); no behavior change to existing retrieval.
> - **`source_lang`/`mixed`/`content_hash` on `:Passage`** (Neo4j-only — DD1: passages have NO Postgres mirror) — model + Cypher ON CREATE/MATCH + `upsert_passage` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)). New helpers `set_source_lang_for_source` (tag-only backfill) + `get_source_ingest_state` (skip-gate read: hash+canon+chapter_index).
> - **Ingester** ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)): `resolve_source_lang` (declared chapter `original_language` wins, else `detect_primary_language`, "mixed"→flag); **content-hash skip-gate** (skip embed+re-bill ONLY when text+canon+chapter_index all match — a draft→publish canon flip or reorder still re-ingests); **cost metering** via existing `record_spending` (DD2 — fixes the pre-existing embed-token leak; `pool` threaded through the publish handler + backfill); `backfill_source_lang` (tag-only, per-chapter declared lang). `backfill_published_passages` now passes declared `source_lang` + `pool`; both wired at PUT embedding-model ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py)).
> - **book-service** already returns `original_language` in `/internal/books/{id}/chapters` (server.go:2244) → no Go change; `BookClient.list_chapters` surfaces it verbatim.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge-service unit suite **3017 passed** (source_lang/cost-metering/skip-gate tests + regression tests for canon-flip, chapter reorder, model-change, regional-subtag). **LIVE-SMOKE deferred → folds into M2's mandatory cross-service smoke** (M2 dual-indexes vi passages through this same ingest path + asserts source_lang) — `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - **/review-impl (cold-start adversary) — 1 HIGH + 2 MED FIXED, 2 LOW accepted:** **HIGH** — the skip-gate ignored `embedding_model`; since the model-set path's `_delete_project_graph` deletes only graph labels (`Entity/Event/Fact/ExtractionSource`), NOT `:Passage`, a model change would have skip-gated re-embedding → stale-dimension vectors. Fixed: `get_source_ingest_state` returns `embedding_model` + the skip condition compares it (re-ingests on model change). **MED** — draft-index path (`raw_search.py`) didn't meter embed spend → now passes `pool` (guarded); + `source_lang` now normalized to ISO-639-1 primary subtag (`zh-CN`→`zh`) so M4 ranking won't drift. **Accepted+documented (LOW):** `get_source_ingest_state` LIMIT-1 is safe (one ingest writes identical hash/canon/index/model to all chunks); `record_spending` best-effort silent-fail is intentional.
>
 **✅ M2 EVENT + DUAL-INDEX SHIPPED (this session).** A chapter's ACTIVE translation is now dual-indexed as `source_lang`-tagged `:Passage` nodes — index-only (NEVER re-extracts Layer 1, R1).
> - **Producer (translation-service):** `translation.published` outbox event (`aggregate_type='translation'` → `loreweave:events:translation`) emitted at ALL 3 active-version chokepoints (DD5): manual `set_active_version`, human-block-edit `patch_translation_block`, and the auto-promote in `chapter_worker._finalize_chapter` (the one that emitted NOTHING before — only when the promote actually changed the active row). One envelope via [translation_events.py](../../services/translation-service/app/translation_events.py).
> - **vi-text retrieval:** new internal `GET /internal/translation/chapters/{chapter_id}/active-text?target_language=` ([main.py](../../services/translation-service/app/main.py)) returns the active version's flat text (translated_body, else assembled from translated_body_json) — the translated text lives in translation-service, NOT book-service.
> - **Consumer (knowledge-service):** new [translation_client.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/clients/translation_client.py) + `handle_translation_published` ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py)) resolves project by book_id → fetches vi text → `ingest_chapter_passages(text_override=vi, source_lang=vi, canon=True)`. Registered: stream `loreweave:events:translation` ([consumer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/consumer.py)) + dispatcher ([main.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/main.py)).
> - **Language-scoped passage identity (DD1):** `passage_canonical_id` now includes `source_lang` so vi/zh chunks of the SAME chapter (shared `source_id=chapter_id`, kept clean for hit→chapter mapping) are DISTINCT nodes; `delete_passages_for_source` + `get_source_ingest_state` gained a `source_lang` filter so re-ingesting vi never wipes zh (and vice-versa). vi uses `source_type='chapter'` → the existing retriever finds it (M4 adds language ranking). Empty/'unknown' lang keeps pre-M2 ids byte-identical.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3022 passed** (canonical-id distinctness, text_override bypass, handler dual-index/skip-no-text/skip-no-project); translation-service **137 relevant + 2 emit-envelope** tests pass, py_compile clean.
> - **✅ LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (2026-06-24, on the running stack) — clears `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.** Target: Dracula book `019ef35c-36c3-…` / chapter `019ef35c-3f4c-…` (active vi translation, knowledge project `019ef35e-d08f-…`, embed model `019e7f71-…`). **(1) Endpoint:** `active-text?target_language=vi` → 5939 chars assembled from `translated_body_json`. **(2) Event path:** inserted `translation.published` outbox row in `loreweave_translation` → worker-infra relay (`published_at` set) → `loreweave:events:translation` → `knowledge-extractor` group → handler → **6 vi `:Passage`** created (was 0; the 6 originals are `source_lang=null`). **(3) Data model (DD1):** vi passages carry `source_lang='vi'`, clean `source_id=chapter_id`, `embedding_1024` (dim=1024, V1), `canon=TRUE`, `content_hash` set. **(4) Idempotency:** re-emit (relayed + consumed, lag 0) → still **6** vi passages, no dupes/re-embed. **⚠ The smoke caught a real defect mock-VERIFY can't:** the deployed knowledge-service image was **stale** (cached Docker `COPY` layer) → running consumer subscribed to only 3 streams (no `translation`) → the event would've been silently dropped. Fixed via `--no-cache` rebuild + `--force-recreate`; confirmed the consumer now subscribes to all 4 streams. (Cost-metering `record_spending` is best-effort/non-blocking — lightly covered live; phantom-pricing tracked separately in `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING`.)
>
> **✅ M3 READER-LANGUAGE STORAGE SHIPPED (this session) — book-service only.** Per-(user,book) reader-language preference, server SSOT + cross-device (DD3).
> - **Table** `user_book_prefs(user_id, book_id, reader_language, updated_at, PK(user_id,book_id))` in book-service schema ([migrate.go](../../services/book-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go)) — no FK (matches `reading_progress`; existence gated at handler).
> - **Public endpoints** `GET/PUT /v1/books/{book_id}/reader-language` ([reader_language.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/reader_language.go)) — view-gated via `canViewOrPublic` (grant OR public book, so a reader of a shared/public novel keeps a pref; non-grantee private book → 404, no oracle). PUT validates a BCP-47-ish tag (`langTagRe`, ≤35 chars); empty/whitespace → clears (delete → null). NOT localStorage (CLAUDE.md). Registered NOT in the mutating-grant table (per-user data = view-tier write, like favorites); GET added to `grant_mapping_test` readRoutes.
> - **Internal resolver** `GET /internal/books/{book_id}/reader-language?user_id=` (token-gated, user_id param like `getBookAccess`) — the cross-service source for M4 retrieval + M7 consumers. The FALLBACK chain (reader-pref → detected query lang → source lang) lives at the CONSUMER (M4) where query+source langs are known — M3 only exposes the stored value.
> - **VERIFY:** book-service `go build`+`go vet` clean; `internal/api` green (ReaderLang nil-pool auth/tenancy/validation + grant-mapping). **✅ LIVE-SMOKE on real PG18** (`loreweave_book_m3smoke` throwaway): set→GET→**AC3 cross-request read**→internal-resolver→upsert→clear→null + **per-user isolation** (two users, independent prefs, no shared row) all PASS. Full book-service suite green — also fixed a **pre-existing** stale `config.TestLoadValidation` (didn't set the now-required `CONFIRM_TOKEN_SIGNING_SECRET`; one-line, same-service, fix-now).
>
> **✅ M4 LANGUAGE-AWARE RETRIEVAL SHIPPED (this session) — D5 soft-weighting (cross-service: book + knowledge).** The core user-facing win: a reader's language preference softly re-ranks hybrid search so they get content in their language first.
> - **Both legs carry `sourceLang`:** semantic from the passage prop ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py) `passage_to_hit`); lexical from the chapter's `original_language` — book-service `lexical-search` now selects `c.original_language` in all 3 SQLs + threads it through `buildLexicalHit` ([search.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/search.go)).
> - **Soft language ordering, [hybrid_fusion.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/hybrid_fusion.py) `apply_language_preference`** (revised by /review-impl — see below): a **stable matched-first partition** applied as the FINAL ordering pass (post-rerank, post-floor, pre-cap). Reader-language hits move to the front, preserving the upstream rerank/RRF order within the matched + unmatched groups; nothing is dropped (soft, robust to partial translation). `"mixed"` matches any pref. `normalize_lang` = ISO-639-1 primary subtag (mirrors M1 `resolve_source_lang`). Scale-independent (no weight to tune).
> - **🔎 /review-impl — 2 HIGH FIXED (both masked by the original smoke):** (1) the boost was **inert under rerank** — applied pre-rerank, but `pool_n ≥ len(fused)` always, so the cross-encoder re-sorts the whole list by relevance and discards it → moved the language step to the FINAL pass + made it scale-independent (the additive `w_lang`/`settings.lang_pref_weight` is removed). (2) the RRF fusion key `_hit_key` **omitted `sourceLang`**, so a chapter's vi + zh/en passages (same `chapterId`+`surface`+`chunkIndex`) collided and one language was dropped BEFORE ranking → added `sourceLang` to `_hit_key` (mirrors `passage_canonical_id`/DD1). Both have regression tests (rerank-on ordering; both-languages-survive-fusion). Re-smoked live (rerank default-on path): `language=en`→6 en then 6 vi (**n=12, both survive**); `language=vi` & reader-pref→6 vi then 6 en.
> - **Resolver chain** ([raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)): `?language=` → the CALLER's stored reader-language (M3, via new `BookClient.get_reader_language` → `/internal/books/{id}/reader-language`) → `detect_primary_language(query)` → None. It's the SEARCHER's pref (caller), not the resolve-to-owner project user. `run_hybrid_search` gained `pref_lang` (None default ⇒ wiki in-process path unchanged).
> - **VERIFY:** book-service `go build`+`go vet` clean, api tests green; knowledge **98 unit** pass (boost math/sort/mixed/regional, resolution chain, both legs carry sourceLang); provider-gate OK. **✅ LIVE-SMOKE (cross-service) on the Dracula stack:** reader-pref `vi` resolved via book-service internal endpoint → boost applied (`score=fused+0.0164`); with **identical-embedding en/vi clones** (language = sole variable) `language=en`→en ranks #0, `language=vi` & reader-pref→vi ranks #0 — the boost alone flips ranking, M3→M4 chain end-to-end. (Confirmed the M2 dual-index is bug-free: a clean re-index yields exactly 6 vi; an earlier "12 passages" was smoke-data pollution from consumer replay-from-0 + repeated manual emits, now reset.)
>
> **✅ M5 PART 1 SHIPPED (this session) — C5 predicate labels + C4 kind name_i18n.** M5 is larger than the plan's "size M" (4 features across glossary + knowledge); sliced by risk boundary.
> - **C5 predicate labels (DD7, knowledge-service):** [predicate_labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/predicate_labels.py) — `humanize(snake)` fallback + curated vi map for the ~24 common KG predicates (open vocab ⇒ unknown predicates degrade to humanize). Endpoint `GET /v1/knowledge/predicate-labels?language=&codes=` ([labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/labels.py)) — backend-served (R5), `codes` resolves a set else returns the catalog. 13 tests.
> - **C4 kind name_i18n (DD4, glossary-service):** migration `0037_kind_name_i18n` ([kind_name_i18n.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/kind_name_i18n.go)) — `name_i18n JSONB` on `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`/`book_kinds` (inherits each tier's scope, no new tenancy surface) + admin-seeds the 12 System kinds in vi (en `name` stays the en label). The internal ontology reads (`/internal/users/{id}/glossary-standards`, `/internal/books/{id}/ontology`) now surface `name_i18n` via `loadKindNameI18n` (JSONB→[]byte→unmarshal, graceful nil on un-migrated DB), tier-correct (a user kind shadows by code AND label). **Tenancy:** System is admin-seed-only (never a user write — the `entity_kinds` bug); per-user/book label authoring deferred (`D-KG-ML-PERUSER-LABELS`). Verified on real PG: `character`→"Nhân vật", `location`→"Địa điểm".
>
> **✅ M5 PART 2 SHIPPED (this session) — C9 entity-name vi read + C7 graph-view/timeline label resolution + the System→book label-inheritance fix.** AC1 now works: a vi reader's KG graph-view shows vi kind, entity-name + predicate labels.
> - **C9 (glossary-service):** new internal `POST /internal/books/{book_id}/entity-display-names` ([entity_display_names_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/entity_display_names_handler.go)) → `{entity_id, display_name, translated}[]`, resolving each entity's `name`/`term` attr to a language via the SAME `attribute_translations` join as the public `display_language` list (reuses the existing translate pipeline's idempotent population — no new write/migration). Internal-token gated, book-scoped, soft-absent for unknown ids. The entity-name vi **population** rides the existing batch-translate (`internalApplyTranslations`, idempotent on `(attr_value_id, language_code)`, never overwrites `verified`); C9 is the READ the KG joins on.
> - **C7 (knowledge-service):** `GraphNode` gains `kind_label`/`name_label`, `GraphEdge` gains `edge_type_label`, `EdgeTimeline`/`TimelineInstance` gain `edge_type_label`/`target_label_localized` (all additive, None ⇒ canonical source-fallback = AC1's "explicit fallback marker"). Pure decorators [graph_labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/graph_labels.py) + reader-lang param hygiene [reader_lang.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/reader_lang.py) (shared with M4). `read_graph` + `read_edge_timeline` ([graph_views.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/graph_views.py)) gained `?language=` → caller's reader-pref fallback; resolve kind→`name_i18n[lang]` (glossary ontology client, now carrying `name_i18n` + a `kind_labels(lang)` helper), predicate→`resolve_predicate_label` (C5), entity name→glossary C9 batch (TRANSLATED-only, so name_label=None is an honest fallback). Best-effort throughout (glossary outage → canonical graph, never 500). `read_graph` reuses the grant gate's already-resolved `(owner, book_id)` (project_meta_dep) — no extra query.
> - **🔑 Real gap caught by the live-smoke + fixed now (correctness):** for a BOOK-bound KG project, kind labels resolve from `get_book_ontology`→`book_kinds.name_i18n`, which is EMPTY (C4 seeded only System). Per the LOCKED tier-merge (System defaults → Per-book, higher shadows by code), a book kind must INHERIT the System vi label unless overridden. `internalBookOntology` (and the user-standards shadow path) now merge `system_kinds.name_i18n ⊕ tier.name_i18n` via a new `mergeLabels` ([internal_ontology_read.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/internal_ontology_read.go)). Without this, book-bound graph-views (the common case) showed NO kind labels.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3067 passed** (new: 11 graph_labels pure + 1 graph-view localization router + 3 glossary-client `fetch_entity_display_names` + 5 existing graph-view tests fixed for the new deps); glossary `go build`/`vet` clean + real-PG tests green (C9 4 tests, book-ontology System-inheritance, GlossStandards). provider-gate OK. **✅ AC1 LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (cross-service: knowledge→glossary→PG→Neo4j):** seeded a vi name translation + a 2-node `character`/`ALLY_OF` graph on book `019ed9d0…`; `GET /v1/kg/projects/{id}/graph?language=vi` → node `kind_label="Nhân vật"` (System label inherited by the book kind — proves the merge fix live), `name_label="A-ri-a"` (C9), edge `edge_type_label="đồng minh của"` (C5); untranslated node → `name_label=null`; **no-language → fully canonical (back-compat)**. Smoke artifacts cleaned up.
>
> **✅ M6 SHIPPED (this session) — CJK lexical leg via a Neo4j `cjk` full-text index (D12 / V6).** A short Chinese/Japanese/Korean keyword (the pg_trgm-FAIL case) now recalls the right passage.
> - **V6 probe (decisive):** `db.index.fulltext.listAvailableAnalyzers()` → Neo4j ships a built-in **`cjk`** analyzer (bi-grams); Postgres `pg_available_extensions` → only `pg_trgm` (NO `zhparser`/`pg_jieba` → those would need a custom image = infra change). **Decision (per the plan's "pick by probe + infra cost"): Neo4j `cjk`** — zero infra change, and `:Passage` already carries `source_lang` (M1/M2). The book-service Postgres `language`-param/`pg_jieba` route from the plan is therefore moot; book-service trigram stays as the script-agnostic fallback (its `ILIKE` substring still finds exact CJK, just unranked/unindexed).
> - **Schema:** `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX passage_text_cjk_ft … OPTIONS {indexConfig:{fulltext.analyzer:'cjk'}}` over `:Passage(text)` ([neo4j_schema.cypher](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_schema.cypher), idempotent, applied on startup).
> - **Repo:** `find_passages_by_fulltext` + `lucene_escape` + `PASSAGE_CJK_FT_INDEX` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)) — global FT index → oversample + post-filter on `user_id`/`project_id`/`source_lang`/`canon` (mirrors `find_passages_by_vector`); raw query Lucene-escaped (CJK runs kept) so a stray `+`/`:` can't break the parse; blank → no call.
> - **Retriever:** new `query_has_cjk` (codepoint test — a bare proper noun is too short for language detection) + a `_cjk_lexical` leg ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py)) fused as a THIRD RRF input, gated on `mode!=semantic AND query_has_cjk`. **Additive — empty for Latin queries, so non-CJK retrieval is byte-identical to pre-M6.** Best-effort (FT index missing / query error → `degraded['cjk_lexical']`, never 500). Needs no embedding model (works on an un-embedded project).
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3076 passed** (new: `query_has_cjk`, `lucene_escape`, `find_passages_by_fulltext` wiring + blank-skip, retriever CJK-leg-fuses + Latin-skips; an autouse stub makes the pre-existing CJK-query retriever tests see the leg as additive-empty). **✅ AC LIVE-SMOKE PASSED:** seeded a zh passage (`柳无尘走上天剑峰，见到了姜子牙。`) under the real book owner; `search?query=天剑&mode=lexical` (**2-char** — the exact pg_trgm-FAIL case) → returns the block (cjk leg, relevance 2.53); `query=姜子牙` (3-char) → relevance 5.05; `degraded={}`. Smoke artifacts cleaned up. The cjk index was confirmed created on container startup.
> - **Note (cosmetic):** `passage_to_hit` labels every passage hit `matchType:"semantic"` regardless of which passage leg (vector vs cjk-FT) found it — a passage-node-vs-chapter-node distinction, not a correctness issue. Tracked as `D-KG-ML-M6-MATCHTYPE` if it ever matters for UI.
>
> **✅ M7 SHIPPED (this session) — consumer wiring + headline E2E. THE EPIC IS COMPLETE.** Reader-language now threads end-to-end so a vi reader gets vi-first grounding + an honest coverage note.
> - **knowledge (C12):** `drawers/search` + raw-search take `?language=` → soft matched-first partition (not a filter — off-language hits still surface) + a `coverage` note ({reader_lang,total,in_language,partial,note}) via new `hybrid_fusion.language_coverage`; each drawer hit carries `source_lang` ([drawers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/drawers.py), [raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)).
> - **composition (C6):** `pack()` resolves the author's reader-language ONCE (`BookClient.get_reader_language` → book-service M3 internal) + threads `language` into the glossary `select_for_context` (per-language aliases) + knowledge `search_drawers` (vi-first lore) lenses, base + delta ([pack.py](../../services/composition-service/app/packer/pack.py), [lenses.py](../../services/composition-service/app/packer/lenses.py)). Best-effort (None → source-language).
> - **chat:** already wired (S6 `display_language` → `build_context`); the FE sends the reader language.
> - **FE (C12):** drawers search sends the UI language; `RawDrawersTab` shows the coverage banner; `DrawerResultCard` badges each hit's `source_lang`; +i18n (en/vi/ja/zh-TW).
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3079** + composition pack/client + FE **20** green; tsc clean; provider-gate OK. **✅ HEADLINE E2E LIVE (≥3 services):** (1) drawers `?language=vi` on identical-embedding vi/zh clones → **vi first** (cosine tied 0.4888 ⇒ ordering is purely the language partition) + `coverage {total:2,in_language:1,partial:true}`; (2) book-service internal reader-language → `vi`; (3) **composition grounding** `works/{p}/scenes/{n}/grounding` (composition pack → book reader-lang=vi → knowledge drawers/glossary `language=vi` → provider-registry embed) → `grounding_available=true, token_count=4589, no warnings`. **✅ FE browser smoke (Playwright):** on the built bundle the drawers Raw tab renders the **coverage banner** + a per-hit **language badge** on every hit (`vi×7, zh×1`), driven by the live API. **GOTCHA:** a pre-existing **host vite-dev server** (a node process from a different/stale working copy) shadows :5174 ahead of the docker nginx container — so the built FE was smoked via a temp container on the compose network (`docker run --network infra_default -p 5199`). Also fixed a pre-existing **infra drift**: recreating the frontend cascaded an `api-gateway-bff` restart that crash-looped on a missing `ROLEPLAY_SERVICE_URL` (the BFF `requireEnv`'s it; compose never set it) → added it to the BFF env in [docker-compose.yml](../../infra/docker-compose.yml).
>
> **🎉 EPIC DONE — the driving scenario works:** a vi reader who can't read the zh/en source gets KG content, kind/predicate/entity labels, lexical recall, retrieval ranking, cowrite grounding, and coverage signalling in Vietnamese. **Branch `feat/kg-multilingual` is ready to PR into main.** Remaining tracked rows are all out-of-scope deferrals (see below). **Suggested next:** open the PR, or pick up `D-KG-ML-M4-EVAL` (formal multilingual eval golden-set) when a corpus exists.
>
> **▶ Deferred — debt-minimisation pass (2026-06-24): the list was triaged + shrunk; only 3 genuine rows remain, each clearing the defer-gate.**
> - `D-KG-ML-M4-EVAL` — **BLOCKED (gate 4):** formal multilingual eval golden-set needs a curated corpus + judge ensemble (large/structural). The matched-first partition is parameter-free (no weight to sweep); the mechanism is proven by unit tests + the live reordering smoke. Target: when a multilingual eval corpus exists.
> - `D-KG-ML-WIKI` — **OUT OF SCOPE (gate 1):** per-language wiki article BODIES is a separate feature (translate long-form wiki text), not part of the KG localization layer.
> - `D-KG-ML-PERUSER-LABELS` — **OUT OF SCOPE (gate 1/5):** per-user kind-label AUTHORING UI/API. System+Book tiers cover the scenario today (a user overrides a label via a book kind; C4 merge gives System defaults). Build only if users ask to author their own per-user kind labels.
> - `D-KG-ML-DRAWERS-METER` — **PRE-EXISTING (not this branch) + product decision (gate 4/5):** whether read-path search embeds should bill the user's BYOK budget. `drawers/search` computes `embedding_cost_usd` but doesn't `record_spending`; this predates kg-multilingual. Deferred pending a product call.
>
> **✅ Recently cleared (this debt pass):**
> - ✅ `D-KG-ML-M6-MATCHTYPE` — **FIXED:** `passage_to_hit` gained a `match_type` param; the cjk full-text leg now labels its hits `"lexical"` (was the hardcoded `"semantic"`). +retriever test.
> - ✅ `D-KG-ML-MULTI-TRANSLATION-LANG` — **CLEARED:** design proven — `test_canonical_id_distinct_per_language` now asserts zh+vi+en translations of ONE chapter are 4 distinct ids (incl. back-compat). (A live smoke with a real 2nd target language is naturally blocked until a book has one; the coexistence design is proven.)
> - ✅ `D-KG-ML-PERLANG-EMBED` — **WON'T-FIX:** the V1 probe PASSED (bge-m3 is genuinely multilingual → single-vector-space premise holds), so a per-language embedding model is unnecessary. Reopen only if a future target language shows weak recall.
> - ✅ `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING` — **VERIFIED MITIGATED:** the phantom-cost concern was "`cost_per_token` gets a model UUID → fallback rate", but BOTH the ingest ([passage_ingester.py:432](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)) and drawers ([drawers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/drawers.py)) pass `embed_result.model` (the RESOLVED provider name); local models map to $0 via the `bge-`/`ollama/`/`lm_studio/` prefixes in [pricing.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/pricing.py). Residual edge (a provider reporting a non-name model string) folds into pricing.py's existing "swap for a dynamic pricing table" note — not this branch's debt.
> - ✅ `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + ✅ `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE` (live-smoke PASSED 2026-06-24).
