# ▶▶ (prior) roleplay-service v1 COMPLETE (R0→R3; cutover done) · branch `feat/roleplay-service` · HEAD `<pending>` · 2026-06-24

> **Building the lean Rust `roleplay-service` (the MMO roleplay-service seed) — R0→R3 continuous XL run.** Spec [2026-06-24-roleplay-scripted-acting.md](../specs/2026-06-24-roleplay-scripted-acting.md), detailed design [2026-06-24-roleplay-service-detailed-design.md](../specs/2026-06-24-roleplay-service-detailed-design.md), plan [2026-06-24-roleplay-service.md](../plans/2026-06-24-roleplay-service.md). LOCKED: Rust now; lean (System+Per-user tiers only; book-tier/reality-db/executive/ES deferred & seam-ready); reuse chat-service for turn loop+voice+debrief (roleplay makes NO LLM calls in v1).
> - **✅ R0 — infra gate (commit `aa3f7c98`).** Shared kit `crates/service-http` (serve/health DB-aware/ProblemDetails+`message`/`require_user` JWT HS256→UserId + `require_internal`/JSON trace+X-Trace-Id/prometheus/config/db::init). roleplay-service scaffold + gateway `/v1/roleplay` proxy + `language-rule.yaml`→rust + `loreweave_roleplay` DB + compose **port 8225** (8221 taken by lore-enrichment). Also fixed pre-existing `jobs-service: python` lint drift. Live: `/v1/roleplay/livez` 200 through gateway.
> - **✅ R1 — scripts + start-orchestration (commit `<pending>`).** `migrations/0001_init.sql`: `roleplay_scripts` + `rp_sessions` + `rp_memory`; partial-unique `uq_rp_system_code` + `uq_rp_user_code` (NULLS NOT DISTINCT); tier CHECK (system/user; book forward-schema); 3 System presets seeded (genre=`interview`, mirrored from chat M7, idempotent). `models.rs` (Script + DTOs; scenario/rubric as Value, round-trip). `charter.rs` (freeze scenario→charter: goal||premise, checklist||beats, phases default; emits the exact chat `WorkingMemory` seed shape `{version,charter,state}` + rubric sidecar). `handlers/scripts.rs` (CRUD: list System∪own DISTINCT-ON-code own-shadows-System, get/create/patch/delete; writes `WHERE owner_user_id=uid` so System/other→404; create forces tier='user'; identity from `Extension<UserId>`). `handlers/start.rs` (load visible script→resolve model (override>default, else 400)→freeze charter→reqwest chat `POST /internal/chat/sessions` X-Internal-Token (chat-first EC-3)→insert rp_sessions+rp_memory in one tx). chat-service `internal.py` += `POST /internal/chat/sessions` (`InternalCreateSession`; the extracted `/start` INSERT). OpenAPI `contracts/api/roleplay-service/v1/openapi.yaml`.
> - **VERIFY:** 18 rust tests GREEN (11 kit incl. expired/bad-sig/non-uuid/**no-exp** JWT rejects; 3 roleplay charter+http; integration on live PG: tenancy denies + start round-trip + `rp_memory` read-back); clippy `-D warnings` clean; ai-provider-gate OK (no LLM); language-rule PASS. **`/review-impl` DONE (gate)** — tenant isolation verified (cross-user/System write→404 live-tested), internal endpoint **not gateway-reachable** (404) + token-gated, SQL all-bound. **1 MED fixed**: kit `require_user` now requires `exp` (a no-exp forged token would never expire) + locked by test. **CROSS-SERVICE LIVE-SMOKE:** through the gateway, GET scripts (3 presets) + POST `/start`→201→ real `chat_sessions` row in `loreweave_chat` w/ frozen seed (goal+4 phases+rubric) + `rp_memory`/`rp_sessions` owner=JWT account.
> - **✅ R2 — FE re-point + reused acting (commit `<pending>`).** `frontend/src/features/interview/`→`features/roleplay/` (git-moved file-by-file — a vite watcher locks the dir rename). `api.ts`→`roleplayApi` (`GET /v1/roleplay/scripts` bare array; `POST /scripts/{id}/start`→`{session_id}` then `chatApi.getSession` bridges to the full ChatSession). types `SessionTemplate`→`Script`/`script_id`. `useRoleplaySetup`, `RoleplayPage`, PersonaPicker use scripts/`script_id`. **acting stays `/v1/chat`** (reused ChatView, seed-anchored); **debrief stays chat M6 `/evaluate`**. Nav→**Roleplay** (en `Roleplay`/vi `Nhập vai`); route `/roleplay` + `/interview`→`/roleplay` redirect. **Also hardened chat-service M6 `/evaluate` parser** (`parse_json_object` now uses `raw_decode` — small local models append text after the JSON → was 502 "Extra data"; fix-now, load-bearing for the smoke + improves the reused debrief for all small models; +unit test).
> - **VERIFY:** FE tsc clean; eslint clean (touched files); roleplay PersonaPicker 5/5; full FE suite **2168 pass** (18 fail = **pre-existing `world.json` i18n parity** drift, a different feature, NOT in my diff). chat parser fix validated standalone (raw_decode) + unit test. **R2 GATE — M7 browser smoke GREEN end-to-end on `/v1/roleplay`** (Playwright, real stack + lm_studio Qwen2.5-7B): login → Roleplay nav → 3 presets listed (from `/v1/roleplay/scripts`) → pick FAANG SWE + model → Start → `POST /v1/roleplay/scripts/{id}/start`→201 → `getSession`→ChatView → sent a turn → **AI replied in-character** (posed a coding problem; seed/charter anchored) → End & evaluate → `/evaluate`→201 → **scorecard rendered with the 4 faang_swe charter checkpoints + STAR**. The Rust path matches the proven M7 UX.
> - **✅ R3 — cutover (commit `<pending>`; human POST-REVIEW approved "retire + delete templates.py").** Retired chat-service `/v1/chat/templates*`: unregistered the router + **deleted** `app/routers/templates.py` + `tests/test_templates_router.py` (no consumer remained — FE fully on `/v1/roleplay`; only composition uses its own `/v1/composition/templates`). KEPT: `/evaluate` (M6 debrief), `POST /internal/chat/sessions` (roleplay's start target), the turn loop + M3 anchoring. `session_templates` table + its seed left in place (harmless, test data). chat-service rebuilt+recreated. **Post-cutover live verify:** `/v1/chat/templates`→404; `/v1/chat/sessions`→200; `/v1/roleplay/scripts`→3 presets; `/start`→201 (full orchestration intact).

All gates passed: `/review-impl` (R1), cross-service live-smokes (R1 API + R2 browser end-to-end), human POST-REVIEW (R3). **▶ NEXT: PR `feat/roleplay-service` → main** (or continue the deferred tracks below).

Fix: `docker compose up -d --force-recreate --no-deps api-gateway-bff`. If `/v1/roleplay` 502s after a stack churn, check `docker exec infra-api-gateway-bff-1 sh -c 'echo $ROLEPLAY_SERVICE_URL'` and force-recreate.
> - **✅ `D-RP-START-RECONCILE` CLEARED** — the post-chat-create local write is now best-effort + idempotent (`persist_session` with `ON CONFLICT (session_id) DO NOTHING`): a failure logs a structured recoverable error and still returns the usable session (was: 500 → user retries → DUPLICATE chat session, orphaning the first). The idempotent insert IS the backfill primitive (safe to re-invoke); a re-start is a no-op (integration-tested: one rp_sessions/rp_memory row). When the executive (v2) lands it can backfill-on-read from the chat seed if `rp_memory` is absent — no separate reconcile worker needed.

World.json i18n parity drift is pre-existing, out of scope. `/review-impl` LOWs accepted (start.rs upstream-path tests, model_ref ownership at /start, float time_budget→null) — documented in the R3 review, not carried as rows.

# ▶ PRIOR — **interview-roleplay POC COMPLETE (M1→M7)** · branch `feat/interview-roleplay` · HEAD `112c907d` · 2026-06-24

> **Anti-drift interview-practice roleplay — the POC for the future roleplay-service — is DONE end-to-end (M1–M7).** Spec [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../specs/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md), plan [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../plans/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md). Brain-analogy anti-drift: `working_memory` = pinned **charter** (frozen goal, written only by the goal authority) + **state** (executive-rewritten, safe-when-wrong); `anchoring` = pin (primacy) + tail (recency); `executive` = out-of-band state updater. Goal-authority seam = the world-model swap point for roleplay later.
> - **✅ M1–M5** (prior commits 7335248e…a65ae5d8): contracts + `session_templates` (tenancy: System owner NULL / Per-user `UNIQUE(owner_user_id,code)`) + `working_memory_seed`; templates CRUD + frozen-charter seed; anchoring text+voice (shared helper, EC-3/EC-7); `working_memory` block as SSOT in knowledge-service; the `executive` worker (state-only, `covered` monotonic, charter immutable by construction — no `update_charter` exists). M5 **behaviorally proven** with real local Qwen2.5 7B.
> - **✅ M6 — `/evaluate` scorecard (commit `53b832bd`).** `POST /v1/chat/sessions/{id}/evaluate` — non-agentic pipeline scoring transcript + final `working_memory` + template rubric → structured `Scorecard` (STAR/clarity/filler/per-checklist/tips), stored as a `scorecard` ChatOutput. Coercion safe-when-wrong: per-checklist verdict rebuilt from `charter.checklist` (model can't drop/invent), scores clamped, `partial` server-set (EC-13). EC-10 no-model → 409. Portable (no `response_format` — lm_studio quirk). 23 tests; chat-service suite 442 green.
> - **✅ M7 — seed + frontend (commit `<pending>`).** 3 System templates seeded via the chat-service migration (idempotent `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on the partial unique index; admin/platform path — users clone, never mutate System). FE `features/interview/` (React-MVC): `useInterviewSetup`/`useEvaluation` hooks (controllers), `PersonaPicker`/`EndEvaluateBar`/`ScorecardView` views (render-only, props-driven), `InterviewPage` **reuses** the chat `ChatView` (text + voice, **no fork**; embedded mode, never unmounted) + scorecard overlay. Route `/interview` + sidebar entry (en/vi nav). 9 FE tests; typecheck/lint clean.
> - **✅ LIVE-SMOKE (cross-service, real Qwen2.5 7B):** templates list via API → start practice → real transcript → `POST /evaluate` → **201 in 7.7s**, scorecard checklist rebuilt to exactly the 4 charter items (model marked 2 covered / 2 not, matching the transcript), `partial=True`, persisted as a `scorecard` ChatOutput. M6 now proven-live, same bar as M5. (`scratchpad/m6_evaluate_smoke.py`.)
> - **▶ NEXT:** **POST-REVIEW #3 (human checkpoint) — present + WAIT.** Then optional **browser smoke** (test account: `/interview` → pick persona → answer a couple turns → End & evaluate → scorecard). After approval: merge `feat/interview-roleplay` → main (PR). The chat-service image was rebuilt+recreated this session with M6+M7 code (live-verified in container).
> - **Deferred (tracked):** **`D-PROVIDER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT`** (architecture C — gate: out-of-scope, cross-service / new feature) — provider-registry should NORMALIZE structured-output (canonical intent → per-provider dialect; prefer `json_schema`, accepted by BOTH OpenAI and lm_studio) instead of passthrough-ing raw `response_format` (which 400s `json_object` on lm_studio). Until then, LLM-JSON callers omit `response_format` + parse robustly (executive + evaluate both do). Target: a dedicated provider-registry branch. See memory `provider-structured-output-quirk`.
> - **Branch state:** test account `019d5e3c-…` (claude-test@loreweave.dev / Claude@Test2026) drives real local LLM smokes (Qwen2.5 7B `019eb620-…`). Stack up: chat-service :8212 + knowledge-service :8216 healthy (current code in containers); lm_studio :1234.

> 1. **✅ M1 `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`** — ai-gateway federation now forwards `X-Project-Id`. Added `projectId` to `Envelope`, read in `extractEnvelope`/`extractAdminEnvelope`, forwarded in `executeTool`/`adminHeaders` ([federation.service.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/federation/federation.service.ts), [handlers.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/mcp/handlers.ts), [admin-handlers.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/mcp/admin-handlers.ts), [admin-federation.service.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/federation/admin-federation.service.ts)). **Live: `kg_build_graph` + `kg_build_wiki` now MINT through the gateway (`no_project_in_scope=False`) — the exact calls that failed pre-fix.** Tests: handlers.spec + admin-federation.spec (27 green); TSC clean.
> 2. **✅ M2 `D-WIKI-ENTITY-FREQ-GATE`** (renamed — CORRECTS the prior "draft-vs-active" framing) — `kg_build_wiki` found 0 entities on a 1-chapter book because `_resolve_entity_ids` called `known-entities` at the **default `min_frequency=2`**; the Go handler IGNORES `status` and gates on `COUNT(chapter_links) >= min_frequency`, so a single-chapter book (every entity freq 1) returned 0. Fix: `list_entities` gains a `min_frequency` param; the wiki resolver passes `min_frequency=1` ([glossary_client.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/clients/glossary_client.py), [build_wiki_effect.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/ontology/build_wiki_effect.py)). **Live DECISIVE proof (root cause settled): on the fresh book — all 26 entities `status='draft'` — `known-entities?status=active&min_frequency=1` → 26, `&min_frequency=2` → 0. Status is ignored; the gate was purely chapter-frequency, NOT draft-vs-active.** Tests: knowledge unit 31 green.
> 3. **✅ M3 `D-ENRICH-MCP-OWNER-GATE`** — wired a `GrantClient` shim into lore-enrichment (shared `loreweave_grants` SDK) + lifespan init/close + a **VIEW** grant gate in `auto_enrich` BEFORE the glossary read; fail-closed (book-service outage → NONE → 404, never an existence oracle). The MCP tool passes `grants=get_grant_client()` EXPLICITLY (it calls the handler as a plain fn — `Depends` defaults don't resolve) ([grant_client.py](../../services/lore-enrichment-service/app/clients/grant_client.py), [gaps.py](../../services/lore-enrichment-service/app/api/gaps.py), [mcp/server.py](../../services/lore-enrichment-service/app/mcp/server.py)). **Live: through the gateway, a foreign book_id → `{success:false, status:404}`; the real owner resolves `grant_level=owner` (gate allows, no false-deny).** Tests: gaps_api + mcp_server 21 green.
> 4. **✅ M4 `D-COMPOSE-GENERATE-WORKER-POLL`** — added Tier-R `composition_get_generation_job` (mirrors `composition_get_prose`; user-scoped repo `.get` + a project-ownership assert so a foreign-project job_id can't be read through this Work) ([mcp/server.py](../../services/composition-service/app/mcp/server.py)). **Live: federated in the gateway catalogue (129 tools).** Tests: composition unit 43 green (owner-ok / foreign-project / missing).
> - **VERIFY (live, 4 rebuilt images, through the gateway):** combined smoke [scripts/smoke_journey_gaps_batch.py](../../scripts/smoke_journey_gaps_batch.py) → RESULT: PASS (M1 both kg tools mint, M3 foreign-book 404, M4 poll-tool federated) + the direct M2 freq-gate probe + the M3 owner/foreign `/access` probe. provider-gate + language-rule clean; ai-gateway TSC clean; lore-enrichment 979 tests collect (no import cycle).

The KG/wiki direct-on-knowledge workaround is retired — M1+M2 carry it through federation.
> - **✅ KG-benchmark M4+M5 CLEARED 2026-06-22 (commit `e66a7308`):** surfaced `gate_failures` on `GET /benchmark-status` (from `raw_report`); `BenchmarkBadge` reason-aware copy (`insufficient_runs`→"inconclusive, needs ≥3 passes"; never "low-quality" at good recall); `BuildGraphDialog` leading prereq checklist (LLM→embedding→benchmark ✓/✗/⋯); `not_benchmark_project` toast de-obsoleted (sandbox) + model-swap warning; new `contracts/api/knowledge-service/benchmark.yaml` (benchmark-run/status + gate_failures + the `kg_run_benchmark` tool). 13 knowledge + 56 FE tests; tsc + spectral clean. (FE not browser-smoked — well unit-covered; backend field unit-tested.)
> - **✅ `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-COST-UNITS` CLEARED 2026-06-22 (commit `7969a08b`):** the enrichment cost columns/fields were named `*_usd` but always held TOKENS (C1 ruling). Renamed end-to-end: DB `enrichment_job.*_cost_usd`/`max_spend_usd`→`*_tokens` (guarded in-place RENAME + retype NUMERIC(14,2) — a token count overflows the old (10,4); resume reads new key w/ legacy fallback); API request `max_spend_usd`→`max_spend_tokens` (jobs/auto-enrich/compose/mcp), response already unit-neutral (fixed the drifted OpenAPI); FE sends `max_spend_tokens`, JobsPanel shows `tok` not `$`, i18n "(USD)"→"(tokens)" ×4 locales. Also fixed a latent break (test_auto_enrich_api wasn't grant-stubbed after the M3 owner-gate). 934 unit + 280 FE tests; **LIVE: migration applied on the real DB (renamed+retyped, healthy); GET /jobs → 200 with real token costs (13994/11231/36)**.
> - **✅ `D-KG-PASSAGE-BACKFILL` CLEARED 2026-06-23 (passage-creation gap, root-caused live):** running the full Dracula journey surfaced that **wiki produced 0 articles** (and enrichment 0 proposals) — `skipped_no_grounding`, because the KG project had **0 `:Passage` nodes**. Root cause: passages are ingested by the `chapter.published` **event handler**, which SKIPS when no project/embedding exists yet ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py) lines 144/194); the **natural flow publishes chapters BEFORE the KG project + embedding model exist**, so the publish event fires too early and **nothing backfills** (`backfill-orders` only re-indexes existing passages). Proven via `index-drafts` (on-demand ingest → 8 passages where the journey made 0). **Fix:** `backfill_published_passages` ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)) ingests passages for all PUBLISHED chapters as canon, wired best-effort into `PUT /embedding-model` ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py) — the moment passages become ingestable; idempotent; a model CHANGE re-ingests at the new dim). 24 ingester + 35 embedding-model + 76 passage/event/raw-search tests; provider-gate clean. **LIVE: natural flow (publish→project→embedding) now backfills 6 canon `:Passage` nodes** where it made 0 before (`verify_passage_backfill.py`). NOTE surfaced: `book_chapter_publish` is propose→confirm — the journey drivers never confirmed it, so chapters stayed `draft` (extraction reads drafts; wiki needs published+grounded).
> - **✅ END-TO-END PAYOFF RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-23 (with a CONFIRMED publish):** fresh book `019ef35c` / project `019ef35e` — natural flow (confirm-publish → create project → set embedding → backfill **6 passages** → KG build → wiki → enrich). **Wiki 0→20 articles · Enrichment 0→3 proposals (job completed, 30,131 `actual_cost_tokens`) · chapter written (2330 chars)** — the two grounding-dependent steps that were 0 now produce real output. Also caught + fixed a stale `lore-enrichment-worker` (pre-rename image → `column actual_cost_usd does not exist`); recreating it fixed enrichment. **Deploy note: after a schema rename, recreate worker containers too, not just the API service.**
> - **✅ MERGED `origin/main` 2026-06-23 (merge commit `b8070be6`) — branch now 0 behind main.** PR #42 (extraction-knowledge-architecture) reconciled most prior defers (`D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES`, `D-EXTRACTION-ADMISSION-CONTROL`, `D-CACHE-MODEL-KEY`, `D-KG-WORKER-GRADED-EFFORT`…). Conflict resolution: ledger.go renumbered this branch's migration `0035→0036` (collided with main's `0035_multirow`); 4 translation extraction files took main (superset: grant-clamped effort + planner-wiring); 2 hidden auto-merge artifacts fixed (duplicate `reasoning_effort` kwarg in `extraction_worker.py` sig + `actions.py` call); 2 HEAD tests aligned to main's API. VERIFY post-merge: py_compile all services+SDKs; Go glossary+book build; translation extraction 42 + knowledge 45 + lore-enrichment 39 green; provider-gate clean.
> - **▶ NEXT / BRANCH STATUS:** **all of this branch's deferrals are CLEARED.** The only tracked-open item is **`D-EXTRACTION-REHOME-KNOWLEDGE`** — XL, **pre-existing on main** (NOT this branch's debt), **blocked on `world-core-foundation`**, design ready — it carries forward as main's existing debt. A couple of live-smoke rows (`D-S5-LIVE-SMOKE` needs a BYOK web-search key) are infra gaps, not code defects. **Branch is ready to PR into main.**

Test acct `019d5e3c-…` (claude-test@loreweave.dev / Claude@Test2026). Models: gemma `51ea9fd7-…` + bge-m3 `019e7f71-…`.

# Session Handoff — `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` · **EXTRACTION RE-ARCH — FND+RE+PROV+OBS SHIPPED; MERGE/PLAN/CACHE remain** · (prior: design complete; `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` — 26/26, PR #39) · 2026-06-22

> **▶ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE — extraction re-arch epic COMPLETE. 🎉 All 7 lanes + every follow-on shipped: FND✅ RE✅ PROV✅ OBS✅ MERGE✅ PLAN✅ CACHE (slice1 cache-gate / 3a retention / 3b replay / 3c MinIO offload)✅ + `D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING` Part 1 (split-aware cost estimate)✅ + Part 2 (planner feasibility gate)✅. `D-SDK-DISTRIBUTION-SPLIT` RESOLVED (PR #41 → main; planner ships in the rebuilt runtime — live-verified in-container). Nothing extraction-epic remains — even `D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` is closed (the unplannable-path live E2E ran green on the real stack; both translation images rebuilt `--no-cache`). NEXT = a NEW track: either the KG-ontology consolidated live E2E (the ▶ block below) OR the architecture-audit Crit/High backlog (see `docs/analysis/`, memory `architecture-audit-2026-06-20`). Ask the user which.**
> - **✅ D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 2 — planner feasibility gate (commit `a37c0527`; DESIGN [2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md](../specs/2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md), Option B).** KEY REFRAME: `plan()` is a token-budget STRUCTURE planner (estimates, not text payloads) → it CAN'T replace the windower (which makes prose); its value is the `Unplannable` signal. So Part 2 wires it as a **pre-flight feasibility GATE** over the existing windowing, NOT a replacement: build a Unit per (window×batch), run `plan()`, and any unit it refuses (an oversized block that can't fit even alone) → record a new `unplannable` batch-outcome + **skip its LLM call** (no truncation, no tokens) → chapter derives `completed_with_errors` (INV-F15) so the block is VISIBLE. The unit id IS the `{window_idx}:{batch_idx}` coordinate ⇒ **zero remap** onto the cache-gate/OBS/provenance seams (my earlier "structural XL" fear was wrong — the gate touches none of them). Fires only when input won't fit with the minimum output reservation = exactly when the executor would truncate (no false-positive skip — a flagged window couldn't extract anyway). Best-effort: any planner failure → ungated (prior path). New OBS taxonomy `unplannable` (distinct from `truncated`). VERIFY: 83 extraction unit tests + **FULL LIVE E2E** (`D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` — see below); provider-gate clean.
> - **✅ D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE (unplannable path) — DONE live on the real stack.** Auth (test acct) → created a 348k-char single-block chapter in the Dracula book → `POST /v1/extraction/.../extract-glossary` → worker → **`extraction_batch_outcomes.status='unplannable'`** + chapter `completed_with_errors`, job done in <1s with **NO LLM call**. Clean before/after: the SAME job on the pre-gate image produced `llm_error` (the oversized window hit `LLMInvalidRequest` context-overflow — the exact S1–S5 failure); the gated worker produced `unplannable` (graceful skip). **GOTCHA caught (memory `docker-stale-copy-layer-false-smoke`):** the first rebuild served a STALE app COPY layer (old code, no gate) while the SDK layer was fresh — so my initial "in-container `plan()` snippet" smoke was a FALSE pass (it tested the SDK, not the worker's gate). Fixed with `build --no-cache` + `up --force-recreate`; re-verified the gate code is in the container before re-running. Both translation images are now rebuilt `--no-cache` (current code live). Test chapter cleaned up. The broader full-pipeline-WITH-entities E2E (needs a live LLM producing entities) remains nice-to-have, not blocking.
> - **✅ CACHE D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 1 — split-aware cost estimate (commit `30c7549d`).** `estimate_extraction_cost` ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py)) now models each (chapter × kind-batch) as a `chunk`-splittable planner Unit (`max_units_per_call=1` so the per-unit input sum == real per-call input — extraction injects window text PER batch call, never shared) and splits oversized chapters into windows, so the quote tracks the executor's real fan-out (+ surfaces `unplannable`/`model_fit_warning`). Extracted the worker's context resolver into shared [extraction_model.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_model.py); the HTTP route + MCP start-extraction tool resolve the REAL model context and thread it in (so the quote uses the SAME budget the executor will). ANY planner failure (ImportError or plan() error) degrades to the flat heuristic — a cost estimate must never 500 job creation. VERIFY: 4 cost + worker 11 + mcp 36 + extraction subset 32 green; provider-gate clean.
> - **✅ CACHE Slice 3a — retention (commit `36193284`, not pushed).** `purge_stale_raw_outputs` ([extraction_cache.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_cache.py)) keeps the latest K content-hash GENERATIONS per (owner, book, chapter) + purges older (cache growth bounded by edit-history depth, not edit count) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/retention` (the scheduler seam). Destructive-op safety: current gen is always rank-1 (never purged even at keep→floored-1); rows join on content_hash so a gen is never half-purged; DENSE_RANK over-keeps on ties; partition keyed by owner_user_id (INV-9). VERIFY (live PG): keep-K / scope / tenant-isolation test; 66 extraction tests green; provider-gate clean.
> - **✅ CACHE Slice 3b — replay (commit `0f097422`, not pushed).** New [extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py) `replay_chapter_from_cache` re-drives the glossary writeback from a chapter's CACHED parse at **$0 LLM** (recovery: failed writeback / cleared entities / re-materialize) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/replay`. Faithful-by-construction: proceeds only when CURRENT text still hashes to the cached `content_hash` AND the source job's profile still hashes to the cached `profile_hash` (else no_cache / profile_unavailable → fresh extraction) — so re-stamped evidence offsets + links + attribute-actions are all valid. Gating: grant (EDIT on book, asserted-user re-verified), tenancy (reads ONLY caller's own cache, writes caller-attributed — INV-9), confirm (confirm=False → dry-run PREVIEW no write; confirm=True → idempotent writeback w/ the worker's writeback_key). **/review-impl (self): 1 MED** (duplicated `_merge_window_entities` → import the worker's single source of truth — guards a future merge-key drift) **+ 1 LOW** (writeback failure → distinct `writeback_failed` status). VERIFY (live PG): 5 replay tests (preview-no-write / confirm-writes-merged / content-drift / profile-unavailable / tenant-isolation) + 3 cache + extraction+dispatch subset green; provider-gate clean.
> - **✅ FND M0 — `finish_reason` propagation (the blocker).** Verified the gateway already propagates it (streamer→aggregator→`result.finish_reason`, committed Apr; live-confirmed: real chat + extraction `llm_jobs` carry it in the column AND `result`). Wired the consumer: typed `Job.finish_reason` SDK accessor ([models.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_llm/models.py)) + the worker reads it per batch, records `batch_finish_reasons`, **warns on `length` truncation** (the invisible 26-scenario data-loss signal). Tests: SDK 2 + worker 2.
> - **✅ FND M1 — concurrency + two-ledger (the 4-HIGH "concurrency" finding).** Migration **0032_extraction_concurrency** ([extraction_concurrency.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/extraction_concurrency.go)): `normalized_name` (GENERATED off trigger-maintained `cached_name`) + `uq_entity_dedup` partial unique index + `uq_evidence_dedup` + `extraction_writeback_log`. [`extraction_handler.go`](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go) `bulkExtractEntities` rewritten: per-book `pg_advisory_xact_lock` + **whole-chapter transaction** + `writeback_key` idempotency (replay=no-op) + content-hash stored + **idempotent evidence** (`ON CONFLICT`) + transactional outbox (threaded `pgxRWQuerier` through 4 helpers; mcp_server + perlang test callers updated). Worker computes `content_hash`/`writeback_key` + a **source-drift precondition** (re-fetch+compare → skip stale) + passes the two-ledger fields ([extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py), [glossary_client.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_client.py)).

> - **◐ lane RE — chat increment DONE (commit pending push); MCP-param + effort-auth NEXT.**
>   - **✅ Resolver + the wiring no-op fix + `/no_think`.** SDK `reasoning_fields(directive)` ([reasoning.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_llm/reasoning.py), exported) turns a `ReasoningDirective` into provider wire fields (adaptive/non-reasoning → omit; effort → `reasoning_effort`+`chat_template_kwargs`), unifying translation's `thinking_llm_fields`. **The live no-op is FIXED:** chat `stream_response` now resolves reasoning (per-msg `thinking` + session default → `resolve_reasoning` against `infer_reasoning_control(creds.provider_kind, model_name)`) and **both** `_stream_via_gateway` AND `_stream_with_tools` forward the fields (`_apply_reasoning_kwargs`) — they never did before, so the chat thinking toggle was dead. `/no_think`·`/think`·`/effort=high` parsed + stripped server-side (`parse_inline_effort`, RE-3, highest precedence). Tests: SDK +3, chat +3 (helpers/parser); SDK reasoning 11 + chat stream 36 green; provider-gate clean.
>   - **✅ MCP `reasoning_effort` param + effort-auth grant ceiling (the HIGH finding).** `clamp_effort_to_grant` ([grant_deps.py](../../services/translation-service/app/grant_deps.py): View=none/Edit=medium/Manage·Owner=high). `translation_start_extraction` MCP tool gains a `reasoning_effort` param (+ `thinking_enabled` kept as the medium alias), **clamped to the caller's grant at mint**; the confirm effect ([actions.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/actions.py)) **re-clamps against a fresh grant read** so a Manage→Edit downgrade in the confirm-TTL can't replay a now-too-high paid effort (+ old-payload back-compat). Tests: clamp +2; mcp/extraction/confirm/grant 60 green; provider-gate clean. **/review-impl: 1 fixed** (old confirm token lacking `reasoning_effort` falls back to its `thinking_enabled` bool).
>   - **▶ RE follow-ups (tracked, defer-eligible):** ✅ **`D-RE-WORKER-GRADED-EFFORT` CLEARED 2026-06-22** — the extraction worker now honors graded `reasoning_effort` (low/medium/high), no longer collapsing to medium-or-none. Threaded through `CreateExtractionJobPayload` → `job_params` + the `extraction.job` publish msg → worker read+validate (out-of-vocab → bool fallback) → `_process_extraction_chapter` → `effort_band_for(thinking_enabled, reasoning_effort)` (cache band) + the gateway wire fields via the shared SDK `reasoning_fields(ReasoningDirective(...))` (replaces the local `thinking_llm_fields` medium-or-none in extraction; the bool stays the back-compat alias True⇒medium). Files: [extraction.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py) + [actions.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/actions.py) + [extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py). Tests: +3 (graded-high / explicit-none-disables-thinking / bool-fallback-medium assert the exact gateway `input`), 103 green (worker+cache+mcp+grant+extraction); provider-gate clean. **LIVE-SMOKE caught + fixed a real bug the mocks missed:** a first attempt persisted `reasoning_effort` into a non-existent `extraction_jobs` column (that column lives on `extraction_raw_outputs`/the CACHE ledger) → 500 on job create; reverted (the worker consumes effort from the publish msg + the cache already records it — no `extraction_jobs` column needed). Re-smoke: `reasoning_effort=low` flowed to gemma and turned thinking ON (`finish=length`, output budget burned on hidden reasoning) — proof the field reaches the model (extraction's DEFAULT path is thinking-OFF, unchanged). **Note:** any low/medium/high enables thinking → a weak local model can over-think extraction into truncation; default (none) stays clean — pairs with `D-RE-EFFORT-COST-ESTIMATE`. ✅ **`D-RE-EFFORT-COST-ESTIMATE` CLEARED 2026-06-22** — `estimate_extraction_cost` now scales the OUTPUT reservation by `reasoning_effort` (`_EFFORT_OUTPUT_MULTIPLIER` none/off=1.0, low=1.5, medium=2.5, high=4.0 — reasoning tokens count toward output), threaded from BOTH callers (the HTTP/confirm create path [extraction.py] + the MCP mint estimate [mcp/server.py]). So a high-effort quote no longer under-represents spend (the clamp ceiling was already enforced; this fixes the *estimate*). Input tokens unaffected; unknown effort degrades to 1.0. +1 unit test (monotonic none<low<high, omitted==none, garbage==baseline); 40 green (windowing+mcp); provider-gate clean. `D-RE-OTHER-AGENTIC-EFFORT` (deep-research + translation MCP tools should adopt the same param+clamp). **RE lane essentially complete** (chat no-op fix + resolver + /no_think + MCP param + effort-auth); these are polish.

The extraction evidence INSERT populates **chapter_index + chapter_title** from the entity's first chapter link (was: only chapter_id + text — the literal §P3 gap).

Worker stamps per-chapter after the drift precondition ([extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py)). Glossary persists DEFENSIVELY ([extraction_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go) `evidenceProvenanceFields`): closed-enum only, clamp offsets (≥0, start≤end), keep them only for exact/resolved, else degrade to 'unverified'. **T1 is closed by construction** — glossary never persists a raw model number; the search path can't fabricate a citation. **/review-impl: 1 MED fixed** — content-keyed evidence dedup left a stale offset when an edited chapter kept a byte-identical quote that moved; `ON CONFLICT … DO UPDATE` on the provenance columns only (latest-validated-wins; preserves unique-by-content + the in-tx poison guard; true replays still short-circuited upstream by `writeback_key`). > - **✅ lane OBS (M2, observability) — batch-outcome taxonomy + SSOT + reconciliation.** New [extraction_outcomes.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_outcomes.py): `classify_batch` → closed taxonomy `ok|empty_valid|truncated|validation_rejected|llm_error` (truncation wins over salvaged entities; a non-empty array with 0 survivors is `validation_rejected`, an empty array is `empty_valid` — the literal 26-scenario "0 entities reads as clean success" bug); `chapter_status_from_outcomes` (INV-F15: chapter `completed` only if EVERY batch clean, else `completed_with_errors`); `compute_event_id` (redelivery-stable, INV-O13); `reconcile_from_rows` (INV-O12 re-derivation). [extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py) `parse_and_validate_with_stats` exposes the raw-vs-validated counts (the empty/rejected discriminator; `parse_and_validate` kept as a back-compat wrapper). Migration: `extraction_batch_outcomes` SSOT table ([migrate.py](../../services/translation-service/app/migrate.py), UNIQUE(event_id)). Worker ([extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py)) records a per-batch outcome (incl. the previously-silent LLM-error `continue` paths), persists them best-effort (`_persist_batch_outcomes`, never fails the job), and DERIVES the chapter status from the taxonomy — and threaded `completed_with_errors` through the **resume done-set + convergence counters + job rollup** so a with-errors chapter isn't re-run and the job status reflects it. **/review-impl: 1 MED fixed** — the same-txn outbox PROJECTION used `aggregate_type='chapter'`, which routes onto the statistics stream (mis-delivery) AND has no consumer; dropped the emission (SSOT rows + reconcile fully deliver the visibility), tracked `D-OBS-BATCH-OUTCOME-PROJECTION`. VERIFY (live): outcomes/reconcile unit 12 + parse-stats 1 + **real-PG SSOT idempotency/reconcile** + worker 4 + provenance/truncation green; provider-gate clean. Verified no downstream reader breaks on the new chapter status (only the worker resume query reads it; the job-detail API passes it through raw, and job-level `completed_with_errors` already existed).
> - **✅ lane MERGE (M5, P4 merge policy) — COMPLETE (slice 1 + slice 2).** Plan [2026-06-22-extraction-merge-policy.md](../plans/2026-06-22-extraction-merge-policy.md). **Slice 2 (this session, defers cleared not carried):** the `append` action (atomic JSON-array dedup-merge under the writeback lock, idempotent by normalized value → skip-reason `unchanged`; `appendDedupMerge`/`parseListValue`); the authored **`merge_strategy` is the DEFAULT** (the profile overrides) via `loadAttrStrategyMap` + `strategyToAction` — an absent profile action now resolves to the attribute's authored strategy (`fill_if_empty`→fill safe default, `replace/overwrite`→overwrite, `append`→append, `manual`→skip 'manual'); **restore-revision now stamps restored source values `confidence='verified'`** (a human restore is a curation action the verified-clobber guard then protects). **Behavior note:** an absent-action attr now fill-if-empties (was silently skipped) — safe (fill never clobbers), surfaces more extracted data. Per-item append tombstones/provenance ride the pre-existing `D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES`. VERIFY (live PG): append unit 6 + 3 DB tests (append idempotent / strategy manual+append / restore→verified) + full glossary api 59.7s green; provider-gate clean. **Slice 1 (earlier):** Migration **0034_merge_policy** ([merge_policy.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/merge_policy.go)): `entity_attribute_values.confidence` (the per-SOURCE-value trust marker — only translations had one; DEFAULT 'machine') + `merge_strategy` on `system_attributes`/`user_attributes`/`book_attributes` (§2.5 safe default, consumed in Slice 2). **Verified-clobber guard (INV-8 / T2)** in [extraction_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go) `mergeExtractedEntity`: a human-`verified` source value is NEVER overwritten by a machine re-extraction — skipped with reason 'verified', checked at write time. **Producer:** the editor PATCH ([attribute_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/attribute_handler.go)) and apply-edit ([apply_edit_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/apply_edit_handler.go)) now stamp `confidence='verified'` on a human source-value write, so the guard protects real data. **Skip-reason taxonomy** (ends silent skips, F-append): `mergeExtractedEntity` returns `[]attrSkip{code,reason}` (`no_action`|`fill_occupied`|`verified`) surfaced as `attributes_skipped_reasons` (additive to the bare code list). VERIFY (live PG): 2 new DB tests (verified value not overwritten + skip reason 'verified'; fill_occupied + no_action) + full glossary api 63.8s + migrate (isolation) green; provider-gate clean. **/review-impl: no HIGH; 1 LOW tracked** (`D-MERGE-RESTORE-VERIFY` — restore-revision doesn't mark the restored value verified; correct fix needs the revision snapshot to carry `confidence`, structural).
> - **✅ lane PLAN (planner SDK) — two-phase split/pack.** New [planner.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_extraction/planner.py) in `loreweave_extraction` (reuses `ContextBudget`/`estimate_text_tokens`; exported from the package). `plan(PlanRequest)` is PURE + two-phase (architecture §3.1, §8.4): **Phase 1 normalize/split** — a unit whose est input/output exceeds the per-call budget is split along its `split_axis` (chunk→kind→attr); irreducible → `Unplannable` (surfaced to the cost-gate, never silently truncated — the S1–S5 fix). **Phase 2 pack** — fit in-budget units up to the budget AND `max_units_per_call`. `per_call_budget` scales the output reservation by `reasoning_effort` (`effort_output_multiplier`, composes the reasoning spec); each `LLMCall` carries stable `unit_ids` for 1:1 VALIDATE attribution (§8.4.6); **fan-out guard** (`model_fit_warning` when one unit explodes past `fan_out_warn_threshold`); **cost range** low/expected/high with INJECTED pricing (no hardcoded price/model). VERIFY: planner 11 + budget 35 green; provider-gate clean. **/review-impl: no HIGH/MED** (1 LOW — `expansion_ratio>1` can mark a tight sub-unit Unplannable rather than split once more; safe — surfaces, never truncates). **Consumer = CACHE** (the executor): the worker's `plan_kind_batches` is unchanged; PLAN is wired into the executor when CACHE lands (the plan's "CACHE needs PLAN").
> - **◐ lane CACHE (M6, raw-output cache) — Slice 1 DONE (the cache-gate / LLM-skip); planner-wiring + replay deferred.** Plan [2026-06-22-extraction-raw-cache.md](../plans/2026-06-22-extraction-raw-cache.md). Migration `extraction_raw_outputs` (the EXECUTE ledger, §8.1/§2.1) in [migrate.py](../../services/translation-service/app/migrate.py) — tenant-scoped UNIQUE incl. `profile_hash` (the /review-impl HIGH: a profile change must miss, never reuse the old profile's parse). Worker ([extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py)) cache-gate: on a hit reuse the cached parse + **spend 0 new tokens**, replay the stored outcome; on a miss call LLM → `put_batch` (pre-chapter-links; links re-attached per-job via `_accept`). `effort_band` participates in the key (different effort ≠ cache collision). Only CLEAN batches are cached ({ok, empty_valid}) — a truncated/validation_rejected batch is NOT cached so a re-run can recover (the /review-impl HIGH#2). VERIFY (live PG): cache round-trip/idempotency/tenant-scope/profile-miss + worker cache-hit (LLM skipped) + truncated-not-cached + full extraction suite green; provider-gate clean. **/review-impl (2 passes): 2 HIGH fixed** — (1) `profile_hash` in the cache key (a profile change must miss); (2) only-cache-clean-batches (a truncated/rejected batch must not become sticky). **1 MED tracked `D-CACHE-MODEL-KEY`** — the model is intentionally NOT in the key (§8.1 content-addressed), so a model switch reuses the prior parse; add model-to-key or a force-refresh if a deployment wants re-extract-on-model-upgrade.

> - **✅ D-KG-WORKER-GRADED-EFFORT — BUILT (M; spec [2026-06-22-kg-worker-graded-effort.md](../specs/2026-06-22-kg-worker-graded-effort.md)).** worker-ai now honors the stored effort. New SDK module [reasoning_wire.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_extraction/reasoning_wire.py) (`reasoning_wire_fields` — "none"/None → `{}` byte-identical, graded → `{reasoning_effort, chat_template_kwargs}`; differs from `loreweave_llm.reasoning_fields('none')` which emits an explicit disable). Threaded `reasoning_effort: str = "none"` through [pass2.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_extraction/pass2.py) `extract_pass2` → the public `extract_{entities,relations,events,facts}` + their `_extract_via_llm_client` → the 4 `build_*_submit_kwargs` (spread the wire fields into `input`). worker-ai [runner.py](../../services/worker-ai/app/runner.py): `JobRow.reasoning_effort` + `_get_running_jobs` SELECT (`j.reasoning_effort`, NULL→"none") + sync `_extract_and_persist` param threaded to `extract_pass2` (both chapter + chat_turn callers pass `job.reasoning_effort`) + **decoupled** `_start_decoupled_chunk` stashes it into `resume_state` so [decoupled_extract.py](../../services/worker-ai/app/decoupled_extract.py) `assemble_entity_submit`/`assemble_trio_submits` rebuild the submits with the SAME effort on resume. **D1 carve-out honored** — recovery + precision-filter stay force-thinking-off (cheap structural passes; drift-locked by a test). **D2** no re-clamp (trusted; clamped at mint). VERIFY: SDK 41 (new test_reasoning_effort + pass2) + worker-ai 288 (incl. decoupled resume_state round-trip + JobRow column + sync threading + D1 drift-lock + the **decoupled-dispatch stash lock** — calls the real `_start_decoupled_chunk` + asserts `resume_state.reasoning_effort`) + knowledge back-compat 92 green; provider-gate clean. **In-container proof:** rebuilt worker-ai `--no-cache` + recreated → the running image's `build_entity_submit_kwargs(reasoning_effort="high")` emits `reasoning_effort`+`chat_template_kwargs` (kills the SDK-distribution-split + stale-COPY-layer risks). **/review-impl (2 self passes): no HIGH/MED; 2 LOW fixed** (D1 carve-out drift-lock + the decoupled-dispatch stash lock). **Pass 2 verified the decoupled "harder half" sound by tracing the consumer**: `_persist_inflight` re-serializes the FULL rs (`json.dumps(rs)`) so `reasoning_effort` survives every entity→trio resume persist; `fold_entity_job`/`apply_entity_result` preserve it (`dict(rs)`). **▶ LIVE-SMOKE deferred → `D-KG-WORKER-GRADED-EFFORT-LIVE-SMOKE`** (full real-LLM `kg_build_graph` effort=high → worker-ai → gateway `llm_jobs.input` carries reasoning fields — needs a real reasoning-capable BYOK model + a linked book with chapters; the throwaway stack's only high-effort job uses placeholder model refs `test-llm`. The worker→gateway input passthrough is structurally unchanged + the identical wire shape was already live-proven for translation in Wave 1).

> - **◐ D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES — XL epic IN PROGRESS on this branch (user-directed). Slice 1 DONE.** Spec [2026-06-22-glossary-multirow-attr-values.md](../specs/2026-06-22-glossary-multirow-attr-values.md). **Slice 1 (additive core):** new shared [textnorm](../../services/glossary-service/internal/textnorm/textnorm.go) package (`Normalize`/`ParseList`/`IsList` — the SINGLE normalize impl, api delegates → guaranteed dedup parity with the backfill); migration **0035** [multirow_attr_values.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/multirow_attr_values.go) — additive child table `entity_attribute_value_items` (item_value/item_norm/sort_order/confidence/status/source_chapter_id, `UNIQUE(attr_value_id,item_norm)`) + Go backfill (materializes existing list values, idempotent ON CONFLICT); [attr_value_items.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/attr_value_items.go) (`rebuildItemsCache` = the SOLE writer of the `original_value` cache [INV-MR1], `appendListItems`, `ensureItemsMaterialized` lazy seed, `dedupNormalized`); the extraction **append** action rewired to the per-item model (each element a child row w/ provenance; `original_value` stays the write-synced cache so all ~15 readers are unchanged). **/review-impl (self): 1 INV-MR1 divergence fixed** — a first-touch no-op re-append of a legacy scalar seeded an item but skipped the cache rebuild (`original_value` stayed scalar while items existed); fix = `ensureItemsMaterialized` returns `seeded` + always rebuild when seeded||added (canonicalizes scalar→active-item array). VERIFY (live PG): full glossary api 71s + new TestBulkExtract_AppendCreatesItems / AppendScalarReappendCanonicalizes / TestMultirowBackfill / TestDedupNormalized + the append regression; migrate clean-DB green; build/vet/provider-gate clean. **✅ Slice 2 DONE — all writers routed through items (divergence closed).** New `syncListItems`/`syncListItemsByID` (route when value IsList OR the attr is already item-managed — catches a list overwritten with a scalar) + `replaceListItems` (clear+set the item set) + `resyncEntityListItems` (rebuild all of an entity's list items). Wired: extraction create/fill/overwrite (machine + chapter provenance), editor PATCH + apply-edit (verified), restore (verified, post-batch resync), merge-entity alias union (machine). `rebuildItemsCache` no longer touches `original_language` (the value writers own it — clobber fixed). **The slice-1 overwrite→append divergence is closed** (locked by TestBulkExtract_OverwriteThenAppendNoResurrection). **Scope note:** the verified-clobber guard stays **row-level** (a verified EAV still blocks the whole machine merge); the *per-item* guard (append machine items ALONGSIDE verified items) is a semantic change deferred with slice 3. VERIFY: full glossary api **66s green** + the new overwrite/scalar-canonicalize tests. **✅ Slice 3 DONE — per-item verify/tombstone endpoint (the product win).** `PATCH /v1/glossary/books/{book}/entities/{entity}/attributes/{attr_value_id}/items/{item_id}` ([attribute_item_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/attribute_item_handler.go)) — body `{status?:active|tombstoned, confidence?:machine|draft|verified}`; grant-gated (Edit) + entity-in-book + item-belongs-to-attr-value guard; flips ONE item then `rebuildItemsCache` (tombstone drops it from `original_value` → every reader excludes it for free) + bumps the entity version + emits the `glossary.entity_updated` event (glossary-sync→Neo4j + staleness consumer see it), all in-tx. VERIFY: full glossary api **65s green** + TestPatchAttributeValueItem_TombstoneAndVerify (tombstone-drops-from-cache / verify-flips-confidence / 404 ownership guard / 400 validation). **MULTIROW backend epic COMPLETE** (slices 1-3). **Follow-ups (not blocking):** the per-item verified-clobber guard (append machine items alongside verified — a merge-semantics change); a GET items endpoint + FE surface to SHOW per-item provenance/offer tombstone; an MCP tool. **✅ LIVE SMOKE DONE (real stack).** Rebuilt glossary-service `--no-cache` → boot ran migration 0035 on the live DB (**90 items / 43 attr-values backfilled**, ledger recorded `0035_multirow_attr_values`). Drove a real per-item tombstone via the deployed endpoint (test-account JWT, Dracula entity): unauth→401 (route gated), authed→200 → **cache dropped the item** (`["Jonathan Harker","The Driver","Passengers"]` → `[…,"The Driver"]`), item marked `tombstoned`, `glossary.entity_updated` event emitted (glossary-sync→Neo4j + staleness consumers). Un-tombstone (status=active) restored the cache (proved the reverse). Live Dracula data restored after. `D-MULTIROW-LIVE-SMOKE` cleared.

<details><summary>Design-complete handoff (FND now built — kept for context)</summary>

> **▶ extraction/LLM-pipeline re-architecture, BUILD via the parallel plan.** Branch `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` (off the glossary work). The 26-scenario test surfaced 5 architectural gaps; this session did the **full design**, now COMPLETE and build-ready:
> - **Design docs (committed):** [architecture rev 2](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-architecture.md) (§7–§11 = a 4-lens adversarial evaluation: concurrency/failure/scale/tenancy, with the scenario matrix + hardening contracts), [reasoning-effort](../specs/2026-06-21-reasoning-effort-control.md), [**detailed design**](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-detailed-design.md) (every open question RESOLVED — decisions table §1 + DDL §2 + interfaces §3 + 15 enforceable invariants §4), [**parallel plan**](../plans/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-plan.md).
> - **The 5 gaps → one missing layer:** an explicit `PLAN → EXECUTE+CACHE → VALIDATE → WRITEBACK → OBSERVE` spine. P1 observability (services exist, unwired), P2 unified planner (scattered across 5+ modules), P3 evidence provenance (columns exist, unpopulated), P4 merge/append policy (no append, silent skips), P5 raw cache (spec'd, was deferred).
> - **Key design verdicts to honor in BUILD:** the **two-ledger model** (raw-output cache ≠ writeback-coverage log — else a failed writeback permanently masks un-landed entities = data-loss amplifier); **concurrency contract** (glossary has NO `UNIQUE(book,kind,name)` today → concurrent jobs dup; need constraint + per-book advisory lock + transactional whole-chapter writeback + idempotency key + content-hash precondition); **batch-outcome taxonomy** (empty_valid ≠ validation_rejected ≠ truncated — the literal 26-scenario bug); **two-phase planner** (split-down, not just pack-up; extraction has no chapter-chunking); **provenance trust** (model offsets are INV-6 untrusted, validate not trust); **verified-clobber guard** (merge never overwrites human-verified); **tenancy** (owner_user_id + grant gate + INV-6 + redaction on every new table/event). 4 HIGH findings are design-blocking → resolved in the detailed design.
> - **Build = 7 parallel lanes (plan §2):** FND (M0 finish_reason → M1 concurrency/two-ledger — the blocker), RE (reasoning-effort + the wiring no-op fix), PLAN (planner SDK), OBS, PROV, MERGE, CACHE. Critical path FND→PROV→MERGE; RE+PLAN start at minute 0. `extraction_handler.go` is the hot file — FND→PROV→MERGE sequence on it, never concurrent.
> - **Gate decision (PO-reversible):** Option A — pipeline-first, build in translation+glossary now, cache behind an interface (physical re-home to knowledge deferred = `D-EXTRACTION-REHOME-KNOWLEDGE`). 3 tracked deferrals: that + `D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES` + `D-RAWCACHE-MINIO-OFFLOAD`.
> - **First BUILD action next session:** start lane **FND** (M0 finish_reason propagation, then M1) + lane **RE** in parallel. The RE wiring fix (chat thinking toggle is a live no-op today) is a standalone user-visible win.

</details>

# ▶ Dracula end-to-end JOURNEY verification (live, 2026-06-22) — steps 1–8 PROVEN; 9–11 remain

> **The real product scenario, driven live on the actual Dracula book** (`019eeb09`, test account) instead of clearing micro-deferrals. Journey: create book → agent web-search+suggest ontology → approve → import → extract glossary → translate glossary+chapter to vi → build KG → build wiki → enrich → write chapter.
> - **✅ 1 Create book / ✅ 2 Agent-suggested ontology (book-optimized: `vampire/hunter/victim/werewolf/witch/artifact` + defaults) / ✅ 3 Approve (12 kinds, 52 attrs adopted) / ✅ 4 Import (**4 published chapters now** — fetched the full public-domain novel from Gutenberg #345 since the repo ships only ch01, split it, bulk-ingested ch2–4 via `POST /v1/books/{id}/chapters/bulk`; a stray `OVERSIZED unplannable E2E` draft sits at sort 99) / ✅ 5 Extract glossary (30 entities) / ✅ 6 Glossary→vi (233 attr translations) / ✅ 7 Chapter→vi (`vi/completed`).** All verified by live DB state, not handoff claims.
> - **✅ 8 Build KG — DONE this session (was the key blocker).** Drove `POST /v1/knowledge/projects/{id}/extraction/{estimate,start}` live. **Neo4j now holds the Dracula graph: 65 Entity (Count Dracula, Jonathan Harker, Castle Dracula, Carpathians, London…), 17 Event, 3 Fact, 10 Passage, 15 ExtractionSource + 60 EVIDENCED_BY + 21 RELATES_TO** (job complete, 65 items, $0.06, gemma + bge-m3, scope=all). **🔴 BLOCKER SURFACED (real UX gap → fix): build-KG is gated behind the K17.9 embedding benchmark, which had NEVER been run here** (0 benchmark runs). The non-obvious trip: `min_runs: 3` — a `runs=1` benchmark returns `passed:false` DESPITE perfect recall@3=1.0/mrr=1.0 (bge-m3 passes cleanly at runs=3; 1024-dim neg-control override 0.70 applied). The agent's `kg_build_graph` mints a token fine but extraction 409s `benchmark_missing` until the user benchmarks their embedding model — the journey never surfaces this prerequisite. Also: changing the project embedding model returns `extraction_status:disabled` + deletes prior nodes (vector-space reset). **`D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX`** (tracked; ANALYSIS DONE → [docs/specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md](../specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md)). **Verdict: primarily a DESIGN gap (an unreconciled contradiction) + 2 implementation gaps.** The benchmark WAS designed as a visible "Run benchmark" step gating Confirm (RAID brief `05_build-graph-gates.md`, UX review P0 KN-1/BL-16) and the FE wires that button — but: **(a) DESIGN contradiction** — the gate keys benchmark to `(user, project, embedding_model)` (extraction.py:696) and the FE button is on the REAL project, yet the runner REFUSES any project that already has chapter/chat/glossary passages (`runner.py:223-227` `not_benchmark_project`); all three can't hold once the project has content, so on a non-trivial project the UI's only remedy structurally can't clear the gate (the RAID brief scoped "NO backend changes" assuming the endpoint fit — it didn't). I only cleared it because change-embedding had just emptied the project. **(b) IMPL** — `core.py:159` short-circuits `runs<min_runs` BEFORE metrics → `runs=1` returns `passed:false` at `recall@3=1.0` with no reason + a misleading FE "low-quality results" badge (FE always sends runs:3, so only API/agent callers hit it — i.e. exactly my drive). **(c) DESIGN** — a successful run leaves ~10 synthetic `benchmark_entity` passages, no cleanup (pollution). **(d) IMPL** — `kg_build_graph` warns but there's NO `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool → agent dead-end. **Fix breakdown:** R1 decouple benchmark→(user,embedding_model) on an auto-sandbox (root cause, needs a plan + contract change); R2 add `fail_reason` + stop FE mislabelling a perfect short run; R3 clamp/reject `runs<min_runs` on the interactive endpoint; R4 add `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (MCP-first). R2+R3 are fix-now-small; R1+R4 need a plan. **▶ BATCH-FIX BUILD (plan: [2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md](../plans/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md)) — BACKEND M1–M3 DONE 2026-06-22 (commits 7f3502af, ccf52484, 0d8306e2, 17c89fa0):** **M1/R1** re-keyed the gate to `(user, embedding_model)` (`BenchmarkRunsRepo.get_latest_for_model`) + routed the run to a hidden per-(user,model) sandbox (`get_or_create_benchmark_sandbox`; migration `is_benchmark_sandbox`; list-excluded) — **live-proven:** ran the benchmark on the content-bearing Dracula project (65 entities) → passed, **NO `not_benchmark_project`** (old code 409s), sandbox created with the fixtures, real project's `benchmark_entity` count stayed 10 (**zero new pollution**), sandbox absent from list API. **M2/R2+R3** `gate_failures()` named codes (`insufficient_runs` ≠ a quality fail) threaded to the response/to_json + the runner clamps `runs` up to `min_runs` — **live-proven:** `runs=1` → `passed=True, runs=3, gate_failures=[]` (the perfect-but-failed trap is gone). **M3/R4** `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (handler + arg model + definition + shim; OWNER-only, direct, runs on the sandbox) — catalogue unit-green (TOOL_DEFINITIONS 26). Full knowledge unit suite **2988 green**; provider-gate clean. **REMAINING: M4 (FE) + M5 (contract)** — R2 badge copy (`insufficient_runs` → "inconclusive, needs ≥3 runs" not "low-quality"), R5 `BuildGraphDialog` prerequisite checklist, R6 model-swap warning copy + the OpenAPI `gate_failures`/tool additions. The FE is isolated copy/checklist on a now-working backend; the journey's KG-build dead-end is ALREADY cleared by the backend.
> - **✅ 9 Build wiki — DONE 2026-06-22.** Drove `POST /internal/knowledge/books/{id}/wiki/generate` (S2S) for 3 entities → **3 wiki articles generated** (Jonathan Harker / Count Dracula / Mina; 2.3–5KB bodies, `generation_status=needs_review` from the local-model verify pass), job complete, $0.15 via gemma. **🔴 GATE SURFACED: wiki-gen is flag-gated OFF** (`WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` → the `wiki_gen_processor` consumer never runs, job sits `pending` forever); had to restart knowledge-service with `KNOWLEDGE_WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=true`. Another un-surfaced journey prerequisite (like the KG benchmark) → **`D-JOURNEY-WIKI-FLAG` ✅ FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9):** the consumer now ALWAYS runs (it's idle/free until a user-triggered, cost-gated job arrives — gating it off-by-default only produced silent eternal pends); spend stays bounded per-request (`max_spend_usd` + the cost-gated trigger), not a platform env. `wiki_gen_enabled` deprecated→default True, no longer gates the consumer. **Live-proven:** restarted knowledge-service with `WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` → the wiki-gen consumer still started.
> - **✅ 10 Enrichment — DROVE IT + FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9).** `POST /v1/lore-enrichment/projects/{id}/auto-enrich` → scanned 30 entities, detected 30 gaps. **The user's call was right — gemma is fine, the CODE was weak; 3 code bugs found + fixed (NOT a model problem):** **(A) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-EMPTY-COMPLETION`** — one empty LLM completion raised `CompletionSeamError` that escaped the per-gap handler and **FAILED the whole job**, discarding the gaps that worked. Now caught as a per-gap skip (runner.py). **(C) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-VERBATIM`** — the gen prompt said "use ONLY the excerpts"+"don't invent" but never "synthesize", so gemma safely **copied the source verbatim**, which our OWN anti-plagiarism gate then auto-rejected (self-defeating loop); both language templates now demand paraphrase/re-creation (generate.py). **(B) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-COST-UNITS`** — `spent=8409` is **8409 TOKENS not $8409** (the cap is token-denominated per the C1 PO ruling, but columns/logs say `*_cost_usd`); relabelled the log, the DB/API `*_usd`→tokens rename is structural (migration+FE) and tracked. **Live-proven (re-ran after the fix):** job **COMPLETED** (was `failed`), **4 proposals `review_status='proposed'` NOT `rejected`** — gemma now PARAPHRASES (Borgo Pass 地理"连接比斯特里茨与布科维纳的山间路径" — synthesized, not the verbatim English source), clearing the gate. Confidence 0.30 is the H0 quarantine floor (awaiting human review), not a rejection.
> - **✅ 11 Write new chapter — DRIVEN AGENT-SIDE 2026-06-22 via the new `composition_generate` MCP tool.** Through the gateway: `composition_create_work` → `composition_outline_node_create` (chapter+scene; both kinds require a real book `chapter_id` per the `outline_chapter_required` constraint, so a new book chapter `019eef4c` was created first) → `composition_generate` (propose, mints a `composition.generate` token) → confirm (`/v1/composition/actions/confirm`) → the confirm effect ran `engine.generate_chapter(persist=True)` IN-PROCESS → **gemma drafted a full gothic chapter (real Stoker-voice prose) → PERSISTED to the book draft (draft_version 2), status `completed`.** (`grounding=False`/`canon=skipped_no_cast` because the minimal scene carried no `present_entity_ids` → voice-only grounding; a scene with cast would pull KG/glossary canon.) **The grounded cowrite ENGINE is now agent-reachable.** Step 10 was likewise re-driven agent-side via the new `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` MCP tool (job `019eef45` → 2 synthesized `proposed` proposals: Borgo Pass / Bistritz, paraphrased not verbatim).
> - **Scenario status:** the 2 tool-gaps are closed and BOTH new tools are real-execution-proven agent-driven on the Dracula book. **FULL FRESH-BOOK AGENT-DRIVEN RUN done 2026-06-22** (fresh book `019eef55…`, KG project `019eef5d…`, all through the gateway MCP surface unless noted):
>   - ✅ **1 create** (`book_create`) · ✅ **2 ontology** (`glossary_web_search` + baseline adopted + `vampire`+`hunter` custom kinds via `glossary_propose_new_kind`→glossary confirm; verified in `book_kinds`) · ✅ **3 import** (`book_chapter_bulk_create` real Dracula ch.1 + publish) · ✅ **4 extract** (`translation_start_extraction` propose→confirm → **26 entities**) · ✅ **5 translate→vi** (`translation_start_job` propose→confirm → completed).
>   - ✅ **6 KG build** — `kg_build_graph` propose→`/v1/kg/actions/confirm` → **26 Entity nodes in Neo4j** for project `019eef5d…`, `extraction_status=ready` (benchmark via REST `benchmark-run` PASSED, embedding model via PATCH — config steps with no MCP surface). **🔴 NEW GATEWAY GAP `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`:** project-scoped KG tools resolve the project from `X-Project-Id`, but **the ai-gateway federation DROPS `X-Project-Id`** → through the gateway they 'no project in scope'; DIRECT to knowledge `/mcp` (X-Project-Id set) they mint fine. So KG/wiki were driven **direct on knowledge `/mcp`** (still MCP tool-calls; the new composition/enrich tools are immune — they take `project_id`/ids as explicit args). Re-confirmed `D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX` (fresh project → embedding set → `extraction_status=disabled` until the benchmark runs; `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool absent from the deployed gateway catalog).
>   - ◐ **7 wiki** — `kg_build_wiki` propose mints + the confirm reaches the effect, but **422 "no entities … extract the glossary first"**. **⚠️ ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED 2026-06-22 + FIXED (M2 above, `D-WIKI-ENTITY-FREQ-GATE`):** this was NOT draft-vs-active. The `known-entities` Go handler IGNORES `status` and gates on `COUNT(chapter_links) >= min_frequency` (default **2**); the wiki effect called it at the default, so a single-chapter book (every entity freq 1) returned 0. Proven live: all 26 are `draft`, yet `status=active&min_frequency=1` → 26 and `&min_frequency=2` → 0. (The OLD Dracula book worked because it has 4 chapters → freq ≥2, not because its entities were active.) Wiki resolver now passes `min_frequency=1`.
>   - ✅ **8 enrich** (`lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` — the NEW tool, **through the gateway**) → real synthesized proposal on the fresh book once KG embeddings existed (e.g. Borgo Pass 地理/features, `review_status=proposed`). First pass before KG produced 0 (retrieval needs the book embeddings the KG build creates). · ✅ **9 write** (`composition_generate` — the NEW tool — propose→confirm → **real gothic chapter generated + persisted to the fresh book draft, draft_version 2**).
>   - **Verdict (original run): 8/9 driven; the 2 blockers (X-Project-Id drop + wiki freq-gate) were then ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED in the batch above, and the journey RE-RUN clean 9/9 through the gateway (see the ✅ PAYOFF RUN line in the NEXT block).** Non-gateway-MCP steps in the original run were config-only setup (embedding-model pick, benchmark) + the KG/wiki tools (then blocked by `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`, now forwarded). **All four agent tools (`kg_build_graph`, `kg_build_wiki`, `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich`, `composition_generate`) now work cleanly through the gateway end-to-end.**
> - **Note:** running the benchmark on the real Dracula project (user-approved) loaded golden-set fixtures into it; the post-extraction graph still reads as clean Dracula entities.

KG deferred surface now **100% cleared — no nice-to-haves left.**

LIVE in-browser: AdoptPicker lists real templates, the loss warning lists the exact dropped customization + gates the adopt button until acknowledged. **KG epic deferred surface fully cleared.**

On branch `feat/knowledge-graph-ontology` (PR #40).

>   - **`kg_project_create`** (`D-KG-LF-PROJECT-CREATE-MCP`, commit `229db03d`) — the book↔KG bootstrap (the schema/extraction/wiki tools all need "the current project"; was REST-only). New `app/tools/project_tools.py` + executor/definitions/server wiring. Class-W; book-bound create is book-OWNER-only (mirrors REST, no oracle); idempotent. 5 unit tests; LIVE: created a project via deployed `/mcp`, id returned (cleaned up).
>   - **`glossary_web_search`** (`D-KG-LF-WEBSEARCH-MCP`, commit `f24bd20a`) — free-form topic research (the existing `glossary_deep_research` is entity-scoped). New `web_search_tool.go` reusing `webSearch`+`neutralizeWebText`+`safeHTTPURL`; class-R read, INV-6 neutralized, non-http(s) dropped. 4 unit tests; LIVE: listed + dispatches, clear not-configured error (returns results with a BYOK web_search model).

Plan: [`docs/plans/2026-06-21-kg-build-wiki-mcp-tools.md`](../plans/2026-06-21-kg-build-wiki-mcp-tools.md). **build_graph** resolves `embedding_model` from the project (the campaign/internal-dispatch precedent), requires the extraction `llm_model` as an arg, delegates to the shared `_start_extraction_job_core` (K17.9 benchmark gate enforced at confirm, warned in preview). **build_wiki** resolves entity ids (explicit subset, else ALL book entities at confirm), creates+enqueues the wiki-gen job. Key design: extraction/wiki/redis deps are built INSIDE the confirm/preview branch, NOT injected into the shared route signature (route-injecting them broke unrelated admin/system confirm paths — caught + fixed). knowledge unit **2989 passed**; provider-gate clean; LIVE (rebuilt image): 25 tools, both present, `kg_build_graph` minted a confirm_token (seeded project), `kg_build_wiki` clean book guard. Commits `229db03d` (build_graph) + `069921f2` (build_wiki). **Enrichment stays UI/REST (not requested as MCP).**
> - **Stack note:** the running infra is on this branch's rebuilt images (knowledge/glossary/api-gateway-bff/frontend); the admin RS256 key + browser seed data were throwaways (removed after the smokes).
> - **✅ Translated-chapter view UX gap WIRED (2026-06-22).** The canonical `ChapterTranslationsPage` (`/books/:b/chapters/:c/translations` — language switcher, version sidebar, side-by-side compare) was fully built but only reachable from a translation-matrix cell click. Wired it into the two surfaces where a user lands after translating: a **"View translations"** row action (Languages icon) in the workspace chapter list ([ChaptersTab](../../frontend/src/pages/book-tabs/ChaptersTab.tsx)) + a **toolbar button** (Eye icon, `guardedNavigate` so an unsaved draft prompts to save) in the chapter editor ([ChapterEditorPage](../../frontend/src/pages/ChapterEditorPage.tsx)). i18n keys ×4 locales (`books.chapters.action.translations`, `editor.view_translations[_title]`). **Fixed a pre-existing broken test** (ChapterEditorPage.test.tsx lacked a `QueryClientProvider` after the C17 chat-models `useQuery` landed) + added a regression-lock for the new button. VERIFY: editor test 3/3, book-tabs 12/12, `tsc --noEmit` clean. Frontend image rebuilt to ship it. **Note:** the agent's *Dracula* ontology WAS book-optimized (proposed Vampire/Hunter/Victim/Artifact kinds + TURNED_BY/HUNTS/PROTECTS/FEARS/LOCATED_IN edges — not bare defaults).
> - **✅ Glossary-assistant UX + propose→APPROVE proven end-to-end (2026-06-22).** Driven by the user testing the Dracula book on local gemma-26b. **Commits:** `a15a8bac` (skill prompt), `7f9df5dc` (unified dock), `1d51a400` (auto-confirm card), `e1ed27b2` (live result).
>   - **Approve flow was never actually exercised before** — the agent *proposed* book-optimized kinds (Vampire/Hunter/Victim/Artifact + edges) but they were never approved, so the book showed only the 6 adopted defaults. Drove propose→approve live: 4 book-native kinds + attributes (with descriptions) now in `book_kinds`/`book_attributes`; patch-defaults + delete-unneeded also proven (DB-verified). There IS an automated approve-flow test (`action_confirm_test.go` mints `descSchemaCreateKind` → confirm → asserts `book_kinds` row).
>   - **Skill prompt fix (`glossary_skill.py`)** — the agent proposed bare KINDS, never ATTRIBUTES, and adopted defaults ship with NO description (which the extraction prompt reads as the per-attribute instruction). Now: every kind gets 3-6 attributes, every attribute gets a specific description, curate-adopted-defaults guidance, and the `glossary_book_patch` FLAT arg-shape (weak models defaulted to the entity-edit `changes` diff → silent no-op). Live-verified proactive (hunter: methods/allegiance/notable_kills/motivation, each described).
>   - **🔴 GUI approve gap FIXED (the headline) — `D-GLOSSARY-AUTOCONFIRM`** — the local model mints the confirm_token but never calls the frontend `glossary_confirm_action` tool, so NO Approve card rendered (only prose "confirm in your dashboard"); a GUI-only user couldn't approve. The FE rendered the card ONLY from a suspended frontend tool. Fix: `AssistantMessage` now AUTO-renders the confirm card from any completed propose result carrying a LIVE confirm_token (reuses ConfirmCard/ConfirmActionCard, synthetic record, resume no-ops, exp-decoded to skip stale replay, deduped vs explicit). Plus the live SSE path now keeps the tool `result` so the card shows during the turn, not only after reload. (Caught only because the user insisted on a REAL in-GUI test — prior API-POST approvals had masked it.)
>   - **Unified the book AI assistant** — `BookAssistantDock` was mounted inside GlossaryTab (glossary-tab only); hoisted to `BookDetailPage` so ONE book-context-aware dock serves every workspace tab. Reader keeps its own.
>   - **Deferred:** `D-GLOSSARY-SYSTEM-ATTR-DESCRIPTIONS` — **CLEARED 2026-06-22 (F1 built, migration 0035; see the F1 bullet below).** (Was: system_attributes 93/93 + book_attributes had EMPTY descriptions; now the 81 authored System cells carry extraction-ready descriptions, propagated sync-only.) `D-KG-SCHEMA-APPROVE-LIVE` (naturally-next) — KG schema propose→approve (`kg_schema_edit`→`/v1/kg/actions/confirm`) still untested live; needs an adopted project-scoped schema first (adopt_template). `D-GLOSSARY-WEAKMODEL-BATCH` (won't-fix-now) — gemma-26b is unreliable at multi-step curation batches (wrong arg shapes, tangents); reliable per-op + with capable models (gpt-4o).
> - **✅ Extraction-quality & approve-flow fixes — F2 + F3 BUILT (2026-06-22).** Spec [`docs/specs/2026-06-22-glossary-kg-extraction-quality-fixes.md`](../specs/2026-06-22-glossary-kg-extraction-quality-fixes.md) (CLARIFY-signed-off). Commits: `4a77e864` (F2a/F2b), `245344f3` (F3a), `830b33dd` (F3b), `7a406a20` (F2c/F3c).
>   - **F2 — KG schema approve fixed.** The auto-confirm card mis-routed `kg_`-prefixed (non-dotted) descriptors to the glossary endpoint (422). `descriptorDomain` now maps `kg_*` → the `kg` domain → ConfirmActionCard → `/v1/kg/actions/*`; added a GET `/v1/kg/actions/preview?token=` alias so the shared card previews KG like every domain (BFF already proxies `/v1/kg/*`). +7 tests. Adopt-first guidance added to the knowledge skill (F2c).
>   - **F3a — tolerant `glossary_book_patch`.** Accepts the entity-edit `changes[]` diff shape weak models kept emitting (silent no-op before) and folds it onto the flat fields (nil-only; drops a hallucinated base_version). +4 tests.
>   - **F3b — one-shot `glossary_propose_new_kind` with `attributes[]` (the headline reliability win).** The kind + its attributes are created in ONE confirm token / ONE transaction (atomic, rollback→re-proposable), collapsing the fragile propose-kind→approve→propose-each-attr→approve chain. +2 real-PG tests. **LIVE-PROVEN:** natural request → agent made ONE propose_new_kind call WITH attributes (powers/coven, each described) → ONE approve → `witch` kind + both attrs (with descriptions) landed atomically. F3c stay-on-task guard kills the web-search/list-templates tangent.
>   - **✅ F1 `D-GLOSSARY-SYSTEM-ATTR-DESCRIPTIONS` — BUILT (2026-06-22).** Spec [`docs/specs/2026-06-22-F1-system-attr-descriptions.md`](../specs/2026-06-22-F1-system-attr-descriptions.md). New migration **0035_system_attr_descriptions** ([system_attr_descriptions.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/system_attr_descriptions.go), registered in [ledger.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/ledger.go)): an idempotent, non-clobbering `UPDATE system_attributes` over the spec's 81 authored `(kind_code, attr_code)` cells (name/term display keys omitted) that sets `description` + recomputes `content_hash` with the EXACT inline md5 the seed/`attrContentHash` uses — so the new hash equals a fresh seed-with-description (the parity adopt + G5 Sync depend on). `COALESCE(TRIM(description),'')=''` guard ⇒ admin edits survive + re-run = 0 rows. **Decision honored: sync-only, NO backfill** — new books carry descriptions at adopt; existing books pull via `sync_apply take_theirs` (`keep_mine` preserves a local edit). Authored table re-verified against `domain.DefaultKinds` (exact 81/93). **LIVE-SMOKE (real dev DB `loreweave_glossary`, rebuilt+restarted glossary-service → RunChain applied 0035 on actual startup):** `schema_migrations` now has `0035`; `system_attributes` 0→**81** descriptions (name/term still 0); `character.aliases` hash moved `0be5469a…`→`5ae3679e…`; and **22,736 existing system-sourced book_attributes now drift** (`source_hash ≠` updated System `content_hash`) across 303 books — the exact surface `glossary_book_sync_available` lists, so existing books pull via Sync. **End-to-end on a REAL book via the gateway:** on `019ee969` (万古神帝) the extraction-profile started at **0/60** descriptions; `GET …/sync/available` listed **51** attribute updates; `POST …/sync/apply take_theirs` applied all 51; the profile then read **51/60** with `character.aliases: Other names, titles, epithets…` — the authored text now flowing through the worker's cross-service extraction-profile. VERIFY (real PG 5555, throwaway DBs): migrate-pkg mechanics test (93 empty→81 set + 12 name/term untouched; hash moves + equals seed-formula; idempotent; non-clobber) + api-pkg propagation test (adopt-before-edit → `sync/available` lists attrs with authored `theirs` → take_theirs pulls / keep_mine preserves; new-adopt carries descriptions immediately; idempotent count=2 on shared DB) — both green; full glossary module `-p1` green (api/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc); gofmt + vet + ai-provider-gate clean. **M3 extraction-prompt smoke not run** (the adopt→`book_attributes.description` landing — exactly what `extraction_prompt.py` reads — is proven by the api test; a full worker e2e rides `D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE`).
> - **Unpushed:** the branch is many commits ahead of `origin/main` (push only on explicit go-ahead).
