# Session Handoff — `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` · **GLOSSARY TRACK COMPLETE + ALL 26 SCENARIOS VERIFIED (2026-06-21)** · **merged `main` 2026-06-21 (MCP fan-out epic)** · 2026-06-21

> **▶ SCENARIO TEST — 26/26 COMPLETE (2026-06-21, local Gemma-4-26B-QAT, book `019ee969`).** Finished the full S1–S26 sweep on a healthy stack. Results table in [`docs/test/2026-06-21-glossary-assistant-scenario-checklist.md`](../test/2026-06-21-glossary-assistant-scenario-checklist.md). **This session drove the 14 untested scenarios:** the 5 reads (S16 evidence / S17 chapter-links / S18 unknowns / S19 merge-candidates / S21 revisions), S14 create-evidence (W) + S15 create-chapter-link (W) direct writes, **S2** book kind+attr create (DB-verified `faction`/`leader`, book-scoped), **S3** `glossary_book_set_kind_genres` delta-add xianxia (DB-verified), **S8** re-extract confirm→Apply→job→completed (0 entities this run = local-model malformed-JSON artifact, pipeline graceful), **S12** restore-revision confirm→Apply (`restored:true`), **S22** admin (assistant called 0 tools + refused; `TestAdminMCP_*` suite green: transport-gate 401 / absent-from-user-catalog / RS256 confirm / user-token-rejected), **S23** grant-deny (18 ownership/grant tests green inc. `MutatingRoutesRejectViewGrantee` + `*FailsClosed`), **S24** injection (planted hostile evidence → assistant flagged it as a prompt-injection, called no merge/delete, no PWNED — INV-6), **S25** tri-lingual no-confusion (Vietnamese convo + Chinese source + English target → `propose_aliases language_code=en`). Earlier this branch: S1/S4a/S5/S6/S7/S9/S10/S11/S13/S20/S26. **S8 token-truncation bug — `D-EXTRACTION-TOKEN-TRUNCATION`, FIXED + live-re-verified (don't blame the model):** the S8 "0 entities" was NOT a weak model — direct LM Studio replay proved **output-token truncation**: `plan_kind_batches` budgeted only the input schema, packing **7 kinds into one call** whose output hit `finish_reason=length` at `max_tokens=12000` → cut mid-array → 0 parseable entities. Fix: cap `MAX_KINDS_PER_BATCH=3` (root cause — bounds per-call output), raise `max_tokens` 12000→20000, and repair truncated/fenced arrays instead of discarding them ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py) + [extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py)); +5 regression tests. **Live:** same chapter now extracts 9 created + 5 skipped across 3 clean `finish=stop` batches (glossary 10→19). Grammar-constrained decoding tried but a permissive schema ran away → tracked `D-EXTRACTION-CONSTRAINED-DECODING` (needs tight per-kind schema). **1 LOW finding — `D-BOOKPATCH-GENRE-ERRMSG`, FIXED + live-re-verified:** `glossary_book_patch` returned a misleading "no live row with that code" when an attribute exists under a different genre (genre is identity, not a patchable field). The attribute-level not-found branch now explains the `(kind, genre, code)` identity + the delete+recreate path ([book_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/book_tools.go)) + a regression test in `TestBookTool_PatchAttributeLevel`; glossary-service rebuilt + redeployed; on re-drive the agent got the new message and **self-corrected** (called `glossary_book_delete` to delete+recreate rather than the old 3-retry dead-end).

> **▶ LIVE SCENARIO TEST (2026-06-21, fresh stack @ HEAD, local Gemma-4 26B QAT via LM Studio).** Built a fresh fixture (book `019ee969` "万古神帝 Scenario Test", 4 real chapters of 万古神帝, adopted xianxia ontology: 2 genres / 9 kinds / 59 attrs) on claude-test; drove the glossary assistant via the chat API (gemma user_model, priced $0.10/$0.40 per Mtok). **Gemma is a RELIABLE tool-caller** (better than the old local 35B). **Verified live:** S0 federation (book_list→glossary_book_ontology_read→grounded), **S7 extraction** (9 correct entities: 张若尘/池瑶 chars, 玉漱宫 loc, 神武印记 item, 第一中央帝国 org), **S4 translation** (propose_translation → 张若尘=Zhang Ruochen etc., draft), **S6 per-language aliases** (propose_aliases → `["Zhang the Sword Saint","Master of the Divine Mark"]` JSON-array draft — the exact chip-editor/composePerLanguageAliases format), **S11 status-change** (class-C card mints AND full round-trip: glossary `/actions/confirm` → draft→active, updated=2), **S9 merge** (full round-trip: preview→Apply→loser soft-deleted + journal_id, winner kept), **S10 reassign_kind** (preview surfaces the accurate DATA-LOSS warning: 10 char-attrs dropped on character→organization), **S1 create** + **S13 edit** (both correctly SUSPEND for human Apply — INV-1, no premature write), reads (ai_suggestions/jobs/search/ontology/get_entity), settings_list_models. ~17 distinct scenarios/tools green; both confirm paths (translation + glossary) proven end-to-end. **2 M3 BUGS CAUGHT + FIXED (commit `e3a03779`, unpushed):** (1) the MCP extraction path enqueued an EMPTY extraction_profile → 0 batches → 0 entities + a $0 cost estimate (agent omits the profile; now DERIVED from the book's auto-selected ontology in both the mint estimate and the shared core); (2) `model_source` hardcoded `platform_model` → a user_model ref 404'd as LLMModelNotFound (now `user_model`, caller-pays). Regression test + 5/5 extraction tests pass. **S5 deep-research — LIVE-PROVEN (2026-06-21).** The external `local-web-search-service` (sibling repo, SearXNG-backed, §8-conformant, keyless) is live on `host.docker.internal:15487`. Registered a keyless BYOK `web_search` credential + `searxng-default` user_model (favorite) for claude-test, wired `PROVIDER_REGISTRY_URL` into glossary's compose env. Drove `glossary_deep_research` on 张若尘 → confirm card (paid-op cost gate) → Apply → **5 real web sources attached as `reference` evidence** with accurate canon (Baidu Baike: "张若尘是飞天鱼所著玄幻小说《万古神帝》的男主角…"); clean http(s) URLs + neutralized snippets (INV-6 held). **1 code bug fixed:** `internalWebSearch` rejected an empty `secret_ciphertext`, breaking the **keyless** local backend the §8 contract promises — now passes `""` through (the adapter omits the Authorization header). The ENTIRE glossary assistant (S0/S4/S5/S6/S7/S9/S10/S11/S13 + reads) is now live-proven on local backends (Gemma LLM + SearXNG search). **`D-S5-LIVE-SMOKE` discharged.** (✅ **`D-S5-WEBSEARCH-HANDLER-TEST` CLEARED 2026-06-22** — [web_search_handler_integration_test.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/web_search_handler_integration_test.go): DB-seeded resolution test for `internalWebSearch` × 3 — **keyless** (empty `secret_ciphertext` → upstream gets NO `Authorization`, no decrypt-of-"" 500), **keyed** (real secret decrypts → `Authorization: Bearer …`), **strict gate** (chat-only model → 404, web_search never defaulted). Green vs the real provider-registry DB; full prr api pkg + provider-gate clean.)

> **▶ "CLEAR THE 3 REMAINING GLOSSARY-TRACK ITEMS" /loom run (2026-06-21) — COMPLETE.** User: clear (#1 S5 deep-research, #2 S4 review-card FE, #3 pipeline live-smoke), then run 20+ scenario tests. Chose the ambitious options (full web-search S5 + S4 chat card + batch dialog). **All shipped this run (7 commits).** Remaining before scenario testing: a fresh stack rebuild (the running stack predates these commits) + register a BYOK web-search credential for the S5 live path.

When the `aliases` attribute + a translation language are selected, that language's set renders as a **JSON-backed chip editor** (add/remove tags) with the existing draft/verified confidence badge; serializes with `JSON.stringify` — the exact format the BE writer (`glossary_propose_aliases` `json.Marshal`) and reader (`composePerLanguageAliases` `json.Unmarshal`) expect, so it round-trips with the agent. `AttrTranslationRow` gains an `attrCode` prop; `EntityEditorModal` threads `attrCode={def.code}`; `aliasTagsFromValue` parses+dedupes (mirrors BE `dedupStrings`), returns null for a non-array value → textarea fallback (no silent drop). **/review-impl (this work): 1 MED fixed** — the chip editor was first gated on `field_type === 'tags'`, which over-broadened to `members`/`participants`/`tropes`/`parties`/`related_characters` (all `tags` too, but CSV-source + NOT aliases-scoped on the BE → would store JSON vs CSV-source drift); re-scoped to `attrCode === 'aliases'` (matches the BE `code='aliases'` contract) + a regression test. **1 LOW fixed** — dedup on parse (React key collision guard). **Accepted/documented:** emptied set saves `"[]"` (harmless; reader filters to empty); JS `JSON.stringify` vs Go `json.Marshal` `&`-escaping round-trips identically through `JSON.parse`/`json.Unmarshal`; blur-before-save race matches existing `AttrTagsCard` UX. **VERIFY:** `AttrTranslationRow.test.tsx` **7/7** (chips from JSON, create writes `["Ghost"]`, remove+patch writes `["Yan Mo"]`, dedup, legacy-string fallback, non-aliases tags stays textarea, non-tags textarea); tsc clean on touched files (repo-wide `tsc` exits 2 only on pre-existing missing `@dnd-kit/*` in the composition feature, unrelated). 8 files: `AttrTranslationRow.tsx`, `EntityEditorModal.tsx`, 4× `entityEditor.json` (`tags_add` key), the new test. **S6 per-language aliases is now COMPLETE end-to-end (BE vertical + chat-send + human edit surface).**

Each VERIFY-gated by the integrator (re-ran every suite): book go test green, compose 26, transl 23 MCP+9 regression (816 full), settings 5+3. **3 DB-gated settings tests PASS live on real PG** (H13 SecretRedaction, UserScopeIsolation, ModelDeleteConfirmRoundTrip). `ai-provider-gate` OK repo-wide.

Rebuilt 5 images + brought the real stack up; smoked chat→gateway→provider live: all 5 providers federate (75 tools), `_meta.tier` survives the wire on every tier, H10 accurate, real owner-guarded `book_list`/`settings_list_providers` calls route non-error. **2 real integration bugs found+fixed (unit tests missed both):** (1) book Dockerfile didn't COPY the kit → image build fail; (2) book `/mcp` mounted without `/mcp/*` wildcard → provider went unavailable after the first 30s federation refresh (go-sdk routes message traffic under the path subtree; provider-registry mounts both — book now does too).

Kit regression tests added (descriptor-tamper, `uuid.Nil`-owner, at-expiry). **Post-fix live-smoke 20/20** — all 5 providers boot with the new secret + federate, `jobs_get` federates, real Tier-R calls route. Settings 3 DB-gated tests + the new cross-user-confirm reject PASS live on real PG; book DB-gated replay/stale-version PASS live.
> - **✅ Wave 3 — S-WORKFLOW DONE (this session).** Added `app/services/workflow_skill.py` (`WORKFLOW_SKILL_PROMPT`) — the cross-service ORDERING fragment (chapters→translate→glossary→wiki sequence, async-dependency waits, H4 scope honesty, partial-chain honesty), appended to the universal skill on the agui surface (concatenation, shared cache slot). `test_stream_service` asserts it injects (33 pass). All 10 slices now built.
> - **✅ COMPOSE C — END-TO-END LLM eval DONE, live-green (this session).** Drove REAL chat turns through the full stack (chat-service → real LLM [lm_studio qwen3.6-35b on host :1234, letuhao1994's registered model] → tool decision → ai-gateway federation → provider → grounded answer), dev-JWT minted for the model owner. **Proven across 2 providers:** (book) "how many books do I have?" → LLM called `book_list` → returned the real book "Vạn Cổ Thần Đế" → grounded answer; (settings) "what models do I have?" → `settings_list_models` → real model list **with NO secrets (H13 live through the agent)** → grounded answer. AG-UI stream carried the full RUN_STARTED→REASONING→TOOL_CALL→RESULT→TEXT→RUN_FINISHED sequence. **Found + fixed a real shipping-blocker:** book MCP output structs used `uuid.UUID`, which the go-sdk infers as JSON "array" ([16]byte) while MarshalJSON emits a string → the server rejected its own non-empty output (the empty-list COMPOSE-B smoke hid it). Fixed (output UUIDs → string) + DB-gated regression test, re-ran e2e green. Commit `e8cc9cf2`.
> - **✅ Browser pass (Playwright) + 3 deferrals CLEARED (this session).** Real-browser pass on the live FE (claude-test login → /chat → qwen3.6-35b): proved the chat works end-to-end in-browser — real LLM tool-calling rendered `⚙ jobs_list ⚙ jobs_summary` chips + real job data (Active 2/Completed 175). **Found + fixed a real CORS bug** (`x-loreweave-stream-format` missing from the BFF `allowedHeaders` → cross-origin agui chat preflight-blocked; the whole MCP-fanout FE runs on agui). `ui_navigate` didn't fire — verified in code it IS advertised (always-on core via `generic_frontend_tool_def`; the model used find_tools-discovered jobs tools, so the core incl. ui_navigate was present) → a local-35B model limitation, not a code bug; the ui_*/confirm/diff renderers stay covered by 113 vitest. **3 LOW deferrals cleared:** D-MCP-H7-AGGREGATE-CAP (turn-total Tier-A ceiling=12), D-MCP-BOOK-CREATE-QUOTA (maxBooksPerUser=200, both surfaces, DB-test live), D-MCP-PY-GUARD-NIL-PARITY (zero-UUID owner reject). Commits `14431efa`/`9fcd4a29`/`1583691c`/`589b8b1b`.
> - **✅ CONFIRM-SEAM LIVE-FIX — DONE, live-green (2026-06-21, this session).** The optional "confirm/diff card Apply→resume with a stronger tool-caller" pass turned into a real integration audit driven by gpt-4o (the test account's registered OpenAI model — a competent tool-caller, unlike the local 35B). It **proved gpt-4o reliably calls MCP write tools** (book_list→book_update_meta real mutation→book_chapter_publish token mint, confirm gate held: chapter unpublished at suspend) and **caught a blocker + 3 real seam bugs the read-only smokes never hit** (they only ever exercised reads / settings_list_models, never a Tier-W **commit** through the BFF with a user JWT): **(0)** book-service was in a **crash loop** — the running container predated the `CONFIRM_TOKEN_SIGNING_SECRET` compose default (`must be ≥32 chars`) → all book MCP tools unavailable; fixed by recreating the container. **(1) [HIGH]** the BFF didn't proxy `/v1/book/actions/*` (it matches `/v1/books` plural; confirm_action builds singular `/v1/book/actions`) **or** `/v1/settings/actions/*` (no `/v1/settings` proxy) → book+settings confirm round-trips 404'd at the gateway; fixed with two thin passthrough proxies in [`gateway-setup.ts`](../../services/api-gateway-bff/src/gateway-setup.ts) (mirror the C21 worlds pattern). **(2) [HIGH]** translation `/v1/translation/actions/*` was **internal-token-gated** (the FE Apply sends the user JWT → 401), unlike book/glossary/composition which are JWT-gated; switched to `get_current_user` + a bound-user assertion (`claims.u == JWT caller`) mirroring book-service. **(3) [MED]** book-scoped chats advertise the legacy glossary-only `glossary_confirm_action` beside the generic `confirm_action`, so the model can confirm a book action via the glossary tool → FE renders the glossary `ConfirmCard` → posts to `/v1/glossary/actions/confirm` → 422 (fails *closed*, breaks the round-trip); fixed in the FE by routing the confirm card **by descriptor domain** (dotted `book.publish` → generic `ConfirmActionCard`; non-dotted glossary descriptors → legacy `ConfirmCard`) + `ConfirmActionCard` now derives the domain from the descriptor when `args.domain` is absent. **VERIFY (all live on the running stack):** all 5 confirm domains now route at the BFF (book/settings 404→422, translation 401→400 — all reaching their service with the user JWT); **full book Tier-W round-trip GREEN end-to-end through the BFF** — gpt-4o minted a `book.publish` token, then GET `/v1/book/actions/preview`=200 + POST `/v1/book/actions/confirm`=200 `action_done` (chapter published, revision created), then unpublished to restore (the exact path the FE Apply drives, incl. the #3 mis-pick re-route); translation 16 unit tests green incl. a NEW `test_confirm_rejects_token_bound_to_a_different_user`; FE tsc clean + ConfirmActionCard 10 (incl. `descriptorDomain` + domain-derivation) + AssistantMessage 5 vitest green.

FE editors: book `AttributeEditorPanel` (+4-locale i18n) + cms `AttributesAdminPanel` got an "AI assistance" section. Go test `TestSystemAttrAIAssist` green; full glossary api suite green; cms 41 pass.

Each wraps the same core query the HTTP handler uses (extracted `queryEntityRevisions`/`queryUnknownEntities` so handler+tool share one source). Test `TestPipelineReadTools_GatesAndData` (grant gate, entity-in-book guard, seeded-unknown surfaces); full glossary api suite green.

Decision: **additive Edit-level writes are class W (direct), NOT class C** — avoids the confirm-spine Manage-bump; the confirm spine is reserved for the destructive/high-impact writes. Test `TestPipelineWriteTool_ChapterLinkGuards`; full api suite green.

All Manage-gated (mint + confirm); each effect re-validates against current state and **binds opaque param entity-ids to `claims.BookID`** (cross-book token can't touch another book). Tests: `TestPipelinePropose_*` (input guards, status_change round-trip + replay-422, merge round-trip soft-delete+journal+replay, reassign data-loss preview).

Pass 1 verified the tenancy binding (every effect scopes to `claims.BookID`; `mergeOne` skips cross-book/cross-kind losers; `loadKindMap` book-scoped+live; `reassignEntityKindCore` validates kind+entity in book), single-use replay, authority binding (Manage, proposer==redeemer), correct actor attribution (proposer not LLM); 1 cleanliness fix (`cap` builtin shadow → `limit`). >    - **M3 — `translation_start_extraction`** (translation-service MCP, class-W confirm). Extracted `_create_extraction_job_core` from the HTTP `create_extraction_job` (DRY); new `DESC_START_EXTRACTION` confirm descriptor + effect in `actions.py` (re-authorizes the bound book + asserts chapters-under-book, then runs the core — **binds to `claims.resource_id`, never the payload book**; NO H14 re-price — the extraction estimate is a deterministic token projection so confirm==mint). The tool estimates via `estimate_extraction_cost` + mints the confirm token; progress polls via `jobs_get` (kind `glossary_extraction`). glossary-service untouched. Tests: tool-mint, non-owner deny, confirm→core round-trip, chapter-not-under-bound-book 403.
>    - **M4 — `glossary_propose_translation`** (glossary-service MCP, **class-W**). Batch propose target-language NAMES for entities → writes `attribute_translations` on the entity's display (name/term) attr value at **confidence='draft'**, **NEVER overwrites 'verified'** (`ON CONFLICT … WHERE confidence <> 'verified'`, RowsAffected 0 → skipped). Per-entity results; emits `emitTranslationChanged`. Cores `entityDisplayAttrValue` + `upsertDraftTranslation`. Test `TestPipelineTranslateTool_Guards` (Edit gate, batch skips, draft write, **verified-protection unchanged**).
>    - **/review-impl (M3 confirm/cost + M4): NO HIGH/MED.** Verified M3 tenancy binds to the signed `claims.resource_id` (payload book ignored), grant re-auth + chapter-binding mirror `start_job`, caller-pays model resolution; M4 cross-book entities skipped, verified never clobbered. **LOW (accepted):** M3 mint estimate can be approximate if glossary is down at mint (confirm re-fetches + 502s if still down → no spend; estimate is approximate by design); M4 no BCP-47 validation (consistent with the existing HTTP `createTranslation`).
>    - **Deferred:** `D-GLOSSARY-PERLANG-ALIASES` (S6 — per-language aliases are a data-model decision, out of M4 scope; source-language alias edit already works). `D-PIPELINE-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` / a M3/M4 live-smoke (chat→start_extraction/propose_translation→confirm→verify; unit-proven on real PG, spine live-smoked at CP-3/CP-5).

> **▶ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE — Tiered-MCP-Tools epic is COMPLETE (CP-0→CP-7), PUSHED, and the CP-6 admin write-chain is LIVE-PROVEN end-to-end (API-level).** **`D-CP6-ADMIN-E2E` discharged (API-level, 2026-06-20):** drove the real flow on the running stack — `claude-test` logs in → `/v1/admin/session` mints a real RS256 admin token → gateway `/mcp/admin` `glossary_admin_propose_create` (level=genre) → `/v1/glossary/actions/admin/confirm` → the System genre landed in the DB (`tier=system`, verified) → cleaned up via `propose_delete`+confirm. **Negative proven live:** a regular user confirming an admin-authority token at the user `/actions/confirm` → **501** (no cross-authority confirm); `/mcp/admin` no-token → 401; user `/mcp` → no admin-tool leak. **Dev-stack fix (regression I introduced at CP-5):** glossary was verifying admin tokens against a CP-5 *throwaway* `ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM` that didn't match auth-service's real signing key → all real admin tokens 401'd. Fixed by deriving glossary's public key from auth-service's `ADMIN_JWT_LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM` and recreating `glossary-service` with it (its other env is self-contained in compose). **⚠️ NOT durable** — the corrected key is only in the running container's env (no `infra/.env`); a full stack restart would re-empty it. **Dev-ops follow-up:** persist a matched pair via `infra/gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` into a gitignored `infra/.env` (`ADMIN_JWT_LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM`→auth, `ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`→glossary). **LLM-streaming layer NOW LIVE-PROVEN (2026-06-20, after the Docker engine recovered):** rebuilt chat-service with T4c + recreated it; drove a real admin chat turn (gpt-4o, `admin_context` + `X-Admin-Token`) — the agent called `glossary_admin_propose_create` (TOOL_CALL_RESULT `{"ok":true,"result":{"authority":"admin","confirm_token":…}}`, i.e. chat-service forwarded the token to `/mcp/admin` and routed the call) then `glossary_confirm_action` → `RUN_FINISHED status:suspended` (the exact confirm-card handoff the cms hook consumes). Smoke sessions cleaned up, 0 genre residue (suspend-before-confirm = no writes). **`D-CP6-ADMIN-E2E` is now FULLY discharged** — deterministic write-chain (propose→confirm→DB write) AND the LLM-streaming layer both live-proven; only the literal browser pixel-click of the cms card is unexercised (the hook/card are unit-proven + the suspend data-contract is live-proven). **Admin-key durability FIXED for real:** the matched keypair is persisted to a gitignored `infra/.env` (`ADMIN_JWT_LOCAL_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM`→auth, `ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`→glossary), **proven to survive a full stack restart** (the real admin token verifies post-reboot). The CP-5 throwaway-key regression can no longer recur. **Note (unrelated infra):** C: was ~8.5 GB free (Docker engine crashed from disk pressure) — Docker's data is on D: (~0.1 GB on C:); the C: hogs are dev caches (`.cache\huggingface` 25 GB, `.claude\projects` 18 GB, `%TEMP%` 15 GB, `.lmstudio` logs, Ubuntu WSL 27 GB) — user is handling cleanup. **T5 cross-cutting (CP-7):** no new code — its deliverables landed incrementally by the append-only, file-split design: per-surface skill prompts (book/user `GLOSSARY_SKILL_PROMPT` updated in T2/T3; admin `GLOSSARY_ADMIN_SKILL_PROMPT` in T4c); security curation tests INV-T4/T6 (chat-service `test_admin_surface`: book⇏admin + admin⇏book on the fresh AND resume paths; ai-gateway `admin-federation.spec` line 59: the user `/mcp` `computeCatalog` contains zero `glossary_admin_*` names; glossary `TestAdminMCP_AbsentFromUserCatalog`); per-surface live-smokes (T1 book CP-2, T2/T3 user CRUD+sync CP-3, T4 admin CP-5 + the running-image INV-T6 re-check at CP-6). **CP-7 consolidated gate — ALL GREEN together (2026-06-20):** chat-service pytest **353**, glossary `go test ./internal/api` admin/action/g7 GREEN, ai-gateway jest **29**, cms-frontend vitest **21** + `tsc`/`vite build` clean. The generalized class-C confirm spine (CP-0/Foundation) underlies every tier's confirm. **Unpushed commits this epic:** `25c16879` CP-0+Foundation · `99db6907` T1 book · `72175a27` T2+T3 · `3e91ae93` T4 admin (glossary+gateway) · `e62920a9` T4c chat surface · `aee24267` T4d cms panel. **Deferred (both LOW, tracked not skipped):** `D-CP6-ADMIN-E2E` — full browser cms→System-write smoke (needs chat-service rebuilt with T4c + an admin user-model + Playwright; both halves unit-proven, gateway→glossary federation CP-5-proven). `D-T4-ADMIN-PREVIEW-CURRENTSTATE` — admin `/actions/admin/preview` echoes token params vs re-rendering current DB state; resolve-or-accept when the T4d card's preview UX is revisited.

> **▶ NEXT SESSION — branch `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage`. Active work: the GENRE·KIND·ATTRIBUTE RE-ARCHITECTURE (supersedes the old SS-5→SS-7 plan). SS-4 (system_kinds rename + user_kinds CRUD + T1 lock) SHIPPED earlier this session. Then the epic was re-architected: the lost-since-March primary model recovered — genre is a first-class TIERED level (system/user/book) alongside kind; attributes keyed by (kind × genre × code); System/User = standards (templates, no entities), a Book = a sovereign instance scaffolded from standards by copy-down; every reference is a PLAIN per-tier FK (no polymorphism). PROVEN by the G0 spike. Authority spec [`…genre-kind-attribute-tiering.md`](../specs/2026-06-19-genre-kind-attribute-tiering.md); build plan [`…genre-kind-attribute-build.md`](../plans/2026-06-19-genre-kind-attribute-build.md) (G1–G6, all R1–R6 + O1–O4 ratified). Running as a long semi-attended /loom; commit gates approved interactively (R5).**
> - **🔨 G1 — schema rebuild + seed BUILT (this session, additive).** `migrate.go` `UpGenreKindAttr` (12 new tables: `system/user/book_genres`, `system/user/book_kind_genres`, `system/user/book_attributes`, `book_kinds`, `book_active_genres`, `entity_genres` — plain per-tier FKs, gen_random_uuid PKs, `content_hash`/`source_ref`/`deprecated_at` for Sync+boundary-independence) + `SeedGenreKindAttr` (derives system standards FROM the seeded system_kinds: O3 genre vocab incl universal/xianxia/mystery, O4 universal-linkage on every kind, attrs lifted to (kind,universal)). Wired in main.go after UpUserKinds. **REFINEMENT vs plan: G1 is ADDITIVE** — legacy `genre_tags[]`/`genre_groups`/`system_kind_attributes` + entity-FK repoint + wiki/KG clear all move to **G4** (so each milestone's suite stays green; FE-broken-window still per R3). VERIFY: `go test ./... -p 1` green (api 21.6s) + new `TestGenreKindAttr` (ephemeral DB) + gofmt/vet clean. /review-impl: no HIGH, 2 cheap fixes applied (vocab-assumption doc, re-Up idempotency assert), 2 MED + 1 LOW deferred (below).**
> - **🔨 G2a+b — genre OpenAPI contract + genre-tier CRUD BUILT (this session, `f2901ec8`).** Authored the FIRST glossary REST OpenAPI `contracts/api/glossary-service/kinds_genres_attributes.yaml` (11 paths/11 schemas: genres + attributes + kind-genres; system read-only, user CRUD, attach-by-code; book/sync extend in G3/G5). Genre tier shipped: `GET /v1/glossary/genres` (merged system+user, tier-tagged) + `/user-genres` CRUD + `/user-genres-trash` recycle bin (`user_genre_handler.go`, routes in server.go), clone-from-system (FK→422, dup→409, delete guarded GLOSS_GENRE_IN_USE). VERIFY: 4 api tests green incl. **TenantIsolation** (B→404 on A's genre, B's list excludes A's) + merged-read system-read-only + clone/dup/422. Distinct from legacy per-book `genre_groups` (retires G4).
> - **🔨 G2c — attributes + kind↔genre links BUILT (this session).** `attribute_def_handler.go`: system attributes READ-ONLY (`GET /system-attributes?kind_id=&genre_id=`) + user attributes CRUD (`/user-attributes`) with **attach-by-code** (kind_id/genre_id must be the caller's live user-tier rows → 422 otherwise; `ownsUserKind`/`ownsUserGenre` predicates; content_hash computed in Go matching the seed's md5 format). `kind_genre_link_handler.go`: `/user-kinds/{id}/genres` list/PUT-set(replace, validates every id owned)/PUT-one/DELETE. VERIFY: 3 api tests green — SystemAttributes read-only+populated, **AttachByCodeAndTenancy** (system kind/genre→422, B→A's kind→422, dup→409, B patch/delete A's attr→404), **LinkTenancy** (PUT set, add/del, B's genre→422, B→A's kind links→404); full `go test ./... -p 1` green (api 21.9s); gofmt clean.
> - **▶ G2 COMPLETE.** **🔨 G3a — book adopt (copy-down) + ontology read BUILT (this session).** `book_adopt_handler.go`: `POST /v1/glossary/books/{book_id}/adopt {genres:[code],kinds:[code]}` copies the picked System genres/kinds (+ their kind↔genre links + attributes) into the book tier — `universal` always adopted+active (O4), `unknown` kind always adopted (E6), source_ref/source_hash recorded; idempotent (per-book `pg_advisory_xact_lock` + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). `GET …/ontology` = book-LOCAL single-tier read (book_* only, no system_*/user_* join — the spike's Moment B). Manage-gated adopt / View-gated read. VERIFY: 2 api tests green — CopyDownAndOntology (universal+unknown present, char 'name' attr book-local, idempotent re-adopt, adopt-more grows set, ontology==adopt) + NonOwnerForbidden (403 on both); full `go test ./... -p 1` green (api 21.5s); gofmt clean.
> - **🔨 G3b — book-tier CRUD BUILT (this session, `D-GKA-BOOK-CRUD`).** `book_ontology_handler.go` + `book_ontology_patch.go`: the Manage workspace's editing surface for a book's adopted/native ontology. `/v1/glossary/books/{id}/ontology/{genres|kinds|attributes}` create/patch/delete + `PUT …/active-genres` (matrix columns) + `PUT …/kinds/{id}/genres` (matrix row); matrix read = the existing `GET …/ontology` `kind_genres`. All writes **Manage-gated** (shared `requireBookManage`/`bookOntologyTarget`); **DELETE = soft `deprecated_at` cascade** (genre→its attrs + active/link/entity_genres rows; kind→its attrs + links — boundary independence + Sync). Book-native rows carry `source_ref=NULL`; attribute create enforces the **book-local FK guard** (kind_id/genre_id must be live rows of THIS book → 422, blocks a cross-book ref the raw FK would accept). Contract extended (`book-ontology` tag, 11 paths incl. retroactive adopt/ontology, 11 schemas, `Forbidden` resp). Generic spec-driven PATCH plumbing (`scanPatchFields`/`applyBookUpdate`, table/col are constants → no SQLi). **VERIFY:** 3 new api tests green — FullLifecycle (create/patch/delete all 3 levels + active replace-set + kind-genres row + cascade-on-delete asserts), NonOwnerForbidden (403 on every write), **EditCollaboratorDeniedManage** (an *edit* grantee reads /ontology=200 but every write=403 → proves the gate is Manage, not mere auth); full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` green (22.8s); gofmt/vet/build/provider-gate clean. Single-service change (glossary + contracts) → no cross-service live-smoke token needed. /review-impl-grade self-review folded a `requireBookManage` extraction (killed 7 `_=userID` + 4 duplicated preambles).
> - **🔨 D-GKA-ADOPT-USER-TIER BUILT (this session, caller-scoped — PO chose (a)).** Adopt now merges **System → the adopting CALLER's User tier**: for each picked code, the caller's user-tier row shadows the System one (user rows inserted FIRST, System with `ON CONFLICT (book_id,code) DO NOTHING` fills the rest). All 3 levels — genre/kind/attribute (+ kind↔genre links unioned). No new contract (owner≈caller under the Manage gate; the person setting up uses their own customizations — arguably more correct than strict owner-scoping). `source_ref='user:<id>'` distinguishes the provenance; user genres/attrs carry their `content_hash`, user kinds get an inline md5 (no content_hash col); `is_hidden=NOT is_active`, `sort_order=0` (user_kinds has no sort_order). VERIFY: new `TestBookAdopt_UserTierShadowsSystem` (xianxia+universal genres + character kind + a 'name' attr all user-customized → user-sourced; romance not customized → system-sourced) + existing adopt/CRUD suites green; full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` 22.9s; gofmt/provider-gate clean.
> - **🔨 G4 — DESTRUCTIVE CUTOVER DONE (this session, user-approved big-bang; built via a 9-agent Workflow + human foundation/verify).** The entity layer now lives on the BOOK tier. **Migration** (`migrate.go`): `UpGlossaryCutoverG4` (TRUNCATE entities CASCADE; repoint `glossary_entities.kind_id`→`book_kinds`, `entity_attribute_values.attr_def_id`→`book_attributes`; rewrite `recalculate_entity_snapshot` to book tier, source `'book'`) + `UpMergeCandidatesG4` (merge_candidates.kind_id→book_kinds) + `UpGlossaryCutoverG4Cache` (restore cached_name/search_vector maintenance on the book-tier snapshot) + `UpGlossaryDropLegacyG4` (DROP `genre_groups`, `system_kind_attributes`, `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`.`genre_tags`). Wired LAST in main.go + the test chains. **Seed rewrite:** `SeedGenreKindAttr` now derives system_genres/kind_genres/attributes from `domain.DefaultKinds` in Go (no longer reads the dropped table/column). **Handlers retargeted to book-local:** extraction (loadKindMap/loadAttrDefMap[universal-genre DISTINCT ON]/createExtractedEntity/mergeExtractedEntity/findEntityByNameOrAlias/getKnownEntities/writeExtractionProfile[genre_groups→book_active_genres]), entity detail, glossary_translate, internalTranslationGlossary, listKinds (→system_kind_genres/system_attributes), and **O2** — `confirmSchema`/`createKindFromParams`/`createAttrDefFromParams` now mint into the **BOOK** tier (token-bound bookID, book-scoped, `errNotAdopted`→422; a user's assistant can no longer mutate the shared catalogue). Legacy per-book `genre_groups` routes/handlers RETIRED (`genres_crud.go`/`genres_handler.go`/`domain/genres.go` deleted). **E3 KG clear:** `reset_glossary_anchors` added to knowledge-service (manual maintenance fn, not auto-run). **Precondition:** a book MUST be adopted before entities can be created in it. **VERIFY (human-run, not trusted from agents):** full `go test ./... -p 1 -count=1` GREEN across api(53s)/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc; `go build`+`go vet` clean; no production request-path reader of the dropped objects remains (grep-verified — all refs are migration DDL/seed/comments/DefaultKinds struct); provider-gate OK; gofmt clean; knowledge entities.py compiles. **Dev-DB cutover NOT run** — it's a deploy step; only the throwaway `loreweave_glossary_test` was migrated.
> - **🔬 /review-impl on G4 (this session) — found + fixed a CRITICAL data-loss bug.** The cutover's `TRUNCATE glossary_entities CASCADE` was UNGUARDED; migrations re-run every service boot (execGuarded has no ledger), so every production restart after cutover would have wiped ALL entities + wiki. Tests never surfaced it (they want a clean slate). FIXED: the TRUNCATE now runs only while the OLD `glossary_entities_kind_id_fkey` (→system_kinds) still exists — i.e. exactly once per DB, pre-cutover. Regression: `TestGlossaryCutoverG4_GuardPreventsRepeatTruncate` (migrate pkg, ephemeral DB: cutover → seed entity → re-run cutover → entity SURVIVES + FK repointed). Other findings accept+documented: revision-restore would break on a pre-cutover snapshot (impossible under full-reset — cutover cascades entity_revisions); extraction on an un-adopted book silently skips all entities (`D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD`, LOW). Writer audit otherwise clean (createEntity was the only miss, fixed in 222786b4).
> - **🔨 G5 — book SYNC (diff/apply) DONE (this session, + D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH resolved).** `book_sync_handler.go`: `GET /v1/glossary/books/{id}/sync/available` (View-gated diff — compares each adopted book row's frozen `source_hash` vs the live source's current content hash: `update_available` when divergent, `source_retired` when the source was deleted/purged; up-to-date rows omitted) + `POST …/sync/apply` (Manage-gated, per-row `keep_mine`|`take_theirs`; `take_theirs` overwrites the SEMANTIC/hash surface [genre→name; kind→name+description; attribute→name+description+field_type+is_required+options] + refreshes `source_hash`; `keep_mine` bumps `source_hash` only; validate-all-before-mutate + same per-book advisory lock as adopt; cross-book id → 0 rows, no leak). **D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH fixed**: `createUserGenre`/`patchUserGenre`/`patchUserAttribute` now (re)compute `content_hash` (was never set on genres / never refreshed on patch → sync was blind to user-standard edits). Contract: `sync` tag + 2 paths + 5 schemas. **VERIFY:** full `go test ./... -p 1` GREEN (api 42s + 5 new G5 tests: diff/apply all-choices [kind+attr take_theirs full-surface, genre keep_mine], source-retired, user-edit-refreshes-hash end-to-end, patchUserAttribute hash, View/Manage grant gates; events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc); gofmt/vet/provider-gate clean; contract YAML valid. **/review-impl: 2 MED coverage gaps fixed in-loop** (kind take_theirs + attr full-surface were unasserted; patchUserAttribute hash untested) + invariant doc at `attrContentHash`. Single-service (glossary + contracts) → no cross-service token needed.
> - **🔬 /review-impl ALSO found + FIXED a pre-existing G4 PROD TIME-BOMB (this session).** The migration chain leaked a `system_kinds` `pg_attribute` slot **per run**: base `Up` did `ALTER TABLE system_kinds ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS genre_tags` (re-add) while G4e `DROP COLUMN genre_tags` removed it — Postgres never reclaims dropped-column slots without a table rewrite, so each service boot leaked one slot toward the 1600-column ceiling (after which the service can't start). It had already exhausted the shared test DB (1590 dropped slots → blocked the events+migrate test packages). **FIX (`migrate.go`):** `genre_tags` FULLY RETIRED from the additive chain — removed from `system_kinds` CREATE + the re-add ALTER + the `unknown`-kind seed + `Seed()` insert, and from `user_kinds` CREATE; G4e's `DROP COLUMN … IF EXISTS` kept as a ONE-TIME legacy cleanup (no longer fights a re-add). `genre_tags` was write-only mid-chain (only `Seed` wrote it; `SeedGenreKindAttr` derives `system_kind_genres` from `DefaultKinds` in Go, never read the column; handler `genre_tags` are output aliases from `system_kind_genres`). **PROVEN:** repeated full-suite migration runs now hold the dropped-slot count steady at 1590 (no growth) and the full suite is green; a FRESH DB from the fixed code carries 0 dead slots.
> - **✅ G6 — FE rebuild COMPLETE (all screens + D2 backend + G6f integration; epic done, this session long /loom run).** XL; plan [`…2026-06-19-g6-frontend-rebuild.md`](../plans/2026-06-19-g6-frontend-rebuild.md). **Committed (all tsc-clean, NOT pushed):** **G6a** (`38166cef`) data layer `tieringApi.ts`+`tieringTypes.ts`; **G6b(1)** (`541d6021`) `useBookOntology`/`useStandards`/`lib/tiering`/`TierChip`/`FieldTypeBadge`; **G6b(2)** Manage workspace (`ManageWorkspace`/`AdoptPicklistModal`/`OntologyColumn`/`AttributeEditorPanel`) + `glossaryTiering` i18n (en); **G6c** Attribute matrix (`MatrixScreen`/`AttributeMatrix`/`KindSelect`/`ActiveGenrePills`/`MatrixCellInspector`); **D2 backend** per-entity genre override (`entity_genres_handler.go` GET/PUT `…/entities/{id}/genres` — universal-auto-included, tenant-guarded, real-PG test `TestEntityGenres_SetGetAndGuards`; contract + FE client) — built FIRST per user request to unblock the entity form; **G6e** Sync (`SyncScreen`/`SyncDiffTable`/`useSync`); **G6d** merged entity form (`TieredEntityForm`/`useEntityForm`/`GenrePillSelector`/`AttributeField` — create→setEntityGenres(D2)→persist values via existing EAV). All under `frontend/src/features/glossary/components/tiering/` + `hooks/`. **✅ G6f DONE (2026-06-20, this session — integration into the LIVE GlossaryTab + /review-impl).** `GlossaryTab` now mounts the 4 tiered screens: new `OntologyShell` (Manage/Matrix/Sync sub-nav + back) under a single `ontology` view replacing the old `kinds`/`genres` views; new `CreateEntityModal` (pick a BOOK kind → `TieredEntityForm`) replacing the old create dropdown. **G4-seam correction (the load-bearing win):** post-G4 `glossary_entities.kind_id`→`book_kinds`, so `createEntity` 422s on a system-kind id → the old dropdown was already broken for adopted books; creation now uses `book_kind_id`. **Retired** `KindEditor.tsx`+`AttrEditorModal.tsx` (orphaned child)+`GenreGroupsPanel.tsx`+`GenreFormModal.tsx`; trimmed dead `glossaryApi.createGenre/patchGenre/deleteGenre` (kept `listGenres` — SettingsTab; kept `useEntityKinds`+`createKind/createKindAlias/reassignEntityKind` — E3 unknown-review still uses them, verified). **Filter/count now read the BOOK ontology** (`useBookOntology`) not system kinds; system kinds kept only for the E3 unknown panel. **i18n** vi/ja/zh-TW `glossaryTiering` authored+registered (parity clean; placeholders audited intact) + 4 G6f-orphaned `books.json` keys removed ×4 locales. **VERIFY:** tsc clean (only pre-existing @dnd-kit); vitest 93/93 in glossary+book-tabs incl. 12 new (`useEntityForm` MED-1 + namespacing, `OntologyShell` tabs, `CreateEntityModal` pick-kind + busy-guard); i18n:check glossaryTiering+books parity clean (only pre-existing `settings.world`/`world`/`knowledge`/`composition` en-only drift remains — UNRELATED to G6, see `D-I18N-PREEXISTING-DRIFT`). **/review-impl: no HIGH/MED; 2 LOW fixed in-loop** — #1 `CreateEntityModal` now blocks Esc/backdrop/✕ during an in-flight create (`TieredEntityForm onBusyChange`, tested); #2 filter+count repointed to book kinds (`D-GKA-FILTER-BOOKKINDS` cleared). **Playwright smoke deferred `D-GKA-G6F-PLAYWRIGHT-SMOKE`** (stack down — only infra-postgres up; recipe: adopt → manage → matrix → entity create multi-genre → sync on test account). LOW accepted: `D-GKA-CREATE-MODAL-ESC-GUARD` superseded by the #1 fix; OntologyShell tab-switch unmounts screen-local selection (conventional, react-query keeps data); no GlossaryTab integration test (heavy query surface, repo norm). **Scope deferrals tracked:** `D-GKA-FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY` (cross-tier "override into my USER tier" clone flow — book workspace is the core), `D-GKA-FE-QUICKCREATE-MODAL` (genre/kind quick-create uses window.prompt for now). **/review-impl on D2+G6d (this session):** fixed MED-1 (`useEntityForm` no longer writes a genre override when the selection == book default → created entities FOLLOW book active-genres instead of freezing a copy; spec §3). Tracked: **`D-GKA-ENTITY-MULTIGENRE-VALUES`** (MED — `createEntity` makes value rows `DISTINCT ON (code)` universal-preferred, so a keep-both conflict like `rank·xianxia`+`rank·romance` only persists ONE value; the merged form's per-genre conflict cells can't both store until the EAV layer is keyed per (kind,genre,code) — backend decision; non-conflict fields work today), **`D-GKA-ENTITY-GENRES-RESET`** (LOW — `PUT …/entities/{id}/genres` always inserts ≥1 row [universal], no way to clear back to book-default; add a DELETE or empty-clears semantics). G6a–G6e + D2 + G6d + review-fix PUSHED this session; **G6f commit NOT yet pushed**. **G4/G5 follow-ups:** `D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD` (LOW — bulkExtract on an un-adopted book skips silently; prefer an explicit "book not scaffolded" 4xx); `D-GKA-G4-LIVE-SMOKE` (cross-service stack-up adopt→extract→translation-glossary on book-local tables — cutover is real-PG-proven on the test DB; full stack live-smoke deferred); `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` (LOW — `Seed()`/schemaSQL still CREATE+populate `system_kind_attributes`+`genre_tags` each startup only for the drop to remove them; idempotent + correct but wasteful — remove from base schema/seed once stable); entity_handler `createEntity` kind-validation against `system_kinds` was retargeted (verify it points at book_kinds — RETARGET-SPEC §4 flagged it). RETARGET-SPEC.md documents the book-tier test pattern (adoptTestBook/bookKindID/bookAttrID).
> - **✅ G-DEBT PARALLEL CLEAR (2026-06-20, this session — fan-out triage + sync/parallel exec).** Ran an 8-agent read-only triage Workflow over the genre·kind·attribute debt (each → build-ready plan + parallel/sequential/gated verdict), then cleared the safe batch: **FE lane (parallel, background agent)** `D-GKA-FE-QUICKCREATE-MODAL` ✅ — `QuickCreateModal` replaces the `window.prompt` genre/kind quick-create in ManageWorkspace (busy-guard, i18n ×4 parity, 5 vitest). **BE lane (sequential, shared test DB)** ✅ — `D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD` (bulkExtract on an un-adopted book now 422 `GLOSS_BOOK_NOT_SCAFFOLDED` instead of silent skip; `TestBulkExtract_UnadoptedBookReturns422`); `D-GKA-ENTITY-GENRES-RESET` (empty `genre_ids` body now clears the override back to book-default; clear-to-default asserted in `TestEntityGenres_SetGetAndGuards`); `D-GKA-SYNC-USER-SOURCE-VISIBILITY` (the user-tier source in sync diff+apply is now scoped to the caller's `owner_user_id` — a collaborator can't see/pull another user's private user-tier value; it reads as `source_retired`; `TestBookSync_UserSourceScopedToOwner`; decision: caller-scoped enforces the already-LOCKED "user-tier = private to owner" rule, not a new product call). **VERIFY:** full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` green (46s) + gofmt/vet clean; FE vitest 5 + tsc clean + glossaryTiering parity clean. **Re-diagnosed `D-GKA-ENTITY-MULTIGENRE-VALUES` (MED) — it is NOT a migration:** the EAV schema is already genre-correct (`UNIQUE(entity_id, attr_def_id)`, and `attr_def_id` encodes genre via its `book_attributes` FK). The collapse is `createEntity`'s value-seeding `SELECT DISTINCT ON (ba.code) … WHERE ba.kind_id=$1` — it keeps ONE attr per code (universal-preferred), so a keep-both conflict gets one value row. Fix = seed one row per (kind,genre,code) [drop the DISTINCT-ON-code collapse] + verify the snapshot read tolerates 2 same-code rows (~50 LOC, careful, NO migration). **Still undone (gated/large, treat as not-done):** the live `loreweave_glossary` DB cutover (G4 ran on test DB only); `D-GKA-G4-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-GKA-G6F-PLAYWRIGHT-SMOKE` (stack down); `D-GKA-FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY` (~850 LOC, 4 sub-milestones — User-tier UI; needs UX placement decision); `D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN` + `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT` (~350 LOC — admin write surface for System standards; blocked on the platform admin-identity epic, can ship behind a placeholder `requireAdmin`); `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP`/`D-GKA-SYSTEM-KINDS-RECLAIM` (migrate.go, optional/deploy-time, marginal — gated). `D-I18N-PREEXISTING-DRIFT` (en-only world/knowledge/composition/books.settings.world keys — unrelated to G, fails `i18n:check`).
> - **✅ DEV-DB CUTOVER DONE + `D-GKA-ENTITY-MULTIGENRE-VALUES` CLEARED (2026-06-20, this session — user confirmed all DB data is disposable test data).** **Cutover:** dropped+recreated `loreweave_glossary` and ran the CURRENT migration chain (built `go build` binary, ran it → `migrate.Up` applies before any broker, then stopped it). Verified live: all 12 tiered tables present, legacy `genre_groups`/`system_kind_attributes` GONE, `glossary_entities.kind_id` FK → `book_kinds`, System standards seeded (7 genres/13 kinds/93 attrs/26 links), **0 dead `system_kinds` column slots** (fresh DB ⇒ `D-GKA-SYSTEM-KINDS-RECLAIM` moot for this DB). Recreate was chosen over in-place cutover precisely because it sidesteps the dead-slot accumulation + the guarded-TRUNCATE. The cross-service Playwright smoke (`D-GKA-G6F-PLAYWRIGHT-SMOKE`) still needs the full stack up. **MULTIGENRE (PO chose "genres-at-create"):** `createEntity` now accepts optional `genre_ids` — it validates the override (universal auto-included, live-book-genre tenant check) BEFORE the tx, then in ONE tx inserts the entity + `entity_genres` override + seeds one value row per (genre,code) of the kind RESTRICTED to the entity's genres (was `SELECT DISTINCT ON (ba.code)` which collapsed keep-both conflicts to one). FE: `glossaryApi.createEntity(…, genreIds?)` + `useEntityForm` collapsed create→setGenres→patch into create-with-genres→patch (dropped the separate `setEntityGenres` round-trip + its import). The snapshot tolerates 2 same-code rows (it `jsonb_agg`s an array). `POST /entities` is uncontracted (glossary entity CRUD predates the OpenAPI), so `genre_ids` is an additive no-contract change. **VERIFY:** full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` green (48s) incl. `TestCreateEntity_MultiGenreKeepBothValues` (2 'rank' rows persist + override written) + all createEntity-default-path tests; gofmt clean; FE `useEntityForm` 5 vitest + tsc clean. **Still undone (gated/infra/large):** `D-GKA-G4-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-GKA-G6F-PLAYWRIGHT-SMOKE` (stack down); `D-GKA-FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY` (~850 LOC, 4 sub-milestones, UX decision); `D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN`+`D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT` (~350 LOC, blocked on admin-identity epic); `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` (migrate.go, optional/deploy-time).
> - **✅ LIVE SMOKES PASSED — `D-GKA-G4-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-GKA-G6F-PLAYWRIGHT-SMOKE` BOTH CLEARED (2026-06-20, this session; full stack brought up against the cutover dev DB).** Rebuilt glossary+frontend images (this session's code), brought up the backend set then the whole gateway+frontend chain (15 services healthy; provider-registry needed a 2nd `up` after a rabbitmq dial race — known startup ordering, not a defect). **G4 backend smoke (`infra/g4-live-smoke.ps1`, real auth→book→glossary cross-service):** register+login (auth) → create book (book-service) → adopt `{genres:[fantasy],kinds:[character]}` (glossary resolves the OWNER grant via book-service `ResolveAccess` — the cross-service seam) → GET ontology = **2 genres / 2 kinds / 13 attributes** copied from System standards → create entity with `genre_ids:[fantasy]` (MULTIGENRE genres-at-create) → entity_genres persisted `{fantasy,universal}`, `uses_book_default=false` → **a 2nd user GETs the ontology → 403** (tenant boundary enforced). Confirmed adopt is PICK-based (empty body → only universal+unknown; not a bug). **G6F UI smoke (Playwright, test account through the gateway on :5174):** Glossary tab renders the G6f shell (Ontology + New Entity buttons); a pre-cutover book correctly reads UNADOPTED → "Adopt standards…" → AdoptPicklistModal shows **7 system genres + 13 system kinds** (matches DB) → pick fantasy+character → Adopt → ManageWorkspace 3-column (Genres/Kinds/Attributes) renders → Entities tab header "0 entities · 1 kind" (reads book ontology, unknown hidden) → New Entity → CreateEntityModal kind-picker → TieredEntityForm (Universal section, 13 char×universal attrs + droppable Fantasy chip) → fill name → Create → "Aria Livesmoke / 👤 Character / Draft" in the list. **DB-verified:** the created entity has **13 attribute value rows** (one per char×universal attr, seeded via book_active_genres) + **0 override genres** (uses_book_default, selection==book default). Proves the cutover DB + tiered model + cross-service grants + G4 create-seam fix + MULTIGENRE all work end-to-end live through the gateway. **Still undone (gated/large only):** `D-GKA-FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY` (~850 LOC, UX decision); `D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN`+`D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT` (~350 LOC, admin-identity epic); `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` (migrate.go, perf-when-pain).
> - **✅ FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY DONE + LIVE-SMOKED (`D-GKA-FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY`, 2026-06-20, /loom L, long run; commits `73998267` M1, `2b5601ef` M2, `62a9c68a` M3, + M4).** The per-user User-tier standards UI. **PO chose: standalone `/standards` route + top-level sidebar entry** (tier-correct — User-tier standards are per-user like BYOK, not per-book). Plan [`…2026-06-20-fe-standards-library.md`](../plans/2026-06-20-fe-standards-library.md). New `frontend/src/features/standards/` (MVC; reuses glossary `tieringApi`/`tieringTypes`/`TierChip`/`FieldTypeBadge`). **M1** route+sidebar+`StandardsShell`(Genres/Kinds/Attributes)+`useUserStandards` (merged System+User, **User shadows System by code** → a cloned code = one editable USER row; `cloneGenre`/`cloneKind` via `clone_from_*`, shares useStandards keys so a clone refreshes the adopt pick-list). **M2** AttributesPanel (user-kind×user-genre picker → user attrs + system parents' attrs read-only + create attach-by-code via `AttributeFormModal`) + kind↔genre link editing (`KindGenresModal`/`useKindGenres`, replace-set). **M3** create-from-scratch + edit (`StandardFormModal`) + soft-delete for genres/kinds/attrs + recycle bin (`TrashDrawer`/`useStandardsTrash` restore/purge). `tieringApi` += `createUserKind`/`patch`/`delete` + 6 recycle-bin methods (no new contract — all G2 routes). `standards` i18n namespace ×4 + `nav.standards` ×4 (full parity). **VERIFY:** vitest **20/20** standards (merge-shadow, clone ids, attr form value/options parsing, link seed+save, std-form genre-vs-kind, trash list/restore/purge); full suite **2007 passed**, the 18 failures are pre-existing `D-I18N-PREEXISTING-DRIFT` (composition/world parity) + `@dnd-kit/core` missing-dep composition suites — untouched namespaces, **0 regressions**; tsc clean (11 pre-existing composition only); i18n standards+common parity clean. **LIVE click-through (rebuilt FE image, gateway :5174, test account):** `/standards` renders 7 SYS genres + 13 SYS kinds; clone Fantasy genre + Character kind → both flip **SYS→USER** with Edit/Delete; Attributes tab → pick Character×Fantasy → create "Bloodline" (code auto-derived `bloodline`, USER, attach-by-code through the gateway) → delete → gone. Proves the whole surface end-to-end. **System-tier writes stay out** (`D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN`, blocked on admin-identity).
> - **✅ SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN + ADMIN CMS DONE + LIVE-SMOKED (`D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN` + `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`, 2026-06-20; commits `3d1763e3` Lane A, `f90cb1fc` Lane B+C; pushed).** The admin write surface for the platform System tier + a separate thin CMS to drive it. Built fan-out (BE myself for the auth boundary; 2 background agents scaffolded+wired the CMS). **Lane A — glossary admin API (`3d1763e3`):** `requireAdminScope` verifies an RS256 admin JWT via shared `contracts/adminjwt` (`ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`, base64-or-PEM), scope `admin:write`; POST/PATCH/DELETE for `system-genres`/`system-kinds`/`system-attributes-admin` with `content_hash` recompute (clears `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`); `universal`/`unknown` guarded un-deletable. A valid HS256 USER token is rejected (RS256-only) → a regular user can never mutate System tier. Unset key → 503 fail-closed. Test: `TestSystemTierAdmin_*` (CRUD, 401 user-token, 403 scope, 503 unconfigured, hash recompute). **Lane B — auth admin-session exchange (`f90cb1fc`):** `POST /v1/admin/session` — a logged-in user who is an ACTIVE `admin_principal` SELF-mints an RS256 admin JWT (subject from their own token, authority only from the DB row; non-admin 403, disabled 404, rate-limited, audited). New `authjwt.LocalKeySigner` = production-safe in-process RSA signer for DEV/self-hosted (no KMS); `main.go` selects KMS→local→disabled; `AdminIssuanceEnabled` now true for either path. Gateway proxies `/v1/admin`→auth. Tests `TestAdminSession_*`. **Lane C — `cms-frontend/` (NEW, separate from `frontend/`):** Vite+React+TS+Tailwind thin admin UI on :5175→gateway `/v1`; login does the admin-session exchange + stores the admin token; Genres/Kinds/Attributes CRUD panels wired to the admin endpoints (13 vitest; tsc+build green). **infra:** compose passes admin keys through (disabled by default→fail-closed) + a `cms-frontend` service; `gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` mints a dev keypair; auth+glossary Dockerfiles now `COPY contracts/adminjwt`. **LIVE-SMOKED** (`admin-cms-live-smoke.ps1`, full stack, dev local-RSA — auth signs + glossary verifies, **kid matched** `bd90034e…`): login → `/v1/admin/session` (role=admin) → System genre CREATE/PATCH/DELETE through the gateway; plain USER token → 401; non-admin → 403. **/review-impl (auth boundary, no HIGH/MED):** user-token bypass impossible (RS256 vs HS256, proven), no self-escalation (authority from `admin_principals` only), scope-gated, key-identity-pinned (kid/iss/aud/exp), fail-closed everywhere. LOW/accept: adminSession doesn't check session-revocation (consistent with every access-token endpoint), break_glass not distinguished (pre-existing R3), admin token in CMS localStorage (15m TTL, re-exchanged on 401). **For a persistent dev CMS:** run `infra/gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` once, export the two vars, `docker compose up -d --build auth-service glossary-service api-gateway-bff cms-frontend`, then seed an `admin_principals` row for your user. **Remaining GKA debt:** `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` — now CLEARED by the migration-ledger bullet below.
> - **▶ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE — Glossary Assistant: Tiered MCP Tool Expansion (specced + planned + ambiguity-resolved; NOT built).** The agent's MCP surface only covers the pre-tiering world (entities + flat schema); the tiered model (System/User/Book ontology, adopt, sync, user standards) + admin System tier are HTTP-only. ~16 new tools across 5 phases. **Three design artifacts are DONE this session** (all committed, see below): the **spec** [`…tiered-tools.md`](../specs/2026-06-20-glossary-assistant-tiered-tools.md) (§11 edge-case register from a 13-case stress-test; §5.1 confirm-token contract; §12 resolves 8 deep-dive ambiguities against real code) + the **parallel build plan** [`…tiered-tools-buildplan.md`](../plans/2026-06-20-glossary-assistant-tiered-tools-buildplan.md) (DAG with compose points CP-0..CP-7 = sync barriers, async streams between them). **▶ FIRST ACTION next session: CP-0 (confirm contract, ~1 design pass) → FOUNDATION (`/loom`, human-in-loop, `/review-impl` on F1).** Foundation = F1 generalized confirm-token machinery (glossary `action_confirm.go` + `consumed_tokens` as a NEW ledger chain entry `0030_*` — go through RunChain!) ∥ F2 shared helpers (code→id resolvers, base-version 409, read-tool rename `list_kinds`→`list_system_standards` + new `book_ontology_read`) ∥ F3 reusable FE confirm-card. Then T1 book → CP-2 → T2 sync ∥ T3 user → CP-3 → CP-4 admin contract → T4a glossary/admin ∥ T4b gateway/admin → T4c chat AdminContext → T4d cms chat panel (long pole, own mini-design) → T5. **Locked decisions:** admin MCP is a PHYSICALLY SEPARATE `/mcp/admin` endpoint, transport-gated by RS256 before `tools/list`, with the gateway exposing a separate `/mcp/admin` downstream too (INV-T6 end-to-end — gateway federates by tool-name into one catalog, so admin names would leak otherwise); admin authority = RS256 `X-Admin-Token` never `X-User-Id`; every System write human-confirm-gated; confirm-tokens single-use (`consumed_tokens`) + re-validated at confirm; patch tools carry a base version (content_hash / updated_at) 409-on-drift; adopt = class C; 3 verbs × `level` arg; per-tier tools register in their OWN Go file (`RegisterXTools`) to enable parallel streams. Two phase-level opens (not blockers): T4d CMS chat-panel mini-design (port main-FE SSE infra), `admin:read` scope (lean reuse `admin:write`). **Unpushed commits this session:** `acce1820` (spec+handoff), `2831ee20` (ambiguity resolutions), `023ad55e` (build plan) — on top of pushed `eb8536e1` (migration ledger).
> - **✅ MIGRATION LEDGER DONE — `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` CLEARED (2026-06-20, this session; L; plan [`…migration-ledger.md`](../plans/2026-06-20-glossary-migration-ledger.md); NOT pushed).** Root-fixed the create-then-drop churn properly: the glossary chain was LEDGER-FREE (every idempotent step replayed each boot — most visibly CREATE+seed→DROP of `system_kind_attributes` per boot; harmless recycle but pure churn, and entangled with the extraction_audit_log FK + the pre-cutover snapshot fn's JOINs so it couldn't be removed in isolation). New `internal/migrate/ledger.go` (`schema_migrations` table + `EnsureLedger`/`ApplyOnce`/`RunChain` over an ordered 29-step `chain` `0001_schema…0029_glossary_drop_legacy_g4`) makes each step run **exactly once**; `main.go`'s ~155-line unconditional sequence collapses to one `migrate.RunChain`. **Purely additive — ZERO migration SQL changed**; every step keeps its own internal guard (the cutover's FK-existence TRUNCATE guard intact as defense-in-depth independent of the ledger). The 2 async background backfills stay OUT of the chain (non-blocking, self-limiting). **Behavior change (documented loudly in code):** seeds are now ledgered → new `DefaultKinds` seed data needs a NEW chain entry, not auto-pickup on deploy. **VERIFY:** `go build`/`vet`/`gofmt` clean; **6 migrate tests** green (3 new: fresh→no-churn, idempotent×3, apply-once incl. failed-step-NOT-recorded; + the new **`TestRunChain_TransitionPreservesData`**; + 2 pre-existing rename/seed); api test pkg compiles. **LIVE transition smoke on the REAL un-ledgered dev DB** (`loreweave_glossary` — already cut over, no ledger): clean boot → **29 ledger rows**, `system_kind_attributes` dropped, **2 entities / 13 system_kinds / 10 book_kinds PRESERVED** (cutover TRUNCATE did not re-fire); 2nd restart idempotent + healthy + ledger stable. **/review-impl: 1 MED + 1 LOW FIXED in-loop** — MED: `EnsureLedger`'s bare `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` ran OUTSIDE the migration advisory lock (concurrent-startup race → possible `os.Exit(1)` on one replica; Postgres CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS isn't concurrency-safe) → now routed through `execGuarded`; LOW: added the repeatable transition/data-loss regression test (was live-smoke-only). Accept+documented: seed-semantics footgun; no enforcement of append-only chain ordering (standard migration discipline, noted in the `chain` doc). Single-service (glossary only) → no cross-service token needed.
> - **Deferred (G-epic):** `D-GKA-VOCAB-DROP` (MED — seed represents only the canonical genre vocab; exotic runtime genre_tags dropped in the transient G1→G4 window; moot under R2 full-reset, documented in-code). `D-GKA-SYNC-USER-SOURCE-VISIBILITY` (MED — `sync/apply` resolves a `user:<id>` source without scoping to the caller; a Manage-grantee's `take_theirs` pulls the book-owner's CURRENT private user-tier value, incl. post-adopt edits. Narrow [only the adopted row's semantic fields, only via explicit pull, owner did seed it into the shared book] + arguably the intended provenance-tracking semantic; accept unless a stricter model is wanted). `D-GKA-SYSTEM-KINDS-RECLAIM` (LOW ops — the leak is FIXED [no growth] but existing long-lived DBs [the shared test DB; any dev/prod migrated before the fix] still carry the accumulated dead `system_kinds` slots; they're stable + under-ceiling now, but a future migration ADDing a `system_kinds` column would hit the cap → reclaim via a one-time table rewrite [pg_repack or recreate] when convenient. Fresh DBs are clean). `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT` (LOW drift — a FUTURE system-tier admin-edit handler MUST recompute `content_hash` or Sync goes blind to system-standard edits; invariant documented at `attrContentHash`; no such handler exists today). `D-GKA-G4-CONSUMER-RETARGET` (the drops — all G4, DONE). **Recently cleared:** `D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH` ✅ (G5 this session — user create/patch now recompute content_hash; covered by tests); `D-GKA-BOOK-CRUD` ✅ (G3b); `D-GKA-ADOPT-USER-TIER` ✅ (caller-scoped); `D-GKA-USERBOOK-COVERAGE` ✅. Bulk-merge `createKindAlias` retarget + per-book KG `relation` level still deferred from SS-4. **G4 follow-ups unchanged:** `D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD` (LOW), `D-GKA-G4-LIVE-SMOKE` (cross-service stack-up), `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` ✅ CLEARED (migration ledger — see the bullet above; the chain now runs each step once, so `system_kind_attributes` is no longer recreated/dropped each boot).
> - **🔨 SS-4 RENAME SCOPE VERIFIED (2026-06-19, PO chose physical rename via regex+test):** `entity_kinds`→`system_kinds` + `attribute_definitions`→`system_kind_attributes` is **glossary-service-ONLY** (~151 + 86 Go refs + migrate.go SQL + tests). knowledge-service/worker-ai `entity_kinds` hits are the variable `known_entity_kinds`/comments — NOT the table, do NOT touch. Regex guards: `entity_kind_aliases` (distinct table, 8 refs — the trailing `s` in `entity_kinds` keeps them distinct) + `known_entity_kinds` (glossary may also use this var — word-boundary the regex). Migration = idempotent `ALTER TABLE IF EXISTS entity_kinds RENAME TO system_kinds` (+ attr) AND switch the `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` to the new names (fresh DBs). Approach: ALTER-rename in migrate.go → regex sweep glossary Go → `go build ./...` + full `go test` → then the rest of SS-4 (user_kinds CRUD + UserKindsPage + orphan migration + remove user-facing kind write routes). FK column `glossary_entities.kind_id` stays named `kind_id` (polymorphic rename is SS-7).
> - **🔨 GLOSSARY KIND-TIERING EPIC — KICKED OFF (2026-06-19).** Root cause (now LOCKED in CLAUDE.md "User Boundaries & Tenancy"): `entity_kinds` is global + user-mutable → one user's kind edit hits everyone (self-hosted≠single-user misconception). Target: system(T1, admin-only) → per-user(T2 `user_kinds`, SS-4) → per-book(T3 `book_kinds`, SS-5), merged-resolution, polymorphic kind-ref (SS-7). Designs exist (docs 93-96, Approved); SS-1/2/3 foundations shipped; **SS-4/5/6/7 unbuilt**. Live-smoke → `D-GRANT-INSTANT-REVOKE-LIVE-SMOKE` (stack down). **✅ M3 `D-RAWSEARCH-CANON-WIRING` DONE (2026-06-18, /loom + /review-impl, plan [`…rawsearch-canon-wiring.md`](../plans/2026-06-18-rawsearch-canon-wiring.md)).** Grounded finding: production indexed canon ONLY (sole caller = `chapter.published` → `ingest_chapter_passages(revision_id=pinned)`); `:Passage` had no canon flag → `surface="canon"` was a hardcode that was *accurate*. PO chose on-demand owner-only draft indexing + owner-only draft visibility. **No migration** — added `Passage.canon: bool = True` (null=canon via `coalesce(node.canon, true)`), stamped in the upsert cypher; `ingest_chapter_passages(canon=)` (published=True, draft path=False). `surface` (`canon|all`) threads to BOTH legs — semantic `find_passages_by_vector(include_drafts=)` + lexical `surface=` (also fixed a latent bug: the lexical leg defaulted to `draft` while semantic was canon, so a "default" search mixed surfaces). `passage_to_hit` labels surface from the flag. New `POST /v1/knowledge/books/{id}/index-drafts` — owner-only (grant `OWNER`; NONE→404, lesser→403), paginated `editorial_status=draft` enumeration (never clobbers canon; publish re-ingest auto-flips), `canon=False`. raw_search `surface` param + owner downgrade (non-owner `all`→`canon` via `caller==project.user_id`). `book_client.list_chapters` (paginates past book-service's 100-cap — no silent truncation) + lexical `surface`. FE: owner-only Canon/Incl.-drafts toggle + "Index drafts" button (`useIndexDrafts` ownership-gated) in RawSearchPanel + 4 locales (subtitle now "published"). Gateway prefix-proxies `/v1/knowledge` (no spec change); no knowledge OpenAPI domain. **VERIFY:** knowledge M3 suites 98 (incl. NEW `test_passages_repo_canon.py` infra-free cypher-wiring guards + integration `test_find_passages_by_vector_canon_filter`); FE raw-search 33 + tsc 0; provider-gate OK. Live infra unavailable → `D-RAWSEARCH-CANON-WIRING-LIVE-SMOKE` (recipe: own a book w/ ≥1 draft + ≥1 published chapter, index-drafts → `surface=all` returns the draft, `surface=canon` doesn't; collaborator `all` stays canon). **/review-impl: no HIGH; MED test-gap FIXED in-loop** (the canon cypher had no locally-runnable test → added unit wiring guards + integration round-trip). **Deferred:** `D-RAWSEARCH-CANON-PUBLISHED-DRAFT-EDITS` (LOW — a published chapter's newer unpublished draft edits aren't draft-indexed; `editorial_status='published'` so not enumerated — deliberate no-clobber choice) + `D-RAWSEARCH-INDEX-DRAFTS-PUBLISH-RACE` (LOW — a chapter published between enumerate & ingest could briefly downgrade canon→draft; owner-only, self-heals next publish). **🔨 B8 XL COMPLETE — all milestones (M1 active-job query, M2a grant-SDK extract, M2b instant-revoke, M3 canon-wiring) shipped.**
> - **🔬 LIVE-SMOKE (2026-06-18, stack up, images rebuilt knowledge/book/glossary/worker-infra):**

Publish → async canon passage ingest; `POST index-drafts` (indexed=1) → draft passage. Neo4j held exactly **1 canon=TRUE + 1 canon=FALSE** passage. Retrieval through the gateway: **draft-term + `surface=canon` → 0 hits** (draft excluded by `coalesce(canon,true)`); **draft-term + `surface=all` → 1 hit `surface="draft"` rel=0.989** (the differentiation). Earlier wiring checks also hold (route/param/422/owner-gate/404/409). Cleanup: DELETE project → Neo4j passages 0 (re-proves `D-KNOWLEDGE-PROJECT-DELETE-NEO4J-ORPHAN` purge) + DELETE book.

Closes the B4a "needs a graph'd project with a rerank model" blocker.

The cache-eviction leaf (needs a pre-cached grant) is the only unproven step; the new cross-service wiring is proven. **FOUND + FIXED a real LOW in-smoke:** the new Python `start_revoke_consumer._revoke_loop` treated the redis-py-8 idle BLOCK timeout (`redis.exceptions.TimeoutError`, raised on a quiet stream) as a generic error → logged a WARNING+traceback every 5s (log spam). Fixed to catch it as the normal "no new events" signal + re-block silently ([loreweave_grants/__init__.py] `except RedisTimeoutError: continue`, the D-REDIS8-CONSUMERS pattern the Go consumer already followed). Re-smoked: knowledge-service up 25s idle → **0 "read failed" lines** (was ~4-5). Deferred `D-GRANT-INSTANT-REVOKE-CACHE-EVICT-LIVE-SMOKE` (needs a pre-cached grant to observe the actual eviction).

Confirms a REAL priced cost flows the identical path; relay/consumer are cost-source-agnostic. Fully cleaned up (campaigns + usage_seen + usage_outbox + llm_jobs/job_event_outbox deleted, 0 residue). **Finding (LOW):** registered OpenAI chat models mostly have empty `pricing {}` (gpt-4o-mini + one gpt-4o) → submit 402s `LLM_QUOTA_EXCEEDED model pricing not configured`; only the `019d5e3c` gpt-4o is priced. VERIFY: learning 186 passed; compose valid; provider-gate OK. **/review-impl: 1 MED fixed in-loop** (the rollback invariant was untested + the fake tx was a no-op → faithful snapshot/restore + a fault-injection rollback test); raising-`_finalize` verified tolerated by both callers (sweep try/except + BaseTerminalConsumer bounded-retry + sweeper backstop). Live-smoke → `D-S5BEVAL-LEARNING-OUTBOX-LIVE-SMOKE` (stack down: infra-postgres exited; recipe = bring up infra, rebuild worker-infra+learning, run a campaign w/ an eval judge → outbox row → relay → `campaign_chapters.eval_fidelity_score`). LOW deferred: `D-S5BEVAL-OUTBOX-PERSISTENT-FAIL-NO-CAP` (a permanently-failing insert sweeper-loops, no attempt cap — loud > the old silent drop).

Fixed (glossary-service, NO migration): `sweepRecipeDrift`/`sweepKgDrift` now suppress re-insert when a `dismissed` row matches the **drift-signature** (recipe = stored from-version `source_id`, re-surfaces on regen; kg folds `current_hash`, re-surfaces on a new hash), and the `is_knowledge_stale` badge is guarded in both spots. Nothing to build. **B4a = 7 of ~17 smokes evidenced.** Harvested from completed campaigns: S1 dispatch, claim (transitive), **S4a threading** (`llm_jobs.job_meta->>'campaign_id'` on 386/26/… child jobs). Fresh runs: **S3b backoff** (5s re-dispatch cadence) + **cancel + cancel-prop** — the headline: a **K17.9 benchmark fixture** (passing row; gotcha: TRIM the `embedding_model` or the gate's exact-match silently fails) let the campaign dispatch a REAL running extraction job (campaign_id stamped on `extraction_jobs` too), then cancelling propagated to the running child → extraction job `running`→`cancelled` (`job.cancelled` outbox emit) + campaign terminal `cancelled`; cleaned up (0 Neo4j nodes — cancelled at items_processed=0). **relay/consumer NOT campaign-evidenced** (all spend local-$0 = the default; needs a priced run). **+S5a estimate** ✅ (`POST /v1/campaigns/estimate`: 5 stages priced `is_local`/lm_studio, real tokens — resolves est_usd-NULL = not a gap, campaigns just launched without the wizard band). **B4a = 11/17 evidenced** (2026-06-18 added **S2 idempotency + S3a governor + breaker-pause**, proven live by driving the real saga driver — live process + real PG + real Redis bus — against a seeded campaign at `DRIVER_MAX_INFLIGHT_PER_CAMPAIGN=1`: governor held `dispatched=1,pending=4` for 3 ticks while leased/renewing → budget=0; draining the in-flight chapter released exactly 1/tick → moving ceiling; done/in-flight chapters never re-dispatched; XADD'd `knowledge.chapter_failed`+`LLM_CIRCUIT_OPEN` → campaign `running`→`paused` "auto-paused: provider circuit open". The worker's actual circuit-TRIP is the upstream non-deterministic half, out of scope. Fully cleaned up — dispatch-seam method builds no graph). Remaining ~4 (rerank needs a graph, 2 browser, relay/consumer priced run). **✅ PRODUCT BUG FOUND + FIXED: `D-KNOWLEDGE-PROJECT-DELETE-NEO4J-ORPHAN`** (/loom M + test) — `delete_project` deleted only the Postgres row → orphaned the whole Neo4j graph (≥7 orphans observed, 019ec73e=279 nodes). New `neo4j_helpers.purge_project` (project_id-scoped `DETACH DELETE` + drops only this project's summary indexes, NOT the shared dimension ones) wired best-effort into `delete_project` after the owner-gated Postgres delete. Tests: neo4j_helpers 31 + public_projects 87. **✅ LIVE-SMOKED + SWEPT (2026-06-18, image rebuilt + pushed `3dfb2199`):** endpoint smoke (create `general` project → inject 3 nodes + a summary index → `DELETE` → 204; Neo4j 3→0, index dropped, shared indexes survived, PG row 0, log `purged neo4j … 3 nodes, 1 indexes`); then swept the EXISTING orphans — **106 graphs / 2342 nodes** (not ~7; only 11 of 108 graphs had a live PG row) DETACH-DELETE'd by the live-set predicate + the 1 orphan's 3 summary indexes dropped, 2 live graphs + 9 shared indexes intact. Audit: `docs/raid/evidence/neo4j-orphan-sweep/2026-06-18-orphan-audit.txt`. **Net: found a real bug, fixed it, live-proved it, AND reclaimed 2342 orphaned nodes — net debt shrinks.** Fresh throwaway 1-ch campaign run (created→started→cancelled→fully cleaned up, 0 residue): **S3b backoff** (knowledge `benchmark_missing` 409 → saga re-dispatched at exact 5s intervals, attempts=3) + **cancel** (running→cancelling). **Remaining ~7 need a benchmark-FIXTURED extraction run (Neo4j build+cleanup) or are non-deterministic/browser:** cancel-prop-to-a-running-child (this run 409'd pre-job so `knowledge_job_id` was NULL — nothing to propagate; needs the K17.9 fixture), rerank-byok, S2 idempotency, S3a governor, S5a estimate (NULL — confirm not-recorded-vs-gap), breaker-pause (non-deterministic), S5c+S6 (browser). The saga demonstrably completes E2E (multiple `completed` campaigns exist).
> - **✅ `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION` SHIPPED (2026-06-17, /loom L + /review-impl, design [`…composition-retry-design.md`](../specs/2026-06-17-composition-retry-design.md)).** The earlier "bifurcated → not clean" triage was REFINED: retryability is **per-job** (worker-drivable ⟺ reconstructable), not per-kind (composition `kind` = the free-form `operation`, identical for the worker + inline paths). **Design realized with NO migration** via the existing `job_projection.params` JSONB: composition `create` emits `params.retryable = is_worker_drivable(operation, input)` (a job is worker-drivable iff the server stamped a recognized `input.worker_op`); jobs-service `derive_control_caps(status, kind, *, retryable=None)` offers RETRY on failed when `kind ∈ _RETRYABLE_KINDS OR retryable is True`; both read sites (`routers/jobs.py` `_with_caps` + `control_job` gate + `sse.py`) pass the flag. composition `internal_job_control` routes `retry` → `_retry_generation_job_core`: 404-not-owned → 409-unless-failed → 409-not-worker-drivable (re-checked on the row) → 409-worker-disabled → re-submit a NEW pending job (copies `input`, `idempotency_key=None`, **guarded create for BOTH chapter-draft writers `chapter_generate`+`stitch_chapter`**) → enqueue. **/review-impl: 2 MED fixed** — (1) drivability was over-broad via an `operation` fallback (inline stitch / user `operation="generate"` falsely retryable → instant re-fail); tightened to the server-set `worker_op` only + stamped `worker_op` on the stitch (engine.py) + decompose (plan.py) worker inputs; (2) stitch retry bypassed the chapter in-flight guard → now guarded. FE/SDK ZERO change (Retry data-driven off `control_caps`). VERIFY: jobs-service 29 (caps incl. TestClient read-API wiring) · composition 95 (retry/emit/worker/plan/engine). **Retry now complete for translation + extraction + video_gen + composition.** Deferred: `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION-SSE-CAPS` (LOW — a freshly-failed composition job's LIVE SSE frame omits the retry cap since the failed emit carries `params=None`; read API authoritative, refetch/reconnect recovers it) + `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION-LIVE-SMOKE` (needs worker stack + a deterministically-failing composition job). Lore stays the only un-wired retry (`D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-LORE` — sync in-process → async-decouple refactor).
> - **✅ `D-S4C-ACCOUNTBALANCES-DROP` SHIPPED (2026-06-17, DB migration).** Dropped the inert token-quota wallet (retired at S4c; USD `spend_guardrails`/`platform_balances` are the live enforcement). usage-billing `migrate.go`: removed the `account_balances` CREATE + appended idempotent `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` (rollback = restore CREATE → empty table; no data to lose). Removed the dead `GET /v1/model-billing/account-balance` handler + route (decision: remove, no FE/gateway consumer). Rewrote the idempotency test to assert on `usage_logs` request_id UNIQUE (NoDoubleDeduct→NoDoubleWrite). VERIFY: build+vet clean; **real-PG test PASS** on a throwaway DB (migrate.Up clean w/ table removed; DROP removes an existing one; /record idempotent = 1 audit row). Dev DB drops on next usage-billing restart. **Money-path migration — /review-impl recommended.**

VERIFY: jobs-service caps 16 + video-gen 14 (+5 retry); provider-gate clean. **Retry done for translation + extraction + video_gen.** Composition RE-SCOPED 2026-06-17 → NOT clean (bifurcated: auto/worker-op jobs store full params in `input`, but cowrite/streamed SSE handlers pack the prompt live + LLM inline → the row lacks the packed prompt; retry differs per operation/mode → fresh-context design task, deferred). FE `JobsFilters.KINDS` += `wiki_gen` + i18n×4. **Systemic fix (rode this slice):** `glossary_extraction` + `wiki_gen` are SECONDARY kinds whose service control endpoints (translation/knowledge) handle only the PRIMARY table → unified Cancel/Resume would 404 → gated **view-only** in jobs-service `derive_control_caps` (`_VIEW_ONLY_KINDS`); users control via native panels; unified control wiring tracked `D-JOBS-SECONDARY-KIND-CONTROL`. **/review-impl: no HIGH/MED**; all 4 LOWs fixed (terminal status-guards on complete/fail/pause so they emit only on a real transition; reconcile merge sorts by datetime not ISO-string; cap-truncation test added; starvation made an explicit soft-bound doc).
