(G) **router 5-step flow** — (1) self-merge guard NEW; (2) `get_entity` source capture pre-merge (replaces ADR §5.4's "merge_entities Cypher returns aliases" — cleaner separation: Postgres I/O stays out of Neo4j repo); (3) `_C17_COLLISION_PRECHECK_CYPHER` UNWIND aliases + match other live entities in same scope+kind, 409 alias_collision on hit; (4) `merge_entities` surgery (existing); (5) post-merge alias-map writes for source.aliases ∪ source.canonical_name (best-effort try/except per HIGH-2 fix) + repoint_target chain re-point (best-effort) + new `aliases_redirected` count in response. (H) **NEW `app/db/migrations/backfill_entity_alias_map.py`** — one-shot CLI walking every non-archived `:Entity`, writing alias-map rows for each alias != canonical_name. Cross-tenant scan (operator-triggered, documented), idempotent ON CONFLICT, resumable on interruption. Skips empty-canonical-alias defensively. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved before BUILD)**: (1) Postgres table (vs Neo4j edge); (2) mirror-scope key matching `entity_canonical_id` shape; (3) error-on-conflict (refuse merge if source alias names a third live entity); (4) ship one-shot backfill (vs lazy-only). Cycle scope: DESIGN+BUILD bundle (5b) — single cycle ships ADR + KSA amendment + code. **Closes D-K19d-γb-03 FULL.** **Files: 15 code + 3 docs = 18 total.** Code: migrate.py + NEW entity_alias_map.py repo + NEW merge_entity_at_id helper + entity_resolver.py + 2 writers + router + deps.py + NEW backfill helper + 5 test files (2 NEW + 3 MOD). Docs: NEW ADR + KSA amendment + SESSION_PATCH + plan. **Verify.** pytest full knowledge-service **1557/1557** (+34 from C16 baseline 1523: 13 repo + 5 DDL + 5 resolver + 5 backfill + 6 router (3 + 3 review-impl regression locks)). Imports clean; AliasReason Literal exposes ('merge', 'backfill') matching DDL CHECK; KSA §5.0 amended with "Alias-redirect on merge" subsection. **Plan progress**: 35 items / 22 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 DONE (12/12)** · **P4 DONE (C14a + C14b)** · **P5 2/3 DONE (C16 ✅ + C17 ✅)** · remaining: C18 DESIGN-first · C15 trigger-gated · user-gated × 2.
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 47 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C16-BUILD BE [XL]; ships Option B from ADR — closes D-K20α-01 budget-integration half FULL [HEAD b668017] — Forty-seventh cycle. **First P5 BUILD cycle.** Plan-completion mindset advances P5 from DESIGN-only into shipped code. **/review-impl invoked + caught 1 HIGH (router call sites bypassed gate) + 5 lower-severity (4 accepted by ADR/precedent + 1 deferred as C16-DEF-01); HIGH fixed in-cycle:** (HIGH-1) public regen endpoints (`/v1/knowledge/me/summary/regenerate` + `/v1/knowledge/projects/{id}/summary/regenerate`) AND `internal/summarize` did NOT pass `summary_spending_repo` to the regen helpers — feature was bypassed by every manual regen path (FE button, worker-ai). Only the K20.3 scheduler enforced the cap. Fixed: NEW `get_summary_spending_repo` factory in `app/deps.py` + 3 router endpoints add `Depends(get_summary_spending_repo)` + helper kwarg threading + 3 regression-lock tests asserting kwarg propagation + 2 fixture updates so 25 cascading-broken tests stub the new dep. (LOW-1 deferred as C16-DEF-01) `budget.py` error reason wording "across all projects" stale after summary_spent fold-in (cosmetic, user-facing for own data). (4 accepted) Prom counter / DB recorder divergence (ADR §5.4 alert path); pre-check race window (K16.11 precedent, "best-effort"); `assert scope_id is not None` strippable with `python -O` (defensive, helpers enforce upstream); pre-check `estimated_cost=0` allows one-regen over-cap (matches K16.11 semantic). **Six substantive blocks.** (A) **migrate.py DDL.** NEW `knowledge_summary_spending` table at tail of DDL: `(user_id UUID NOT NULL, scope_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (scope_type IN ('global')), month_key TEXT NOT NULL, spent_usd NUMERIC(10,4) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` + composite PK `(user_id, scope_type, month_key)` + `idx_summary_spending_user_month`. CHECK restricted to 'global' (closed Literal mirror); future scope expansion requires coordinated migration of CHECK + Pydantic Literal in one PR. Header comment documents the rationale and the K16.11 vs new-table branching. (B) **NEW `app/db/repositories/summary_spending.py` SummarySpendingRepo.** `__init__(pool)`; `record(user_id, scope_type='global', cost: Decimal)` with cost<=0 no-op + ON CONFLICT atomic UPSERT additive (`spent_usd = spent_usd + $4`) — month rollover via PK shape (no manual reset, mirrors sweeper_state). `current_month_total(user_id) -> Decimal` SUM with COALESCE(...,0). `_current_month_key()` duplicated from budget.py (3-line, cheaper than circular import). 8 unit tests cover zero/negative no-op, first insert, additive UPSERT, month rollover, zero-when-no-rows, per-user isolation, prior-month exclusion. (C) **`app/jobs/budget.py` extension.** `check_user_monthly_budget` adds second SUM query against new table; `spent = project_spent + summary_spent`. Same BudgetCheck dataclass shape — no breaking change to ~20 callers. 4 budget tests extended `_mock_user_pool` with 3-tuple fetchrow side_effect (third response = summary_totals=Decimal('0') default). (D) **`app/jobs/regenerate_summaries.py` wire-in (4 touchpoints).** Step 3.5 NEW: pre-check after empty-source guard, before LLM call — `check_user_monthly_budget(pool, user_id, estimated_cost=Decimal('0'))`; on breach → `RegenerationResult(status='no_op_budget_exceeded')`. Step 8 post-success recorder branching: `scope_type='global' → ctx.summary_spending_repo.record(user_id, 'global', cost_usd)`; `scope_type='project' → record_spending(pool, user_id, project_id, cost_usd)` (existing K16.11 path). Both gated on `ctx.summary_spending_repo is not None` for DI consistency (~20 test sites unchanged). `RegenerationStatus` Literal extended with `'no_op_budget_exceeded'`. 3 NEW integration tests in `test_regenerate_summaries.py` lock pre-check short-circuit (provider not awaited), global-branch recorder kwargs, project-branch K16.11 dispatch. (E) **`app/jobs/summary_regen_scheduler.py` 4-fn wire-through.** `sweep_projects_once`, `sweep_global_once`, `run_project_regen_loop`, `run_global_regen_loop` all gain optional `summary_spending_repo: SummarySpendingRepo \| None = None` kwarg + thread it to regen helpers. 3 NEW scheduler integration tests lock kwarg forwarding (project + global) + None default propagation. (F) **3-router-site wire-up + DI factory.** `get_summary_spending_repo` factory in `app/deps.py`. `routers/public/summaries.py` (×2 endpoints) + `routers/internal_summarize.py` (×1 endpoint) all add `Depends(get_summary_spending_repo)` + thread `summary_spending_repo=...` to helper. `app/main.py` lifespan instantiates single `_summary_spending_repo` shared across both K20.3 task creates. 3 NEW regression-lock tests assert public+internal endpoints forward the kwarg using `dependency_overrides` sentinel pattern. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved before BUILD)**: (1) Option B (vs A phantom-project / C unified ledger); (2) greenfield no-migration (project pre-live); (3) closed Literal scope_type with explicit migration story; (4) ADR includes implementation sketch — context-fresh. **Closes D-K20α-01 FULL** (was partial-cleared at K20 β+γ on metric half — now closes the budget-integration half too via cross-table SUM in check_user_monthly_budget). **Files: 13 code + 3 docs = 16 total.** Code: migrate.py + summary_spending.py NEW + budget.py + regenerate_summaries.py + summary_regen_scheduler.py + main.py + deps.py + 2 routers + 5 test files (1 NEW + 4 MOD). Docs: SESSION_PATCH + plan + ADR §7 closing checklist. **Verify.** pytest full knowledge-service **1523/1523** (+22 from C14b baseline 1501: 8 repo + 5 DDL + 3 scheduler-wire + 3 regen-integration + 3 router-wire). Imports clean with full env. RegenerationStatus Literal includes 'no_op_budget_exceeded'. Migration idempotent. **Plan progress**: 35 items / 23 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 DONE (12/12)** · **P4 DONE (C14a ✅ + C14b ✅)** · **P5 1/3 DONE (C16 ✅ DESIGN+BUILD)** · remaining: C15 trigger-gated · C17+C18 DESIGN-first · user-gated × 2.
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 46 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C16 🏗 DESIGN-first [XL]; budget attribution for global-scope regen ADR — closes D-K20α-01 BUILD-blocker — Forty-sixth cycle. **First P5 🏗 DESIGN-first cycle.** Plan-completion mindset advances from P4 (C14 done) into P5. **C15 DEFERRED** (Neo4j fulltext index, P4 trigger-gated until any user >10k entities) per honest "fire when profiling shows pain" criterion — opposite of C14b's user-override at trigger gate. **No /review-impl invoked** (DESIGN-first cycle has no code surface to review-impl deeply; Stage 2 self-adversarial caught + fixed F5 in REVIEW-CODE phase). **3 substantive ADR sections.** (A) **Decision Option B**: NEW `knowledge_summary_spending` table keyed (user_id, scope_type, month_key) with `record_summary_spending` helper. Rejected Option A (phantom-project polluting `knowledge_projects` requires filtering at every query callsite + frontend leak) and Option C (unified spending ledger — XL refactor touching every existing record_spending callsite incl. K10.4 atomic try_spend pattern; correct architecture for usage analytics but premature for closing D-K20α-01). B doesn't block C — future cycle can refactor on top if usage-analytics demand emerges. (B) **Implementation sketch shovel-ready for next BUILD cycle**: DDL with `scope_type IN ('global', 'project')` CHECK + composite PK + idx_summary_spending_user_month; `SummarySpendingRepo` with `record(user_id, scope_type, cost)` + `current_month_total(user_id)` (matches K16.11 record_spending pattern, ON CONFLICT UPSERT for atomic month rollover via PK shape); `check_user_monthly_budget` extension (one additional SUM query against new table + Decimal addition to existing project_spent); `regenerate_global_summary` wire-in next to existing Prometheus increment at line 569; 15 test cases enumerated (8 repo unit + 1 CHECK constraint integration + 3 budget integration + 3 scheduler integration). DDL regression tests for sweeper_state-style source-scan locks. Recording-order semantic locked in §5.4 (Stage-2 fix): record AFTER provider success BEFORE guardrails — provider already billed regardless of downstream guardrail outcome. (C) **4 open questions explicitly deferred to BUILD-cycle CLARIFY**: (1) does project regen ALSO bypass budget gate today? — re-audit `regenerate_project_summary`; if yes, expand C16-BUILD to cover both; (2) sanity cap on cost arg? — recommend trust upstream per K16.11 stance; (3) FE wire shape change? — recommend extend existing `current_month_usd` aggregation, no new field; (4) auto-pause on global regen budget breach? — recommend pre-check semantic only (no per-item granularity, accept slight over-cap on prediction miss). **Closing checklist** (§7) gates D-K20α-01 fully-cleared status until BUILD cycle ships migration + repo + budget extension + scheduler wire + DDL regression tests + 8 unit + 3 budget integration + 3 scheduler integration tests + plan row [x] AND `/review-impl` 0 unresolved HIGH/MED. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved)**: (1) Option B; (2) greenfield (no migration, project hasn't gone live); (3) closed Literal scope_type with explicit migration story for future expansion; (4) ADR includes implementation sketch — context-fresh decision, makes BUILD cycle shovel-ready. **Closes** (DESIGN-first sign-off): C16-DESIGN gate. **Does NOT close** D-K20α-01 partial (BUILD cycle is the closer). **Files: 3** (1 NEW ADR + SESSION_PATCH + plan update). **Verify.** No code → no tsc/pytest. ADR doc-complete: 7 numbered sections, 5 subsections in §5, 4 explicit open questions, 9-item closing checklist, clickable file:line refs throughout. **Plan progress**: 33 items / 20 cycles · P1+P2+P3 done · P4 C14 done (C15 trigger-gated) · **P5 🏗 1/3 DESIGN-signed-off (C16)** · remaining: C17 + C18 DESIGN-first, plus user-gated × 2.
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 45 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C14b BE [L]; resumable cursor state — new sweeper_state table + SweeperStateRepo + reconciler integration with mid-sweep resume + natural-completion clear — Forty-fifth cycle. **Second P4 cycle.** Closes D-K11.9-01 cursor-state + P-K15.10-01 cursor-state portions (BE halves). **C14 fully shipped** (C14a schedulers + C14b cursor). User override of the "defer until profiling shows pain" trigger accepted at CLARIFY — plan-completion mindset. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 1 MED + 3 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed MED + 1 LOW in-cycle (1 LOW false-finding retraction + 2 accepted):** (MED#1) no DDL regression test for sweeper_state — convention in test_migrate_ddl.py grep-asserts every new table; future migration could drop/rename silently. Fixed: 3 NEW tests (table_present + schema_shape regex asserting scoped columns + no-cross-db-FK regression lock matching existing convention). (LOW#2) counter double-count window when upsert_cursor raises after successful reconcile — counters incremented before upsert would stay high on re-reconcile. Fixed: swapped order — `upsert_cursor` FIRST, counters second. Both orderings safe (reconcile idempotent) but swap gives cleaner semantics; inline docstring explains the risk-profile comparison. All 4 cursor regression tests still green post-swap (resume + natural-clear + error-isolation behaviors preserved). (LOW#3 FALSE FINDING retracted) I flagged missing index on knowledge_projects(user_id); my audit missed that `idx_knowledge_projects_user_all ON knowledge_projects(user_id)` already exists from K16.12. (LOW#4 accept) `UUID(user_id)` defensive by UUID column type. (COSMETIC#5 accept) JSONB str fallback for asyncpg<0.28 is cheap defensive code. **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **migrate.py DDL.** NEW `sweeper_state` table at tail of DDL (matches CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS convention): sweeper_name TEXT PK + last_user_id UUID (nullable for "cursor cleared" state) + last_scope JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb (escape hatch for future per-user-sub-scope sweepers) + updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). No FK on last_user_id (cross-DB forbidden convention). Header comment documents the sweeper_name=reconcile_evidence_count callsite + quarantine-cleanup's documented absence. (B) **NEW `app/db/repositories/sweeper_state.py`.** `SweeperStateRepo(pool)` with 4 async methods: `read_cursor(sweeper_name) -> UUID | None` (minimal shape for typical caller); `read_cursor_full(sweeper_name) -> tuple[UUID|None, dict] | None` (scope-aware; json.loads fallback for asyncpg str path vs dict path); `upsert_cursor(sweeper_name, last_user_id, last_scope=None)` — partial-UPDATE semantic: omitting last_scope on UPDATE preserves existing via conditional SQL branch (regression test locks the invariant); `clear_cursor(sweeper_name)` idempotent DELETE. Module docstring explains crash semantics (upsert-after-success keeps cursor at last-good-user on raise). (C) **reconciler scheduler integration.** `SWEEPER_NAME='reconcile_evidence_count'` constant (grep anchor for all cursor callsites). `_LIST_USERS_SQL` gains `$1::uuid IS NULL OR user_id > $1::uuid` seek predicate with `ORDER BY user_id` for deterministic resume. sweep_reconcile_once signature extends with optional `sweeper_state_repo: SweeperStateRepo | None` (back-compat: None = C14a behavior). Flow: read_cursor at sweep start → log resume if non-null → fetch users with seek → per-user: reconcile + upsert_cursor (MED#2 order swap) + counter increment → natural-completion clear_cursor after for-loop. Per-user raise bypasses upsert so cursor stays at last successful user; next sweep resumes from the next unprocessed. `run_reconcile_loop` threads repo through. quarantine_cleanup_scheduler.py: docstring-only note explaining WHY no cursor (self-advancing `pending_validation=true` filter + global-sweep has no natural per-user key; sweeper_state table already supports adding a quarantine PK entry if future design changes). main.py lifespan wires `SweeperStateRepo(get_knowledge_pool())` once, passes to `run_reconcile_loop`. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved)**: (1) per-user UPSERT frequency (hobby-scale round-trip cost trivial, cleanest resume semantic); (2) clear on natural completion only (sweep-level-exception / lock_skipped leave cursor for resume); (3) scoped schema with last_scope JSONB escape hatch; (4) quarantine cursor = doc-only (self-advancing filter). **Closes D-K11.9-01 cursor-state + P-K15.10-01 cursor-state.** C14 fully shipped (C14a + C14b). **Files: 8** (6 code/test + 2 docs SESSION_PATCH + plan). **Verify.** pytest full knowledge-service **1501/1501** (+17 from C14a baseline 1484: 10 repo + 4 scheduler integration + 3 DDL regression). All 32 scheduler+repo tests + 26 DDL tests green including NEW /review-impl regression locks. Stage-2 order-swap doesn't regress any existing test (resume + natural-clear + per-user-error all still pass). Migration idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS); first post-deploy sweep reads null cursor → iterates all users. **Plan progress**: 33 items / 19 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 DONE (12/12)** · **P4 C14 DONE (both C14a + C14b)**. C15 remains trigger-gated (fire when >10k entities/user). Next actionable: **P5 🏗 C16** (budget attribution ADR).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 44 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C14a BE [L]; reconcile + quarantine scheduler loops created with advisory locks, metrics, lifespan teardown — unblocks the two K11.9 + K15.10 callable-but-never-scheduled sweepers — Forty-fourth cycle. **First P4 cycle.** C14 split at CLARIFY into C14a (create missing schedulers, L this cycle) + C14b (add cursor hardening, M deferred) per user proposal. Audit revealed the plan's single C14 row understated scope: no sweep loops existed at all — just per-user/global callable functions that operators never ran. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 3 MED + 1 LOW + 2 COSMETIC; fixed 3 MED + 1 LOW actionable in-cycle (2 COSMETIC accept, 1 MED documented):** (MED#1) quarantine sweep's single-call pattern caps drain at 1k facts/12h = 2k/day — large burst (e.g. worker-ai down 2 days → 10k stale facts) would take 5 days to drain. Fixed: inner-loop drain in `sweep_quarantine_once` iterates `run_quarantine_cleanup` until returned_count < limit OR `DEFAULT_MAX_DRAIN_ITERATIONS=10` fires; each iteration is a bounded Cypher transaction at ≤limit rows so per-transaction risk stays fixed. 3 new regression tests (burst drain across 3 iterations; max-iterations safety cap pathological case; limit=None single-shot semantics). 10× drain rate (10k/sweep × 2 sweeps/day = 20k/day). (MED#2) `pg_try_advisory_lock` on pooled connection can persist across pool-return on hard process crash — pre-existing K20.3 pattern inherited. Not code-fixable without connection-dedicated-per-lock refactor spanning all 4 existing schedulers. Documented in module docstring with full semantics + why it's narrow (Python try/finally handles normal exceptions; only SIGKILL bypass + backend session still alive would expose the edge — in practice process termination closes the Postgres session too). (MED#3) `reconcile_sweep_total{outcome='errored'}` / `quarantine_sweep_total{outcome='errored'}` semantics ambiguous from Help strings alone — a sweep with 5/10 users erroring fires `errored+1` not `+5`. Fixed: metrics.py Help strings + counter-semantics docstring block now explicit ("sweeps-with-≥1-error, not per-user-error count") + derived-query examples for ops. (LOW#4) `_LIST_USERS_SQL` filtered `is_archived = false` (mirrored K20.3) excluding users with only archived projects — those still have drift-prone Neo4j nodes; reconciler is cheap (no LLM, no $), covering them protects against un-archive resurrecting bad state. Fixed: dropped filter; source-scan regression lock test greps SQL literal at test time to catch a future PR re-adding it for symmetry with K20.3. (LOW#5 accept) `ReconcileResult.total` computed property unused — per-label counters more informative for logs. (COSMETIC#6 accept) reconciler/quarantine/retention/summary-regen scheduler structural similarity — premature to abstract `BackgroundLoop` base while each has domain-specific args. (COSMETIC#7 accept) zero-user list path still increments `completed` metric — correct semantic ("sweep ran without error" regardless of user count). **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **reconcile_evidence_count_scheduler.py (NEW, 210 LOC).** `sweep_reconcile_once(pool, session_factory)` + `run_reconcile_loop(pool, session_factory, interval_s, startup_delay_s)`. Advisory lock key `20_310_004`. Cadence 24h daily + 25min startup stagger. Per-user iteration: `SELECT DISTINCT user_id::text FROM knowledge_projects ORDER BY user_id` (no is_archived filter post-LOW#4) → per-user Neo4j session open → `reconcile_evidence_count(session, user_id=uid, project_id=None, limit_per_label=None)` — K11.9 helper reconciles all 3 labels (Entity/Event/Fact) in one call per user. Per-user error isolation: try/except around each user; errored count + skip, sweep continues. Stage-2-fix: direct attribute access on Pydantic `ReconcileResult` (not `getattr(..., 0)` which would mask schema drift). Metrics `reconcile_sweep_total{outcome}` 3-label pre-seed. (B) **quarantine_cleanup_scheduler.py (NEW, 200 LOC).** `sweep_quarantine_once(pool, session_factory, ttl_hours, limit, max_drain_iterations)` + `run_quarantine_loop(...)`. Advisory lock key `20_310_005`. Cadence 12h twice-daily + 30min startup stagger. Global sweep (user_id=None) with inner-loop drain (MED#1 fix). `QuarantineSweepResult` gains `iterations` field for ops visibility. Metrics `quarantine_sweep_total{outcome}`. (C) **main.py lifespan wire.** Both schedulers gated on `settings.neo4j_uri` alongside K20.3 summary regen (all 3 need Neo4j sessions); 2 lifespan tasks + teardown cancellation blocks mirroring K20.3/K19b.8 pattern. Advisory lock keys 001-005 fully reserved: 001=K20.3-project, 002=K20.3-global, 003=K19b.8-retention, 004=C14a-reconcile, 005=C14a-quarantine; 006 next. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved)**: (1) reconciler 24h + quarantine 12h cadence; (2) quarantine global sweep not per-user (1 Cypher call per drain iteration); (3) reconciler per-user iteration with project_id=None (1 call per user = all 3 labels); (4) lock keys 004/005 + startup 25m/30m stagger. **Closes D-K11.9-01 partial + P-K15.10-01 partial (BE half)** — both sweepers now on background-task cron. **C14b (M, deferred)**: cursor hardening (`sweeper_state` table, resumable-from-mid-user-iteration) to fire only when profiling shows pain. P4 trigger criterion honored. **Files: 10** (2 NEW scheduler + metrics.py MOD + main.py MOD + 2 NEW test files = 6 code; 2 NEW deferral items + 1 SESSION_PATCH + 1 plan update = 4 docs). **Verify.** pytest full knowledge-service **1484/1484** (+18 from C12c-b adj-baseline 1466: 9 reconciler + 9 quarantine + 18 total new tests). All 18 scheduler tests green incl. 4 new /review-impl regression locks (drain-burst + max-cap + limit=None + archived-users-SQL-scan). Stage-2 Pydantic-direct-attribute-access fix re-verified; duck-typed test stubs still work. **Plan progress**: 32 items / 18 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 DONE (12/12)** · **P4 1/2 done (C14a ✅)**. Remaining: C14b (cursor, M, deferred per P4 trigger) · C15 (Neo4j fulltext index, S, defer until user crosses 10k entities) · P5 🏗 × 3 DESIGN-first · User-gated × 2.
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 43 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C12c-b FE [L]; glossary_sync scope radio + book_id gate + 4 locales + drift-lock + retry-fallback hardening — Forty-third cycle. C12c-b strictly FE, closing the D-K19a.5-06 pair opened by C12c-a. Plan said "S FE-only" but workflow-gate required **L** (7 files: BuildGraphDialog + test + 4 locales + drift-lock + SESSION_PATCH); honest-sizing at CLARIFY. **P3 tier 12/12 DONE** after C12c-b — entire Gap Closure P3 closed. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 0 MED + 3 LOW + 2 COSMETIC; fixed 3 LOWs + 2 COSMETICS accept:** (LOW#1) `initialValues.scope='glossary_sync'` on newly book-unlinked project left ALL radios orphaned — availableScopes filter excluded glossary_sync but `scope` state stayed at 'glossary_sync' → no radio checked. Pre-existing pattern also affecting chapters retry. Fixed: `openScope` snaps to `defaultScope` when `(rawOpenScope === 'chapters' || rawOpenScope === 'glossary_sync') && !project.book_id`. Both scopes covered by one fix. 2 regression tests (glossary_sync fallback + pre-existing-bug-for-free chapters fallback). (LOW#2) no retry pre-fill test for scope=glossary_sync — added test locking that `initialValues={scope: 'glossary_sync'}` on book-linked project renders the radio as checked. (LOW#3) Vi `noBookHint` initial phrasing "phạm vi chương và đồng bộ bảng từ vựng bị ẩn" was wordy — refined to "các phạm vi dựa trên sách (chương, đồng bộ bảng từ vựng) bị ẩn" grouping as "book-based scopes" with examples; more idiomatic, future-proof for additional book-dependent scopes. (COSMETIC#4 accept) no test for glossary_sync in chat-only project edge — already covered by extended hidden-when-null-book test. (COSMETIC#5 accept) no FE→BE integration test — pre-existing gap for all scopes. **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **BuildGraphDialog.tsx**: `ALL_SCOPES += 'glossary_sync'` (inserted between chat and all to match render order); `availableScopes` memo filter extends to both `chapters` AND `glossary_sync` gated on `!!project.book_id` — single filter, single `noBookHint` covering both; LOW#1 `openScope` fallback logic mirrors the filter condition (book-required scopes fall back to defaultScope when book is absent). (B) **i18n × 4 locales**: new `projects.buildDialog.scope.glossary_sync` key + updated `noBookHint` to mention both hidden scopes. Per-locale: en `'Glossary sync'`, ja `'用語集の同期'`, vi `'Đồng bộ bảng từ vựng'`, zh-TW `'詞彙表同步'`. Vi noBookHint refined post-/review-impl to the book-based-scope grouping. (C) **Tests**: BuildGraphDialog.test.tsx extends existing hidden-when-null-book test with 1 assertion (glossary_sync also hidden) + 2 new tests (renders-when-book + start-payload threads scope correctly with scope_range omitted) + 3 NEW /review-impl regression tests (retry pre-fill + 2 book-unlink fallbacks); projectState.test.ts BUILD_DIALOG_KEYS += 'buildDialog.scope.glossary_sync' — drift-lock catches a locale that forgets the key. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (3 user-approved)**: (1) Hide glossary_sync when !book_id (mirror chapters) vs grey-out — hide for consistency with existing UX. (2) Short labels per locale, no hint text — symmetry with other scopes. (3) Skip Storybook peer — IdleChaptersScope already renders the new radio. **Closes D-K19a.5-06** (FE half; paired with C12c-a BE). **P3 tier 12/12 DONE** — Gap Closure P3 fully closed. **Files: 8** (7 code/test + 1 docs SESSION_PATCH + 1 plan update). **Verify.** `tsc --noEmit` clean. vitest knowledge+lib **479/479** (+5 from C13 baseline 474: 2 new glossary_sync component tests + 3 /review-impl regression tests). No BE changes — C12c-a shipped the authoritative 422 guard. **Plan progress**: 30 items / 17 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 12/12 DONE** (C10..C13 + C12a..C12c-a+C12c-b all shipped). Remaining actionable: **P4 × 2** (C14 resumable scheduler, C15 Neo4j fulltext) · **P5 🏗 × 3** DESIGN-first (C16 budget attribution, C17 entity-merge canonical-alias, C18 event wall-clock date) · **User-gated × 2** (C19 multilingual fixtures, C20 Gate-13 walkthrough).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 42 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C12c-a FS [L]; glossary_sync BE unblock — new glossary-service entity list endpoint + worker-ai runner branch + knowledge-service sync endpoint + fix scope='all' to include glossary — Forty-second cycle. C12c SPLIT at CLARIFY into C12c-a (FS L this cycle = BE end-to-end) + C12c-b (FE S next cycle = scope radio). Plan-said "S FE-only blocked" understated reality — per memory `feedback_scope_audit_before_batching`, audit caught that glossary-service had no list endpoint + worker-ai had a TODO at line 621 silently completing glossary_sync jobs with 0 work. Honest sizing reclassified to FS L. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 3 MED + 3 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed ALL 7 in-cycle (6 actionable + 1 accept-with-doc):** (MED#1) start_extraction_job had no guard for scope='glossary_sync' + book_id=null — estimate endpoint guards at line 230 but start didn't, creating no-op jobs. Fixed: 422 check at extraction.py:376 mirroring the estimate guard + 3 new tests (422 on null-book, 201 on book-present, 201 on scope='all' null-book — confirms guard is scope-specific). (MED#2) K15.11 helper ON MATCH SET omitted project_id — first-call-wins semantic made project_id drift inevitable for users with two projects sharing a book. Pre-existing bug, C12c-a is first production caller. Fixed by adding `e.project_id = $project_id` to ON MATCH (latest-sync wins, least disruptive) + source-scan regression test greps the Cypher literal at test time + module docstring documents the semantic change. (MED#3) glossary branch had no per-entity retry counter unlike chapters' _MAX_RETRIES_PER_ITEM=3 — flapping glossary-service would loop forever. Fixed: retry_glossary_<id> cursor key mirroring chapter retry pattern + `retry_exhausted` error log on 3rd attempt + advance cursor past flapping entity + regression test. (LOW#4) 502 boundary leaked raw Neo4j exception text across service boundary — fixed with opaque "failed to merge glossary entity" message; logger.exception keeps details server-side; test asserts sanitized wire. (LOW#5) items_total pre-count drift on partial enumeration — glossary-service flake mid-pagination made `_enumerate_glossary_entities` return partial list with wrong total. Fixed: function signature changed to `(list, complete: bool)` tuple; runner skips _set_items_total when complete=False; regression test seeds page1+None-page2. (LOW#6) test coverage for 5xx mid-enum added (covered by same test as LOW#5). (COSMETIC#7) 200-page ceiling is unreachable (HARD_CAP=5000 ÷ 100 items/page = 50 max pages) but kept as defense-in-depth against BE cursor regression. **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **glossary-service Go** — NEW `GET /internal/books/{book_id}/entities?cursor=<b64>&limit=100` paginated endpoint returning `{items:[{entity_id, name, kind_code, aliases, short_description}], next_cursor}`; cursor = base64-JSON of entity_id UUID (total ordering = correct seek predicate); filter `alive=true AND deleted_at IS NULL` (both guards — alive is soft-delete, deleted_at is tombstone); hard cap 200/page; short_description joined via attribute_definitions LEFT JOIN; **peek-ahead pagination with MED#1 fix** — track rowsScanned separately from items length so empty-name filter doesn't break cursor advance (if DB returns limit+1 with one row name-filtered, items has limit entries but rowsScanned=limit+1 emits cursor — previously would silently terminate). NEW EntitiesListTotal Prometheus counter. 5 Go tests (4 unit no-DB: auth + bad UUID + bad cursor 4-case parametrized; 1 DB-gated cursor walk w/ short_desc round-trip; 1 DB-gated MED#1 name-filter regression). (B) **worker-ai** — NEW `GlossaryClient` + `GlossaryEntity`/`GlossaryPage` Pydantic-adjacent dataclasses (frozen, tuple aliases) in clients.py with graceful-degrade to None on httpx error/5xx/shape drift. NEW `KnowledgeClient.glossary_sync_entity` method posting to new knowledge-service endpoint + GlossarySyncResult dataclass. NEW `_enumerate_glossary_entities(client, book_id, cursor) → (list[GlossaryEntity], complete: bool)` helper — paginates via BE cursor, resume via `last_glossary_entity_id` UUID-ASC skip, HARD_CAP=5000, 200-page safety, LOW#5 partial-enum flag. NEW `_GLOSSARY_SYNC_COST_PER_ITEM = Decimal("0.0")` — glossary has no LLM call so cost=0 but still goes through _try_spend so pause/cancel detection stays uniform. NEW glossary branch in `_process_job` gated on `scope in ('glossary_sync', 'all') and book_id is not None` — runs AFTER chapters+chat tail (flips the behavior of scope='all' to actually cover glossary, fixing the TODO at line 621 which silently excluded glossary from "all"). Per-entity: _refresh_job_status → _try_spend(0.0) → knowledge_client.glossary_sync_entity → error branches (fatal fail / retry with counter / success) → _advance_cursor → _record_spending(0.0) → items_processed++. items_total pre-count now includes glossary (when complete). config.py +glossary_service_url + glossary_client_timeout_s. main.py wires GlossaryClient into poll_and_run. `process_job` + `poll_and_run` sigs gain 5th arg `glossary_client: GlossaryClient`. **All 17 existing test_runner tests migrated to 5-arg sig + 4 NEW glossary tests + 2 NEW /review-impl regression tests = 23/23.** (C) **knowledge-service** — NEW `POST /internal/extraction/glossary-sync-entity` thin handler wrapping K15.11 `sync_glossary_entity_to_neo4j` (previously dead code — only test-caller before C12c-a; now has 1 production caller). 8 new endpoint tests. K15.11 helper itself modified: ON MATCH SET adds `e.project_id = $project_id` (MED#2 fix) + module docstring updated. start_extraction_job gains MED#1 glossary_sync+book guard. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (3 user-approved)**: (1) GlossaryEntity endpoint shape rejected known-entities reuse (semantic mismatch — "known" = anchor context, not "all") + POST-with-body + streaming. (2) Per-entity RPC over single-shot (preserves pause/cancel granularity, matches chapter flow). (3) Cost model B: cost=0 but still through _try_spend (uniform pause/cancel). Plus user-approved option 1 flip: scope='all' now iterates glossary (making the name semantically honest). **Closes D-K19a.5-06 BE half**. **P3 tier 10/10 DONE** after C12c-a (C10 ✅ + C11 ✅ + C12a ✅ + C12b-a ✅ + C12b-b ✅ + C13 ✅ + C12c-a ✅; C12c-b next = FE S only). **Files: 15 code/test + 3 docs = 18**. **Verify.** glossary-service Go: build clean, `go test ./internal/api/` ok (12 tests incl. 4 new unit + 2 DB-gated). worker-ai: 23/23 pytest. knowledge-service: 1466/1466 pytest (+4 from 1462 baseline: 3 start-endpoint 422 tests + 1 ON MATCH source-scan regression). tsc not applicable (no FE in C12c-a). **Plan progress**: 29 items / 16 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 10/10 BE-done after C12c-a** (only C12c-b FE S pending — strictly FE-only: scope radio in BuildGraphDialog + 4 locales + drift-lock + `!project.book_id` gate). Next actionable: C12c-b FE (close P3 completely), or P4 C14 (resumable scheduler cursor state, L).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 41 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C13 FE [L]; Storybook dialog stories via MSW + shared story-helpers — Forty-first cycle. Sixth Gap Closure P3 cycle; **P3 tier 9/9 done after C13**. Only C12c remains in P3 (blocked on glossary-service BE sync surface). Pure FE infra: msw@2.13.5 + msw-storybook-addon@2.0.7 devDeps; `.storybook/preview.tsx` wires `initialize({onUnhandledRequest:'warn'})` + `mswLoader`; NEW service worker committed at `frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js` (9120 bytes, msw init output). NEW `.storybook/fixtures/knowledge.ts` (14 typed factories — projectFixture/projectFixtureNoBook, benchmarkStatusPassed/Failed/NoRun, benchmarkRunFixture, costEstimateFixture, jobSummaryFixture, extractionJobWireFixture, changeModelNoOpFixture/changeModelResultFixture, userCostsFixture, userModelsFixtureChat/Embedding/All). NEW `.storybook/msw-handlers.ts` (7 endpoint factories — benchmarkStatus/Run, estimate, startExtraction, updateEmbeddingModel, userCosts, userModels + HandlerOptions {delayMs: number|'infinite', status, body} + isJsonSafe runtime narrow). NEW `.storybook/story-helpers.ts` (findConfirmButton scoped to `[role="dialog"]` dropping Cancel/Close by text; findRunBenchmarkButton for picker CTA; waitForSelects option-value-aware 5s timeout querying `document` to reach Radix portal; waitForSingleSelect wrapper). 3 story files × 19 stories: BuildGraphDialog 11 (IdleAllScope, IdleChaptersScope, ChaptersScopeWithRange, EstimateLoading, EstimateError422, Confirming, ConfirmErrorBudgetExceeded, BenchmarkNoRun, BenchmarkFailed, BenchmarkPassed, BenchmarkRunFromCTA) + ChangeModelDialog 5 (SameModelSelected, DifferentModelSelected, Confirming, ConfirmError, NoOpResponse) + ErrorViewerDialog 3 (WithJob, ErrorOnly, LongStackTrace) zero-MSW. `@sb/*` Vite alias + tsconfig paths collapse `../../../../.storybook/...` imports. MockAuthProvider.user reshaped from pre-existing `{id}` (K19a.8 latent bug — user?.user_id resolved to undefined, useUserCosts fell back to 'anon' queryKey) to production `{user_id, display_name: string|null, email}` matching `src/auth.tsx:UserProfile`. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 1 MED + 4 LOW + 2 COSMETIC; fixed all 7 in-cycle:** (MED#1) VERIFY was static-bundle-only — ran `npm run storybook` + Chrome DevTools MCP to live-smoke stories: ErrorViewer zero-MSW render ✓; BuildGraph.IdleAllScope fires 4 GETs (costs + 2×user-models by capability + benchmark-status) all 200 ✓; BuildGraph.Confirming play() fires POST /estimate + POST /start [200] after 2s delay ✓; BuildGraph.BenchmarkRunFromCTA play() fires POST /benchmark-run [200] after 1.5s + follow-up GET /benchmark-status refetch via queryClient invalidation ✓; ChangeModel.Confirming play() fires PUT /embedding-model?confirm=true [200] after 2s ✓. **DISCOVERED DURING SMOKE**: Radix Dialog portals to `document.body` so `canvasElement.querySelector*` and `within(canvasElement)` miss dialog subtree entirely — extracted story-helpers.ts querying `document` with option-value-aware waitForSelects (native `<select>` initially renders placeholder-only until models useQuery resolves; gating on target option value avoids render-timing race that threw `Value "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" not found in options`). (LOW#2) userModelsHandler returned merged chat+embedding for any `?capability=` query → degraded preview fidelity (LLM dropdown showed bge-m3, embedding dropdown showed claude-haiku). Fixed: fixture param is union `{items} | {chat?, embedding?, fallback?}`; handler inspects `new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('capability')` to branch. (LOW#3) MockAuthProvider.user.id → production UserProfile.user_id mismatch (pre-existing K19a.8, now exercised by every BuildGraphDialog story via useUserCosts). Fixed: renamed field + widened display_name to `string | null` matching production. (LOW#4) benchmarkRunHandler exported factory was unused by any story (no story play-clicked the CTA). Fixed: NEW 11th BuildGraph story `BenchmarkRunFromCTA` wires benchmarkStatusNoRun() + benchmarkRunHandler + play() uses findRunBenchmarkButton. (LOW#5) `ambientHandlers(opts)` used structural `'status' in opts.estimate` discriminant — fragile to future CostEstimate field additions. Fixed: explicit `type EstimateMode = {mode:'happy'|'loading'|'error', ...}` tagged union. (COSMETIC#6) `'../../../../.storybook/fixtures/knowledge'` 4-level imports. Fixed: `@sb/*` alias in both `.storybook/main.ts` viteFinal + `tsconfig.json:paths`. (COSMETIC#7) `errorOr` blind cast to JsonBodyType would accept a Date/Promise/Map at compile-time via `body: unknown`. Fixed: `isJsonSafe` recursive runtime narrow — non-POJO bodies fall back to `{__unserializable: typeof value}` envelope so story shows _something_. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (6 user-approved defaults)**: (1) msw-storybook-addon not hand-rolled setupWorker. (2) Full 19-story matrix (original was 18; C13a +1 added post-LOW#4). (3) Shared fixtures module. (4) Shared handler factory module. (5) a11y `test: 'todo'` globally (upgrade deferred). (6) Skip cross-locale story drift-lock (Storybook dev-only, projectState.test.ts already covers runtime keys). **SIZE reclassified at CLARIFY** from plan-said M to workflow-gate-required L (6 files / 4 logic / 1 side-effect). Real post-BUILD count: 13 files + 1 gitignored lock. **Closes** D-K19a.8-01. **P3 tier 9/9 DONE** (C10 ✅ + C11 ✅ + C12a ✅ + C12b-a ✅ + C12b-b ✅ + C13 ✅); C12c remains blocked. **Files: 13** (6 MOD: SESSION_HANDOFF + MockAuthProvider + storybook/main + storybook/preview + package.json + tsconfig; 7 NEW: 3 storybook infra + 1 SW + 3 stories). **Verify.** `tsc --noEmit` clean post-all-fixes. Vitest knowledge+lib **474/474** (no regressions from C12b-b baseline; stories are tsc-only per existing vitest exclude pattern). `storybook build --test` success (3 new story chunks + knowledge fixtures + msw-handlers bundle). Live smoke confirmed 4 story variants exercise the full MSW/React/play() stack with no console errors beyond React Router future-flag warnings. **Plan progress**: 28 items / 15 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 9/9 done (C13 ✅)** · C12c blocked. Next actionable: P4 C14 (resumable scheduler cursor state, L) or P5 🏗 DESIGN-first (C16/C17/C18).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 40 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C12b-b FE [L]; Run-benchmark button + loading UX + 5×error-code toast map — Fortieth cycle. Fifth Gap Closure P3 cycle; **P3 tier 8/9 done after C12b-b** (C12b split complete — BE + FE shipped). Pure FE: NEW useRunBenchmark mutation hook + inline RunBenchmarkButton in EmbeddingModelPicker + 9-key i18n × 4 locales + drift lock. The button auto-appears in BuildGraphDialog, ChangeModelDialog, and ProjectFormModal since they all compose the picker. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 1 MED + 3 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed MED + 3 LOWs in-cycle, 2 accepted-with-doc:** (MED#1) model-swap mid-mutation renders stale toast — user clicks Run on bge-m3, changes dropdown mid-run to text-embedding-3-small, toast says "recall 0.82" but user reads it as describing the NEW selection. Fixed: `projects.form.benchmark.success` now interpolates `{{model}}` from `resp.embedding_model` — toast self-discloses scope. (LOW#2) no placeholder-presence drift lock for the new interpolation keys; a translator who drops `{{recall}}`/`{{message}}`/`{{model}}` would render the literal template text in a toast. Fixed: 2 new `it.each(LOCALES)` assertions in projectState.test.ts mirror the C7 `jobs.detail.eta` pattern. (LOW#3) hook test coverage missed `runs=null` explicit path — TS-unsafe caller could bypass the TS-narrowed `{runs?: number}` type and ship null to BE. Fixed: new `passes runs=null straight through` hook test pinning the pass-through behaviour so a future edit that coerces null→undefined can't mask a BE validation regression. (LOW#4 accept+doc) unmount-during-mutation drops the toast — BE still completes + persists + invalidates the queryClient-level cache, but the component-level onSuccess/onError never fires. Documented in useRunBenchmark.ts docblock; matches useRegenerateBio pattern. (LOW#5 accept+doc) button placement inside the outer `<label>` — label-click forwarding doesn't trigger when the direct target is a `<button>`, but it's a structural smell. Documented; matches existing BenchmarkBadge placement. (COSMETIC#6 accept+doc) rapid double-click race — React's `disabled={isPending}` updates synchronously within the event tick so practically unexploitable; BE sentinel catches the edge. Documented. **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **FE types + api.** NEW `BenchmarkRunResponse` wire type (mirrors C12b-a BE's 10-field projection) + `knowledgeApi.runBenchmark(projectId, runs?, token)` POST helper. Body shape: `JSON.stringify(runs !== undefined ? {runs} : {})` so `runs=undefined` sends `{}` and BE defaults to 3; `runs=null` passes through to BE 422 (LOW#3 lock). (B) **NEW useRunBenchmark hook.** Mirrors useRegenerateBio error-parser pattern verbatim. `RunBenchmarkErrorCode` closed union over 5 BE codes + `'unknown'`. Prefix-invalidate `['knowledge', 'benchmark-status', projectId]` on success — covers every `[..., projectId, <model>]` variant so the badge refreshes regardless of which model is currently focused. Defensive: throws `unknown` error synchronously when `accessToken`/`projectId` missing, so a forgetful caller gets a fast-fail instead of a BE round-trip. (C) **NEW RunBenchmarkButton in EmbeddingModelPicker.** Inline button rendered after BenchmarkBadge, gated on `projectId && value && !data.passed` (so it shows for both no-run AND failed, hides once passed). Click fires `mutation.mutate({runs: 3})` — `runs` hardcoded (CLI + L-CH-09 methodology; BE validates 1..5). Disabled + label swap to "Running benchmark…" while pending. `runBenchmarkErrorMessage(t, code, detailMessage)` helper maps 6 codes to localised toast copy. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved defaults).** (1) Button lives inside EmbeddingModelPicker — blast radius: BuildGraphDialog + ChangeModelDialog + ProjectFormModal all get the CTA automatically. (2) Direct-run, no confirm dialog — picker sits adjacent to model select so intent is clear; 15-60s spinner signals work-in-progress. (3) Inline button spinner, no modal overlay — user can tab away and come back; react-query keeps the mutation alive. (4) `runs=3` hardcoded, not exposed to user. **Closes** D-K19a.5-07 (FE half). **Defers** C12c (glossary_sync, blocked on glossary-service BE sync surface) + C13 (Storybook dialogs via MSW, M). **Files: 9** (2 code mods + 1 new hook + 4 i18n locales + 2 new test files + 1 drift-lock extension). **Verify.** 474/474 FE knowledge+lib GREEN (+9 from C12b-a baseline 465: +11 hook + +10 picker + +1 null hook test + +8 placeholder drift locks, minus 21 uncounted deltas from prior integration). `tsc --noEmit` clean. 11 hook tests + 10 component tests = 21 new C12b-b tests total. **Plan progress**: 27 items / 14 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 8/9 done (C12b-b ✅)** · remaining: C12c (glossary_sync, blocked), C13 (Storybook dialogs, M).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 39 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C12b-a BE [L]; on-demand POST benchmark endpoint with empty-project guard + provider-flake detection — Thirty-ninth cycle. Fourth Gap Closure P3 cycle; **P3 tier 7/8 done after C12b-a** (C12b split into C12b-a ✅ = BE + C12b-b deferred = FE). Knowledge-service BE only: NEW `app/benchmark/` module + runner.py orchestrator + new POST `/v1/knowledge/projects/{id}/benchmark-run` endpoint. Reuses K17.9 harness (AsyncBenchmarkRunner + fixture_loader + persist) so the request-path is a thin sibling of the CLI's `_run_cli`. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 2 MED + 3 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed all 5 non-cosmetic findings in-cycle:** (MED#1) empty-project guard filters `p.source_type IN KNOWN_SOURCE_TYPES` = `{chapter,chat,glossary}` — correct today (only `passage_ingester.py` writes `:Passage` with literal `chapter`) but silent drift risk if a future PR adds a new producer without extending the set. Fixed: added source-scan regression-lock `test_known_source_types_cover_every_real_upsert_passage_callsite` that greps `passage_ingester.py` at test time for `source_type="..."` literals and asserts each is in `KNOWN_SOURCE_TYPES`. Catches a future PR that writes `source_type="note"` without updating the guard. (MED#2) initial draft used `asyncio.Lock` + pre-check `lock.locked()` then `async with lock:` — atomic today in single-threaded asyncio only because no await sits between the two lines, but fragile to refactor (a future `logger.info(...)` insert would silently break serialization). Swapped to a pure-sync `set[tuple[str,str]]` sentinel with `_try_mark_running` / `_mark_done`; check-and-add is atomic-by-construction and immune to await insertion. 2 new tests: `test_sentinel_cleared_after_successful_run` + `test_sentinel_cleared_after_fixture_incomplete_raise` (regression lock against the try/finally unwind). (LOW#3) no test pinned the "benchmark_entity NOT in KNOWN_SOURCE_TYPES" invariant — a drift that accidentally added it would 409 every re-run. Fixed: `test_benchmark_source_type_not_in_known_set`. (LOW#4) partial fixture load (embedder flake on N-of-10 golden entities) was proceeding silently to persist a false-negative `passed=False` row, which looks identical to a retrieval regression in the FE badge. Fixed: new `FixtureLoadIncompleteError` raised when `loaded < expected`, mapped to `502 embedding_provider_flake`. Refuses to persist the row. 2 new tests: `test_fixture_incomplete_does_not_persist` (critical contract) + router `test_post_benchmark_run_502_on_fixture_incomplete`. (LOW#5) `_has_real_passages` Cypher literal had no direct test — all unit tests mocked the function; a typo like `p.source_type = $real_types` (= instead of IN) would silently return no rows and no-op the guard. Fixed: `test_real_passage_count_cypher_has_safety_clauses` asserts the string literal contains the three safety clauses (`user_id`, `project_id`, `IN $real_types`). (COSMETIC#6 skip) `passes_thresholds()` called 3× — pure comparisons, no I/O. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (4 user-approved defaults).** (1) Option A isolation: empty-project guard via `KNOWN_SOURCE_TYPES` filter. Rejected Option B (auto-provisioned `__benchmark__` project) and Option C (`is_benchmark` flag column with migration). Matches `eval/fixture_loader.py:54` documented assumption that benchmarks target a dedicated project. (2) 120s sync timeout default — no background-task pattern because a benchmark that fails mid-run without an obvious owning task is worse UX than a long request. (3) `runs` kept tunable 1..5, default 3 (matches CLI + L-CH-09). (4) Golden-set source hardcoded to `eval/golden_set.yaml`. **SUPPORTED_PASSAGE_DIMS pre-flight**: `nomic-embed-text` (dim 768) is in `EMBEDDING_MODEL_TO_DIM` but not in `SUPPORTED_PASSAGE_DIMS` — a project set to that model would have failed inside `upsert_passage` with a 500. Added explicit guard → `409 unknown_embedding_model` with a different error message, caught upfront. **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **NEW app/benchmark/ module.** `app/benchmark/__init__.py` (7 LOC docstring) + `app/benchmark/runner.py` (~230 LOC). Typed exception hierarchy `BenchmarkRunError` → 5 subclasses: `NoEmbeddingModelError`, `UnknownEmbeddingModelError`, `NotBenchmarkProjectError`, `BenchmarkAlreadyRunningError`, `FixtureLoadIncompleteError`. Frozen dataclass `BenchmarkRunResult` wire projection (10 fields: run_id, embedding_model, passed, 7 metric floats, runs). `_REAL_PASSAGE_COUNT_CYPHER` multi-tenant safe (uses `run_read` helper which asserts `$user_id`). `run_project_benchmark(...)` orchestrator: validation ladder → sentinel check-and-add → load yaml → fixture upsert (raises on partial) → AsyncBenchmarkRunner.run → persist → finally release sentinel → return result. (B) **Router POST handler.** `POST /v1/knowledge/projects/{project_id}/benchmark-run` under existing `/v1/knowledge/projects` prefix. Body `BenchmarkRunRequest { runs?: int 1..5 default 3 }` optional. Returns `BenchmarkRunResponse`. 404 on cross-user/missing (KSA §6.4 anti-leak), 4×409 error_codes, 1×502 error_code, 422 on runs out of range. Local imports of `app.benchmark.runner` + `get_embedding_client` keep the module-load surface of `extraction.py` minimal (harness eval imports don't pull in on every request). Module-top `get_knowledge_pool` bound in the router namespace (matches sibling endpoint pattern). (C) **Tests.** NEW `tests/unit/test_benchmark_runner_service.py` 15 tests: validation ladder (5), happy-path call-order (1), runs propagation (1), defensive 404 (1), sentinel held/cleared/cleared-on-raise/no-persist (4), 3 regression-lock tests (invariant + source-scan passage_ingester + cypher safety clauses). NEW `tests/unit/test_public_benchmark_run.py` 13 tests: 404, 4×409 with error_codes, 1×502, 4×422 parametrized runs-out-of-range, 3×200 (default runs / explicit runs / no body). **Closes** C12b (BE half). **Defers** C12b-b (FE Run benchmark button + loading UX + 502 error handling) + C12c (glossary_sync, blocked on glossary-service BE surface). **Files: 5** (1 init + 1 runner + 1 router mod + 2 test files). **Verify.** NEW service tests **15/15**; NEW router tests **13/13** = 28/28 C12b-a. BE adjacent 1405/1405 (incl. existing benchmark_metrics 23, benchmark_runs_repo 5, public_benchmark 6, internal_benchmark 8). `pytest tests/unit/ --ignore=<pre-existing-ssl-issues>` 1405 passed + 12 pre-existing httpx/SSL errors in `test_circuit_breaker` + `test_embedding_client` + `test_book_client` + `test_glossary_client` + `test_config` (verified unrelated via earlier stash baselines). **Plan progress**: 26 items / 13 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 7/8 done (C12b-a ✅)** · remaining: C12b-b (Run benchmark CTA FE, M), C12c (glossary_sync, blocked), C13 (Storybook dialogs, M).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 38 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C12a FS [XL]; paired chapter-range picker + runner-side scope_range enforcement — Thirty-eighth cycle. Third Gap Closure P3 cycle; **P3 tier 6/8 done after C12a** (C12 split into C12a ✅ + C12b deferred + C12c blocked). Cross-service BE + FE: NEW book-service endpoint `/internal/chapters/sort-orders` + knowledge-service client + new repo method + event-handler runner gate + FE picker in BuildGraphDialog + i18n × 4 + cross-locale drift lock. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 1 MED + 4 LOW + 0 COSMETIC; fixed MED + 1 LOW in-cycle, 3 LOWs accepted:** (MED#1) BE `_extract_chapter_range` accepted reversed range `[50, 10]` silently — validator rejected non-int/negative/wrong-length but not `from > to`. Persisted to DB, then runner gate's membership test `lo ≤ sort_order ≤ hi` with lo > hi was vacuously false → every chapter silently skipped with no error signal. Not exploitable (user's own extraction no-ops) but misleading. Fixed: 3-line addition to `_extract_chapter_range` raising 422 on reversed range; existing `test_estimate_scope_range_malformed_rejected` gains `[50, 10]` case. (LOW#2) `list_active_for_project` had no unit or integration test — SQL `status IN ('pending','running','paused')` filter + cross-user scoping unverified. Added 2 integration tests (mirroring existing `list_all_for_user` patterns) for status filter + cross-user isolation, gated on `TEST_DATABASE_URL`. LOW#3 accept: retry flow doesn't prefill `chapter_range` into `initialValues`. LOW#4 accept: per-event `list_active_for_project` query cost (indexed; profile first). LOW#5 auto-closed by MED#1. **Four substantive blocks.** (A) **book-service Go.** NEW `postInternalChapterSortOrders` handler on `POST /internal/chapters/sort-orders` — mirrors `postInternalChapterTitles` shape verbatim (200-cap, scan_error_count best-effort, rows.Err() fatal). Returns `{sort_orders: {uuid: int}}`. Keeps existing titles endpoint untouched. 3 Go tests (empty list, oversized rejected, invalid JSON). (B) **knowledge-service client + repo.** NEW `BookClient.get_chapter_sort_orders(ids) → dict[UUID, int]` with graceful-degrade pattern returning `{}` on any failure (httpx error, non-200, JSON shape drift). NEW `ExtractionJobsRepo.list_active_for_project(user_id, project_id) → list[ExtractionJob]` with `WHERE user_id=$1 AND project_id=$2 AND status IN ('pending','running','paused')` — uses existing `idx_extraction_jobs_project` + `idx_extraction_jobs_active` partial index. `scope_range` JSONB normalised via `_row_to_job`. (C) **knowledge-service event handler.** `handle_chapter_saved` gains C12a runner gate after existing project/embedding/Neo4j checks. Fetches active jobs; for each `scope='chapters'` job, collects `scope_range.chapter_range` with isinstance + non-bool + ≥0 defence. Computes disjoint union (not outer envelope — [10,20] ∪ [40,50] excludes 30, includes 45). If ≥1 chapter-scope job is unbounded (no range), full ingest wins the union. For bounded cases, fetches this chapter's sort_order via `get_chapter_sort_orders` and skips with DEBUG log when outside all ranges. Graceful degrade on sort_order fetch failure — over-ingests rather than silent skip. 6 event-handler unit tests cover: no active chapter jobs → ingest; active chapter job no range → ingest; in-range → ingest; out-of-range → skip; 2-job disjoint union with gap → correct skip/ingest; fetch failure → over-ingest. (D) **FE BuildGraphDialog.** Two number inputs (From/To) visible only when `scope='chapters'`. `chapterRange` useMemo (declared BEFORE `estimateQuery` that consumes it — TDZ fix during BUILD) parses and validates: both empty → full scope; both set + from ≤ to → valid bounded range; anything else (partial, reversed, negative, non-integer) → inline invalid hint + Confirm disabled. Wired to both estimate and start payloads as `scope_range: {chapter_range: [from, to]}`. Estimate queryKey extended with range key so preview refreshes as user adjusts range. Form reset clears range on dialog reopen. 4 new tests (picker scope-gating, scope_range threaded on confirm, invalid-hint + disabled Confirm on reversed range, omitted when inputs empty). **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (6 user-approved defaults).** (1) C12 split per user call: C12a=items 1+2 this cycle, C12b=item 4 next cycle, C12c=item 3 blocked. (2) NEW book-service endpoint, not extending titles — avoids C6 breaking change. (3) Gate only on active chapter-scope jobs with bounded range. (4) Disjoint union check. (5) Graceful degrade on fetch failure. (6) FE empty=full scope. **Closes** D-K19a.5-04 + D-K16.2-02b. **Deferred** D-K19a.5-07 → C12b; D-K19a.5-06 → C12c (blocked). **Files: 16** (Go 2 + Python 5 + FE 4 + i18n 4 + docs 2 including drift lock). **Verify.** Go `TestPostInternalChapterSortOrders_*` 3/3. Python `test_event_handlers.py` **16/16** (+6 C12a); `test_extraction_estimate.py` `test_estimate_scope_range_malformed_rejected` expanded with reversed-range case; 247/247 BE adjacent extraction/events. Integration 2 new tests (gated on live Postgres). FE knowledge+lib **444/444** GREEN (+4 from C11 baseline 440). `tsc --noEmit` clean. Pre-existing BE SSL/truststore errors in test_book_client/test_glossary_client/test_embedding_client verified unrelated (same env issue as earlier cycles). **Plan progress**: 25 items / 12 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 6/8 done** · remaining: C12b (Run benchmark CTA, L), C12c (glossary_sync, blocked), C13 (Storybook dialogs, M).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 37 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C11 FS [XL]; cursor pagination for extraction jobs history + Load more affordance. Thirty-seventh cycle. Second P3 cycle. Cross-service BE+FE: new cursor codec + SQL row-value/NULLS-LAST predicate + response envelope + FE infinite query + Load more button + i18n × 4 + cross-locale drift lock. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH (blocked early) + 1 HIGH + 1 MED + 3 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed HIGH + MED + 1 LOW in-cycle, 2 LOW + 1 COSMETIC accepted:** (HIGH#1) 9 integration-test call sites in `test_extraction_jobs_repo.py` treated `list_all_for_user`'s return as a list but the new tuple return `(rows, next_cursor)` silently broke them — tests would fail the moment someone runs the suite against live Postgres. Fixed: unpacked all 9 sites to `(rows, _) = await repo.list_all_for_user(...)`. (MED#2) history polling removed at initial design (to avoid N-page refetch storm) created a UX regression: users watching the Jobs tab wouldn't see newly-completed jobs without manual Load more or tab navigation. Fixed: restored `refetchInterval` as a **function-form** gated on `pages.length ≤ 1` — single-page users get 10s freshness back, power users who click Load more accept a frozen view (their explicit opt-in → their explicit refresh). (LOW#3) no integration test exercised the novel 4-branch NULLS-LAST OR SQL predicate. Added 2 new integration tests — walk-7-through-pages-of-3 (asserts pages concat to same order as single-shot + no dupes + null terminal cursor) + tied-completed_at-tiebreak (asserts limit=1 walk visits every row exactly once). LOW#4 (accept): mutation invalidation of `['knowledge-jobs']` now refetches all N loaded history pages — bounded by user click-rate (pause/resume/cancel); acceptable at hobby scale. LOW#5 (accept): `fetchMoreHistory` is a fresh closure every render; single consumer, zero impact. COSMETIC#6 (accept): cursor 500-char limit not explicitly tested — FastAPI's Query validation is trusted. **Four substantive blocks.** (A) **BE repo.** NEW `CursorDecodeError` exception + `_encode_cursor(c: datetime|None, r: datetime, j: UUID) → str` + `_decode_cursor(raw: str) → tuple` (base64-urlsafe JSON, defensive against binascii.Error + UnicodeDecodeError + json.JSONDecodeError + field-shape ValueError/TypeError). `list_all_for_user` signature returns `(list[ExtractionJob], str | None)` tuple + `cursor: str | None = None` kwarg. Active path: simple 2-key row-value compare `(j.created_at, j.job_id) < ($cur_created, $cur_job)`. History path: 4-branch NULLS-LAST OR covering (cursor non-null + row non-null, lower completed_at), (cursor non-null + row non-null, equal completed_at + lower tiebreak), (cursor non-null + row null — null always after non-null under NULLS LAST so any null row is "after"), (both null + lower tiebreak). `next_cursor` populated only when exactly `effective_limit` rows returned. (B) **BE router.** NEW `ExtractionJobsPage` envelope `{items: list[ExtractionJob], next_cursor: str | None}` + `cursor: str | None` Query param (min_length=1, max_length=500); maps `CursorDecodeError` → 422 with `malformed cursor: {detail}`. Response shape is a breaking change for external callers hitting `/extraction/jobs` directly but FE updates together. (C) **FE.** `api.ts` gains `ExtractionJobsPageResponse` + `listAllJobs(..., cursor?: string)`. `useExtractionJobs` hook: history switches to `useInfiniteQuery` with `initialPageParam: ''` + `getNextPageParam: (last) => last.next_cursor ?? undefined` + `cursor: pageParam || undefined` in queryFn so empty-string initial is not sent. Active stays `useQuery` with `.items` unwrap. NEW returned fields: `hasMoreHistory`, `fetchMoreHistory` (gated on `hasNextPage && !isFetchingNextPage`), `isFetchingMoreHistory`. Conditional `refetchInterval` returns `HISTORY_POLL_MS` (10s) when `pages.length ≤ 1`, `false` otherwise. `ExtractionJobsTab` drops `COMPLETE_VISIBLE_LIMIT=10` slice (cap was meaningless once paginated) + renders Load more button below history gated on `hasMoreHistory`, disabled + `jobs.loadingMore` label while `isFetchingMoreHistory`. (D) **Tests.** Unit codec tests (7 cases — 2 roundtrips + 5 decode rejections: invalid base64, non-object payload, missing required fields, bad UUID, bad datetime). BE router tests updated 6 existing + added 3 new (next_cursor in envelope, cursor threaded to repo, malformed → 422). Integration tests unpacked 9 sites + 2 NEW real-Postgres cursor-walk tests. FE hook tests updated 4 existing + 5 new C11 pagination tests. FE component tests updated 1 (caps-removed) + 4 new Load-more tests. i18n locale drift lock +2 paths. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (6 user-approved defaults).** (1) Cursor history-only. (2) Envelope for both groups. (3) base64-JSON opaque cursor. (4) Single Load more below entire history. (5) Drop COMPLETE_VISIBLE_LIMIT. (6) Active polling unchanged. **Closes** D-K19b.1-01 + D-K19b.2-01. **Files: 17** (BE 5 + FE 8 + docs 2 + existing-test updates counted separately). **Verify.** BE `test_extraction_job_status.py` **19/19** + NEW `test_extraction_jobs_cursor.py` **7/7** = 26/26 unit. BE 185/185 adjacent. Integration tests not run in CI (require live Postgres) but signature-fixed + 2 new real-predicate tests. FE knowledge+lib **440/440** GREEN (+8 from C10 baseline 432: 5 useExtractionJobs C11 + 4 ExtractionJobsTab C11, minus 1 caps-test replacement). `tsc --noEmit` clean. Pre-existing BE test_config/SSL failures verified unrelated. **Plan progress**: 23 items / 11 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 4/7 done (C10 ✅ + C11 ✅)** · remaining P3: C12 (dialog UX, L), C13 (Storybook dialogs via MSW, M).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 36 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C10 FS [XL]; timeline entity_id + chronological range filters. Thirty-sixth cycle. First P3 cycle. Cross-service BE+FE: 3 new Cypher WHERE predicates + router entity_id resolution + FE TimelineFilters component (entity search dropdown + chronological range inputs) + TimelineTab wire + i18n × 4 + cross-locale drift lock. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 1 MED + 7 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed MED + 2 regression tests in-cycle, 7 LOW + 1 COSMETIC accepted:** (MED#1) chronological inputs fired BE call per keystroke (no debounce) — user typing "1500" produced 4 BE calls. Fixed: TimelineFilters gained internal `afterInput`/`beforeInput` state + 400ms debounced commit effect + parent-reset sync effects. 2 regression tests: 4-keystroke-coalesce-to-1-commit + parent-reset-no-re-fire. Entity-search dropdown was already debounced via `useDebounced(250ms)`, so chrono inputs now match the same UX. (LOW#2 accept) useEntities fires even when dropdown not rendered (search<2 chars) — pre-existing pattern matching EntityMergeDialog. (LOW#3 accept) whitespace-only entity_id passes `min_length=1` and silently returns empty timeline — no security issue. (LOW#4 accept) no live-Neo4j integration test for participant_candidates Cypher — deferred, unit mocks only. (LOW#5 accept) entity picker no keyboard a11y (arrow/enter/escape) — matches EntityMergeDialog gap. (LOW#6 accept) case-sensitive participant match (pre-existing). (LOW#7 accept) no perf index on chronological_order — defer until profiling. (LOW#8 accept) client-side reversed-range hint + BE 422 double-guard — harmless redundancy. (COSMETIC#9 accept) whitespace-only alias passes `if c` truthy check — Pydantic blocks user input path, only legacy extraction writes could leak. **Four substantive blocks.** (A) **BE repo.** `_LIST_EVENTS_FILTER_WHERE` gains 3 new parameterised predicates: `($after_chronological IS NULL OR e.chronological_order > $after_chronological)` + mirror `before_chronological` + `($participant_candidates IS NULL OR ANY(c IN $participant_candidates WHERE c IN e.participants))`. Semantic: `NULL` = no filter; `[]` = match nothing (used when entity specified-but-not-found); populated list = ANY-match. `list_events_filtered` signature expanded with 3 kwargs + reversed-chrono `ValueError` validation. (B) **BE router.** 3 new Query params with validation (`entity_id: str | None, min_length=1, max_length=200`; chronological ≥ 0). Resolution: `get_entity(user_id=str(jwt_user_id), canonical_id=entity_id)` with JWT-threaded user_id for cross-user safety. Dedup via set-comprehension `{ent.name, ent.canonical_name, *ent.aliases}` + `if c` drops empty strings. Missing entity → `participant_candidates=[]` (NOT `None`) so Cypher's `ANY(c IN [] WHERE ...)` = false → zero rows; this collapses the 404 path to empty timeline per KSA §6.4 anti-existence-leak. Chronological reversed-range mirrors the existing narrative validation → 422. (C) **FE.** `TimelineListParams` +3 optional fields; `listTimeline` URL param threading additive; `useTimeline` queryKey adds 3 filter entries (null sentinels). NEW `TimelineFilters.tsx` component: entity search dropdown (reuses `useEntities` with min-2-chars 250ms debounce matching `EntityMergeDialog`; project-scoped when `projectId` is set) — selected entity renders as chip with X-clear, dropdown uses `onMouseDown` + blur-delay to avoid click-before-blur race. Chronological range: two number inputs with internal debounce (400ms, /review-impl MED#1). Reversed-range hint shows inline while BE remains authority. TimelineTab gains 3 filter state hooks, renders `<TimelineFilters>` between project select and list, resets secondary filters on project change (C8 pattern via existing `handleFilterChange` which also resets pagination offset). (D) **Tests.** BE `test_timeline_api.py` +6 C10 tests (entity_id resolves, not-found-collapses-empty, chronological threaded, reversed → 422, empty-string → 422, all-3-combined). FE NEW `TimelineFilters.test.tsx` 7 tests (renders, entity dropdown happy, entity chip + clear, chrono debounce coalesce 4→1, parent-reset no-re-fire, reversed-range hint, project-scoped search). `useTimeline.test.tsx` +3 C10 tests. `projectState.test.ts` `TIMELINE_KEYS` +9 paths. i18n × 4 locales +9 new keys. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (6 user-approved defaults).** (1) entity_id server-side resolution → participant candidates; no 404 leak on miss. (2) Ordering stays `event_order ASC`; `sort_by` deferred. (3) Chronological range mirrors narrative semantics. (4) Entity picker UX reuses `useEntities`. (5) Cross-user safety via JWT user_id. (6) Entity picker project-scoped when project_id set. **Closes** D-K19e-α-01 + D-K19e-α-03. **Files: 16** (BE 3 + FE 10 + docs 2 + tests of 3 kinds already counted). **Verify.** BE `test_timeline_api.py` **18/18** GREEN (+6 C10); 213/213 BE adjacent entity/event/timeline. FE knowledge+lib **432/432** GREEN (+10 from C9 baseline 422: 6 TimelineFilters + 1 TimelineFilters debounce regression + 3 useTimeline C10). `tsc --noEmit` clean. Pre-existing BE test_config/SSL failures verified unrelated via earlier stash. **Plan progress**: 21 items / 10 cycles · P1 done · P2 DONE (7/7) · **P3 2/5 opened (C10 ✅)** · remaining P3: C11 (cursor pagination), C12 (dialog UX), C13 (Storybook dialogs).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 35 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C9 FS [XL]; entity optimistic concurrency via If-Match + unlock endpoint. Thirty-fifth cycle. Eighth Gap Closure P2 cycle; **P2 tier DONE (7/7)** after C9. Cross-service BE+FE: Entity.version field + coalesce backfill + atomic FOREACH Cypher + unlock helper + 4 Cypher write sites bumping version + If-Match router contract + NEW /unlock endpoint + FE ifMatch threading + NEW useUnlockEntity + Unlock CTA in detail panel + i18n × 4 + cross-locale drift lock. **/review-impl caught 1 HIGH + 0 MED + 4 LOW + 1 COSMETIC; fixed HIGH + 1 LOW in-cycle, 3 LOWs documented-accept, 1 cosmetic skipped:** (HIGH) pre-C9 entities permanently uneditable — `_node_to_entity` coalesced missing version to 1 but all 4 Cypher `coalesce(e.version, 0)` / `coalesce(t.version, 0)` defaulted to 0, so FE's `If-Match: W/"1"` always 412'd against `current_version=0`. Unit tests mocked `run_write` and never hit this path. Fixed: aligned all 4 Cypher coalesce defaults to 1 + added docstring invariant note linking the two sides; (LOW#2) added source-scan regression lock `test_cypher_version_coalesce_default_matches_read_path` that reads the 4 Cypher string literals at import time and asserts absence of `coalesce(.version, 0)` — cheap, no Neo4j needed; also added `test_update_entity_pre_c9_node_with_expected_version_1_applies` unit round-trip at expected_version=1 with applied=True; (LOW#3 accept) no live-Neo4j integration test — source-level lock suffices; (LOW#4 accept) Entity test fixtures across codebase aren't typed as Entity so new required fields aren't enforced at stub sites — broader refactor; (LOW#5 accept) unlock bumps version on no-op (already-unlocked) — idempotent, cheap, keeps "any user-facing write bumps" invariant uniform; (COSMETIC#6 skip) `patch_entity` / `get_entity_detail` param ordering — functional. **Four substantive blocks.** (A) **BE repo.** NEW `Entity.version: int = 1` Pydantic field; `_node_to_entity` coalesces missing → 1 (pre-C9 backfill). Atomic FOREACH `_UPDATE_ENTITY_FIELDS_CYPHER`: `WITH e, coalesce(e.version, 1) AS current_version` + `FOREACH (_ IN CASE WHEN current_version = $expected_version THEN [1] ELSE [] END | SET ...)` + `RETURN e, current_version = $expected_version AS applied`. One Cypher round-trip: applied=True → helper returns updated Entity; applied=False → helper raises `VersionMismatchError(current=Entity)` (Entity is pre-write state, unmutated). MATCH-no-row → helper returns None (router 404). NEW `unlock_entity_user_edited` + `_UNLOCK_ENTITY_CYPHER`: no If-Match, idempotent flag flip + version bump. Version bumps added at 4 user-facing write sites: update FOREACH (`e.version = current_version + 1`), unlock (`e.version = coalesce(e.version, 1) + 1`), `_MERGE_ENTITY_CYPHER` ON CREATE (`e.version = 1`) + ON MATCH (`e.version = coalesce(e.version, 1) + 1`), `_MERGE_UPDATE_TARGET_CYPHER` (`t.version = coalesce(t.version, 1) + 1`). System-internal writes (anchor recompute / archive / promote / unlink_glossary) deliberately NOT bumped to avoid spurious 412s on user baseline. (B) **BE router.** Local `_parse_if_match` + `_etag` helpers inline (matches projects.py + summaries.py duplication convention). PATCH strict If-Match contract: 428 Precondition Required on missing, 412 Precondition Failed with `current.model_dump(mode="json")` body + fresh `ETag: W/"{version}"` header on mismatch, ETag header on success. GET detail sets ETag. NEW `POST /entities/{id}/unlock` — no If-Match, returns updated Entity + ETag. `VersionMismatchError.current` widened (duck-typed Any) to accept Entity alongside Project/Summary without pulling Entity import across the Postgres↔Neo4j repo-module boundary. (C) **FE.** `Entity` wire gains required `version: number` + `user_edited: boolean`. `updateEntity(entityId, body, ifMatchVersion, token)` 4-arg signature sends `If-Match: W/"N"` via existing `ifMatch()` helper. NEW `unlockEntity(entityId, token)` POST. `useUpdateEntity` threads ifMatchVersion + onError invalidates detail query on 412 so next-open sees fresh baseline. NEW `useUnlockEntity` hook. `EntityEditDialog` passes entity.version + 412 toast (`entities.edit.conflict`) + dialog close. `EntityDetailPanel` NEW Unlock section gated on `entity.user_edited`: uses `window.confirm` for lightweight flag-flip recovery (non-destructive). i18n 4 locales: 6 new keys (`entities.detail.{unlock, unlockHint, unlockConfirm, unlockSuccess, unlockFailed}` + `entities.edit.conflict`). `projectState.test.ts` ENTITIES_KEYS +6 paths for cross-locale drift lock. (D) **Tests.** NEW `test_entities_mutations.py` (8 unit tests including /review-impl HIGH regression locks). `test_entities_browse_api.py` updated 4 existing PATCH + 8 new C9 (428/422/412 with body+ETag, ETag on GET, unlock happy/404/no-If-Match). NEW FE `EntityDetailPanel.test.tsx` (4 tests). `useEntityMutations.test.tsx` +3 C9. `EntityEditDialog.test.tsx` +1 conflict. **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY (6 user-approved defaults).** (1) /unlock no If-Match. (2) Unlock flips user_edited=false only (no alias reset). (3) COALESCE backfill, no DDL migration. (4) Entity.version wire field. (5) 412 body=current Entity + fresh ETag. (6) 428 strict. **Closes** D-K19d-γa-01 + D-K19d-γa-02. **P2 tier 7/7 — DONE.** **Files: 16** (BE 5 + FE 9 + docs 2). **Verify.** BE entity tests **34/34** (+14 C9 + 2 /review-impl regression locks); BE adjacent 163/163; FE knowledge+lib **422/422** (+8 from C8 baseline 414); `tsc --noEmit` clean; 3 pre-existing BE test_config/SSL failures verified unrelated via earlier stash. **Plan progress**: 19 items / 9 cycles · P1 done · **P2 DONE (7/7)** · next is P3 (C10+).
- (prior) Session 51 cycle 34 summary — Track 2/3 Gap Closure C8 FS [XL]; drawer-search source_type filter + in-card highlight + BE facet counts. Thirty-fourth cycle. Seventh Gap Closure P2 cycle; P2 tier 6/7 done. Cross-service BE+FE cycle: new Neo4j repo helper + Cypher WHERE branch + Literal enum router param + response facet counts (user addendum) + new FE util + new FE component + consumer wiring + i18n × 4 locales. **/review-impl caught 0 HIGH + 3 MED + 4 LOW + 2 COSMETIC; fixed 7 in-cycle:** (MED#1) cross-locale `sourceType.*` key presence test added to `projectState.test.ts` (5 new paths in DRAWERS_KEYS); (MED#2) created `DRAWER_SOURCE_TYPES` tuple in api.ts as single source of truth — `EMPTY_COUNTS` in `useDrawerSearch` + `OPTIONS` in `DrawerSearchFilters` derive from it via `Object.fromEntries` / `.map`, so BE adding a 4th type means ONE edit in api.ts not 3; (MED#3) filter reset on project change in `RawDrawersTab` — holding "Chapter" across projects hid hits in chat-only projects; added regression test; (LOW#4) NEW `test_passages_count.py` — 4 unit tests using mocked `run_read` (pad missing keys, drop+warn on DB drift, empty-result all-zeros, embedding_model forwarded); (LOW#5+6) NEW `DrawerSearchFilters.test.tsx` — 7 tests covering renders/counts-on-typed-pills-only/click-typed/click-Any-after-typed (split because browser does not refire change on checked radio)/checked-reflects-value/disabled-cascades-via-fieldset/missing-key-fallback-to-0; (LOW#7 accept+lock) regression test for OR-regex leftmost-alternative priority in `highlightTokens` — `('he|hell', 'gi')` on "hello" matches "he" only, anchoring the documented simplicity against a well-meaning "fix"; (COSMETIC#9) ternary+?? parenthesised for reader clarity. Skipped COSMETIC#8 (onChange arrow vs direct — React-stable). **Three substantive blocks.** (A) **BE — repo + router.** NEW `count_passages_by_source_type` in `passages.py` runs `MATCH (p:Passage) WHERE user_id+project_id AND ($embedding_model IS NULL OR p.embedding_model = $embedding_model) RETURN source_type, count(*) AS n`; returns dict with EVERY KNOWN_SOURCE_TYPES key padded to 0. Unknown `source_type` from Neo4j (data drift) is dropped + WARNING logged pointing at `KNOWN_SOURCE_TYPES` for extension. `find_passages_by_vector` gains `source_type: str | None = None` kwarg; Cypher template adds `AND ($source_type IS NULL OR node.source_type = $source_type)` matching existing NULL-OR pattern. Router adds `DrawerSourceType = Literal['chapter','chat','glossary']` enum Query param (FastAPI 422s unknowns) + `source_type_counts` response field (user addendum). Count session runs AFTER project 404 check but BEFORE the three early-return branches (no-embedding, unsupported-dim, empty-embedding) so "not indexed yet" states still surface coverage. Whitespace short-circuit returns zero-padded counts (no DB hit). (B) **FE — util + hook + filter component.** NEW `highlightTokens(text, query) → ReactNode[]` util: whitespace-split, ≥2-char tokens (CJK single-char documented limitation), regex escape for safety, case-insensitive match, `<mark>` wrap with `bg-yellow-500/25` tailwind class. React escapes the text children → no XSS. `useDrawerSearch` gains `sourceTypeCounts` return field + `EMPTY_COUNTS` fallback derived from `DRAWER_SOURCE_TYPES` tuple; queryKey includes `source_type ?? null` so changing filter triggers a new BE call. NEW `DrawerSearchFilters` component: native radios inside `<fieldset role="radiogroup">` for free WAI-ARIA keyboard semantics (arrow keys, Tab, Enter/Space); pills styled via Tailwind `peer-checked:` on the input's sibling label. OPTIONS derived from `DRAWER_SOURCE_TYPES` tuple (prepended "Any"). Counts shown only on typed pills (not "Any"). (C) **FE — wiring.** `RawDrawersTab` owns `[sourceType, setSourceType]` state, passes `handleStaleOffset`-style pattern via direct setter (React-stable). Project select onChange now resets `sourceType` to null (MED#3 fix). `DrawerResultCard` gains optional `query?: string` prop; when set, wraps `textPreview(text)` with `highlightTokens`. i18n 4 locales add `drawers.filters.sourceType.{label,any,chapter,chat,glossary}` (en/ja/vi/zh-TW). **Design decisions locked at CLARIFY.** (1) User addendum: BE returns facet counts per source_type. (2) Single-select pill filter (not multi-select). (3) Literal enum for strict validation. (4) Native `<input type="radio">` for free keyboard a11y. (5) Highlight ≥2-char tokens, whitespace-split, case-insensitive. **Closes** D-K19e-γa-01 + D-K19e-γb-01. **Files: 16 (C8 core 12 + /review-impl 4).** BE: MOD `passages.py`, `drawers.py`, `test_drawers_api.py`; NEW `test_passages_count.py`. FE: NEW `highlightTokens.ts` + test, NEW `DrawerSearchFilters.tsx` + test, MOD `api.ts`, `useDrawerSearch.ts` + test, `RawDrawersTab.tsx` + test, `DrawerResultCard.tsx`, `projectState.test.ts`. i18n `knowledge.json` × 4. **Verify.** BE `test_drawers_api.py` 20/20 + new `test_passages_count.py` 4/4 = **24/24**.
